company.description: Lightbringer is a Swedish AI-native patent platform that pairs agentic AI with in-house patent attorneys to handle the full IP lifecycle for deep tech companies, from invention capture and application drafting to portfolio management and competitive intelligence. Its fixed-price subscription model cuts patent filing timelines from roughly two months to days at about half the cost of a traditional law firm, positioning it as a direct alternative to established patent firms. Founded in 2023 and headquartered in Malmö, the company serves 200+ deep tech clients across 17 countries.
company.name: Lightbringer

company.description: Causa Prima operates a two-sided agent-to-agent network for B2B financial operations, deploying AI agents on behalf of both buyers and suppliers to automatically handle invoices, disputes, payment terms and early-payment discounts. Unlike platforms that automate only one side of a transaction, its shared network lets both parties' agents negotiate and settle in real time. The Madrid- and Munich-based fintech, founded by Taulia co-founder Maex Ament alongside Finoa co-founder Henrik Gebbing and Philip Stanislaus, says it already serves more than 3,000 active accounts.
company.name: Causa Prima

company.description: Cortea builds AI "audit quality" agents that review audit reports, disclosure notes and financial statements before sign-off, flagging inconsistencies, filing errors and compliance gaps that human reviewers can miss. Founded in Berlin in 2024 and now headquartered in London, it targets mid-sized audit firms while also plugging into Big Four and boutique practices, with its agents grounded in auditing standards rather than general-purpose AI.
company.name: Cortea

company.description: SolarSquare is a Mumbai-based full-stack residential rooftop solar company, founded in 2015, that handles the entire homeowner journey from design and installation to financing and ongoing maintenance. It began as a B2B solar provider before pivoting to the consumer market in 2021, and has served nearly 50,000 homes across India. The company positions itself as one of India's leading residential solar brands amid rapid adoption of rooftop solar in the country.
company.name: SolarSquare

company.description: ContraVault AI is an AI-powered procurement intelligence platform that helps enterprises identify relevant government and private tenders, assess fulfillment viability, and automate bid and RFP submissions. Its toolset spans risk detection, clause negotiation, document summarization and go/no-go analysis, serving customers across construction, energy, power, defence and aerospace. Founded in 2024 and based in India with a new US presence, it counts large Indian groups such as Voltas, Shapoorji Pallonji and Adani Group among its clients.
company.name: ContraVault AI

company.description: Rocapine is a Paris-based wellness app publisher that applies the mobile-gaming industry's rapid, iterative publishing model to consumer health and wellbeing apps. It partners with development studios to test many app concepts and scale the winners using generative AI to personalize the user experience, with a portfolio that includes Harmony (cycle tracking), That Girl (habit building), and Unchaind. Founded in 2024, the studio reports over $6M ARR across its portfolio within nine months.
company.name: Rocapine

company.description: AttoTude is a Menlo Park, California-based developer of interconnect technology for AI and hyperscale data center infrastructure. The company builds wired terahertz interconnects, pairing an ASIC-based transceiver (AttoEngine) with low-loss dielectric fiber wiring (AttoWire) to move data at high per-lane rates as an alternative to conventional copper and optical links. Founded in 2024 by Dave Welch and Joy Laskar, it targets the interconnect bottlenecks that constrain scaling of AI compute.
company.name: AttoTude

company.description: Waniwani builds AI distribution infrastructure for quote-based financial services, letting insurers, mortgage lenders and similar vendors sell directly inside AI assistants like ChatGPT, Claude and Copilot. It offers an open-source SDK plus paid modules that turn natural-dialogue agents into real-time, personalized quoting channels, and was the company behind the first OpenAI-approved insurer-built app on ChatGPT. Founded in early 2026 and headquartered in San Francisco with offices in New York and Paris, it serves clients across Europe, LatAm, the Middle East, Korea and Australia.
company.name: Waniwani

company.description: AutoVRse is a Bengaluru-based enterprise VR training company founded in 2016 that builds AI-powered immersive training and field-assistance tools for industrial workforces. Its VRseBuilder platform captures expert operational workflows and converts them into VR training modules, digital twins, and AI-guided field support for sectors like manufacturing, heavy industry, automotive, and power. The company serves 50+ enterprise customers globally, including Amazon, Shell, Bosch, NTPC, and JSW Steel.
company.name: AutoVRse

company.description: New Energy Transport (NET) is an Australian zero-emission road freight company building what it bills as the country's first vertically integrated electric trucking platform, operating electric prime movers powered by renewable energy. NET combines its own trucks, charging depots and energy procurement in-house so it can offer electric line-haul freight at diesel-equivalent cost, and is constructing Australia's largest planned heavy electric truck depot at Wilton, NSW. The company deploys modular mobile ultra-fast charging units along freight corridors to extend zero-emission haulage into regional routes.
company.name: New Energy Transport

company.description: Sarvam AI is an Indian artificial intelligence company headquartered in Bengaluru, developing full-stack generative AI with sovereign models that deeply understand India's culture and diversity. Founded in 2023 by Dr. Vivek Raghavan (UIDAI architect) and Dr. Pratyush Kumar, the company builds multimodal foundational models and AI cloud infrastructure tailored for India, supporting population-scale deployment across Indian languages.
company.name: Sarvam AI

company.description: Hydra Host is a data-center software company and Nvidia GPU server marketplace that connects businesses needing AI compute with data centers able to supply it. Founded in 2021 selling automation software to crypto-mining operators, it pivoted to brokering Nvidia GPU servers as AI demand surged, and now manages over 15,000 GPUs across its platform.
company.name: Hydra Host

company.description: Cellares is the first Integrated Development and Manufacturing Organization (IDMO) providing automated cell therapy manufacturing through its Cell Shuttle platform, enabling scalable production of cell therapies with reduced costs and failure rates.
company.name: Cellares

company.description: Podium Automation specializes in rapid design and fabrication of custom UL 508A-listed industrial control panels, using software and automation to deliver panels in days instead of months.
company.name: Podium Automation

company.description: Radical Numerics is a San Francisco AI research lab building toward "general biological intelligence" - multimodal AI systems designed to read, write and design across DNA, RNA and proteins for applications spanning human health and biodefense. Founded by the team behind the Evo and Evo 2 genomic language models (Eric Nguyen, Michael Poli, Stefano Massaroli and Armin Thomas, several with Stanford and Liquid AI backgrounds), it is developing next-generation models such as the Omnii genomic language model. The company launched out of stealth in June 2026 with a $50M seed round.
company.name: Radical Numerics

company.description: Undo is a Cambridge, UK software company that builds time-travel (reversible) debugging technology, producing deterministic recordings of a program's runtime execution so developers can replay and pinpoint the root cause of failures across complex Linux codebases in C/C++, Go, Rust, Java and Kotlin. In 2026 it refocused the technology toward AI software development, offering automated root-cause analysis for AI coding agents that cannot diagnose bugs from source code and logs alone. Its tools are used by major engineering organizations including AWS, NVIDIA, AMD, Bloomberg, Cisco, Siemens and SAP.
company.name: Undo

company.description: NewCore is an identity security company building a platform that discovers, governs and secures identities across the enterprise, treating human employees, machine accounts and autonomous AI agents as first-class identities within a single architecture. Its product offers identity discovery, phishing-resistant authentication and lifecycle governance for agentic workloads, positioning itself against incumbents like Microsoft and Okta. Founded by Dome9 co-founder Zohar Alon with CTO Amihai Neiderman and commercial chief Erez Yarkoni, the company operates from Tel Aviv and San Francisco.
company.name: NewCore

company.description: Sirius Game is an Italian edtech startup, founded by a female team led by CEO Laura Cesaro and based between South Tyrol (Bolzano) and Trento. It builds research-backed, game-based learning solutions that use interactive challenges and missions to make education more engaging and to develop transversal skills such as critical thinking and collaboration. The company serves both schools and corporate training, and recently launched JOY, an educational product for primary schools created with Il Gruppo Editoriale La Scuola and the Free University of Bozen-Bolzano.
company.name: Sirius Game

company.description: Bestie Bite is an AI-driven app that allows users to discover and review restaurants, bars, and hotels through short, authentic videos. The platform converts user-generated content into AI-processed insights, enabling hospitality businesses to analyze customer sentiment and service quality in real time. Users can earn points and rewards by submitting video reviews and completing missions, with the app offering personalized recommendations and a gamified experience for restaurant discovery.
company.name: Bestie Bite

company.description: Deeply is a Seoul-based sound AI company that builds non-verbal acoustic analysis ("machine hearing") technology. Its flagship Listen AI Industrial solution automates the acoustic inspection of manufactured parts, detecting defects and equipment anomalies for quality control and predictive maintenance in smart factories. Founded in 2017, the company reports inspection accuracy above 99.87% and supplies automakers, Hyosung Electric, Korail and the Republic of Korea Army.
company.name: Deeply

company.description: Sentinel DeepActive is a South Korean fintech startup that builds proprietary AI for active investment management. Its in-house Sentinel AI, a transformer-based foundation model, performs daily rebalancing across equities, ETFs and commodities, and underpins a retirement-pension robo-advisor aimed at long-term, downside-protected asset management. The company is a TIPS-program participant developing AI-driven pension and wealth-management products for the Korean market.
company.name: Sentinel DeepActive

company.description: Vaja AB is a Stockholm-based renewable-energy startup, founded in 2023 by serial entrepreneurs Henrik Eskilsson (co-founder of Tobii) and Anders Olsson (founder of Soldags), that develops vertical solar-tracking systems for high-latitude solar farms. Its VajaTrack product uses an aerodynamically balanced, wind-responsive design that pivots panels to follow low-hanging sun while cutting wind loads, producing roughly 25-30% more energy per year than fixed arrays at northern latitudes. The company is opening pilot projects with solar developers and turnkey installations for Swedish landowners.
company.name: Vaja

company.description: Pints AI is a Singapore-based enterprise AI company that helps regulated financial institutions deploy auditable, production-ready AI agents. Its Autothought platform connects to the core systems of banks and insurers to automate work such as underwriting, claims and onboarding while producing a full audit trail for every AI-assisted decision, meeting the compliance requirements of regulators including the Monetary Authority of Singapore, the Reserve Bank of India and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority. The company has live deployments across Singapore, Hong Kong, India and the US.
company.name: Pints AI

company.description: CollimateHealth is a Munich-based medtech startup developing microbeam radiation therapy, a cancer treatment that delivers arrays of ultra-thin, micrometre-scale radiation beams to destroy tumours while sparing healthy tissue and activating an anti-tumour immune response. Spun out of nearly a decade of research at the Technical University of Munich, the company has miniaturised the beamline needed to generate microbeams down to roughly 10 square metres, an order of magnitude smaller than the stadium-scale synchrotrons traditionally required. It works with academic and clinical partners including TUM University Hospital and The Royal Marsden in London.
company.name: CollimateHealth

company.description: Qorelo is a Berlin-based startup that builds an AI-powered intelligence layer to automate and accelerate SAP-based ERP migration and upgrade projects, automating the consultant-heavy documentation and delivery workstreams of SAP Activate. The company says its platform can cut project timelines by up to 45 percent, addressing the shortage of delivery capacity enterprises face ahead of SAP's 2027 ECC support deadline. Founded in late 2025, it already serves enterprise customers in the DACH region, including a major German automotive group.
company.name: Qorelo

company.description: Zumutor Biologics is an immuno-oncology biotech founded in 2013 that develops antibody-based immunotherapies which activate the body's natural killer (NK) cells to attack tumors and improve cancer treatment outcomes. The company is headquartered in the US and runs an R&D laboratory in Bengaluru, India, where it is advancing its clinical-stage cancer therapy pipeline.
company.name: Zumutor Biologics

company.description: Maritime Robotics is a Norwegian developer of autonomous and remotely operated sea drone systems, combining autonomous navigation, vessel design and modular payload technology into integrated uncrewed maritime platforms. Founded in 2005 and based in Trondheim, it serves commercial and defence customers across offshore energy, hydrography, environmental monitoring, fisheries and maritime security. The company has delivered hundreds of autonomous systems worldwide as customers move from pilot projects to fleet-level deployment.
company.name: Maritime Robotics

company.description: Artus AI is a Hyderabad-based startup building an AI-native product management platform that helps software teams decide what to build before development begins. Its system acts as an AI product manager, using specialized agents to evaluate product ideas against customer value, feasibility, cost, risk and business impact. Founded by Yash Vahi and Ashrey Ignise, the platform is used by US government agencies, county technology teams and product managers at Fortune 500 companies.
company.name: Artus AI

company.description: Copperlane is an AI-native mortgage origination platform whose flagship product, Penny, acts as an autonomous AI loan officer that analyzes borrower documents, income, assets and credit files to surface recommendations and draft explanations for human staff. The platform automates document collection, borrower communication and application processing, aiming to cut loan review and preapproval work from hours to minutes for loan officers and mortgage operations teams. Founded in 2026 and backed by Y Combinator (W26).
company.name: Copperlane

company.description: SCAIT (operated by MindMode GmbH) is a Swiss proptech startup whose AI platform helps construction firms analyze complex tenders, flag contract risks, and automatically generate bid submissions, replacing a manual and error-prone process. The company has built early product with innovative Swiss contractors and partnered with the Swiss Contractors Association, giving it access to roughly 2,400 member companies. It targets the Swiss market first, with planned expansion into the wider DACH region.
company.name: SCAIT

company.description: Halo Drive is a Glasgow-based startup building vehicle-aware AI for commercial fleets. Its technology pairs on-vehicle intelligence (Halo Core) with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) networking and a fleet platform, ViewPort, that connects vehicles to live energy, grid, weather, infrastructure and traffic data to coordinate movement, charging and deployment. The company targets fleet operators and OEMs with autonomy and energy-optimisation capabilities, including vehicle-to-grid use of idle assets.
company.name: Halo Drive

company.description: dAIrect is an Italian startup that makes hotels and tourism operators discoverable and bookable inside generative-AI assistants such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity and DeepSeek. Its technology converts hotel website content into structured data that AI systems can read, helping properties win direct bookings and reduce dependence on online travel intermediaries. The company works with around 100 properties and groups including VOIhotels, Mokinba and Italian Hotel Group, and is expanding across Spain, Portugal and Greece.
company.name: dAIrect

company.description: Fraudio is a payments fraud-prevention company that runs a centralized, real-time AI engine to detect fraud, scams and money laundering across the card-payments chain. Its SaaS platform uses machine learning and behavioral analytics on networked transaction data to flag suspicious activity in milliseconds while limiting false declines, serving merchants, payment service providers, acquirers and card issuers. Founded in 2019 with a Dutch base and a growing engineering and product hub in Portugal, its clients include payments firms such as GnosisPay and PayTabs.
company.name: Fraudio

company.description: Rematch is a French media platform for grassroots and amateur sports, founded in 2017 in Bordeaux. Its app and technology let fans, parents, clubs and volunteers film local matches, auto-edit the highlights and distribute them across social platforms, building what the company positions as the global media infrastructure for amateur sport. It expanded into the US in 2024 and surpassed €1M in revenue and over a billion organic views by 2025.
company.name: Rematch

company.description: HIT Mag is a French deeptech startup based in Plouzané, Brittany, spun out of the Lab-STICC laboratory (UBO/CNRS) in Brest. It develops and manufactures high-performance permanent magnets free of rare earths, using a dual materials-and-process innovation aimed at securing a sovereign, eco-friendly European magnet supply chain at competitive cost. Its magnets target automotive and electric mobility, robotics, wind energy, telecommunications and space, with CNES as an early customer for space applications.
company.name: Hit Mag

company.description: Réempro is a French circular-economy company that industrialises the reuse of building materials and professional equipment. It runs the full reuse chain — diagnosis, selective deconstruction, waste management, reconditioning and resale of second-hand materials and furniture — for construction, industry and public-sector clients across France and Belgium. About 70% of its activity is reusing building materials such as sanitaryware, bricks and technical flooring.
company.name: Réempro

company.description:
A fitter world for all!

This has been our motto since we began as a small group of people trying to help each other get fit. Today, FITTR has evolved into a tech-enabled community-first health and fitness platform with more than 3 million users from all over the world.

FITTR caters to individuals as well as corporate clients. Whether you’re a fitness enthusiast or an expert, we’re the right platform for you!

What sets FITTR apart?

*Quantifiable Results: FITTR’s science-backed methods and personalised approach are game-changers when it comes to helping users achieve their fitness goals.

*Human Touch: Our expert coaches go the extra mile to help their clients transform and stay healthy.

*Community: With FITTR, you’re never alone on your fitness journey! Connect with other fitness enthusiasts, share stories, and get inspired through our inclusive community.

Apart from the various services offered, Fittr also hosts its flagship Transformation Challenge (TC) event multiple times a year. This event aims to encourage fitness enthusiasts to transform their lives and become better & healthier by competing against themselves for 12 weeks. This initiative has been a huge success since its inception, with people from every walk of life participating and achieving inspiring transformations by adopting fitness as a lifestyle!

To date, we've been a part of 300,000+ success stories and aim to make 50 million people fit while creating 100,000 meaningful career opportunities in the fitness industry.

Fittr has raised a total of US$13.5 million in funding to date. Its most recent Series A round was led by Dream Capital and Elysian Park Ventures for US$11.5 million.

We have come a long way, but our journey has just begun—and we have miles to go before we make the world a healthier place for all!

To know more about Fittr, please visit fittr.com or use this link: linktr.ee/fittrwithsquats

Interested in joining us in our mission? Hit us up at career@fittr.com
company.name: FITTR

company.description: Dioseve is a Tokyo-based reproductive-medicine startup developing assisted reproductive technology built on iPS-cell differentiation. Its lead product, ReproNest (DIOS-101), is a co-culture system using iPS-derived ovarian support cells to improve the in vitro maturation of immature eggs, aiming to reduce the hormonal stimulation and clinic visits that fertility patients face. The company is pursuing treatments for age-related and hereditary infertility and is backed by Japanese deep-tech and pharmaceutical investors.
company.name: Dioseve

company.description: Kukulcan is a Tokyo-based agritech startup building cultivation-support AI and a B2B matching platform for agricultural produce. Its system forecasts harvest volumes and timing from farm-entered data, helping food businesses source surplus and non-standard vegetables before harvest to cut on-farm food loss. The company also runs initiatives that turn discarded produce into value-added products as part of its food-waste-reduction mission.
company.name: Kukulcan

company.description: Trifa is a Tokyo-based travel-tech startup that operates an overseas eSIM app letting travelers buy mobile data and connect across 200+ countries and regions without a physical SIM or pocket Wi-Fi. Launched in 2021, it has passed 2 million cumulative downloads and ranks as Japan's top overseas-eSIM app, and is expanding from connectivity into adjacent travel services such as payments and booking.
company.name: Trifa

company.description: Clip is Mexico’s leading digital payments and commerce enablement platform serving small and mid-sized businesses. The company provides point-of-sale devices, digital payment acceptance, financial services such as BNPL and business loans, and merchant APIs to help merchants digitize operations and grow sales. Founded in 2012 and headquartered in Mexico City, Clip focuses on converting cash-only merchants to digital payments across Mexico and Argentina. The company emphasizes ease of use, broad payment acceptance, and customer support for SMBs.
company.name: Clip

company.description: Friday4:30 is a London-based B2B SaaS platform that gives food and drink brands an end-to-end system for managing product recalls and supply-chain incidents, from first alert through to resolution. It replaces the spreadsheets and email chains mid-market manufacturers typically rely on during a crisis with structured, live incident response. Founded in 2025 by incident-management expert Emma Sykes and her son Jack Sykes, the company targets the product-recall and food-safety crisis-management market.
company.name: Friday4:30

company.description: Rekise Marine is a Bengaluru-based marine robotics company that designs, builds and integrates autonomous surface and underwater vessels for defence and commercial maritime applications, powered by its own in-house autonomy software. Its portfolio includes the Jaldoot autonomous surface vessel, the Swadheen autonomous survey vessel, and Jalkapi, an extra-large autonomous underwater vehicle developed under the Indian Navy iDEX ADITI program and among the largest unmanned underwater platforms being built in India. Founded in 2017, the company partners with Indian shipyards such as Goa Shipyard Limited and GRSE for vessel construction.
company.name: Rekise Marine

company.description: Trevel is a Gurugram-based premium electric-vehicle ride-hailing platform serving Delhi NCR, offering airport transfers, intra-city rides, and hourly rentals with a focus on reliability, fixed pricing, and zero-cancellation service. Founded in September 2025 by Mishu Ahluwalia, Rishi Raj Kochhar, and Sahil Jindal, it operates an all-electric fleet (MG, Kia, and BYD models) and positions itself as a professional-service alternative to incumbents like Ola and Uber. The company is scaling its fleet across Delhi NCR with vehicle-procurement MoUs with MG Motor India and Kia India.
company.name: Trevel

company.description: Arkeon Technologies is a Gothenburg-based deep-tech startup and Chalmers University spin-out building manufacturing technology for superconducting quantum chips. Its method tunes qubit frequencies after fabrication by driving controlled current pulse-trains through Josephson junction barriers at room temperature, tightening frequency placement to raise yield and lower production costs. The company targets makers of superconducting quantum processors at industrial firms and research institutions.
company.name: Arkeon

company.description: Compuvi is a San Francisco-based LegalTech and RegTech AI company, founded in 2025 by Ataberk Ciftlikli with engineering rooted in Istanbul. Its platform Confinaid is a preventive legal and compliance risk intelligence tool that detects and flags compliance risk at the point of creation across communications, documents, and workflows, combining AI classification with human-in-the-loop review. It targets regulated enterprises in financial services, healthcare, legal, telecom, and energy.
company.name: Compuvi

company.description: Silicon Quantum Computing (SQC) is a Sydney-based global leader in quantum computing, founded in 2017 at the University of New South Wales (UNSW) by Professor Michelle Simmons. SQC is the only company in the world capable of manufacturing quantum chips with atomic precision using its proprietary PAQMan™ process, while delivering quantum-enhanced AI products for enterprises and governments today. The company's products include Watermelon (a quantum machine learning system) and Quantum Twins (a quantum simulator for molecule and materials discovery), and it can design, produce, and test new quantum chips in under one week.
company.name: Silicon Quantum Computing

company.description: Fold is a Bengaluru-based personal finance app that lets Indian consumers automatically track and organize their money across bank accounts, credit cards, UPI, loans, and investments such as mutual funds, stocks, EPF, NPS, and fixed deposits. It pulls data securely through the RBI Account Aggregator framework rather than credential sharing, and layers on tools like spending insights, credit-score monitoring, shared family "Spaces", and an AI assistant. The company positions itself as financial-wellness and automation infrastructure for India's growing base of retail investors.
company.name: Fold

company.description: Satispay is an Italian fintech operating a mobile payment network that lets users send and receive money and pay merchants directly from their bank balance, independent of debit and credit card rails. Launched in 2015 and now serving over 6.5 million users and 450,000+ merchants, it has expanded beyond payments into corporate welfare, value-added services, and a growing suite of investment and pension products. It became Italy's second unicorn in 2022 and is valued in excess of €1 billion.
company.name: Satispay

company.description: Smart Craft develops a cloud-based manufacturing execution system (MES) SaaS for Japanese factories, unifying process management, quality control, and inventory tracking in a single platform. The Tokyo-based company targets the digital transformation of manufacturing shop floors that still rely on paper, Excel, and whiteboards, helping address Japan's skilled-worker shortage through data-driven operations.
company.name: Smart Craft

company.description: Genspark is a Palo Alto, CA based technology company that builds agentic AI for 1B+ global knowledge workers. Founded by veterans from Microsoft, Google, Meta, YouTube, and Pinterest, Genspark orchestrates AI agents to transform business intent into finished deliverables - from board presentations, deep financial models, business ready documents, to full stack web applications, mobile apps, and even video games. Built for knowledge workers who lead, not just act.
company.name: Genspark

company.description: Precision oncology startup that combines genomic testing with AI-powered clinical intelligence to enable personalized cancer treatment decisions for underrepresented patient populations.
company.name: 4baseCare

company.description: ShopAgentic is a German startup building a native agentic commerce system: a squad of specialised AI agents that each own a single e-commerce function end-to-end, from catalogue and dynamic pricing to customer service and fulfilment, while the merchant keeps strategic control. It targets the roughly half of e-commerce running on proprietary, custom-built stacks, working alongside existing infrastructure or standalone. The company was founded in December 2025 by Alexander Ringsdorff and Kai-Thomas Krause, the team behind omnichannel platform NewStore.
company.name: ShopAgentic

company.description: Equal AI is an Indian startup whose Android app deploys an AI voice assistant that screens incoming calls on a user's behalf, gathers the caller's reason, and returns a text summary, transcript, and quick-reply options. Built for India's high spam-call volume, it supports more than 10 languages including code-mixed speech and has grown past one million monthly active users since its 2024 launch. Founded in 2022 by Keshav Reddy, the company also operates KYC and financial-data verification services that predate the consumer call assistant.
company.name: Equal AI

company.description: Agenz is a Moroccan proptech company operating an end-to-end real estate platform that combines property valuation tools, market data analytics, software for real estate professionals, and a transaction marketplace for buyers. Founded in 2021 by brothers Malik and Badr Belkeziz, it targets the transparency and data gaps in Morocco's traditionally informal, broker-driven property market. By May 2026 the platform recorded more than 730,000 monthly visits, placing it among the country's leading property platforms by traffic.
company.name: Agenz

company.description: Remedy Science is a dermatologist-developed skincare brand founded by board-certified dermatologist Dr. Muneeb Shah, the creator known online as @DermDoctor. Launched in 2024, the brand translates an in-clinic, evidence-based approach into accessible products spanning cleansers, treatments, moisturizers and targeted solutions, all formulated without fragrances or common irritants. It pairs an education-first content strategy with omnichannel distribution across its direct-to-consumer site, Amazon and Target.
company.name: Remedy Science

company.description: Definic (formerly Nordics.io) is a Slovak startup building an AI vendor intelligence platform for enterprise IT procurement. It helps large organizations define requirements, match software and services vendors using real project-delivery data rather than CVs or rates, benchmark market pricing, and track vendor performance, replacing spreadsheet-driven sourcing. Founded in 2020 in Kosice, the platform spans 600+ IT suppliers across more than 40 countries and counts large enterprises such as Siemens Healthineers, banks and Big Four consultancies among its users.
company.name: Definic

company.description: Mimir is an Oslo-based startup building an AI-native customer support and operations platform for B2C e-commerce brands. Its AI agent resolves customer conversations and handles operational tasks such as order management, deliveries and returns across email, chat and social, integrating directly with merchants' commerce stacks. The company reached profitability without external funding and serves roughly 60 brands across the Nordics and Europe.
company.name: Mimir

company.description: Project Prometheus is a secretive AI startup co-led by Jeff Bezos and Vik Bajaj, focusing on AI for engineering and manufacturing in sectors including computers, aerospace, and automobiles. The company is in stealth mode and aims to apply AI to the physical economy, with potential collaboration with Bezos' Blue Origin.
company.name: Project Prometheus

company.description: Lhoopa is a Philippine proptech company, founded in 2018, that uses an AI-enabled platform and a decentralized network of brokers, agents, contractors, and notaries to make affordable homeownership accessible across emerging markets. It streamlines the property journey from acquisition to move-in and has helped thousands of low- to middle-income Filipinos buy homes across dozens of cities, with most being first-time buyers. The company is expanding green affordable housing in the Philippines and eyeing regional growth in Southeast Asia.
company.name: Lhoopa

company.description: Manam Chocolate is a Hyderabad-based premium craft chocolate brand operating under parent company Distinct Origins Pvt Ltd, founded in 2021 by Chaitanya Muppala. It runs a fully integrated bean-to-bar value chain, sourcing cacao from a network of over 250 farmers across 3,000+ acres in the West Godavari district of Andhra Pradesh and handling fermentation, chocolate making and retail in-house. The company sells over 330 products through experiential retail outlets in Hyderabad and Delhi and via direct-to-consumer channels across India.
company.name: Manam Chocolate

company.description: Dartle is a Kerala-based vertical B2B SaaS platform built for youth sports and football academies, unifying scheduling, attendance, player performance analytics and parent communication in a single system. It layers AI-driven insights on top of operations management to cut academy administrative workload and improve athlete retention. Founded in 2021, the company serves academies across India, the Middle East, Nepal and the United States.
company.name: Dartle

company.description: Ethereal Machines is a Bengaluru-based deeptech manufacturing startup that designs proprietary multi-axis CNC machines and offers Machining-as-a-Service to deliver high-precision components on demand. Founded in 2014 by Kaushik Mudda and Navin Jain, the company serves the aerospace, healthcare and consumer electronics sectors with a focus on cost-effective and scalable precision engineering.
company.name: Ethereal Machines

company.description: Neion Bio is a New York-based biomanufacturing company that uses genetic engineering to produce recombinant biologics in eggs via its Raptor™ platform. The platform enables scalable and efficient production of virtually any therapeutic protein or biosimilar, removing the capital intensity and process constraints of traditional biomanufacturing. Founded in 2024 by Dimi Kellari (CEO) and Dr. Sam Levin (CTO), with Sven Bocklandt PhD as CSO, the company signed its first multi-product co-development and supply agreement with a major global pharmaceutical company upon emerging from stealth.
company.name: Neion Bio

company.description: Chptr is a New York-based media technology company that distributes time-sensitive, hyperlocal community stories across local television, radio, and digital platforms. Founded in 2020, it began by rebuilding memorial and obituary announcements as a broadcast content category, working with funeral homes to turn family photos and memories into human-reviewed video tributes that can air in under 24 hours. The company partners with broadcasters and funeral-technology providers to reach audiences in 132 U.S. television markets.
company.name: Chptr

company.description: Sybilla Technologies is a Polish space-tech company providing space-observation data and analytics across space situational awareness, space surveillance and tracking, space traffic management, and near-Earth object monitoring. It operates a proprietary global network of optical sensors deployed across six continents, supplying orbital-tracking data to national space agencies, the European Space Agency, and European defence programmes. Bootstrapped since its founding, the company serves customers including ESA, the EU SST programme, UK Space Command, Airbus, and Leonardo.
company.name: Sybilla Technologies

company.description:
THEKER Robotics is a company specialized in robotics, deep learning, and computer vision. It has the capability to design, manufacture, and comprehensively market all types of autonomous machines or robots for process automation within the industrial sector. Their dedication to redefining the future is not limited to their products alone; it extends to their values and actions, solidifying their commitment to make a positive impact on the world by promoting the circular economy.

THEKER Robotics es una empresa de robótica, Deep Learning y visión artificial. Cuenta con la capacidad de diseñar, producir y comercializar de manera íntegra cualquier tipo de máquina autónoma o robot para la automatización de procesos dentro del ámbito industrial. Su dedicación a redefinir el futuro no se limita solo sus productos; se extiende a sus valores y acciones, consolidando su compromiso de dejar un impacto positivo en el mundo apostando por la economía circular.
company.name: THEKER Robotics

company.description: Dream Aerospace Technologies is an IIT Kanpur-incubated deeptech startup developing indigenous green satellite propulsion systems, including its ATOM thruster series and CUBEHOOD propulsion module. The company positions its non-toxic propulsion as a lower-cost alternative to imported hydrazine-based systems for the small satellite market, working with ISRO under an MoU on an in-orbit demonstration mission.
company.name: Dream Aerospace Technologies

company.description: Biodimension is a Bengaluru-based deep-tech life sciences startup that develops biofabricated human tissue and organoid models as human-relevant alternatives to animal testing. Its platform serves pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and cosmetics companies with products such as BioDEpi, BioDFT, BioDPigma, a Pan-Cancer Tumour Microenvironment biobank, and Bioassay-as-a-Service, supporting drug discovery, safety assessment, and research. Founded in 2021 and incubated through VIT, the company has built collaborations with pharma firms, biotech companies, and research institutions across India and Singapore.
company.name: Biodimension

company.description: QubeHealth-Pay is a Mumbai-based vertical fintech that operates a healthcare payments platform for Indian families. Through the Qube app, users pay any hospital, clinic, doctor, diagnostic centre or pharmacy across India with no network restrictions, earning instant cash-backs and accessing interest-free medical finance for expenses insurance does not cover, while the platform also embeds employer health-benefit funds, savings and government benefit funds. It serves over 700,000 users across 200,000+ families, has special partnerships with more than 15,000 healthcare providers, and counts enterprises such as Walmart India, Tata Teleservices and Equitas Small Finance Bank among its clients.
company.name: QubeHealth-Pay

company.description: Madverse Music Group is a New Delhi-based music technology company building AI-enabled infrastructure for independent artists and labels, spanning distribution, publishing, marketing, and fan engagement while letting creators retain ownership of their masters. Founded in 2023 by CEO Rohan Nesho Jain, its platform serves more than 200,000 artists and labels across 45+ countries who have collectively surpassed 3 billion streams. In early 2026 it partnered with Kobalt Music Group for global publishing administration.
company.name: Madverse Music Group

company.description: A technology company providing distributed ledger solutions for financial institutions.
company.name: Digital Asset

company.description: Endurance Energy is a Seattle-based deep-tech startup developing undersea geothermal power generators that tap heat from the seafloor to produce baseload renewable electricity. The company recently launched a subsea pilot with the Kingdom of Tonga and is targeting island grids and military installations as early customers.
company.name: Endurance Energy

company.description: Turnout is an AI-powered consumer service that simplifies how Americans navigate complex government and financial processes, starting with tax debt relief and Social Security Disability claims. It uses AI and expert advocacy to automate and manage government paperwork, reducing errors and speeding up resolutions.
company.name: Turnout

company.description: NS/TX Industries is a Toronto-based food manufacturing and technology platform building whole-cut plant-based meat and seafood alternatives. Spun out of the New School Foods brand, it uses a proprietary directional-freezing and scaffolding process that replicates animal muscle fiber and connective tissue, letting products start raw and cook like conventional meat using off-the-shelf equipment rather than extrusion or cellular agriculture. The company runs a 28,000 sq ft facility, sells its plant-based salmon through 30+ Canadian restaurants via Bondi Produce and Gordon Food Service, and offers R&D and co-manufacturing services across salmon, beef, pork and other whole cuts.
company.name: NS/TX Industries

company.description: PhoenixAI builds an analytical database purpose-built for autonomous AI agents, delivering sub-second SQL on live enterprise data at high concurrency by unifying real-time streaming sources with historical lakehouse data. Formerly CelerData, the commercial company behind the open-source StarRocks project, it relaunched as PhoenixAI in May 2026 to focus on real-time analytics for agentic AI infrastructure. Its platform is used in production by enterprises such as Coinbase, AppLovin, Demandbase and Pinterest.
company.name: PhoenixAI

company.description: Current is a New York-based consumer fintech platform offering banking, payments, liquidity, and credit products designed to help everyday Americans improve their financial outcomes. Founded by Stuart Sopp and Trevor Marshall, the company serves millions of members through its mobile app and has posted three consecutive years of 70%+ growth.
company.name: Current

company.description: Hypha is an AI-native asset intelligence platform that turns fragmented, document-heavy investment workflows into structured, actionable insights across the asset lifecycle. Its tools automate data extraction, financial spreading, covenant monitoring and reporting for institutional investors in private credit, private equity, professional services, healthcare, senior housing and multifamily real estate. Founded in 2025 and headquartered in New York City with an R&D presence in Tel Aviv, the company launched out of stealth in June 2026.
company.name: Hypha

company.description: KOHO is a Vancouver-founded Canadian fintech (launched 2014) that operates as a challenger bank, offering an app-based spending and savings account with a prepaid Mastercard, plus credit-building tools, a line of credit, overdraft protection, international transfers and other everyday money-management features. The company serves roughly 2.5 million Canadians and is pursuing a federal Schedule 1 banking licence to operate as a regulated bank.
company.name: KOHO

company.description: Advancing Analytics is a European data and AI consultancy founded in 2018 and headquartered in London, with additional operations in Portugal. It specialises in building and scaling modern Databricks Lakehouse platforms, MLOps and enterprise AI programmes, and has developed proprietary delivery tooling and AI agents to accelerate data engineering. The firm holds Gold status on the Databricks Brickbuilder Partner Network and is a Microsoft Data & AI and AWS partner, serving clients across financial services, healthcare, life sciences and retail.
company.name: Advancing Analytics

company.description: Diamond Brew is a US consumer-goods startup that makes shelf-stable, brewless coffee pods - hexagon-shaped recyclable aluminum pods of instant espresso made with a proprietary liquid-nitrogen flash-freezing process that dissolve in hot or cold water with no grinding or brewing. Founded by CEO Douglas Yu, the brand markets itself as America's first brewless coffee pod and built early traction direct-to-consumer through TikTok Shop before expanding into retail. Its products span craft espresso and latte formats positioned as a premium upgrade to conventional instant coffee.
company.name: Diamond Brew

company.description: CardNexus is a Bordeaux-based, mobile-first platform for trading card game (TCG) collectors that unifies a peer-to-peer marketplace with collection management, AI-powered card scanning, inventory tracking and real-time valuations across more than 12 games including Magic: The Gathering, Pokemon, Lorcana and One Piece. Launched in March 2026, the platform has grown to over 50,000 users who have inventoried more than 30 million cards, with active sellers in 30+ countries. It was founded in 2025 by a team that previously built the French scaleup WeMaintain.
company.name: CardNexus

company.description: Enera is a UK-based startup building an AI-powered driver experience platform for electric-vehicle charging. Its software pulls driver support interactions, charger telemetry and backend system logs into a single "Control Room" that helps charge point operators see why charging sessions fail, and deploys AI agents that troubleshoot issues and assist drivers across calls, email and messaging. The London-based company runs pilots with UK charge point operators and is expanding across Europe.
company.name: Enera

company.description: OurMind is an Amsterdam-based healthtech company building an AI platform that reduces administrative workload for clinicians and supports them before, during, and after patient consultations. Its first product, Notes, turns consultation conversations into medical documentation and is used by more than 300 general practices and 14 hospitals. Founded by former orthopaedic surgeon Paul Koning, the company works with hospitals to safely implement generative AI in clinical settings.
company.name: OurMind

company.description: ThruFlo Endovascular is an Irvine, California-based neuro-endovascular medical device company developing technology to treat cerebral (brain) aneurysms, with a focus on bifurcation aneurysms that form at the branch points of major blood vessels. Its lead product is a purpose-built, dual-limb Y-shaped flow diverter designed to deploy as a single modality, removing the need for coiling and reducing procedural complexity. The company is pre-commercial, advancing its platform through pre-clinical studies toward an FDA Investigational Device Exemption.
company.name: ThruFlo Endovascular

company.description: Niteshift is a full-stack cloud platform for AI coding agents, founded by former Datadog engineers Sajid Mehmood and Conor Branagan. Rather than a simple file sandbox, it provisions complete development environments with running databases, authentication, workers and seeded data so agents such as Claude Code, Codex and open-source models can run, test and verify changes before opening merge-ready pull requests. The platform is model-agnostic, routes across providers, and bills per minute of environment runtime instead of token usage, targeting engineering teams wary of vendor lock-in.
company.name: Niteshift

company.description: Sunbay (sunbay.io) is a Warsaw-based fintech whose platform automates accounts-receivable and collections for European B2B finance teams. It connects to a company's accounting system, bank and CRM, then chases overdue invoices across email, SMS and AI voice calls, manages escalations and runs a branded payment portal, bringing finance staff in only where human judgement is needed. The product also surfaces credit-scoring and payment-behaviour signals to flag at-risk accounts, keeps receivables data within the EEA for GDPR compliance, and already works with more than 20 businesses across five countries.
company.name: Sunbay

company.description: Mendo is a Paris-based startup that helps enterprises drive adoption of generative and agentic AI. Its platform embeds directly into the AI chat tools employees already use (Copilot, ChatGPT, Gemini, Mistral), guiding usage in real time and providing privacy-oriented analytics that show where AI is creating value. Founded in 2021, it supports over 100 large organisations and roughly 100,000 employees across Europe.
company.name: Mendo

company.description: Dapple operates the Enterprise OS Cloud, a category of AI infrastructure positioned between the public cloud and a private data center. It delivers dedicated, single-tenant, in-country GPU environments for regulated enterprises, governments, and AI-native companies that cannot run production AI workloads on shared infrastructure, managed through a single control plane, interface, and contract. Founded in 2024 by Tricia Martinez and Salam Al-Mosawi, the company runs production AI workloads globally and is an NVIDIA partner.
company.name: Dapple

company.description: Poetic (formerly Forge) is a San Francisco-based AI company building software that learns like AI but runs with the determinism of code. Its platform ingests a company's procedures, training materials, and expert feedback to automate complex, end-to-end back-office processes such as fraud investigations, KYC, disputes, and insurance underwriting, using a purpose-built programming language for directing AI. It serves regulated financial enterprises including SoFi, AIG, and Chime.
company.name: Poetic

company.description: TurnUp is a Ghent-based healthtech startup whose AI platform helps dental and medical practices reduce patient no-shows and last-minute cancellations. It integrates with existing practice management systems to predict at-risk appointments and automate confirmations, cancellations, rescheduling and waiting-list management, and offers a multilingual AI receptionist that contacts patients outside office hours. The company serves more than 250 practices and 2,500 dentists across Belgium, the Netherlands and the UK.
company.name: TurnUp

company.description:
Cyera is the world’s leading AI-native data security platform. Its platform gives organizations a complete view of where their data lives, how it’s used, and how to keep it safe, so they can reduce risk and unlock the full value of their data, wherever it is. 

Backed by more than $1.3 billion in funding from top-tier investors including Accel, Coatue, Cyberstarts, Georgian, Lightspeed, and Sequoia, Cyera’s unified data security platform helps businesses discover, secure, and leverage their most valuable asset - data - and eliminate blind spots, cut alert noise, and protect sensitive information across the cloud, SaaS, databases, AI ecosystems, and on-premise environments.
company.name: Cyera

company.description: Dentroid is a Canberra-based medtech company developing needle-free dental pain management technology. Its patented Nuralyte device uses controlled light energy (photobiomodulation) to induce analgesia in teeth and surrounding tissue, aiming to reduce or eliminate the need for needles in common dental procedures, and the company is also building a photonics-and-robotics platform for precise dental tissue cutting. Founded in 2017 by dentist Omar Zuaiter and robotics engineer Alaa Habeb, Dentroid has run clinical trials with several universities and partners with French dental manufacturer Septodont on global commercialisation.
company.name: Dentroid

company.description: Mobioos builds an enterprise "context layer" that maps business intent and codebase knowledge so AI assistants, autonomous agents and engineering teams operate with trusted, governed context. Its products span a web portal (Fusion) integrating with tools like Jira, GitHub and Salesforce and an IDE plugin (Forge) for editors such as VS Code and Cursor across 50+ languages and 100+ AI models. The France-based deeptech startup targets developers, product teams and CTOs aiming to reduce AI hallucinations, cost and security risk at scale.
company.name: Mobioos

company.description: Camphouse is a Stockholm-based enterprise media operations platform (formerly Mediatool) that unifies media planning and actual spend into a single AI-native system of record for global brands and agencies. It gives marketing teams a real-time view of budgets, campaigns, and performance across markets, agencies, and channels, with customers including L'Oreal and other large enterprise advertisers.
company.name: Camphouse

company.description: Hiway is a Tokyo-based startup building an AI-native CRM/PRM platform for B2B companies that sell through dealers, agencies and partner channels. Its AI agents autonomously handle partner inquiries, generate quotes and track deals by drawing on product masters, pricing and customer history, integrating with existing CRM/SFA systems. Founded in November 2021, the company serves enterprise manufacturers and software vendors managing multi-partner sales networks.
company.name: Hiway

company.description: HerLifeLab is a Japanese femtech startup that operates Vivalle, an online medical service specializing in menopause care. The platform combines online consultations with obstetrics and gynecology specialists and continuous support from nursing counselors, handling interviews, prescriptions, and delivery of treatments such as HRT, herbal medicine, and supplements entirely online. Founded in 2022 and based in Okinawa, it was co-founded by an OIST researcher and a gynecologist.
company.name: HerLifeLab

company.description: Inspira is a Brazilian legaltech building an AI ecosystem for legal practice, consolidating jurisprudence research, document and process automation, and internal knowledge management in one platform. Its tools draw on a database spanning 86 courts and tens of millions of decisions updated daily, and it serves law firms and corporate legal teams including Pinheiro Neto, BMA, Safra, XP, Itaú and Vivo. Founded in 2021 by lawyers, it is headquartered in São Paulo.
company.name: Inspira

company.description: Promake is an Istanbul-based AI platform that lets small and medium-sized businesses set up and run their digital operations through a single natural-language chat interface, covering domain and email setup, website creation, e-commerce infrastructure, payments, and digital advertising with no technical knowledge required. Founded in 2025, it positions itself as a way to close the digital capability gap for SMBs and globally launched in February 2026 following a December 2025 pre-seed.
company.name: Promake

company.description: Myka (Myka Ecosystems Limited) is a Nigerian, NAICOM-licensed digital insurance broker that lets consumers and SMEs discover, compare, and buy policies from multiple underwriters in real time across categories such as health, motor, gadget, home, life, travel and business cover. Founded in 2025 by serial entrepreneur Sim Shagaya (Konga, uLesson) with Muritala Ahmed and Oluwadamilola Okenla, it pairs a digital marketplace aggregating numerous insurers with a community-based agent referral network modelled on agency banking. The company aims to close Nigeria's wide insurance-distribution and penetration gap.
company.name: Myka

company.description: KinetiTec is an Indianapolis-based medical device and SaaS company building SPARK, an in-bed mobility intelligence platform that pairs bedside hardware with cloud clinical software to keep hospitalized patients moving and reduce deconditioning and hospital-acquired complications. Founded by a physician and a mechanical engineer who met at Notre Dame, its device attaches to a hospital bed to enable resistance exercise while tracking patient risk, mobility status, and recovery progress for clinicians. The company targets acutely ill, post-surgical, and older inpatients across acute, ICU, and rehabilitation settings.
company.name: KinetiTec

company.description: Coram AI is a Sunnyvale-based, AI-native physical security platform that unifies video security, access control, visitor management, and emergency response into a single system layered onto an organization's existing cameras and infrastructure. Founded by former autonomous-vehicle AI engineers from Lyft and Zoox, the company applies self-driving AI techniques to detect risks early and let security teams investigate incidents in minutes. It serves more than 1,500 sites across the US and Canada, including Fortune 500 enterprises, school districts, healthcare providers, manufacturers, and places of worship.
company.name: Coram AI

company.description: Skillmaker is a Carolina Beach, North Carolina startup that uses extended reality and artificial intelligence to accelerate skilled-trades workforce training. Its flagship NAPA Autotech Xccelerator program trains auto technicians in roughly 25 days instead of the traditional two years, deployed across 20 states under a distribution agreement with Genuine Parts Company. Founded in 2024, the company is expanding from automotive into other trades.
company.name: Skillmaker

company.description: Ethyreal Bio is a Cambridge, Massachusetts biotechnology company developing precision therapies for thyroid diseases with high unmet need. Its lead program, ETHY-001, is a monoclonal antibody antagonist of the thyroid stimulating hormone receptor (TSHR) designed to block the autoantibody-driven mechanism shared by Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease, delivered via patient-friendly subcutaneous dosing. The company emerged from stealth in June 2026 and plans to begin first-in-human trials in the second half of 2026.
company.name: Ethyreal Bio

company.description: Leaf Agriculture is a San Francisco-based data-infrastructure company for food and agriculture, founded in 2021. Its unified API connects and standardizes farmer-owned data — machinery, soil, weather, satellite imagery, and farm-management records — that otherwise sits in siloed, incompatible systems. Agribusinesses, crop insurers, and ag retailers build on Leaf to power AI tools and field-level decisions, with the platform processing data across roughly 20% of global crop acres annually.
company.name: Leaf

company.description: Relai is an AI infrastructure startup building a verifiable continual learning platform for AI agents, turning real-world failures, traces, evaluations and human feedback into replayable learning environments so deployed agents improve over time without regressing. Founded by University of Maryland computer science professor Soheil Feizi, it is based in Bethesda, Maryland and counts enterprises such as C3 AI among early design partners.
company.name: Relai

company.description: Fearn builds AI software that lets inventors and in-house R&D teams draft patents themselves, scoring the quality of a technical document, generating patent drawings and citing sources to cut drafting time from weeks to minutes at a flat fee per draft. Founded by former Morrison Foerster patent attorney Han Kim and Caltech-trained computer scientist Angela Gao, the company develops hypercompact foundation models purpose-built for intellectual property law.
company.name: Fearn

company.description: Uni Seoul is a Pune-based, Korean-inspired impulse lifestyle and gifting retail brand operating an omnichannel model across India. It sells affordable, trend-driven products spanning plush toys, stationery, home and living, bags, travel accessories, beauty and personal care, and gifting, with over 1,000 SKUs priced between Rs 99 and Rs 2,999. Founded by Gaurav Karmani and Mohit Khurana, the company runs 15 stores across cities including Bengaluru, Mumbai, Pune, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Chennai, Kochi, and Nashik.
company.name: Uni Seoul

company.description: Hoola Health (formerly BabyMD) is a Bengaluru-based paediatric healthcare startup founded in 2024 by Deeksha Senguttuvan. It operates a network of child-first neighbourhood clinics offering consultations, vaccinations, developmental therapies and diagnostics, paired with digital support tools built around the needs of children and parents. Since launching its first clinic it has grown to five clinics in Bengaluru and served more than 20,000 families.
company.name: Hoola Health

company.description: Rivvun AI builds an autonomous AI execution layer that recovers enterprise spend and revenue leakage for large Fortune 1000 organizations. Its agentic platform connects to existing ERP, CRM, and procurement systems (such as SAP, Ariba, and Salesforce) to detect commercial obligations that have not settled as agreed and initiate recovery at the transaction level, with vertical-specific logic for pharma, healthcare, banking, and CPG/retail. Founded by former Icertis executives, the company is headquartered in Seattle with engineering operations in Pune, India.
company.name: Rivvun AI

company.description: GreatAsic is a Malaysia-based fabless semiconductor company that designs custom ASICs and AI System-on-Chip platforms for data center, edge AI, and automotive markets. It is among the first Malaysian design houses to secure access to Arm Holdings semiconductor IP, holding both Arm Flexible Access and Arm Neoverse Compute Subsystems tokens. The company aims to move Malaysia up the semiconductor value chain from chip assembly and testing toward front-end indigenous chip design.
company.name: GreatAsic

company.description: Onibi is a remote-first game studio founded in 2023 and led by Benjamin Devienne, building Tomo: Endless Blue, an AI-powered open-world multiplayer RPG. The studio uses proprietary AI models alongside voxel-based physics and scalable multiplayer infrastructure to generate unique villages, NPCs, quests and stories for each player, with a long-term goal of a user-generated-content platform where players create their own RPG experiences from simple prompts. Its team draws on veterans of titles such as Fortnite, League of Legends and Baldur's Gate 3, working across San Francisco, London and Bordeaux.
company.name: Onibi

company.description: Exponent Energy develops a proprietary energy stack for 15-minute rapid EV charging. The stack comprises e^pack (battery pack), e^pump (charging station), and e^plug (connector) that together enable 0%-100% rapid charge in 15 minutes with a 3,000-cycle lifetime warranty using regular LFP cells.
company.name: Exponent Energy

company.description: NEURA Robotics is a German high-tech company pioneering cognitive and humanoid robotics, developing AI-powered robots that work seamlessly with humans in industries such as manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare. Founded in 2019, it has rapidly grown to over 300 employees and leads in cognitive cobots and humanoid robots.
company.name: NEURA Robotics

company.description: Enlaye is a Boston-based startup building an AI-native Risk Lifecycle Management platform for the construction and infrastructure industries (the "Built-World"). Its software helps contractors, owners, developers and insurers identify, analyze and mitigate project risk—turning fragmented contract and project data into predictive, explainable risk intelligence before exposures become cost overruns, delays or disputes. Founded in 2023, the company serves customers including the VINCI Group across the US, Canada and Europe.
company.name: Enlaye

company.description: Naratix is a Bucharest-based startup building AI-powered infrastructure for global eCommerce, turning raw and inconsistent product information from online stores and marketplaces into structured, standardised and enriched product data at scale. Its platform spans the full product-data lifecycle — taxonomy audits, automated categorisation, attribute enrichment, SEO/GEO content generation and multi-channel distribution — with native integrations into enterprise platforms such as VTEX, Mirakl and Shopware. Founded in 2023, it serves marketplaces, enterprise retailers and global brands across more than 30 countries and positions itself as critical data infrastructure for the emerging era of AI-mediated shopping.
company.name: Naratix

company.description: Nanordica Medical is an Estonian medtech company founded in 2019 that develops antibacterial wound dressings using nanotechnology to treat chronic and infected wounds. Its patent-protected Premotiv technology combines a silk nanofibre dressing with antimicrobial nanoparticles to fight infection while supporting tissue healing, avoiding traditional antibiotics and high-dose silver agents. The company already sells a veterinary wound-care product, Ravimus Vet, in six countries and is pursuing CE marking for its human product ahead of a European launch.
company.name: Nanordica Medical

company.description: Nesto is a Montreal-based financial technology company operating as a digital mortgage lender and B2B financing platform for the Canadian market. It uses AI to compress mortgage underwriting from a day to as little as two minutes while keeping the process auditable and human-supervised, and administers more than $80-billion in mortgages. The company is expanding beyond consumer mortgages to offer AI-powered lending products to other financial institutions.
company.name: Nesto

company.description:
TensorWave is a cutting-edge cloud platform designed specifically for AI workloads. Offering AMD MI300X accelerators and a best-in-class inference engine, TensorWave is a top-choice for training, fine-tuning, and inference. Visit tensorwave.com to learn more.
Send us a message to try it for free.
company.name: TensorWave

company.description: FesariusTherapeutics is a New York-based, commercial-stage medical technology company focused on dermal repair and deep wound care. Its flagship product, DermiSphere, is a hydrogel Dermal Regeneration Template (hDRT) with a patented dual-density collagen microarchitecture designed to speed cellular integration and vascularization during wound healing. The device received FDA 510(k) clearance in 2025 and is in commercial use across multiple U.S. hospital systems.
company.name: FesariusTherapeutics

company.description: SonoThera is a South San Francisco biotechnology company developing nonviral genetic medicines delivered with ultrasound. Its platform pairs a proprietary ultrasound-mediated delivery technology (RIPPLE) with a payload-engineering system (PORE) to enable targeted, redosable delivery of DNA and RNA therapeutics, gene editing, and gene silencing across multiple tissues. Lead programs target Duchenne muscular dystrophy and autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease, with first-in-human trials planned for 2027.
company.name: SonoThera

company.description: Maneva is a Palo Alto and Toronto based industrial AI company building "video-to-action" intelligence for high-volume manufacturing. Its VITA and ALIS agents run on NVIDIA edge devices to turn a factory's existing cameras into real-time systems that detect defects, safety hazards, and production bottlenecks, then trigger automated responses without adding new hardware or relying on the cloud. Founded in 2021 by ex-Google DeepMind engineer Rae Jeong and ex-Magna engineer Kelvin Chan, it serves manufacturers across food and beverage, confectionery, steel, glass, pharmaceuticals, and distribution.
company.name: Maneva

company.description: SWARM Engineering is a decision intelligence company for agrifood and manufacturing that combines domain-trained AI agents with optimization algorithms, helping enterprise operations teams make faster, auditable decisions across supply chain, workforce, and logistics. Built on the operational ontology of these industries, its platform ingests real-time data and runs scenarios across thousands of variables in minutes. Founded by veterans of Microsoft, Palantir, Google, and UiPath, the company serves global brands across the US, Latin America, and Europe.
company.name: SWARM Engineering

company.description: Jedify is a New York-based enterprise AI startup that builds an autonomous "context graph" for data-intensive agentic applications and workflows. Its patent-pending Semantic Fusion technology fuses structured operational data from warehouses, CRMs, and financial systems with unstructured knowledge such as documents, playbooks, Slack, and meeting recordings into a continuously updated, model-agnostic semantic layer that gives AI agents the runtime business context they need to move from prototype to production. Early customers include The Weather Company and Kiteworks.
company.name: Jedify

company.description: Pi is an Israeli-American cybersecurity company building an agentic "product security" platform that automates software vulnerability remediation from design to deployment. Its system acts as a "security brain", ingesting an organization's codebase, cloud infrastructure, past incidents and internal communications (Slack, Teams, email) to separate real vulnerabilities from false positives and ship contextual fixes autonomously at scale. Founded in 2025 by ex-Microsoft security researcher Guy Arazi (CEO) and former Tesla offensive-security lead Yoni Ramon (CPO), it operates from San Francisco and Tel Aviv with roughly 23 employees.
company.name: Pi Security

company.description: Beren Therapeutics is a founder-led, public-benefit biotechnology company in Thousand Oaks, California, developing cyclodextrin-based therapeutics for diseases driven by defective intracellular cholesterol trafficking. Its lead asset, adrabetadex, is a proprietary 2-hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin therapy under FDA Priority Review (PDUFA target November 17, 2026) as a potential first-in-class, disease-modifying treatment for infantile-onset Niemann-Pick disease type C. Beren is the parent company of Mandos LLC and has supported the NPC community through an Expanded Access Program since 2021.
company.name: Beren Therapeutics

company.description: Billables AI builds an AI-native operational intelligence platform for law firms and other professional service providers, centered on automated timekeeping. It connects via API to the tools professionals already use — legal practice management systems like Clio, MyCase and Litify, plus Microsoft 365, Google Workspace and Zoom — to capture how work actually gets done, then turns that data into billing optimization, productivity insights, revenue forecasting and AI-usage tracking for risk and governance. The company serves hundreds of law firms with a focus on midmarket practices, using a privacy-first integration model rather than screen monitoring.
company.name: Billables AI

company.description: WealthReach is an AI-powered organic growth platform built for registered investment advisors (RIAs) and wealth management firms. Founded in 2025 and based in New York, it gives advisory firms tools to improve their search visibility, run personalized prospect outreach, and convert demand into client conversations, packaged as Attract, Convert, and Multiply engines and offerings like its continuously-updating Living Sites. The company has grown quickly, earning adoption across major enterprise platforms and large wealth-management firms.
company.name: WealthReach

company.description: Corca Research is a New York-based software company building a collaborative workspace for mathematics that it bills as "Cursor for math." The product combines WYSIWYG equation editing, real-time collaboration, and AI-powered assistance so engineers, researchers, and other technical professionals can create, compute, and collaborate on mathematical work without switching between legacy tools like MATLAB and LaTeX. Founded in 2023, the company reports more than 10,000 users across fields such as AI research, engineering, finance, and scientific computing.
company.name: Corca Research

company.description: Flux is a code-first engineering intelligence platform that gives engineering leaders ground-truth visibility into what is actually happening across their codebases. It analyzes commits, pull requests, and repository activity to surface real progress, AI impact, technical debt, and delivery risk without ticketing changes or process overhead. Aimed at leaders of GitHub Cloud organizations with 50+ engineers, it ties day-to-day code activity to quality, security, and business outcomes.
company.name: Flux

company.description: F2 is a New York-based AI platform for private markets investors, purpose-built for private credit funds, commercial banks, and private equity teams. It pairs an Excel-native financial analysis engine with agentic workflow automation across deal screening, underwriting, and portfolio monitoring, turning a firm's accumulated deal history into a persistent, queryable institutional-knowledge asset. F2 is deployed across more than 100 funds and banks globally, with customers including Bain Capital, Live Oak Bank, and Jefferies Credit Partners.
company.name: F2

company.description: CameraMatics is a Dublin-based provider of AI-powered fleet intelligence software for commercial transport operators. Its platform combines AI-driven video telematics, advanced driver-assistance systems and real-time operational analytics to help fleet managers cut accident risk, improve driver safety and meet compliance and governance requirements. Founded in 2016, the company serves close to 1,000 fleet customers across the UK, Ireland, mainland Europe and the United States.
company.name: CameraMatics

company.description: Capsa AI builds an AI operating system for private capital, indexing a fund's scattered data across CRM, email, SharePoint and proprietary deal memos into a single searchable knowledge layer, with agentic AI spanning sourcing, due diligence, portfolio monitoring and back-office work. Founded in 2024 by Danyal Oezduezenciler and Callum Downie and based in London and New York, the company is SOC 2 Type II certified, does not train on client data, and is in production at several of the world's largest private capital firms.
company.name: Capsa AI

company.description: Aryon Security operates a Cloud Security Enforcement Platform that shifts cloud security from reactive detection to preventive enforcement, applying consistent policies that block misconfigurations and risks before they reach production across an organization's cloud infrastructure. Founded by veterans of the IDF's elite "Matzov" cybersecurity unit who helped secure Israel's national cloud infrastructure, the Tel Aviv-based company serves mid-to-large enterprises in regulated sectors such as healthcare, banking, insurance, and telecommunications. The platform integrates with existing technology stacks and claims to prevent up to 95% of traditional CSPM-style security gaps.
company.name: Aryon Security

company.description: MetalloBio is a University of Sheffield biotech spinout developing a new class of inorganic antimicrobial compounds to combat antibiotic resistance. Its platform spans broad-spectrum antibiotics designed to resist bacterial adaptation and antimicrobial coatings for medical devices such as urinary catheters, aimed at preventing and treating drug-resistant infections. The company has generated over 55 novel antibacterial compounds and focuses on catheter-associated urinary tract infections.
company.name: MetalloBio

company.description: Two Desperados is an independent mobile game development studio based in Belgrade, Serbia, regarded as the largest independent mobile gaming studio in Southeastern Europe. It builds player-centric casual titles such as Marble Woka Woka, Marble Shooter: Viola's Quest and Nonogram Crossing Logic Puzzle, which together have surpassed 30 million downloads. The studio focuses on high-quality casual experiences for a global audience.
company.name: Two Desperados

company.description: Minerva is a New York-based AI platform for consumer marketing teams, founded in 2023 by Jackson Engles, Daniel Saedi and Matthew Joseph. It unifies a brand's fragmented first-party customer data, enriches it with a proprietary identity graph and 1,000+ attributes, and uses AI agents to create, analyze and optimize marketing campaigns end-to-end. Minerva works with leading consumer brands and organizations across sports, hospitality, financial services and other categories.
company.name: Minerva

company.description: 01Health is a London-based healthtech company building an operating system for specialist healthcare, enabling local clinics to deliver advanced treatments traditionally concentrated in hospitals and major urban centres. Its platform combines clinical oversight, specialist protocols, workflow management, patient communication and AI-powered patient acquisition, and already powers the company's orthodontics brand 32Co and dental sleep medicine brand Aerox Health. Founded in 2022 by former NHS doctor Dr Sonia Szamocki, it has grown to more than 100 employees and is expanding beyond dentistry into other clinical specialities.
company.name: 01Health

company.description: Record OS is a London-based fintech building a modern digital platform for UK Self-Assessment and Making Tax Digital (MTD) filing, serving individuals, sole traders, freelancers and professionals with increasingly complex finances. It combines automated software with mandatory review by a qualified tax professional on every return, positioning itself as a faster, lower-cost alternative to traditional advisers while keeping a human in the loop. Founded by former early Wise employees Dhruv Chadha and Jose Luis De La Pena, the company launched publicly in June 2026 with a six-person team.
company.name: Record OS

company.description: Mygate is a Bengaluru-based technology company offering a full-scale ERP platform for residential communities, founded in 2016. Its app lets resident welfare associations and residents manage visitor and security access, society finances, amenities, communications and daily operations, and it runs a brand-engagement and advertising platform for consumer brands. Mygate serves over 27,000 housing societies and roughly 5.7 million households across India, making it the most widely adopted platform in its category.
company.name: Mygate

company.description: HyperNorm AI is a wealthtech startup building a decision-intelligence platform for registered investment advisors (RIAs) and wealth managers.
