Its software combines causal reasoning, financial intelligence, and explainability to flag portfolios that need intervention and recommend actions aligned with each client's investment mandate. Founded in 2024 and headquartered in Bengaluru with a US presence, it already serves paying customers across the US, Singapore, and India.
company.name: HyperNorm AI

company.description: Rotomate is a Helsinki-based industrial AI startup, founded in 2024, that builds an AI reliability assistant for condition monitoring in heavy industry. Its software continuously analyses machine and sensor data alongside maintenance history and process context to filter out noise, flag emerging equipment failures, explain root causes, and recommend maintenance actions, scaling expert-level analysis across an entire plant. The company already works with European process-industry firms including Metsa Group, SSAB, and Aurubis.
company.name: Rotomate

company.description: BioGeometry (百奥几何) is a Beijing-based AI-native biotech building generative-AI foundation models for the life sciences — a "microscopic world model" that links protein sequence, structure and function to redesign drug discovery. Its GeoFlow model family handles de novo antibody, peptide and enzyme design, paired with an automated wet-lab platform to run a closed design-experiment-learning loop. Founded by AI4S scientist Jian Tang with Turing Award winner Yoshua Bengio as chief scientific advisor.
company.name: BioGeometry

company.description: Spacebackend is a Luxembourg-based aerospace deep-tech startup building Lynapse, an AI-powered engineering platform that automates hardware-software integration for satellites and space systems. The platform interprets complex Interface Control Documents and generates deterministic, flight-ready driver code, aiming to compress mission-critical integration work from years to days. The company concentrates its R&D in Luxembourg and operates a subsidiary in Spain.
company.name: Spacebackend

company.description: ReSmart (formerly RecycleSmart) is a Sydney-based doorstep recycling and reverse-logistics platform that lets households and businesses book on-demand pickups of soft plastics, textiles, e-waste, batteries and other hard-to-recycle items, then sorts them for recycling, reuse, donation or repair. It operates across major Australian cities including Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, Canberra and Hobart, working with around 29 local councils and brand partners such as Qantas, Westpac and Canva. The capital-light model spans both consumer and B2B services, positioning the company in the circular-economy and waste-recovery sector.
company.name: ReSmart

company.description: Ethena is the developer of USDe, a crypto-native, yield-bearing synthetic dollar that maintains its peg through delta-neutral hedging of staked collateral rather than relying on the traditional banking system. The protocol generates yield from derivatives funding rates and basis trades, and as of mid-2026 had roughly 9 billion USDe tokens in circulation. Based in Lisbon, Ethena is increasingly bridging decentralized finance with traditional asset managers through reserve diversification and regulated product partnerships.
company.name: Ethena

company.description: Daimon Robotics Technology is a Shenzhen-based humanoid robotics startup developing high-resolution multimodal tactile sensors, dexterous robotic hands, and wearable teleoperation systems for embodied AI. Co-founded by HKUST robotics-institute dean Wang Yu and CEO Duan Jianghua, it commercialised vision-tactile systems in April 2025 across manufacturing and logistics customers, and is building its own Vision-Tactile-Language-Action (VTLA) model for robotic manipulation.
company.name: Daimon Robotics

company.description: xCARE (Cross Care) operates a Tokyo-based expert talent platform specialized in the medical and healthcare sector, focused on pharmaceutical and medical-device development. It connects companies with registered specialists in areas such as regulatory affairs, clinical trials, and quality systems on a project and time-unit basis, positioning itself as a third option between traditional hiring and full outsourcing. The platform targets Japan's structural shortage of development talent, serving both startups and large enterprises that need flexible access to scarce expertise.
company.name: xCARE

company.description: ALGO ARTIS is a Tokyo-based AI startup, founded in 2021, that builds the OPTIUM Series — a "Planning DX" platform using AI and optimization algorithms to automate complex operational planning across social-infrastructure sectors including electricity, logistics, manufacturing, and transportation. Its technology combines a common optimization engine with industry-specific solutions under a Multi-Vertical strategy, serving organizations such as Kansai Electric Power and Cosmo Energy Holdings.
company.name: ALGO ARTIS

company.description: Valor is a Fort Worth, Texas tech-enabled asset management firm specializing in mineral and royalty management and oil-and-gas back-office outsourcing for mineral owners, operators, and investors. Its proprietary mineral.tech software is a map-based, data-rich platform combining accounting, land management, data analytics, and reporting, and the firm manages millions of acres of mineral and royalty rights across dozens of US states. Valor is investing in AI across its software and services to expand its position in the mineral-management market.
company.name: Valor

company.description: Eclipse is a Paris-based company dedicated to utility-scale battery energy storage in Europe, handling project development as well as the optimization and trading of storage assets. Its proprietary Flowstream platform buys electricity when prices are low, stores it, and resells it when demand pushes prices up, optimizing assets in real time across European electricity markets. Founded in 2023 by former Electra co-founder Augustin Derville, the roughly 20-person company also offers tolling and optimization services to third-party asset owners across France and Belgium.
company.name: Eclipse

company.description: Fintech company building stablecoin-powered payments infrastructure for seamless cross-border transfers, enabling users to send funds, hold stablecoins, and spend via cards compatible with Apple Pay and Google Pay.
company.name: OneDosh

company.description: Climate First Bancorp is the holding company for Climate First Bank, the world's first FDIC-insured, values-based, digital-forward community bank founded to combat the climate crisis. It focuses on financing renewable energy projects, especially residential solar, and offers banking products with an emphasis on sustainability and eco-conscious customers.
company.name: Climate First Bancorp

company.description: Zaro is a London-based enterprise AI startup building an adaptive, AI-native workspace that unifies a company's data, workflows, agents, and applications on top of a shared, owned context layer. Rather than letting knowledge fragment across disconnected AI tools, the platform lets each agent run and app build on the same versioned company context so intelligence compounds over time, while multi-model routing keeps costs down. Founded by Michael Bajwa and Qian Zheng with a team drawn from Convergence (acquired by Salesforce) and PolyAI.
company.name: Zaro

company.description: Retriever is a South Korean cybersecurity startup, founded by KAIST alumni, that builds Klarisk, an AI-powered governance, risk and compliance (GRC) automation platform. Klarisk uses AI agents to interpret regulations and internal controls, analyze policies, logs and evidence documents, and automatically map a single piece of evidence across multiple certification frameworks such as ISMS-P, ISO 27001 and SOC 2. The company aims to cut the cost and manual effort of information-security certification and to support Korean firms expanding into global compliance regimes.
company.name: Retriever

company.description: Antogen is a San Francisco-based specialty contract research organization providing immunopeptidomics and Human Leukocyte Antigen (HLA) testing services. It identifies and quantifies peptide-HLA ligands directly from biological samples to support target discovery, validation, and patient stratification across oncology, immunotherapy development, and autoimmune-disease programs. The company was founded by Zachary Antovich, who previously built and sold HLA-diagnostics firm Linkage Biosciences to Thermo Fisher Scientific.
company.name: Antogen

company.description: Vestd is a UK-based sharetech platform, founded in 2014, that digitises the management and administration of employee share schemes, cap tables, equity ownership and fundraising for private companies. Its FCA-regulated SaaS replaces manual equity processes with a compliant, auditable system and serves thousands of businesses across the UK and India, and it is one of the first licensed operators of PISCES, the new venue for trading shares in private companies. The company competes with platforms such as Carta, Ledgy and SeedLegals and has expanded internationally through its Vestd India operation.
company.name: Vestd

company.description: TVL Capital AG is a Zug, Switzerland-based financial infrastructure startup building Chain-Traded Products (CTPs), a blockchain-native equivalent of exchange-traded products in which structured payoff profiles are issued, managed and settled entirely on-chain via smart contracts. The company targets institutional allocators — pension funds, asset managers and family offices — rather than retail DeFi users, leaning on Switzerland's FINMA framework and its Crypto Valley base. It was founded by senior figures from Morgan Stanley, UBS and The Block.
company.name: TVL Capital

company.description: McEasy is an Indonesian IoT and SaaS company, founded in 2017 in Surabaya by Raymond Sutjiono and Hendrik Ekowaluyo, that builds fleet-management and logistics software for the transportation and supply-chain sector. Its McEasy Platform pairs GPS and telematics hardware with tools for fleet monitoring, delivery and route management, fuel and driver management, and AI-based video safety, serving more than 1,500 companies across Indonesia. The company's goal is to digitize a heavily manual logistics industry and build a connected supply-chain data ecosystem.
company.name: McEasy

company.description: Algebra AI is a Dubai-based company that designs, builds and operates AI systems for mid-market businesses across the GCC as a managed service rather than off-the-shelf software. It studies each client's existing workflows, tools and approval logic, then builds and continuously runs tailored AI-enabled processes, serving clients in financial services, food and beverage, manufacturing and distribution. Founded by former Deliveroo Middle East executive Anis Harb in partnership with its investors, it targets the gap between generic AI tools and costly enterprise deployments.
company.name: Algebra AI

company.description: DataBraid is a Toronto-based insurtech startup building AI-powered software that integrates directly with brokers' management systems to eliminate "portal hopping" — the need for insurance brokers to log into and re-enter data across multiple carrier portals for quoting, applications and claims. It was founded out of venture studio Koru Ventures together with Scoop Insurance, and is led by co-founders Nick Romano and Atif Khan.
company.name: DataBraid

company.description: Uncovr is a Paris- and New York-based surgical AI company that turns surgical and endoscopic video into structured clinical records. Its platform automatically drafts operative reports and procedural coding recommendations from intraoperative video and workflow data, helping hospitals improve documentation quality, coding and reimbursement accuracy, and surgical workflow visibility. Founded in 2025, it positions this structured procedural data as a foundational dataset for surgical AI and is deploying with health systems across the US and Europe.
company.name: Uncovr

company.description: Paymove is a Polish fintech, based in Sopot, that digitizes offline and unattended commerce by replacing legacy hardware like POS terminals with instant QR-code payments requiring no app download or registration. Its use cases span parking, public transit, ticketing, paper invoices and administrative fees, and it is expanding into a broader Payment-as-a-Service platform covering occasional, e-commerce/mobile, and agentic AI-driven payments. The company operates as a regulated Small Payment Institution under the supervision of Poland's Financial Supervision Authority (KNF).
company.name: paymove

company.description: Trustap is a Cork, Ireland-based fintech that provides secure transaction infrastructure for online marketplaces and ecommerce platforms. Acting as merchant of record, it bundles escrow-style payments, fraud detection, fulfilment tracking and dispute resolution into a single integration, serving over 300 platforms across categories from event tickets to vehicles. Its newer Index product extends this trust layer to agentic commerce, making listings discoverable and transactable by AI shopping agents while funds stay in escrow until a human confirms.
company.name: Trustap

company.description: Tap Electric is an Amsterdam-based EV charging software company founded in 2021 by Nico Spoelstra and Dan Pezim. Its app and management platform connect drivers, chargers and energy systems, using real-time electricity prices and grid intelligence to steer users toward cheaper, more grid-friendly charging. The company serves drivers, homeowners, businesses, fleets, installers and charge point operators across the Netherlands, Belgium, the UK and Ireland, with roughly 100,000 monthly users.
company.name: Tap Electric

company.description: Sintropy.ai is an Italian startup that uses wireless, non-invasive sensors and an AI platform to cut energy consumption for retail and food-service chains. Customers including Barilla and KFC Italy report 15-20 percent energy savings without operational changes.
company.name: Sintropy.ai

company.description: Helionis Labs is a Bengaluru-based deep-tech materials startup developing advanced thermal-management solutions for high-power electronics. It makes proprietary printable dielectric materials and an additive-manufacturing process to produce Insulated Metal Substrates (IMS) — a lower-cost, higher-performance replacement for conventional Metal Core PCBs used in EV power electronics, telecom, and industrial lighting. Its edge is owning both the dielectric material formulation and the manufacturing process in-house, reducing India's reliance on imported thermal substrates.
company.name: Helionis Labs

company.description: AI-native platform that unifies legal data, workflows, and business context for in-house legal teams, automating intake, triage, and enabling AI agents integrated with tools like Slack, Salesforce, and email.
company.name: Sandstone

company.description: Silicon Box is a Singapore-based advanced semiconductor packaging company specializing in chiplet integration and panel-level packaging at scale. Its proprietary process serves AI, high-performance computing, automotive, mobile, and IoT customers from a flagship factory in the Tampines Wafer Park, with a second site under construction in Novara, Italy. Founded in 2021 by industry veterans BJ Han, Sehat Sutardja, and Weili Dai, the company is a Singapore unicorn that shipped its 100-millionth unit in October 2025.
company.name: Silicon Box

company.description: Element Biosciences is a San Diego-based genomics and multiomics technology company that develops DNA sequencing systems and analysis tools. Its AVITI platform, launched in 2022, competes with incumbents like Illumina, and the company has since expanded into a broader ecosystem of genomic, transcriptomic, protein, spatial and cellular analysis products serving customers in over 40 countries.
company.name: Element Biosciences

company.description: Tibu Health is a Kenyan healthtech company founded in 2020 by Jason Carmichael, focused on affordable, high-quality primary healthcare for middle-income and underserved communities. Since 2024 it has run a network of embedded "Minute Clinics" placed inside pharmacies and supermarkets, linked through a hub-and-spoke model to a central hub offering medical imaging, lab testing, pediatrics, gynecology and telemedicine.
company.name: Tibu Health

company.description: Standard Bots is a Glen Cove, New York-based manufacturer of AI-native industrial robots, including six-axis robot arms and industrial humanoids, that are designed and assembled in the United States. Its robots are taught through demonstration and observation rather than traditional code, making automation accessible across tasks such as machining, welding, palletizing, assembly and inspection at a lower price point than legacy vendors. The company serves customers ranging from large enterprises like Sunoco, Lockheed Martin, Amazon, NASA and the U.S. Army to hundreds of small and mid-sized manufacturers.
company.name: Standard Bots

company.description: Cellares is the first Integrated Development and Manufacturing Organization (IDMO) providing automated cell therapy manufacturing through its Cell Shuttle platform, enabling scalable production of cell therapies with reduced costs and failure rates.
company.name: Cellares

company.description: Alta Ares is a French defense tech startup specializing in AI-powered computer vision technology for drones, enabling real-time object detection and tracking directly on the edge. The company's solutions are used for military recognition, industrial inspection, and surveillance, with a focus on operational resilience and local data processing.
company.name: Alta Ares

company.description: Golden is an AI-native BI platform that makes data easy, powerful & beautiful. | Golden Analytics is an AI-native BI platform built for data teams who are tired of the tradeoffs in today's tools -- the depth of  self-service analytics tools without the rigidity, the accessibility of a modern design tool, and AI that actually augments how analysts work rather than getting in the way.
company.name: Golden Analytics

company.description: GT Medical Technologies is a medical device company focused on improving the lives of patients with brain tumors through innovative treatments. Their flagship product, GammaTile, is an FDA-cleared bioabsorbable collagen implant embedded with radiation seeds, designed to deliver immediate, localized radiation therapy directly into the surgical cavity at tumor removal to prevent cancer regrowth while minimizing exposure to healthy brain tissue.
company.name: GT Medical Technologies, Inc.

company.description: Vinyl Equity is a Chicago-based financial technology company building modern infrastructure for capital markets and corporate transactions, operating as an SEC-registered transfer agent. Its platform replaces legacy, paper-based workflows with real-time systems for shareholder recordkeeping, equity operations, paying agency, and compliant payment distributions across both private and public markets. It supports issuers through IPOs, M&A, equity plan administration, and ongoing operations via integrated KYC/KYB, tax filing, audit trails, and API connections to equity plan administrators.
company.name: Vinyl Equity

company.description: Morpho operates an open, blockchain-based credit network that lets developers, fintechs, and institutions build customized onchain lending and borrowing markets with independent risk and rate parameters. Founded in 2021 in France by Paul Frambot and co-founders, it has grown into one of DeFi's largest lending protocols, with billions in deposits and adoption by major crypto firms including Coinbase, Kraken, Anchorage Digital, and Galaxy. The protocol positions itself as backend credit infrastructure that traditional banks and asset managers can plug into rather than replace.
company.name: Morpho

company.description: Soffi builds a collaborative development environment for enterprise product teams, turning a company's deployed product into a live workspace where designers and product managers work alongside engineers on real, running software and ship changes back as production-ready code. The platform connects to a customer's codebase, design system, and data, and is built for enterprise use with VPC isolation, role-based permissions, and auditability. Founded by ex-HashiCorp designer Cris Dobbins, the San Francisco company emerged from stealth in June 2026 already working with AI startups and large enterprises.
company.name: Soffi

company.description: Rylo, formerly Nagish, is an AI-powered communication platform built for and with the Deaf and hard-of-hearing community. Founded in 2021, it offers real-time captioning, text-to-speech, and sign language translation through its FCC-certified Rylo Phone app, and is expanding into a broader suite of accessibility tools such as workplace accessibility, sentiment analysis, and real-time fraud detection. The company operates from offices in New York and Tel Aviv.
company.name: Rylo

company.description: Mesoware is a Palo Alto, California-based robotics startup building an AI-powered ecosystem of modular hardware and software that helps manufacturers design, deploy, and scale robotic automation. Its platform lets customers configure productive robotic work cells quickly and handle real-world variation such as differences in part placement, tolerances, and sequence changes without frequent reprogramming, with early applications in assembly, packaging, machine tending, and drone subassembly. Founded by Joe Mattekatt, Shalin Patel, and Pei Liang Guo, it targets small and mid-sized, high-mix manufacturers that lack dedicated robotics teams.
company.name: Mesoware

company.description: Titan is a New York-based company building a banking-native AI platform purpose-built for financial services. Through a secure, private interface it gives banks, credit unions, and fintechs access to foundational models alongside its own purpose-built banking models and AI agents that reason through regulated workflows with built-in compliance, risk, and governance controls. It serves community, regional, and super-regional institutions that need AI infrastructure they can audit and defend to examiners.
company.name: Titan

company.description: Campground is an Oakland, California-based software company offering an operations platform purpose-built for social impact programs. Built by former program managers, it consolidates participant rosters, workflows, milestones, and outcomes into a single source of truth so frontline teams can run programs and report impact without juggling spreadsheets. It serves workforce development, youth employment, college access, and community-based service organizations.
company.name: Campground

company.description: Cordon Technologies is a UK agritech startup building precision crop-spraying systems that help farmers and growers cut pesticide and fertiliser use. Its core product, the Loop, is a dynamic mixing and precision-control system that lets growers adjust the concentration of multiple treatments in real time, turning spraying from a batch process into a continuously controlled one. The company has early commercial traction in the UK vineyard sector and is adapting the technology for other crops including plant nurseries, vegetables, apples and soft fruit.
company.name: Cordon Technologies

company.description: Rem3dy Health is a Birmingham-based health-tech scale-up that uses 3D printing, data science and automation to manufacture personalised nutrition products at scale. Its flagship direct-to-consumer brand, Nourished, produces tailored vitamin gummy "stacks", and the company is extending its platform into broader personalised health categories. Founded in 2019, it sells through UK retailers including Boots, Holland & Barrett and Ocado.
company.name: Rem3dy Health

company.description: Aavuus is a Finnish space-safety startup building a global network of ground-based laser stations that detect and track space debris in Low Earth Orbit, including objects as small as 1 cm that today's commercial systems miss. A spinout from the Finnish Geospatial Research Institute, it turns high-precision laser-ranging data into space situational awareness for satellite operators, launch providers, and space-infrastructure insurers. The company was established in 2025 and emerged through ESA BIC Finland.
company.name: Aavuus

company.description: BazaarNow is a Bengaluru-based quick commerce startup founded in January 2026 by former Zepto operators Priyanshu Jain, Arjun Harish, and Tarithnay Mandal. It promises sub-10-minute delivery of groceries, daily essentials, and higher-margin categories from a network of dark stores across select Bengaluru neighbourhoods. The company competes with Blinkit, Swiggy Instamart, Zepto, Flipkart Minutes, and Amazon Now in India's rapidly consolidating quick commerce market.
company.name: BazaarNow

company.description: Beacon Software is an AI holding company that acquires and partners with vertical software and services companies across various industries such as education, finance, logistics, and recreation. It provides a shared platform of technology, design, fintech, and go-to-market capabilities to help entrepreneurs grow their businesses and modernize traditional industries with AI.
company.name: Beacon Software

company.description: Celebal Technologies is a Jaipur-based enterprise data and AI services provider, co-founded in 2016 by Anupam Gupta and Anirudh Kala. It specialises in data engineering, cloud transformation, supply chain analytics and AI-led automation, helping enterprises modernise ERP and data platforms on stacks such as Databricks, Microsoft Azure, AWS, SAP and Sigma Computing. The company employs over 3,000 professionals — including more than 1,500 Databricks-certified staff — and partners closely with Microsoft and Databricks.
company.name: Celebal Technologies

company.description: Duely is a Belgian startup providing AI-native legal services for mergers and acquisitions. Originally an API-first technology company that built AI for virtual data rooms, it now combines proprietary AI workflows with legal expertise to handle data room preparation, legal due diligence, and transaction-related document review across Europe. Rather than licensing software to law firms, Duely applies its technology directly to the delivery of M&A legal services.
company.name: Duely

company.description: Earlytrade is a construction-focused fintech that runs an early-payment and working-capital marketplace connecting general contractors with their subcontractors and suppliers. Founded in Sydney in 2018, its platform lets subcontractors access payments earlier to ease cashflow pressure, and the company has facilitated over US$3 billion in early payments across a network of more than 211,000 subcontractors. It operates across Australia, New Zealand, the United States, Canada, Ireland and the United Kingdom.
company.name: Earlytrade

company.description: Orbital is a Los Angeles-based space infrastructure company building AI data centers in low Earth orbit, distributing inference compute across a constellation of small, independently deployable satellites that harness continuous solar power and radiative cooling. Founded by Euwyn Poon, who previously built e-scooter company Spin, Orbital is designing its systems around NVIDIA's Space-1 Vera Rubin-class GPU architecture and plans a Pathfinder demonstration flight before scaling to a large compute constellation.
company.name: Orbital

company.description: Integra Robotics, operated by Social Hardware International, is a Bengaluru-based deeptech company building a modular embodied-AI platform for Industry 5.0 and defence. Its portfolio spans collaborative and industrial robotic arms, end-of-arm tooling, unmanned ground vehicles, and advanced vision systems for manufacturing, warehouse automation, security, defence, and subsea use. A shared communication, teleoperation, and autonomous-learning stack underpins a human-in-the-loop data flywheel that turns guided tasks into training data so workflows grow more autonomous over time.
company.name: Integra Robotics

company.description: Stepful is a New York-based healthcare workforce training platform that offers accelerated, affordable, employer-sponsored pathways into allied-health careers such as medical assistant, pharmacy technician, surgical tech and practical nursing. It delivers a vertically integrated "school-as-a-service" model to hospitals and health systems, using AI for personalized learning, clinical simulation and competency assessment to train high school graduates into practice-ready workers without debt. Founded in 2021 and backed by Y Combinator, Stepful partners with 35+ major health systems and connects graduates directly to jobs after certification.
company.name: Stepful

company.description: SyntheticFi is a San Francisco-based fintech and SEC-registered investment advisor that gives independent financial advisors (RIAs) and high-net-worth clients access to low-cost, tax-efficient securities-backed financing. Its platform packages institutional borrowing strategies once limited to hedge funds and the ultra-wealthy, such as box spreads and synthetic variable prepaid forwards, into tools advisors can offer alongside technology, education, and implementation support. Founded in 2023 out of Y Combinator's Summer 2023 batch, the company works with hundreds of advisory firms across the U.S.
company.name: SyntheticFi

company.description: fonio.ai is an innovative Vienna-based company specialized in AI-powered phone assistants. With their technology, businesses can answer calls automatically, while still appearing human and pleasant. Whether it's scheduling appointments, forwarding inquiries or automatically transcribing conversations, fonio.ai integrates directly into existing CRM systems, calendars and databases, enabling modern, efficient customer communication. Particularly noteworthy is the assistant's german, natural voice
company.name: fonio.ai

company.description: Iceye is a Finnish spacetech company specializing in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite technology for Earth observation. It operates the world's largest SAR satellite constellation, providing high-resolution radar imagery and data for sectors such as defense, insurance, disaster response, maritime monitoring, and finance. The company has a global presence with over 700 employees and offices in Europe, the US, Asia, and the Middle East.
company.name: Iceye

company.description: NinjaOne provides a unified, automated endpoint management platform (RMM) that combines device visibility, patching, remote monitoring, vulnerability management, backup, and endpoint security into a single console. Its software targets IT teams and managed service providers, helping customers increase productivity, reduce operational risk, and simplify IT operations across Windows, macOS, Linux and mobile devices. The company serves enterprise and MSP customers globally and reports tens of thousands of customers across 140+ countries and significant annual recurring revenue as of FY2025.
company.name: NinjaOne

company.description: CeQur is a medical device company simplifying mealtime insulin delivery for people living with type 1 and type 2 diabetes. Its flagship product, CeQur Simplicity, is a discreet, injection-free wearable insulin patch clinically shown to improve glycemic control and free patients from multiple daily injections. Headquartered in Horw, Switzerland, the company runs its US commercial operations from Greenville, South Carolina.
company.name: CeQur

company.description:
Weather data with instruments and patented GNSS Meteorology solutions fueling segment specific AI-enhanced forecasts. | Skyfora is a technical frontrunner pioneering the next generation of weather intelligence. Our patented solution transforms existing telecom infrastructure into a high-resolution weather observation network delivering dense, real-time atmospheric data at an unprecedented scale.

By unlocking continuous streams of proprietary weather data, we dramatically improve the spatial and temporal accuracy of AI weather models.
company.name: Skyfora

company.description: German space startup spun off from Technical University of Munich, focused on launch vehicles.
company.name: Isar Aerospace

company.description: Evotrex is a California-based company innovating outdoor travel with intelligent, power-generating RV trailers designed for adventure without limits, enabling off-grid living with advanced energy management and comfort.
company.name: Evotrex

company.description: Merchantee is a Prague-based startup building an agentic marketplace management platform that helps online sellers grow across European marketplaces without expanding their teams. Its full-loop AI system handles pricing, discount campaigns, dynamic price adjustments, and advertising, managing data, decisions, execution, and optimization within seller-defined budget and sales limits.
company.name: Merchantee

company.description: Granarium Technologies is a Finnish deep-tech startup spun out of VTT Technical Research Centre that develops 100% renewable supercapacitors for fast-response energy storage. Its patented platform uses nanocellulose and biocarbon from waste wood and agricultural residues, cutting production capex by up to 80% versus conventional supercapacitors while avoiding finite, mined materials. The devices provide millisecond-scale grid balancing, frequency response and industrial power-quality support, complementing batteries for short-duration needs.
company.name: Granarium Technologies

company.description: The Wedding Company (TWC) is a Bengaluru-based, tech-enabled full-service wedding planning and fulfilment platform that brings venues, décor, catering, photography, and logistics together under one roof. It works with more than 2,000 verified vendors across multiple cities to deliver professionally managed weddings for couples in India and abroad, targeting the country's highly fragmented multi-billion-dollar wedding market. Founded in 2023 by Pawan Gupta and Rahul Namdev, it serves the mid-AOV wedding segment.
company.name: The Wedding Company

company.description: Pathway Power is a San Diego-based independent power producer (IPP) developing utility-scale hybrid and battery energy storage (BESS) projects across U.S. RTO markets, purpose-built for large-load customers, utilities, and grid operators. Its pipeline comprises 13 late-stage hybrid and storage projects totaling roughly 3.2 GWac, targeting load growth from data centers and reshoring in congested grid markets. The founding team previously led BayWa r.e. and brings deep utility-scale solar and storage development experience.
company.name: Pathway Power

company.description: Cavalry Biosciences is a San Francisco-based biotech developing tissue-selective growth-factor medicines, built around engineering IGF-1 into Antibody-Biologic Conjugates that direct the growth factor to specific tissues to avoid the systemic side effects of conventional IGF-1 therapy. Its pipeline targets eye, muscle, and nerve disorders, with lead programs in corneal regeneration, skeletal-muscle wasting, and nerve remyelination. The company is the first biotech to emerge from the Jupiter Bioventures incubator.
company.name: Cavalry Biosciences

company.description: TSFC is a Swiss quantitative trading firm, based in Baar, that runs AI-driven counter-trend strategies for cryptocurrency markets, serving high-net-worth investors and allocators. Its flagship ELEPHANT strategy pairs proprietary AI geometric models for liquidity-zone identification with human analyst validation before each trade, and operates non-custodially via API-only execution on major exchanges so clients retain control of their capital.
company.name: TSFC

company.description: DJ Insight is a Tokyo-based AI startup founded in November 2025 that develops Kagami AI, a platform analyzing non-verbal signals from real-world face-to-face interactions. By processing video and audio, it visualizes facial expressions, voice tone, concentration, and engagement to turn cues that were previously read by intuition into actionable data. The company targets beauty salons first, with planned expansion into education, corporate training, events, tourism, and retail customer service.
company.name: DJ Insight

company.description: Finovox is a French company offering AI-powered document fraud detection used by insurers, banks and other financial institutions to verify the authenticity of identity documents, payslips, invoices and similar paperwork during onboarding, claims and credit decisions. Founded in 2019 and based in Paris, its API and SaaS platform serves more than 70 clients across roughly 15 countries, including names such as Allianz Direct, MetLife, Bouygues Telecom and PwC.
company.name: Finovox

company.description: Klinic is a Frisco, Texas-based healthcare technology company that operates a provider-enablement platform often described as a "Shopify for independent healthcare practices." Its unified stack bundles patient acquisition and marketing, intake, an electronic health record, CRM, care coordination, and revenue-cycle and prior-authorization management so independent providers can run both the clinical and operational sides of a practice. Founded in 2021 with an initial focus on behavioral health, Klinic has expanded to support roughly a dozen specialties including oncology, cardiology, and rare-disease therapies.
company.name: Klinic

company.description: Spektra Games is an Istanbul-based mobile game studio founded in 2020 that builds and publishes high-quality racing and simulation titles for a global audience. Its games, including Highway Racer Pro and Parking Master Multiplayer, have surpassed 100 million downloads worldwide, with Highway Racer Pro ranking as a top racing title on the U.S. App Store. The studio operates a free-to-play model monetized through in-app purchases and advertising.
company.name: Spektra Games

company.description: esVolta, LP is a US developer, owner, and operator of utility-scale battery energy storage systems, founded in 2017 and headquartered in Newport Beach, California. It serves utilities and corporate offtakers across major grid markets including CAISO, ERCOT and WECC, with roughly 2.0 GWh in operation or construction and a pipeline of around 30 projects totaling 25 GWh. The company is a portfolio company of sustainable infrastructure investor Generate Capital.
company.name: esVolta

company.description: CreditChek is a Nigerian credit-data infrastructure company, founded in 2021 by Kingsley Ibe and Lionel Orishane, that aggregates data from financial institutions, credit bureaus, and alternative sources into a single API so lenders can assess the creditworthiness of African borrowers in real time. It has processed over $60 million in credit applications across more than one million unique profiles and reached profitability in Nigeria. The company is now expanding cross-border into East African markets, targeting banks, microfinance institutions, and fintech lenders.
company.name: CreditChek

company.description: Upriver is a San Francisco-based, AI-native data engineering platform that autonomously manages the data layer enterprises rely on to run AI. It connects to an organization's full data stack to resolve data-quality issues, maintain pipelines, and build new datasets end-to-end, pairing a context engine that maps a company's data ontology with coordinated AI agents for verifiable reasoning. The platform partners with Databricks and Snowflake and is used by enterprises such as Unity and DMGT.
company.name: Upriver

company.description: City Therapeutics is a Cambridge, MA biopharmaceutical company developing next-generation RNA interference (RNAi) medicines. Its platform pairs engineered trigger molecules with precise targeting ligands to deliver siRNA therapies beyond the liver to a broad range of tissues, expanding where RNAi drugs can be used. Co-founded in 2024 by former Alnylam CEO John Maraganore, the company is building a pipeline across multiple therapeutic areas, led by CITY-FXI for thromboembolic disease and CITY-RBP4 for Stargardt disease.
company.name: City Therapeutics

company.description: Invisible-Light Labs is a Vienna-based DeepTech company, spun out of TU Wien in 2019, that builds highly sensitive analytical instruments for chemical analysis of nanoscale materials. Its flagship product, EMILIE, uses nanoelectromechanical Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (NEMS-FTIR) to deliver picogram-level chemical identification of sub-micron particles, aerosols, nanoplastics, and biopharmaceuticals available in very small quantities. The system is co-marketed globally with instrument maker Bruker Optics, serving pharmaceutical, environmental-monitoring, and nanotechnology applications.
company.name: Invisible-Light Labs

company.description: Infinity Constellation is a US-based, AI-native holding company that builds new professional-services businesses from scratch rather than acquiring existing firms. Working with repeat founder-operators, it spins up AI-driven companies across fields such as design, finance, hiring, compliance and executive assistance, aiming to launch up to eight per year. Its portfolio includes a branding platform, a construction-permitting automation startup and an AI executive-assistant service, all sharing data and tooling through a common operating model.
company.name: Infinity Constellation

company.description: Kloset Klub (formerly WISI-Oi) is a Cape Town-based circular fashion marketplace where South Africans buy, sell and chat about pre-loved clothing through curated peer-to-peer resale and managed-closet services. The platform handles nationwide delivery via PUDO lockers and secure post-delivery payments, positioning itself as infrastructure for Africa's growing fashion-resale economy. Founded by Phumi Korber and Kim Gouws, it has been recognised by Google for Startups and won AfricArena's Best FemTech award.
company.name: Kloset Klub

company.description: Hygenco Green Energies is an Indian green hydrogen and green ammonia developer founded in 2020 and headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana. It builds and operates commercial-scale production assets on a Hydrogen-as-a-Service model, supplying zero-emission green molecules to hard-to-abate industrial customers; its Project Heartland in Hisar was among India's first 100% green hydrogen plants. The company is scaling a portfolio of green hydrogen and green ammonia projects, including a 1.1 million tonne-per-year green ammonia facility in Odisha.
company.name: Hygenco Green Energies

company.description: MNT-Halan is a leading Egyptian fintech and embedded lending platform providing credit and financial services to underbanked micro and small businesses and consumers in Egypt. The company offers consumer financing, microloans, digital payments, and e-commerce solutions, serving millions of customers through its super-app and network of agents across Egypt.
company.name: MNT-Halan

company.description: Powering a cleaner future | GPS Renewables is India's leading full-stack biofuels firm, offering technology & EPC to project development solutions for bioCNG, Ethanol & Green H2. GPSR is backed by marquee investors including the likes of SBI Capital (Neev), Hivos-Triodos and Caspian. GPSR has been a common factor behind many milestone biofuel projects, including the biogas projects that powered Indore & Panchgani to bag the cleanest city awards in their respective categories, or Reliance Bioenergy's milestone projects such as Barabanki.
company.name: GPSR Arya

company.description: Immuneel Therapeutics is a Bengaluru-based cell and gene therapy company developing affordable CAR-T immunotherapies for cancer. Founded in 2018 by Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw, oncologist Siddhartha Mukherjee and Kush Parwar, it commercialised Qartemi, described as India's first approved CAR-T therapy for Non-Hodgkin lymphoma, priced at roughly a tenth of comparable global treatments. The company runs India's CAR-T trials and is expanding manufacturing and exports across West Asia and Asia-Pacific.
company.name: Immuneel Therapeutics

company.description: Origin Quantum is China's first quantum computing company, founded in 2017 in Hefei by researchers from the University of Science and Technology of China. It is a full-stack developer of superconducting quantum computers and chips, having shipped four generations of machines including the 180-qubit Origin Wukong, and runs a quantum cloud platform used internationally. The company is widely regarded as China's leading quantum computing firm and is preparing for a Shanghai STAR Market IPO.
company.name: Origin Quantum

company.description: Wassist is a London-based startup that turns WhatsApp into a sales and commerce channel for online retailers. Its no-code platform lets a merchant spin up an AI agent trained on their brand simply by entering a store URL, after which the agent answers product questions, recommends items, recovers abandoned carts and guides shoppers through purchases inside the WhatsApp thread. The product integrates with e-commerce tools including Shopify, Klaviyo, Recharge and Yotpo.
company.name: Wassist

company.description: EDGE Markets is a U.S. financial services company building banking, payment, and settlement infrastructure for regulated alternative markets — sports betting, gaming, crypto, and prediction markets. Its first product, EDGE Boost, is an FDIC-insured Visa debit program built specifically for bettors that has processed over $2 billion in transactions since launch. The company is now extending into institutional infrastructure with EDGE Pro, high-throughput deposit accounts for market makers on CFTC-regulated exchanges, and EDGE Connect, a real-time payment rail for gaming and prediction-market operators.
company.name: Edge Markets

company.description: Breaking Change is a Chepstow, Wales-based technology startup building software infrastructure for the global games industry. Its platform combines simulation technology with AI-assisted authoring to help studios model, simulate and maintain the complex systems behind modern games, such as vehicles, weapons, progression and economies, without months of bespoke engineering. Founded in 2025, the company is moving its platform toward studio pilots and commercial launch.
company.name: Breaking Change

company.description: Lexful is an AI-native IT documentation platform built exclusively for managed service providers (MSPs). It automatically captures, maintains, and connects documentation across assets, procedures, and credentials, turning stale records and tribal knowledge into reliable, contextual intelligence that technicians can query in plain English via its "Ask Lex" assistant. Founded by Pinar Ormeci and based in Salt Lake City, the platform integrates deeply with major PSA/RMM tools and positions itself as a real-time system of record for MSP knowledge operations in the AI era.
company.name: Lexful

company.description: Reset (Reset Financial Technologies) is a Menlo Park, California fintech that provides an embedded earned-wage access platform built for credit unions and community banks. Its product gives accountholders fee-free daily access to earned income plus automated cashflow budgeting, delivered directly through their own financial institution rather than a third-party app. The model is designed to drive core deposit growth, premium interchange revenue, and deeper member engagement for the institution while helping working families build more stable finances.
company.name: Reset

company.description: Rejuvenate Bio is a San Diego-based biotechnology company developing gene therapies that target the root biological drivers of aging and chronic, age-related disease. Its platform applies gene delivery, epigenetic reprogramming and longevity science to treat conditions such as osteoarthritis, heart disease and cancer, beginning in companion animals like dogs before extending to human health. The company pairs its veterinary pipeline with longer-term human therapeutic ambitions.
company.name: Rejuvenate Bio

company.description: InfoHawk is an Austin, Texas-based cybersecurity startup building an AI-powered platform to detect and neutralize online fraud, scams, and deception at internet scale. Its software scans the web for fake websites, copied brand assets, and deepfakes, maps the technical infrastructure behind fraud operations, and delivers flagging and takedown capabilities to enterprises through high-performance APIs.
company.name: InfoHawk

company.description: Companion.energy is a Ghent, Belgium-based software company, founded in 2022, that helps large industrial and commercial enterprises manage and optimize energy in real time. Its platform unifies energy contracts, operational systems, and distributed assets (solar, wind, batteries, electric boilers and EV charging) into a single optimization layer that continuously forecasts demand, models market exposure, and autonomously executes energy decisions. It serves enterprises such as TotalEnergies, KPN, the Port of Antwerp-Bruges and Proximus Group, managing over 2 TWh of annual energy use across customer portfolios.
company.name: Companion.energy

company.description: Juno Propulsion is a Seattle-based aerospace startup, founded in 2023 by Purdue PhD graduates Alexis Harroun and Ariana Martinez, developing rotating detonation engines (RDEs) for in-space propulsion. Its RDE design burns nitrous oxide and ethane in a continuous rotating detonation wave to deliver higher specific impulse than conventional chemical thrusters while avoiding toxic hydrazine. The company won a NASA TechLeap prize in 2025 and plans to fly its first commercial RDE, Project Iris, as a hosted payload on a Momentus satellite bus in 2027.
company.name: Juno Propulsion

company.description: PointFive is an AI and cloud efficiency platform that detects deep, root-cause waste across cloud infrastructure, data platforms, and AI workloads, then routes automated remediation to the engineers who own the fix. Running agentlessly and read-only, it helps teams understand spend in plain language and ship optimizations at scale, with customers reporting around 30% cloud cost savings. Founded in 2023, it serves enterprises such as Nubank, E.ON, Hertz, and Swiss Post running $1M+ in annual cloud and AI spend, with 100+ staff across Tel Aviv, London, and the US.
company.name: PointFive

company.description: Clear Robotics (Clearbot) is a Hong Kong-founded maritime technology company building all-electric, AI-enabled unmanned surface vessels for critical infrastructure tasks such as waterway pollution recovery and marine surveillance. Founded in 2020, it operates a fleet of 26 autonomous, zero-emission vessels and also develops autonomous retrofit technology for existing ships. The company has expanded across Singapore, Malaysia and the Philippines and won the Pier71 Smart Port Challenge in 2024.
company.name: Clear Robotics

company.description: BeatpulseLabs builds the data infrastructure layer for enterprise AI, converting raw multimodal data such as speech, video, and music into high-fidelity, rights-cleared training datasets. Rather than relying on generic freelance annotators, it uses in-house domain specialists and client-specific annotation guidelines to label, validate, and structure data for model training, fine-tuning, and evaluation. Founded in 2026 and based in London, the company also supplies ready-made and custom datasets for enterprises that lack sufficient internal training data.
company.name: BeatpulseLabs

company.description: Blnk is a Giza, Egypt-based fintech that runs an AI-enabled digital consumer finance platform, giving merchants instant point-of-sale credit underwriting so their customers can finance purchases on the spot. Founded in 2021, it targets financial inclusion across the Middle East and North Africa, where fewer than 5% of adults hold formal credit, and has onboarded more than a million customers, most of them previously unbanked. Loans run from six to 36 months and are approved in minutes using proprietary machine-learning risk models.
company.name: Blnk

company.description: NewOrbit is a UK satellite manufacturer, founded in 2021 and based in Reading, building spacecraft purpose-engineered to operate in very low Earth orbit (VLEO), the 200-300km band historically considered too hostile for commercial satellites. Its in-house propulsion system lets craft withstand atmospheric drag and atomic oxygen for up to five years, enabling far cheaper high-resolution Earth observation and low-latency, direct-to-device connectivity than conventional higher-orbit satellites.
company.name: NewOrbit

company.description: Volteum is a fleet electrification software provider that helps organisations manage the transition to electric vehicles and operate mixed fleets of diesel, petrol and electric vehicles from a single platform. Its software connects directly to manufacturer vehicle data — requiring no extra hardware — to consolidate charging, mileage and maintenance information, automate reporting and flag inefficiencies, and the company claims it can cut fleet operating costs by up to 30%. Volteum offers an Electric Fleet Planner for transition strategy and Electric Fleet Operations for day-to-day management, serving customers including Royal Mail, Bolt, Lex Autolease, Schneider Electric and Dundee City Council.
company.name: Volteum

company.description: Accelevate Solutions, based in Henderson, United States, develops AI-driven business intelligence solutions, including the world's first AI-Assistant with generative capabilities to optimize fuel usage and operational costs across various fuel types.
company.name: Accelevate Solutions

company.description: GALVANY (GALVANY Energy GmbH) is a Berlin-based climate and energy-technology company, founded in 2022, that runs an end-to-end platform for residential heat pump adoption in Germany. It combines its own GALVANY Cube hardware (built with Panasonic in Pilsen), battery storage and the Fusion energy-management software with customer acquisition, subsidy handling, installation and ongoing operation, with a focus on older multi-family buildings. The company reached €20.1M revenue and positive EBIT in 2025 after installing more than 2,500 systems.
company.name: GALVANY

company.description: PhysicsX is a physical AI company building a new software stack for AI-native engineering, moving large-scale numerical physics simulation to inference with AI to transform how physical systems are designed, tested, manufactured, and operated. The company partners with leading organizations in aerospace & defense, automotive, semiconductors, materials, and energy & renewables.
company.name: PhysicsX

company.description: EQuest Education Group is one of Vietnam's largest private education providers, operating an integrated ecosystem spanning K-12 international and bilingual schools (including the Canadian International School and Newton Grammar School), higher and vocational education, and edtech platforms such as iSMART. Headquartered in Ho Chi Minh City with operations across multiple Vietnamese cities, it serves several hundred thousand students annually and has been backed by global investment firm KKR since 2021.
company.name: EQuest Education Group

company.description: Opereit is a Barcelona-based logistics technology company that builds autonomous AI agents to recover revenue lost across freight and shipping operations. Its platform detects transport invoice discrepancies, identifies lost or damaged shipments, and files and manages carrier claims end-to-end, automating what has traditionally been a manual back-office process. Founded in 2025, it targets the billions that retailers and logistics providers leave unrecovered each year through overcharges and unclaimed credits.
company.name: Opereit

company.description: Estro Tech Robotics is a Kerala-based deep-tech startup building robotics and AIoT (Artificial Intelligence of Things) systems for retail, infrastructure, and industrial applications. Founded in 2020, it first gained attention for India's first UV-sterilising robot during the pandemic and now develops products such as RobAd, an in-store retail advertising robot, alongside inspection robotics for underground utility networks. The company has established distribution partnerships across Nigeria, Botswana, Qatar, and South Korea.
company.name: Estro Tech Robotics

company.description: A-Star Quantum (A*Quantum) is a Tokyo-based quantum computing software company founded in 2018. It develops AQCloud, a cloud platform that blends quantum computing with classical mathematical-optimization and AI techniques to solve large-scale combinatorial optimization problems in logistics, manufacturing, transport, energy, and finance. The company is now moving from an R&D and proof-of-concept phase into commercial expansion, and is also building a proprietary high-speed database to support enterprise AI workloads.
company.name: A-Star Quantum

company.description: Rezonate (formerly KeyReply) is a Singapore-founded AI company, established in 2014, that builds AI agents for enterprise customer and workforce engagement. Originally focused on healthcare, its platform now serves insurance, financial services, sports and other regulated sectors, helping organizations automate engagement workflows, support and service-delivery operations. The June 2026 rebrand and US$5M raise accompany its push into the United States and the scaling of its agentic AI platform.
company.name: Rezonate

company.description: DubGuild is a Tokyo-based AI research company building text-free, voice-focused foundational models known as Spoken Language Models (SLM/LSLM). Rather than chaining speech recognition, an LLM, and text-to-speech, its approach discretizes audio into tokens via a neural audio codec and processes it end-to-end, aiming for real-time natural dialogue with emotion understanding and interruptions. Under the mission "Scaling Speech AI," the company is developing a 30B-scale voice language model and was selected for Japan's government-backed GENIAC generative-AI program.
company.name: DubGuild

company.description: kAIgentic is a Singapore-headquartered agentic AI startup, with core engineering operations in India, founded in 2026 by former Microsoft Asia Pacific president Ahmed Mazhari. It builds an enterprise intelligence and deployment layer that captures an organisation's day-to-day operational logic and turns it into domain-specific AI agents that run in live production under continuous human supervision. The company is focused on safe, governed AI deployment inside highly regulated environments, launching with Japan's SMBC Group as its first customer in banking before expanding to other sectors.
company.name: kAIgentic

company.description: A Solana-based Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network (DePIN) project building a decentralized energy grid where participants can produce, trade, and consume energy peer-to-peer.
company.name: Fuse Energy

company.description: Yamify is a developer-first AI platform enabling freelancers, startups, and web agencies to deploy GPU-powered AI tools quickly, hosted on local African data centers and billed in local currencies. Founded by Luc Okalobé, it functions like an AI app store offering chatbots, automation agents, and language models, aiming to democratize AI infrastructure for Africa and emerging markets.
company.name: Yamify

company.description: DayOne Data Centers is a Singapore-headquartered developer and operator of next-generation hyperscale data centers, providing scalable, reliable digital infrastructure solutions across Asia-Pacific and Europe, including markets such as Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Japan, Hong Kong, and Finland.
company.name: DayOne Data Centers

company.description: Rapidus Corporation is a Japanese semiconductor foundry startup developing and manufacturing advanced logic semiconductors at the 2nm process node using gate-all-around (GAA) transistor architecture, with a focus on shortening cycle times in design, wafer processes, and 3D packaging.
company.name: Rapidus Corporation

company.description: Zimi is a Stellenbosch-based energy technology company offering an integrated electric-vehicle-as-a-service platform for commercial fleet operators in South Africa. It bundles EVs, charging infrastructure, solar energy systems and energy management tools, with a maintenance-lease option that converts capital expenditure into predictable operating costs. Founded in 2021, the company positions its turnkey "Diesel to Digital" model as a way to cut fleet energy costs while reducing exposure to volatile fuel prices.
company.name: Zimi

company.description: Midnight Labs is a Dublin-based AI company that builds an automated intellectual-property enforcement platform for the entertainment, gaming and content industries, detecting and removing piracy, leaks, deepfakes and AI-generated infringement across more than 75 million online sources. Its agentic Enforcement Engine scans, verifies and takes down stolen content while producing court-admissible forensic evidence, and its creator-focused product Ceartas protects the names, likenesses and brands of content creators. The company, which also operates in Tokyo and San Francisco, says it has removed over 2.8 billion pieces of infringing content for streaming platforms, talent agencies, creators and Fortune 100 executives.
company.name: Midnight Labs

company.description: Allen Control Systems (ACS) is an Austin, Texas-based defense technology company building autonomous precision weapon systems for counter-drone defense. Its flagship product, Bullfrog, is an AI-powered robotic weapon station that upgrades widely-used existing weapons such as the M240 machine gun, using computer vision and precision robotics to autonomously detect, track, and engage drone threats without a human operator. ACS positions kinetic defeat as a low-cost, scalable alternative to scarce missiles and jamming, and its systems are deployed with the U.S. Army and Navy.
company.name: Allen Control Systems

company.description: GR3N is a Lugano-based Swiss cleantech company, founded in 2013, that develops chemical recycling technology for PET plastics and polyester fibres. Its patented MADE (Microwave Assisted DEpolymerisation) process can recycle 100% of PET waste — including textiles, films and coloured resins that conventional mechanical recycling cannot handle — into food-grade monomers that can be reused indefinitely. The company is industrialising the technology through MODUS, a first-of-a-kind 40,000-ton/year plant in Spain.
company.name: GR3N

company.description: Oscorp Energy is a Sydney-based robotics startup building AI-powered vision and robotic systems that identify, classify and remove materials from fast-moving waste and recycling streams in real time. Its first product targets lithium-ion batteries, autonomously detecting and removing them before they spark fires in collection trucks, material recovery facilities and recycling sites. The company aims to grow into a broader "Vision OS" platform giving waste operators live intelligence on material flow, contamination and recovery performance.
company.name: Oscorp Energy

company.description: Alloovium is a Brisbane-based startup building an AI-powered workspace for the construction and heavy-industry sector. Its platform unifies the documents and project knowledge scattered across drawings, contracts, specs and reports, letting teams ask questions and get cited answers instantly while automating compliance, safety and end-of-month reporting paperwork. Founded by civil engineer Zander Schweitzer and technical co-founder Cielo Nicolosi, the company has deployed across active construction projects worth more than A$400 million and completed both the Startmate and Y Combinator accelerators.
company.name: Alloovium

company.description: Sensie is a Ghent-based agtech company building wireless plant-health sensors and an AI interpretation platform for professional greenhouse growers. Its first product, Sensie Omni, captures direct physiological signals from the crop - root-zone supply, micro-climate demand and plant stress response - in a single wireless device and translates them into daily irrigation and climate decisions through the Agrimentis intelligence dashboard. Founded in 2025, the company aims to make real-time plant intelligence affordable and scalable for commercial growers, researchers and technology suppliers.
company.name: Sensie

company.description: Resand (formerly Finn Recycling) is a Finnish cleantech company that regenerates and recycles foundry sand, supplying reclaimed sand to foundries on a Sand-as-a-Service model. Its technology recovers used casting sand at close to 100 percent efficiency, cutting the need for virgin sand and reducing sand-related CO2 emissions by up to 80 percent. Headquartered in Nuutajarvi, Finland, the company is scaling across European foundry markets with customers in the DACH region and Central Europe.
company.name: Resand

company.description: Innovafeed is a French biotech, founded in 2016, that produces insect-based ingredients — protein, oil and frass (fertilizer) — from black soldier fly larvae for the aquaculture, pet food and animal-nutrition markets. Its industrial site in Nesle runs a circular model, rearing the larvae on agricultural co-products from a neighbouring starch plant, and the company says it now operates the world's largest facility of its kind. Innovafeed positions its products as a sustainable, locally produced alternative to marine- and crop-based feed ingredients.
company.name: Innovafeed

company.description: JumpStart is an Indonesian automated-retail operator that runs a nationwide network of AI-enabled, cashless vending machines. Founded in 2018 around smart coffee machines, it has grown into a broader platform spanning smart marts, fresh-juice dispensers, ice cream and cotton-candy units, and gamified mystery-box formats. The company positions its machines as a distribution channel for curated Japanese food and beverage brands targeting Indonesian and wider Southeast Asian consumers.
company.name: JumpStart

company.description: Joyvié Health is a UK-based healthtech startup developing patent-pending continence underwear that captures stool in a disposable pouch immediately after excretion, sharply reducing stool-to-skin contact for people living with faecal incontinence. Designed for care home, hospital and home settings, it aims to preserve skin integrity and dignity while easing the caregiver burden. Founded by Zoe Robson, the company is preparing clinical pilots and a direct-to-consumer launch.
company.name: Joyvié Health

company.description: Red Metals is a Charleston, South Carolina-based copper manufacturer working to reshore U.S. copper refining. It combines processing, sorting, and refining into a single integrated operation that converts copper feedstocks, initially domestic copper scrap, directly into finished products such as high-conductivity copper rod, using fewer steps and less energy than conventional methods. Founded in 2025 by Jackson Switzer, the company is building its first 42,000-square-foot facility in North Charleston to supply power infrastructure, data center, electrification and defense demand.
company.name: Red Metals

company.description:
Spacecraft represent the most pressing need across the entire aerospace industry. As more launch vehicles come online and the cost to orbit decreases, more companies launching payloads to space continue to emerge.

For the first time in history, this influx of payload companies combined with reduced launch costs has resulted in a massive increase in need for commercial spacecraft platforms, known as satellite buses. These buses hold the payloads of our customers and are flown on launch vehicles.

Apex manufactures these satellite buses at scale using a combination of software, vertical integration, and hardware that is designed for manufacturing. Our spacecraft enable the future of society: ranging from earth observation to communications and more.

We’d love for you to join us on our mission of providing humankind access to the galaxy beyond our planet.
company.name: Apex Space

company.description: Promedius is a South Korean medical AI company founded in 2019 by researchers from Asan Medical Center, focused on the early detection and management of aging-related and metabolic diseases. Its flagship product, Osteo Signal, screens for osteoporosis risk in roughly three seconds using a standard chest X-ray, surfacing high-risk patients who would otherwise be missed by conventional DXA testing. The company has secured regulatory approvals in Vietnam and Indonesia and is pursuing U.S. FDA and European CE MDR clearance.
company.name: Promedius

company.description: Innefu Labs is an Indian AI company founded in 2010 that builds data-analytics and information-security platforms for national security, defence, intelligence and law enforcement agencies. Its systems power deployments such as India's first National Terrorism Data Fusion Centre and large operational intelligence-fusion centres across the Indian subcontinent, Middle East and Southeast Asia. The company positions itself as a Make-in-India sovereign-AI vendor serving government and revenue-intelligence operations.
company.name: Innefu Labs

company.description: Aadyah Aerospace is an Indian aerospace and defence technology company founded in 2016 by former ISRO scientists and aerospace veterans. It develops mission-critical systems and subsystems including propulsion systems, avionics, guidance and navigation technologies, and electro-mechanical actuators for space, launch vehicle, and defence applications.
company.name: Aadyah Aerospace

company.description: Arincare is a Bangkok-based pharmacy management and healthcare ecosystem platform that connects pharmaceutical manufacturers, distributors, healthcare brands, and pharmacies through integrated SaaS, a B2B marketplace, and e-prescription services. The company serves over 6,000 registered pharmacies and supports more than 12,000 pharmacists across Thailand, positioning itself as core operational infrastructure for the country's independent pharmacy sector.
company.name: Arincare

company.description: Digital-first Mexican fintech offering consumer credit and a cash‑rewards credit card (Plata Card); builds proprietary AI-driven core banking infrastructure to serve underbanked customers and is preparing to launch full banking services.
company.name: Plata

company.description: Marble Technologies is a Lincoln, Nebraska-based robotics and AI company that builds turnkey automation for meat processing. Its flagship "pack-off" lines combine camera arrays, on-site compute and custom machine-learning models to sort, inspect and box cuts of meat, and the company says its systems now handle roughly 3% of daily U.S. beef production. Founded in 2020 by veterans of agtech software firm GrainBridge, Marble reached profitability in 2025 and is extending into X-ray/CT meat inspection and automated de-boning.
company.name: Marble Technologies

company.description: Midddleman is a Nigerian procurement platform that helps African SMEs source wholesale goods directly from Chinese factories and marketplaces such as 1688, Taobao and Pinduoduo. It combines an AI sourcing assistant, Naira-to-RMB payments, a network of vetted China-based agents, and freight logistics into a single app. Since launching its payments product in 2023 it has processed over 2 billion naira in cross-border payments and grown to more than 12,500 users across Nigeria and West Africa.
company.name: Midddleman Technologies

company.description: Cogensus is a Sheridan, Wyoming-based digital health and "AgeTech" company that uses artificial intelligence to support cognitive health in older adults. Its platform pairs an AI companion that holds human-like conversations with seniors — combining mental health, clinical care, and memory preservation — with a data engine that captures cognitive progression and social determinants of health, turning daily reflections into "memory preservation artifacts" for families. The enterprise SaaS product is expected to launch commercially in Q3 2026.
company.name: Cogensus

company.description: Goldenrod Therapeutics is a Houston-based, Fannin-founded precision therapeutics company developing brain-penetrant small-molecule therapies for neurodegenerative and neurological disorders. Its lead candidate, 11h, is an orally bioavailable PDE4 inhibitor in-licensed from the University of Nebraska Medical Center, with a beachhead indication in Friedreich's Ataxia. The company aims to extend the program across CNS conditions including Alzheimer's, Multiple Sclerosis and ALS.
company.name: Goldenrod Therapeutics

company.description: VoltSeal is a New Delhi-based distributed energy storage company serving commercial and industrial customers in India. It installs behind-the-meter lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery systems at industrial sites and pairs them with a software-driven optimization layer, helping customers cut diesel-generator dependence, shift consumption across tariff periods and reduce demand charges. VoltSeal retains operational dispatch control over each deployed unit, with the longer-term aim of aggregating them into virtual power plants that provide grid-balancing services.
company.name: VoltSeal

company.description: Idilio is a Latin American microdrama streaming platform built for Spanish- and Portuguese-speaking audiences, delivering vertical, mobile-first short-form drama series in the tradition of telenovelas. The company runs an AI-powered production engine to create serialized episodes far faster and more cheaply than traditional studios, and operates a creator program, Idilio Creators, that lets independent makers monetize their work on the platform. Co-founded by Gabriela Tafur and Esteban Ramirez and based in Mexico City, it reached 1.5 million downloads across 93 countries within months of launch.
company.name: Idilio

company.description: InCharge Energy is a Los Angeles-based provider of full-lifecycle energy infrastructure services across North America, spanning EV charging, electrical and lighting, and distributed energy resources. It operates one of the continent's largest multi-brand charger service networks — more than 30,000 managed assets for fleets, school districts, and municipalities — powered by its proprietary InControl software platform and an in-house, nationwide field-service organization. The company positions itself as a single accountable partner from project planning and construction through long-term operations and maintenance.
company.name: inCharge Energy

company.description: Flourish is a New York City-based AI startup, founded in 2025 by Thomas Reardon and Rob Williams, building brain-inspired, power-efficient AI models modeled after biological cortical columns. Its system, referred to as Cortex AI, is designed to learn continuously and run natively on consumer edge devices while drawing more than an order of magnitude less energy than conventional large language models.
company.name: Flourish

company.description: Nitrosend is an Adelaide-based AI-native email marketing platform for small and mid-sized businesses. Instead of dragging blocks around a template editor, users describe a campaign in natural language and the platform writes, designs, sends, and reports on it — operable directly from AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Codex via MCP, or the Nitrosend web app. It was founded by serial entrepreneurs Edward and George Hartley, who previously built and sold email-automation tool SmartrMail.
company.name: Nitrosend

company.description: The Sweet Change is a New Delhi-based D2C natural sweetener brand operating under Natrtreets Private Limited. It develops clean-label, zero-calorie, zero-glycemic sugar alternatives — including monk-fruit-based blends — targeting consumers managing diabetes, PCOS and insulin resistance. The company sells direct online and is expanding into quick-commerce and marketplaces.
company.name: The Sweet Change

company.description: Opal Security is a San Francisco-based identity and access governance company whose AI-native platform gives security teams real-time visibility and policy-as-code control over every identity, from employees and contractors to service accounts and autonomous AI agents. Its just-in-time model grants access for a specific task and revokes it automatically, replacing standing, over-provisioned permissions. Opal differentiates by bringing AI agents into the same access graph, reviews, and governance as human and service identities, and is used by enterprises including Databricks, Cloudflare, and Scale AI.
company.name: Opal Security

company.description: Generalist AI develops foundation models for robotics, an approach often called Physical AI, aiming to give robots general-purpose intelligence that transfers across tasks and unpredictable real-world environments. Its GEN-1 model learns from large volumes of physical interaction data rather than text. The company is backed by NVIDIA, Bezos Expeditions, and a roster of leading AI investors.
company.name: Generalist AI

company.description: Volund Manufacturing is a defense-tech startup building low-cost, high-volume gas turbine engines for attritable systems such as long-range missiles and drones. Founded in 2025 in Huntington Beach, California, it runs a vertically integrated factory with a digital manufacturing backbone that lets it iterate jet propulsion designs far faster than legacy producers. It is working with integrators including Anduril, Raytheon, and Lockheed.
company.name: Volund Manufacturing

company.description: Emphere is a Seattle-based cybersecurity startup that automates the remediation of software vulnerabilities rather than just detecting them. Its engine continuously patches CVEs in open-source container base images and distributions such as Ubuntu, Debian, and Alpine, validating each fix on isolated replicas before promotion. The company targets software vendors that sell into banks and other regulated industries, where a single critical vulnerability can block a deal.
company.name: Emphere

company.description: Aveni is an Edinburgh-based AI fintech, spun out of the University of Edinburgh in 2018, that builds assurance and compliance software for banks, wealth managers and financial advisers. Its products — including Aveni Assist, Aveni Detect and the Unified Assurance Platform — run on FinLLM, a proprietary suite of small language models trained on UK financial-services data to monitor both human and AI-agent interactions with consumers.
company.name: Aveni

company.description: Axiom Space is a leader in commercial human space exploration, building infrastructure for human spaceflight, microgravity research, space manufacturing, and orbital data computing, including the Axiom Station as successor to the ISS and next-generation spacesuits.
company.name: Axiom Space
