company.description: AethexAI builds an end-to-end enterprise voice AI stack purpose-built for emerging markets, combining proprietary self-hosted speech models (its Kora series) with managed telephony, orchestration and a no-code/API deployment layer. Its models are trained on localized data to handle regional dialects of English, French and Arabic at low latency and cost, targeting high-volume use cases such as customer support, KYC verification, debt collection and activation across Africa and the Middle East.
company.name: AethexAI

company.description: Intellectible is an Austin-based AI workflow automation platform for enterprise service providers. Its specialized AI engines automate revenue and delivery work that lives in RFPs and proposals, including opportunity capture, pricing, proposal generation, and revenue operations, with a focus on high-stakes, document-heavy government and commercial contracting. Founded in 2023, it serves clients delivering services at scale.
company.name: Intellectible

company.description:
Build in a weekend. Scale to millions. 

Supabase is the Postgres development platform. Start your project with a Postgres Database, Authentication, instant APIs, and realtime subscriptions.
company.name: Supabase

company.description: Lila is a technology company pioneering the application of artificial intelligence to transform every aspect of the scientific method.
company.name: Lila Sciences

company.description: Honeycomb Insurance is a Chicago-based digital property and casualty insurer focused on apartment buildings, landlords, and condominium associations. Its AI-native platform underwrites each property individually using high-resolution imagery and hundreds of structured and unstructured data points, eliminating physical inspections and pricing well-maintained buildings that traditional carriers often overlook. The company operates across 22 US states and manages over $100 billion in insured assets.
company.name: Honeycomb Insurance

company.description: Helion is an Everett, Washington-based fusion energy company developing systems to generate zero-carbon electricity from nuclear fusion. Led by CEO David Kirtley, it is building toward grid-scale commercial fusion and has signed power agreements with Microsoft and steelmaker Nucor. It is among the most heavily funded private fusion developers.
company.name: Helion

company.description: ARTAN Bio is a St. Petersburg, Florida longevity biotechnology company developing mutation-specific codon-suppression therapies. Its platform centers on engineered suppressor tRNA systems (lead candidate ARTAN-102) that recognize disease-causing nonsense mutations and restore normal protein translation, targeting both rare genetic diseases and aging-related conditions. The company funds its research through VitaDAO's decentralized science (IP-NFT) model.
company.name: ARTAN Bio

company.description: WasabiCard is a Singapore-based payment infrastructure platform that bridges stablecoins with real-world financial applications. It enables enterprises, fintechs, and internet-native businesses to issue cards, distribute payouts, and manage cross-border payments on stablecoin-powered rails. The company serves over 500 enterprise clients, has issued more than 500,000 cards, and has processed over US$1 billion in cumulative transaction volume.
company.name: WasabiCard

company.description: Airspeed (formerly Glyphic) is a London- and New York-based AI company building an agent-native execution layer for revenue and go-to-market teams. Its platform deploys autonomous AI agents that act on commercial context across calls, emails, support tickets and CRM systems, automatically updating records, flagging deal risks and generating follow-ups. Founded in 2022 by former DeepMind research scientists, the company positions itself as a "system of action" that turns revenue intelligence into automated execution.
company.name: Airspeed

company.description: Cense is a Swiss crypto compliance and evidence company, a spinout of Glassnode, based in Baar near Zug. Its platform helps banks and other regulated financial institutions manage digital-asset onboarding, compliance and risk by combining on-chain and off-chain intelligence with proprietary heuristics and AI-assisted workflows. The result is standardized, auditable evidence that lets institutions trace fund flows between wallets, exchanges and the traditional banking system and meet AML and regulatory requirements.
company.name: Cense

company.description: Scotch is a Denver-based provider of an all-in-one, cloud-native operating system built specifically for liquor retailers. Its AI-first platform combines point-of-sale, payment processing, automated inventory management, invoice reconciliation, SKU standardization and pricing intelligence to replace the legacy, on-premise systems that have long dominated the heavily regulated beverage-alcohol industry. Founded in 2024 by the team behind Skupos and Drizly, it serves independent and large-format liquor stores across the United States.
company.name: Scotch

company.description: Scispot is a Kitchener-Waterloo, Ontario company building an AI-native operating layer for life sciences labs. Its platform connects instruments, samples, workflows, approvals and data into one traceable system, letting biotech, pharma, diagnostics and CRO/CDMO teams automate routine lab coordination while keeping permissions, audit trails, sample lineage and human review built in. The software is already used by more than 100 labs and supports 250-plus instrument types.
company.name: Scispot

company.description: CereVasc is a Boston-based clinical-stage medical device company developing minimally invasive treatments for patients with neurological diseases. Its lead product, the eShunt System, uses a percutaneous transvenous-transdural approach to endovascularly implant a cerebrospinal fluid shunt, aiming to treat communicating hydrocephalus without invasive surgery.
company.name: CereVasc

company.description: New Dawn Bio is a Dutch agrifoodtech startup based in Wageningen that develops premium wood grown directly from tree stem cells as a sustainable alternative to traditional wood products, aiming to reduce deforestation and environmental impact.
company.name: New Dawn Bio

company.description: Innovorder is a Paris-based restaurant technology company providing an all-in-one cloud SaaS platform for restaurant operators across commercial and contract catering. Its software covers order taking (POS, kiosks, online and QR ordering), payments via its own Innovorder Pay solution, kitchen production, back-office management, AI reporting and customer loyalty, anchored by a proprietary AI layer called Atlas that automates daily operations. Founded in 2014, the company serves quick-service and contract catering clients across France, Morocco, Spain and Italy and has been profitable since 2024.
company.name: Innovorder

company.description: Kodesage is a Budapest- and London-based startup founded in 2024 that offers an AI-powered platform to help enterprises modernize and manage complex legacy software systems. Their platform simplifies code navigation, automates documentation, accelerates employee onboarding, and ensures compliance and security, supporting on-premise and air-gapped deployments for regulated industries.
company.name: Kodesage

company.description: Ona Therapeutics is a Barcelona-based biopharmaceutical company developing first-in-class antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) for treatment-resistant cancers. A spin-off from IRB Barcelona and ICREA based at the Barcelona Science Park, it uses a proprietary patient-driven target discovery platform to identify novel tumor-specific antigens enabling targeted payload delivery. Its lead programs are ONA-255 in breast cancer and ONA-389 in colorectal cancer, both advancing toward the clinic.
company.name: Ona Therapeutics

company.description: TrueFan AI is a Gurugram-based enterprise AI company that builds a generative AI video platform, turning a single short recording into scalable, hyper-personalised video content across multiple languages. Founded in 2020 as a celebrity-engagement product, it pivoted to enterprise AI in early 2024 and now serves 100-plus clients across BFSI, healthcare, consumer goods and media, including Zomato, HDFC Bank and Bajaj Finance. The company is moving toward real-time, interactive AI video agents for customer support, sales and onboarding.
company.name: TrueFan AI

company.description: WeRize is a Bengaluru-based, full-stack fintech building an AI-native social distribution platform for financial products in smaller Indian cities and towns. Founded in 2019 by ex-Lendingkart executives Vishal Chopra and Himanshu Gupta, it connects banks, NBFCs, mutual funds and insurers with customers through a network of local distributors, operating across 5,000+ cities and towns in six languages. The company says it is profitable, with an annualised gross revenue run rate of about $65M as of March 2026.
company.name: WeRize

company.description: SPAKIO is a Mexican flexible-storage platform for individuals and businesses, offering an alternative to traditional self-storage. Through its app, customers quote, schedule pickups, manage digital inventories, and request on-demand deliveries without hauling belongings to a mini-warehouse. The company operates a tech-enabled storage and logistics network across Mexico serving both residential and corporate clients.
company.name: SPAKIO

company.description: Dawar (Dawar - Circular Solutions) is an Egyptian circular-economy startup, founded in 2017, that runs a digital traceability layer for recyclable materials. Its platform records and verifies the movement of recyclables across collection points, terminals and traders, turning fragmented informal collection networks into audit-ready, financeable supply chains. To date it has documented more than 90,000 tons of materials across 22 Egyptian governorates and is exploring expansion into other African markets.
company.name: DAWAR

company.description: Auger is a technology company building an autonomous, AI-native operating system for global supply chains. Rather than layering AI onto legacy ERP systems, its machine-readable platform unifies data, provides end-to-end visibility, and automates decision-making for large enterprise manufacturers and shippers. Founded in 2024 by Dave Clark — former Flexport CEO and the architect of Amazon's logistics and fulfillment network — the company is based in Washington state.
company.name: Auger

company.description: QuantX Labs is an Adelaide-based deep-tech company building optical atomic clocks and quantum sensors for precision timing, navigation and positioning. Its clocks use optical rather than microwave frequencies, which the company says makes them 10 to 100 times more stable than existing systems while remaining smaller, portable and more robust. QuantX supplies defence, space and critical-infrastructure customers, having bootstrapped to revenue by selling timing systems to the Australian Department of Defence and deploying its technology on a SpaceX mission.
company.name: QuantX Labs

company.description: Gutgutgoose is a Queensland-based biotech startup developing personalised probiotics matched to an individual's gut microbiome. It pairs at-home microbiome sequencing with metabolic modelling and AI-driven analysis to design subscription-delivered probiotic formulations, moving past the one-size-fits-all supplements that often fail to engraft in the gut. The company is also building a longitudinal microbiome dataset to support more personalised and predictive gut-health care.
company.name: Gutgutgoose

company.description: Handshake Finance is a Singapore-based fintech building escrow-as-a-service infrastructure for high-trust service industries. Its platform holds customer payments in MAS-regulated, segregated escrow accounts and releases them to service providers against agreed milestones, protecting buyers from pre-payment risk while giving providers confidence to deliver. Founded in 2025, the company is focused first on the renovation and interior design market before extending to other service sectors where payment trust is a structural problem.
company.name: Handshake Finance

company.description: Kalogon, founded in 2019 by former SpaceX engineers and based in Melbourne, Florida, develops smart seating solutions focused on "seated health." Its core technology — combining real-time sensors, adaptive surfaces and personalized control (the Advanced Pressure Management System) — first shipped as the Orbiter smart wheelchair cushion to prevent pressure injuries, and has since grown into a full, Medicare-coded seating system. The company manufactures in-house and is extending the same technology beyond healthcare into aviation and automotive seating.
company.name: Kalogon

company.description: Freshflow is a Berlin-based startup that builds Europe's first AI platform purpose-built for the fresh supply chain in grocery retail. Founded in 2021 by former ETH machine-learning scientist Avik Mukhija, its app guides store staff on what to order, from which supplier, and in what quantity, using a fully in-house, fresh-native AI stack covering demand forecasting, spoilage prediction, and supplier routing. The technology is live at nine grocery retail chains across Germany and France, cutting spoilage by up to 30% per store.
company.name: Freshflow

company.description: Fraganote is a Delhi-based direct-to-consumer perfumery brand founded in 2022 by Arjun Anand and Garima Kakkar. It makes narrative-led, culturally inspired fragrances in small batches with a high concentration of perfume oil for longer-lasting scents, operating in the Eau De Parfum category. The brand sells through its own D2C site, quick-commerce platforms (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart), Nykaa and modern-trade retail.
company.name: Fraganote

company.description: Willow (formerly Webrix) is a Herzliya, Israel-based identity and access governance platform for enterprise AI agents. It connects any AI agent (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Gemini, Codex, n8n and others) to internal systems with runtime-scoped permissions, centralized access controls, audit trails, and full attribution, while detecting and governing shadow AI across an organization. Founded by former Wix engineers, it offers a marketplace of 1,000+ ready-to-use connectors and runs as SaaS, dedicated cloud, or self-hosted including air-gapped.
company.name: Willow

company.description: SolarDew (SolarDew Clean Water B.V.) is a Wageningen-based Dutch cleantech company building solar-powered water purification and desalination systems. Its membrane technology uses only solar thermal energy and gravity — no electricity, high pressure, or chemicals — to turn polluted or saline water into safe drinking water for remote, coastal, and water-stressed communities. The company began industrial production in 2025 and aims to supply clean drinking water to one million people by 2035.
company.name: SolarDew

company.description: Semble is a London-based health-tech company providing an EHR and practice-management platform for outpatient and private healthcare providers across the UK and France. Its software coordinates the full patient journey for solo clinicians, group practices and enterprise healthcare organisations, integrating diagnostics, billing, labs and CRM into one interoperable system. Founded in 2018 as Heydoc and rebranded Semble in 2022, the company supports clinicians who have delivered care to more than 10 million patients.
company.name: Semble

company.description: Collate is a San Francisco-based AI company that automates documentation for life sciences organizations, including regulatory filings, clinical trial paperwork, and product-development documents for drug, diagnostic, and medical-device companies. It was founded by Surbhi Sarna (former CEO of nVision Medical, acquired by Boston Scientific) and Nate Smith (co-founder of Lever), and emerged from stealth in early 2026. The platform cuts document turnaround times by 50 to 90 percent while requiring human verification before export, and has signed roughly 50 customers including major pharmaceutical and medical-device firms.
company.name: Collate

company.description: Apoha is a London and San Francisco-based deeptech company building a data layer for molecular behaviour, measuring how molecules, materials and formulations actually behave under real-world conditions rather than just what they are or look like. Its VIBE platform suspends a pinhead-sized sample in liquid, applies controlled stresses and records more than 1,000 behavioural descriptors in minutes, helping pharma, food and materials companies predict product failures before market. Founded in 2021 by Shamit Shrivastava and Anshika Srivastava, it works with partners including Boehringer Ingelheim, Ethris and THIS.
company.name: Apoha

company.description: Phab is a Mumbai-based, nutritionist-founded protein snack brand offering high-protein, calorie-efficient products such as protein bars, protein milkshakes, and millet wafers for the Indian market. Founded by nutritionist Gayatri Chona, it positions itself as a macro-first nutrition brand and has built an unusually large roughly 50% offline retail footprint across modern and general trade, alongside a growing quick-commerce dark-store presence in tier 2 and 3 cities. It competes with Indian protein-snacking brands including The Whole Truth, Yoga Bar, and Super You.
company.name: Phab

company.description: FirstClub is a Bengaluru-based online grocery startup that positions itself as a quality-first alternative to India's speed-obsessed quick-commerce players. Founded in 2024 by former Flipkart and Myntra executive Ayyappan R, it carries a tightly curated assortment of around 4,000 products, lab-tests staples and quality-checks fresh produce, and develops exclusive items with brands to build consumer trust. Within a year of launch it crossed 1 million orders and reached roughly a $50M annualized GMV run rate.
company.name: FirstClub

company.description: Flok Health is a Cambridge-based digital health company that operates an AI physiotherapy clinic for musculoskeletal (MSK) care. Its platform simulates a live video appointment with a physiotherapist, autonomously triaging, treating and discharging patients across full care pathways. It is the first AI system in Europe to gain Class IIa medical device certification for autonomous care delivery and the first digital MSK service approved as a healthcare provider by the UK Care Quality Commission, with its back-pain service available to NHS patients across eleven NHS areas.
company.name: Flok Health

company.description: Lassie builds autonomous AI systems that handle administrative busywork for small businesses, starting with dental and other doctors' offices. Its agent logs into insurance portals, pulls reimbursements, reconciles them against patient records, updates the system-of-record, and verifies deposited funds — work that previously consumed 100+ hours a month per practice. Founded by early Robinhood and Superhuman product leaders, the company serves 700+ practices across 49 US states.
company.name: Lassie

company.description: Gridly is a Helsingborg-based, AI-native localization and content-operations platform. Its cloud software lets companies organize, translate, and maintain multilingual digital content across products, handling localization, quality assurance, and distribution for content-heavy sectors such as gaming, SaaS, fintech, and medtech. Founded in 2022, it operates internationally with teams in Sweden and Vietnam.
company.name: Gridly

company.description: Perk is a business travel platform that has expanded into integrated travel and expense management by acquiring Yokoy, an AI-powered spend management platform. It offers a unified solution for travel booking, expense management, and spend control, leveraging AI to automate and simplify work for companies worldwide.
company.name: Perk

company.description: HLRBO (Hunting Land Rentals By Owner) operates an online marketplace that connects private landowners with hunters seeking paid access to private land. Founded in 2015 in Minnesota, it has grown into a national platform with leases across all 50 states, offering listing, discovery, secure payments, legal lease agreements, and integrated insurance. The company is layering technology onto the category with parcel-level mapping and AI-driven land scoring.
company.name: HLRBO

company.description: Haga Bioscience (formerly HAGA.BIO) is a Stockholm-based spatial biology company founded in 2024 that specializes in spatial RNA biomarker discovery and clinical translation. Its in situ hybridization technology enables sensitive, specific detection of single nucleotide variants on RNA directly in tissue, aiming to bridge the gap between biomarker discovery and validation across large clinical cohorts. The company positions its platform as a cost-effective, scalable approach to overcoming sample-throughput and cost limitations in translational spatial transcriptomics.
company.name: Haga Bioscience

company.description: AlphaSense is an AI-powered market intelligence and research platform serving the business and financial world. It applies domain-specific AI across a content universe of more than 500 million premium business documents — equity research, earnings calls, expert interviews, filings, and news — to help enterprises extract insights and automate complex research workflows. More than 7,000 organizations, including over 70 percent of the S&P 500, use the platform for high-stakes decisions around investing, M&A, and market entry.
company.name: AlphaSense

company.description: Terra AI builds an AI-native platform for subsurface modeling, fusing geophysics, geochemistry, and drilling data to generate probabilistic 3D models of the underground for mineral and energy exploration. Its generative modeling engine and geology reasoning agent help resource teams cut the time, cost, and risk of exploration across copper, gold, rare-earth, and reservoir projects, with the same technology now applied to enhanced geothermal and carbon storage. The company is backed by Khosla Ventures, BHP Ventures, and Rio Tinto.
company.name: Terra AI

company.description: Factorial is a Barcelona-based company providing an all-in-one HR and business management software platform designed primarily for small and medium-sized businesses. It automates HR processes such as time management, payroll, recruitment, and talent management, and has expanded to include project management, training, and expense management tools. The platform emphasizes high customizability and integrated features to enable data-driven HR decisions and streamline operations.
company.name: Factorial

company.description: Town is a San Francisco-based applied AI company building a personalized AI assistant that works across the tools people already use, including email, calendar, Slack, docs and messaging. Rather than requiring prompts, connectors or setup, its assistant (the "Townie") learns each user's voice, relationships, rhythms and priorities to act more like a chief of staff than a chatbot. It is available across iOS, desktop, web, WhatsApp, Telegram and Slack.
company.name: Town

company.description: FLOQ is an Indonesian digital asset exchange that lets retail and institutional users buy, sell, and trade cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT, and Solana from small starting amounts. The platform is licensed and supervised by Indonesia's financial regulator (OJK) and positions itself around accessibility, education, and security for both first-time and experienced investors.
company.name: FLOQ

company.description: Ingenix.ai is a Warsaw-based biotech startup developing a proprietary multimodal and multiscale generative AI co-pilot platform that simulates clinical drug trials from molecular to population levels, enabling granular predictions of clinical endpoints and adverse events to improve drug development efficiency and reduce failure rates.
company.name: Ingenix.ai

company.description: Paypercut is a Sofia-based fintech startup founded in 2025 that offers a Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) aggregation platform. It enables small and mid-sized merchants in Central and Eastern Europe to access multiple BNPL providers through a single integration, improving approval and conversion rates by routing payment requests to various providers. The platform supports multiple local currencies and features fully digital onboarding, simplifying payments and expanding merchant access to BNPL services
company.name: Paypercut

company.description: Molfar Defence Technologies is a Polish-Ukrainian defence-tech company founded in 2026 that builds tactical radar systems for the early detection, localisation and tracking of small, low-flying drones in contested battlefield conditions. Its proprietary signal-processing and waveform design distinguish micro-UAVs from clutter and are engineered for low probability of detection, available in 90-degree sector and full 360-degree modular configurations that integrate into broader air-defence networks. The company works closely with the Ukrainian Armed Forces and a major Polish defence supplier, and also runs an OSINT-based defence-sector intelligence and due-diligence function.
company.name: Molfar Defence Technologies

company.description: Upstream is a Paris-based startup, founded in 2023 by Louis Lecat and Jonathan Tiret and based at Station F, building an AI-native email inbox designed for humans and AI agents to collaborate within. Its platform lets AI agents sort messages, draft replies in a user's writing style, schedule meetings, retrieve information and follow up automatically, while organising work into shared collaborative channels and connecting external tools via the MCP protocol. The company launched publicly in mid-2026 after an invite-only beta used by thousands of professionals.
company.name: Upstream

company.description: Wordsmith AI is a legal intelligence platform for in-house teams, specializing in AI-driven legal solutions for contract review, document management, and legal workflow automation. The company helps legal teams embed AI agents across organizations to streamline operations and accelerate decision-making.
company.name: Wordsmith AI

company.description: Zuvees is a premium direct-to-consumer gifting platform that pairs curated products with AI-based personalization and recommendation technology to make sending gifts—especially across borders—reliable and effortless. It tackles common gifting pain points such as poor product quality, late deliveries, and the difficulty of international gifting, offering features like AI gift suggestions and pre-dispatch video approval. Launched commercially in the UAE in early 2025, it now serves customers across 50+ countries.
company.name: Zuvees

company.description: Agilitas Sports is a Bengaluru-based, vertically integrated sportswear and athleisure company founded in 2023 by Abhishek Ganguly, former managing director of Puma India and Southeast Asia. It combines in-house R&D, design, manufacturing (through its Mochiko Shoes unit) and retail, and holds exclusive long-term licensing rights to the Lotto brand across India, Australia and South Africa as well as Virat Kohli's One8 label. The company also operates Sportsyard, a large-format multi-brand sports retail chain carrying brands such as Nike, Adidas and Puma.
company.name: Agilitas Sports

company.description: Gigaton (formerly Carbon Re) is a London-based AI company, spun out of UCL and the University of Cambridge, that builds self-learning control software for energy-intensive heavy industries such as cement, steel, glass and chemicals. Rather than sitting on top of legacy systems, its platform replaces the underlying control stack, autonomously adjusting parameters like fuel mix, kiln speed and oxygen to cut energy use, emissions and process instability. It is already deployed by major cement producers including Adani Cement, Heidelberg Materials and Holcim, delivering seven-figure annual savings per plant.
company.name: Gigaton

company.description: INXM is a Berlin-based enterprise AI company, founded in 2025, that builds an AI Process Execution Engine for industrial and business operations. Its "Compiled AI" approach uses large language models to generate deterministic, auditable workflow code that runs reliably across a company's existing ERP, MES and PLM systems rather than replacing them. The founding team comprises former chief digital officers of Isar Aerospace and Volocopter alongside veterans of n8n and Thoughtworks, targeting manufacturers and other regulated industries that need repeatable, compliant automation.
company.name: INXM

company.description: Spirit AI builds the universal brain for the next generation of robotics using general-purpose embodied models that bridge simulation and reality for robust generalization and physical precision in real-world applications.
company.name: Spirit AI

company.description: Propsoch is a Bengaluru-based, buyer-centric property advisory platform that helps Indian homebuyers make independent, data-driven purchase decisions. It evaluates residential projects across more than 80 factors — including builder credibility, construction quality, and appreciation potential — combining proprietary analytics, industry expertise, and consumer feedback into unbiased recommendations.
company.name: Propsoch

company.description: Suno is an AI-powered music company that enables users to create music from simple prompts, serving both casual creators and professional songwriters with advanced generative audio tools.
company.name: Suno

company.description: Atana Elements is a critical-minerals exploration company that pairs AI and machine learning with subsurface and fluid-system expertise drawn from the oil-and-gas sector to discover and de-risk large-scale mineral resources. It targets flowing mineral systems such as lithium brines, hydrogen and helium, plus emerging extraction methods for copper and uranium, focusing on jurisdictions aligned with Western security and investment requirements. Founded in 2025, the company has already licensed prospective lithium resources exceeding 100 million tonnes LCE across the EU and the Americas.
company.name: Atana Elements

company.description: GoComet is an AI-native platform for global trade that automates end-to-end supply chain and freight workflows, including rate procurement, multimodal shipment tracking, document execution and invoice reconciliation. Its software is used by enterprise shippers across pharmaceutical, automotive, chemical and FMCG sectors, with customers such as Sun Pharma, Unilever, Glenmark and Yokohama Tires. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Singapore, the company layers AI agents over its workflow engine for monitoring, analytics and communication handling.
company.name: GoComet

company.description: Spectee is a Tokyo-based AI SaaS company in the resilience domain. Leveraging diverse data sources including social media, weather data, satellite data, and automotive probe data, it collects and analyzes disasters and crises occurring around the world in real time. Its flagship product, Spectee SCR, is a cloud-based supply chain risk management platform that enables manufacturers to visualize multi-layered supply chains and identify disruptions caused by natural disasters, geopolitical events, or operational incidents. Led by CEO Kenjiro Murakami.
company.name: Spectee

company.description: Nardo is an Australian sports technology company, founded in Sydney in 2023, that operates Nardo Experience, a software platform for grassroots and semi-professional sports clubs to design, customise, order and manage team apparel. It replaces a fragmented manual supply chain, originally spanning more than 130 separate touchpoints, with a single digital workflow covering design, live quoting, sampling approvals, and real-time production and delivery tracking. The platform connects clubs directly to a global manufacturing network for faster turnaround and improved cost efficiency.
company.name: Nardo

company.description: Aquapulse is a Bhubaneswar-based integrated aquaculture technology startup that provides AI-driven solutions across the shrimp and seafood value chain. Founded in 2022 by Abhishek Dwivedy and Abhilash Dwivedy, the platform covers farmer network management, aquaculture feed, hatchery operations, and seafood processing, using IoT and AI to optimise production and reduce post-harvest losses.
company.name: Aquapulse

company.description: Hexigone Inhibitors is a Welsh advanced-materials company that makes smart anti-corrosion additives for protective coatings used on metal assets in defence, marine and industrial settings. Its patented Intelli-ion technology releases inhibitor only when corrosion begins, working at low dosages to cut cost and carbon while reducing reliance on heavy metals and toxic chemistries. The company is spun out of the West Wales materials ecosystem and supported by the Welsh Government.
company.name: Hexigone

company.description: Rosada is a Kolkata-based direct-to-consumer kids lifestyle brand founded in 2014 by Shalu and Bhupesh Agarwal, designing and manufacturing personalised, design-led products for babies and young children. Its in-house portfolio spans backpacks, bedding sets, travel accessories, decor and everyday essentials, sold largely through its own website under a digital-first, Make-in-India model. The brand gained national visibility after appearing on Shark Tank India Season 5, which drove a notable rise in revenue.
company.name: Rosada

company.description: IAG is a Czech B2B digital procurement platform for the automotive aftermarket, connecting spare-parts suppliers directly with independent repair shops and retailers so mechanics can search, compare and order parts with real-time pricing and availability. Founded in 2021 by Tomas Kastil, it covers passenger vehicles, trucks and motorcycles and operates across Central and Eastern Europe, targeting a fragmented market worth over 100 billion euros annually.
company.name: IAG

company.description: AgriAku is an Indonesian agritech company founded in 2021 by Irvan Kolonas and Danny Handoko. It launched as a B2B marketplace connecting farmers, retailers, and suppliers, helping small and medium agri-retailers source seeds, fertilisers, pesticides, and other agricultural inputs. The Jakarta-based company has since refocused its model on the distribution and sale of agricultural inputs, particularly fertiliser, where demand is more predictable.
company.name: AgriAku

company.description: Atlantic is an AI-powered workflow and enterprise-knowledge platform that consolidates a company's data scattered across tools like Slack, Jira and Google Workspace into a single, searchable "company brain." Its AI agents analyze emails, documents, tasks and communications to automate workflows — routing work to the right people, flagging delayed tasks and coordinating cross-team processes — while keeping customer data private and out of model training. Founded in March 2026 by teenage founders Rüzgar İmren and Ege Mustafa Çelik, it also offers its own Atlas AI model family.
company.name: Atlantic

company.description: U-ZERO is a Tokyo-based SaaS startup that builds an AI-powered employee engagement platform helping Japanese companies visualize employee sentiment and drive organizational improvement. It was founded in 2024 by Masamune Mimura, the former head of Concur Japan, who spent over a decade building a celebrated organizational culture there. Within roughly a year of launch the service had been adopted by more than 50 companies.
company.name: U-ZERO

company.description: Adaptive Innovations is an AI-native home healthcare provider that emerged from stealth in 2026 (launched quietly in 2025), operating as a full-stack "provider-as-a-service" rather than selling software to legacy agencies. Its proprietary AI operating system automates back-office work — intake, eligibility, scheduling, charting, coding, billing and quality assurance — so clinicians can focus on patient care. Based in New York City and Dallas, the company delivers in-home visits across major Texas metros with a payer-agnostic model.
company.name: Adaptive Innovations

company.description: CargoX is a UAE-based autonomous delivery company operating driverless vehicles across last-mile, middle-mile and long-haul logistics routes. The platform has been piloted on public roads in Abu Dhabi and Dubai with regulatory engagement from Dubai's Roads and Transport Authority and Abu Dhabi Mobility, and is led by former Talabat CEO Tomaso Rodriguez.
company.name: CargoX

company.description: iRegene Therapeutics is a biotechnology company pioneering chemically induced allogeneic cell therapies, focusing on developing transformative treatments for incurable diseases such as Parkinson's disease and retinal degenerative diseases. Their proprietary chemical induction platform enables efficient generation of human cell types with high purity and enhanced functionality for cell therapy.
company.name: iRegene Therapeutics (Chengdu) Co., Ltd.

company.description: Highland Electric Fleets is North America's leading provider of Electrification-as-a-Service, specializing in the electrification of school bus fleets by partnering with school districts, municipalities, and fleet operators to facilitate the transition to electric fleets with services including planning, procurement, implementation, and optimization.
company.name: Highland Electric Fleets

company.description: Arpio is a Durham, North Carolina-based provider of an AI-native disaster recovery and cyber resilience platform for cloud environments. Founded in 2020 by Doug Neumann and Shaw Terwilliger, its software lets enterprises automatically replicate, test, and recover cloud workloads across AWS and Microsoft Azure following ransomware, outages, and other disruptive events, cutting recovery from days to minutes. The platform also automates end-to-end failover testing so organizations can validate recovery plans without production impact.
company.name: Arpio

company.description: Amperesand is a next-generation power infrastructure provider focused on building advanced power conversion solutions for AI data centers and critical power applications. The company specializes in Medium Voltage Solid-State Transformer (MV SST) technology, offering high power density, improved energy efficiency, and scalable performance for hyperscale AI, national defense, and megawatt-level charging solutions. Amperesand operates in the U.S. and Singapore, with manufacturing and R&D centers in
company.name: Amperesand

company.description: Tokenz is a Tokyo-based Merchant of Record (MoR) platform that lets digital content companies sell directly through their own websites rather than the Apple and Google app stores. Founded in 2024, it handles global payments across more than 200 methods, automated consumption-tax and invoicing compliance in 224-plus countries and regions, and multilingual customer support for game studios, manga publishers, and streaming services. It positions itself as a Japan-headquartered alternative to MoR incumbents such as Paddle and FastSpring.
company.name: Tokenz

company.description: Periotherapia is a clinical-stage biotech company spun out of Osaka University, developing antibody therapeutics that target pathological periostin to remodel the disease microenvironment. Its lead candidate, PT0101, is an antibody drug aimed at treatment-resistant HER2-negative breast cancer and is in Phase 1/2a trials. The company is pursuing a novel mechanism to overcome drug resistance in hard-to-treat diseases.
company.name: Periotherapia

company.description: Simbie AI builds clinically-trained, multilingual AI voice agents that function as round-the-clock medical staff for healthcare practices. The agents answer and place patient calls and handle scheduling, prescription refills, insurance, intake and documentation, integrating directly with clinic EHR/EMR systems to cut administrative workload and cost. The New York-based company is backed by Y Combinator and targets independent and small medical practices.
company.name: Simbie AI

company.description: DataMasque is a New Zealand data de-identification and synthetic data platform that helps large, regulated enterprises safely use sensitive information for AI development, testing and analytics. Its software automatically discovers and masks data, replacing it with synthetically identical records so organisations can feed real-world data into AI systems without it leaving their own infrastructure. Founded in Auckland in 2021, it is an AWS Advanced Technology Partner serving customers across financial services, government and telecommunications in New Zealand, Australia and the US.
company.name: DataMasque

company.description: Forage is a San Francisco-based financial infrastructure company that lets retailers and online platforms accept U.S. government benefit payments such as SNAP (EBT), WIC, and HSA/FSA. It is a USDA-approved third-party payment processor whose technology is used at over 100,000 stores across all 50 states, powering benefit acceptance for national retailers and delivery platforms. The company also operates a consumer app that helps low-income families check EBT balances, find SNAP-eligible items, and earn rewards on everyday grocery purchases.
company.name: Forage

company.description: Tavo Biotherapeutics is a Laguna Beach, California-based clinical-stage biotech developing novel therapeutics for ophthalmic diseases, with a focus on preserving vision in glaucoma and retinal disease. Its lead program TAV-001 is a topical therapy targeting the autonomic nervous system for glaucoma, entering Phase 2 in late 2026, while TAV-002 is a bispecific antibody against VEGF and upstream signaling for age-related macular degeneration and diabetic retinopathy.
company.name: Tavo Biotherapeutics

company.description: Radley Health (operating as RadleyCare) is a Cincinnati, Ohio behavioral health startup that connects adults with serious mental illness to a statewide network of certified peer support specialists. It pairs patients with independent-contractor peers in their local communities and documents progress against provider care plans through electronic health record integration. The company is among the first in Ohio to deliver Medicaid-reimbursed peer recovery and family peer support services.
company.name: Radley Health

company.description: Vivilo (formerly Jubatus) is an Italian AI startup whose computer-vision platform automatically recognises faces, race numbers, objects, vehicles, helmets and other elements in event footage and generates personalised photos, clips and short-form videos for each participant within hours of an event ending. Operating a B2B2C model, it works with event organisers and photo/video agencies to automate the slow, manual post-event content process and create a new revenue stream, while letting participants buy, download and share their own content. Its technology spans sports, motorsport, music festivals, trade fairs, concerts and community events.
company.name: Vivilo

company.description: Quobly is a French quantum computing company spun out of the Grenoble semiconductor research base (formerly Siquance, founded 2022) that builds quantum processors from silicon spin qubits using standard FD-SOI chip-manufacturing processes on 300mm wafers. Its thesis is that quantum computing only scales commercially when it leverages the existing semiconductor industry for manufacturing reproducibility, rather than relying on exotic custom hardware. The Alloy product line is engineered to fit into conventional HPC and data-centre infrastructure, with the first system due to be cloud-accessible by the end of 2026.
company.name: Quobly

company.description: Uncover is a Brazilian marketing-measurement company whose marketing mix modeling platform helps brands measure, forecast, and optimize media performance across channels and markets. A certified partner of Google's Meridian framework, it integrates with platforms like Google, TikTok, and Meta and serves large advertisers including Unilever, Reckitt, and Burger King. Founded in 2020 and already profitable, Uncover operates across Brazil, Mexico, Canada, and the United States and is the marketing-measurement market leader in Latin America.
company.name: Uncover

company.description: Layup Parts is a Huntington Beach, California composites manufacturer building software to make ordering custom carbon-fiber and fiberglass parts as fast and simple as online shopping. Founded in 2024 by former Anduril engineer Zack Eakin, it serves aerospace, defense, motorsports and design-studio customers, cutting turnaround from weeks to hours in some cases. The company aims to become the Amazon of composite parts.
company.name: Layup Parts

company.description: 701x is a Fargo, North Dakota agricultural technology company building an integrated technology ecosystem for the beef cattle industry. Its platform combines solar-powered, direct-to-satellite GPS smart ear tags (the xTpro), on-ranch management software, breed-association tools, and DNA solutions into a single connected system that delivers real-time herd health, location, breeding, and calving alerts to ranchers. Founded in 2020 and led by CEO Kevin Biffert, the company operates in the US and Canada and is expanding internationally.
company.name: 701x

company.description: Coralogix is a full-stack software observability platform that helps companies monitor the health and performance of their systems by collecting and analyzing logs, metrics, and traces. Founded in Israel in 2014 and now headquartered in Boston, it serves more than 5,000 customers including IBM, Tradeweb, and JFrog, and is building AI-driven monitoring and incident-response tooling for the age of autonomous software. It competes with Datadog, New Relic, and Splunk in the observability market.
company.name: Coralogix

company.description: StratusGrid is a Chattanooga, Tennessee company building AI-driven cloud infrastructure optimization software. Its flagship platform, Stratusphere, coordinates specialized AI agents that continuously surface cost-saving opportunities, simulate the blast radius of changes under organizational policies, and safely execute infrastructure updates across AWS and Azure. The company targets private equity-backed software firms and multi-cloud enterprises, having bootstrapped to financial stability before raising outside capital.
company.name: StratusGrid

company.description: Oxford Quantum Circuits is a UK-based quantum computing scaleup that builds machines using superconducting qubits and delivers them as a cloud service through partner data centres in London, Tokyo and New York. Customers buy compute time rather than hardware, an approach aimed at regulated sectors such as finance and defence. Its most advanced system runs 32 qubits.
company.name: Oxford Quantum Circuits

company.description: Westmag (Western Magnetics Company) is a U.S. manufacturer of electric drone motors and robot actuators, onshoring production that has long been concentrated in China. The company vertically integrates design, manufacturing, and supply chain on a shared motor/actuator architecture, producing at its Factory 01 facility and headquarters in South San Francisco to serve high-volume drone, humanoid, and mobile-robot makers.
company.name: Westmag

company.description: Novellia is a New York-based health data company that lets patients consolidate up to 20 years of medical records from disparate hospital systems, doctors offices and labs in under 30 seconds, then choose to share de-identified data for medical research. It uses proprietary NLP models to extract structured signals from unstructured clinical text, selling harmonized real-world datasets to top pharmaceutical companies and diagnostics firms. The company positions itself as the only real-world data platform built entirely on information patients elect to contribute.
company.name: Novellia

company.description: Subtle Medical develops AI software that enhances and accelerates medical imaging, using deep learning to denoise scans and speed up MRI, PET and CT workflows so providers can image faster at lower dose. Its vendor-agnostic tools plug into existing radiology systems to expand scanner capacity.
company.name: Subtle Medical

company.description: ZutaCore builds two-phase, direct-to-chip waterless liquid cooling systems that remove heat from high-density AI and data-center processors. Its technology targets the rising thermal loads of AI compute and is backed by major industrial and semiconductor groups.
company.name: ZutaCore

company.description: IMU Biosciences is a London-based biotechnology company spun out of research at the Francis Crick Institute and Kings College London. It pairs high-resolution multi-omic immune profiling with machine-learning analytics, generating more than 100 million immune data points from a single blood sample to build what it describes as the worlds largest immune dataset. The company applies the platform to clinical programmes in stem cell and solid organ transplantation and immuno-oncology, aiming to turn immune profiling into a standard clinical tool for predicting treatment response and guiding precision medicine.
company.name: IMU Biosciences

company.description:
Cyera is the world’s leading AI-native data security platform. Its platform gives organizations a complete view of where their data lives, how it’s used, and how to keep it safe, so they can reduce risk and unlock the full value of their data, wherever it is. 

Backed by more than $1.3 billion in funding from top-tier investors including Accel, Coatue, Cyberstarts, Georgian, Lightspeed, and Sequoia, Cyera’s unified data security platform helps businesses discover, secure, and leverage their most valuable asset - data - and eliminate blind spots, cut alert noise, and protect sensitive information across the cloud, SaaS, databases, AI ecosystems, and on-premise environments.
company.name: Cyera

company.description: NewLimit is a longevity research company focused on extending human healthspan through epigenetic reprogramming.
company.name: NewLimit

company.description: BibliU is a vertically integrated provider of campus store and course-materials solutions for higher education, pairing a proprietary digital learning platform with full-service, managed campus retail operations. It works with more than 100 institutional partners across 30 US states and serves over 170 colleges and universities globally, giving students affordable Day-1 access to required materials. Its hybrid digital-and-retail model, launched after the acquisition of Texas Book Company, sets it apart from providers that handle only digital content or only bookstore operations.
company.name: BibliU

company.description: Advanced NanoTherapies (ANT) is a clinical-stage medtech company developing SirPlux Duo, a dual-drug (sirolimus and paclitaxel) drug-coated balloon built on a biodegradable nanoparticle platform for treating coronary and peripheral artery disease. The technology, exclusively licensed from Cleveland Clinic and granted FDA Breakthrough Device designation, is engineered to actively drive drug uptake into vessel-wall cells as an alternative to permanent implants such as drug-eluting stents. Based in Santa Clara, California, ANT is a T45 Labs portfolio company advancing toward U.S. pivotal trials.
company.name: Advanced NanoTherapies

company.description: Findigs is an AI-native leasing decisioning platform for U.S. residential operators, automating rental application screening, underwriting and approval decisions for single-family and multifamily portfolios. Its system returns an automated yes/no on each application within hours, grounded in post-lease performance data modeled across 400,000+ units, and backs decisions with a contractual fraud guarantee. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in New York, the company positions itself in the proptech/fintech category it calls leasing "revenue quality."
company.name: Findigs

company.description: Phosio is a Corvallis, Oregon deep-tech materials startup developing high-performance thin-film optical coatings for lightweight augmented-reality eyewear. Its proprietary PhosioLux platform integrates bright displays into normal-looking glasses while lowering manufacturing temperatures and cost, aiming to make AI/AR glasses affordable at mass-market scale. Spun out of Oregon State University research, the company also targets adjacent uses of its nanoscale optical materials in semiconductors and lidar.
company.name: Phosio

company.description: Alitheon is a Bellevue, Washington-based optical-AI company founded in 2017, best known for its patented FeaturePrint technology. FeaturePrint uses standard camera hardware to capture the inherent surface details of physical items — industrial parts, luxury goods, and consumer products — and turn them into a unique digital fingerprint for tag-free, barcode-free 1-to-1 serialization. The company applies this to supply-chain security, anti-counterfeiting, and digital provenance, holding 55+ issued patents and a zero-trust verification framework.
company.name: Alitheon

company.description: Archestra.AI is a London-based startup developing an open-source platform that empowers non-technical users to safely build and connect Autonomous AI agents to corporate data, addressing enterprise AI adoption challenges by layering security, permissioning, and compliance on the Model Context Protocol.
company.name: Archestra.AI

company.description: Centrical operates a Performance Intelligence platform for frontline workforces, connecting performance data, AI-assisted coaching, learning, simulations and recognition into a single closed loop that guides employees, managers and increasingly AI agents toward the right actions. Founded in 2013, it serves Fortune 500 enterprises across roughly 150 countries and 60 languages from offices in New York, Tel Aviv and London, with customers spanning major U.S. banks, telecoms and hospitality brands. Analysts at Frost & Sullivan and QKS Group rank it a leader in Workforce Engagement Management.
company.name: Centrical

company.description: Impulse Space is a space technology startup focused on orbital spacecraft and lunar delivery systems, developing stages and landers for practical Moon freight missions.
company.name: Impulse Space

company.description: Contraline is a Charlottesville, Virginia clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel contraceptive options for men and couples. Its lead candidate NES/T is a daily, hormonal, reversible topical gel, and it is also advancing ADAM, a long-lasting non-hormonal reversible contraceptive. The company aims to deliver the first approved pharmacologic contraceptive for men.
company.name: Contraline

company.description: The Ryl Company is a modern beverage maker headquartered in Morristown, New Jersey, and the parent of Ryl Tea, a zero-sugar, functional ready-to-drink iced tea positioned for younger consumers. Its cans deliver tea polyphenols and Vitamin C at fewer than 5 calories, and the brand has built a national retail and direct-store-delivery footprint. Ryl is among the fast-growing players defining the emerging "Modern Tea" category.
company.name: The Ryl Company

company.description: ZeroDrift provides a real-time compliance platform that acts as a communication firewall, automating checks on outbound messages against regulatory requirements and internal policies for regulated industries like financial services.
company.name: ZeroDrift

company.description: Tilt is a London-based live shopping app where sellers run real-time video auctions of fashion, collectibles and trading cards while buyers bid in a TikTok-style feed. Founded in 2021 by former Revolut employees Abhi Thanendran and Neil Shah, it layers AI across listing, pricing and live seller coaching to make live commerce easier for everyday sellers.
company.name: Tilt

company.description: Oplane is a Malmö-based cybersecurity company building an agentic, AI-native security platform for engineering teams that ship software with AI coding tools. Its technology performs automated, expert-level threat modeling — mapping a codebase's architecture, surfacing system-level security requirements, and delivering contextual remediations directly inside developer workflows. Founded in 2022, Oplane runs in production at customers including Miro and Tandem Health.
company.name: Oplane

company.description: Endra is a Swedish construction technology startup that develops an AI-powered platform to automate the design and documentation of Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems in buildings, streamlining traditionally manual and outdated processes.
company.name: Endra Systems AB

company.description: Imiron is a Tokyo-based AI-safety startup founded in August 2024 to close the "deployment gap" between specification and implementation in mission-critical systems such as autonomous driving, robotics, and medical devices. Its platform SpecForge turns abstract, ambiguous requirements into mathematically rigorous formal specifications — using a proprietary DSL called Lilo, Signal Temporal Logic, and large language models — that double as runtime verification and monitoring rules. The company was founded by three researchers spanning automotive R&D (Toyota Central R&D Labs, Denso), the National Institute of Informatics, and Oxford/Imperial College.
company.name: Imiron

company.description: Founded in 2026 by Jono Caseley (formerly of Bain) and Andy Lockey, 3Square is building a "Decision OS" for the AI era: an infrastructure layer that captures an organisation's decisions and institutional judgment so AI agents can act in line with how the company actually thinks, what it has already decided, and the constraints it operates under. The product encodes the reasoning of a team's best people into a queryable decision layer, aimed initially at hybrid engineering organisations so distributed humans and agents can decide faster and more consistently. The London-based company is backed by venture firm Antler.
company.name: 3Square

company.description: Kzone is a South Korean startup operating REMEX, a data-driven B2B re-commerce and reverse-logistics platform that helps businesses redistribute returned, overstock, and distressed inventory across global markets, with operations centered on the United States. Reaching profitability within three years of launch on annual revenue of roughly KRW 12 billion with a team of about ten, the company is building AI agents to automate buyer discovery, deal proposals, sales, and market analysis for global inventory liquidation.
company.name: Kzone

company.description: KorinMi is a Gurugram-based Korean skincare and beauty company that operates aesthetic clinics and sells direct-to-consumer skincare products formulated in South Korea and adapted for Indian skin and climate. Founded in 2024, it offers facial and hair treatments and positions itself as one of India's first dedicated Korean-skincare clinic chains, blending clinical services with product retail.
company.name: KorinMi

company.description: SpeedLabs is a New York-based technology company building a real-time market and pricing engine for sports. Its platform delivers live sports market data and trading infrastructure for operators and partners.
company.name: SpeedLabs

company.description: Noa (noa fit) is a French wellness startup building a premium online fitness brand, starting with an AI-personalised iOS app focused on Pilates, cardio and strength training and designed primarily for women. It was founded by Guillaume Sztejnberg, Shreyas Rajagopalan and Jason Akakpo, repeat consumer-mobile entrepreneurs behind AppTurbo and Green Panda Games (acquired by Ubisoft in 2019). The company aims to grow Noa into a broader wellbeing brand spanning nutrition, meditation, sleep and menstrual-cycle tracking.
company.name: Noa

company.description: NP Company (NP Co.) is a Paris-based, Inria-born startup building AI-native physics simulation software for industrial engineering. Its pre-trained transformer models aim to replace legacy simulators, producing high-fidelity results in seconds rather than days across aerospace, defense, energy, electronics, data centers, and automotive. Founded in 2025 by Emmanuel Menier and Matthieu Nastorg, both AI-for-simulation PhD researchers from Paris-Saclay University.
company.name: NP Company

company.description: MENOU is a Japanese company, founded in 2019, that develops no-code visual-inspection AI for manufacturers. Its "MENOU" platform lets factory staff build and run image-based quality-inspection models without programming, and it has been adopted by more than 200 companies across automotive parts, medical devices, food and consumer-goods sectors. Beyond software, MENOU delivers end-to-end inspection setups spanning cameras, lighting, work-handling mechanisms and robotic arms.
company.name: MENOU

company.description: Oak Hill Bio is a clinical-stage rare disease therapeutics company that acquires and develops promising drug candidates deprioritized by large pharmaceutical companies. Its lead program, rugonersen (OHB-724), is an antisense oligonucleotide entering a Phase 3 study for Angelman syndrome, a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with no approved disease-modifying therapy. The company operates as the trading name of OHB Pediatrics, a subsidiary of Oak Hill Bio Holdings.
company.name: Oak Hill Bio

company.description: Edify is a London-based provider of an AI-powered operations platform for quick-service restaurants, cafes and hospitality chains. The software unifies inventory management, demand forecasting and back-of-house workflows, automating ordering and generating shift-by-shift prep plans, while its Ask Edify analytics agent turns operational data into live dashboards. Founded in 2024 by former coffee-chain operator Ed Barry, it serves brands including Pret A Manger and Dunkin Donuts.
company.name: Edify

company.description: Building next-generation defense systems in stealth
company.name: Mach Industries

company.description: Kosmos is a Chicago-based operational intelligence company building an AI-native platform that helps enterprise support and engineering teams pinpoint the root cause of service incidents faster. It correlates customer cases, code changes, and incidents across tools such as GitHub, Jira, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Datadog, Grafana, and Splunk into a single view, cutting the manual investigation work that slows incident resolution. Founded by CEO Sanjay Gidwani, the company launched commercially alongside its seed round.
company.name: Kosmos

company.description: Plot is a New York-based agentic marketing platform built for the video era. It runs an AI video-intelligence layer that processes hundreds of millions of social videos to surface untagged brand mentions, consumer sentiment, trends, and creators that text-based social listening tools miss, then turns those signals into action through campaign tracking, creator sourcing, and consumer research workflows. Its enterprise customers include Visa, Mastercard, PepsiCo, Fenty Beauty, Benefit Cosmetics, and CAVA.
company.name: Plot

company.description: Board is a New York City-based gaming company that builds a face-to-face tabletop game console: a 24-inch interactive touchscreen that uses its proprietary PieceSense technology to recognize real, physical game pieces and translate manual movements into instantaneous digital gameplay. Founded by Brynn Putnam (creator of the Mirror fitness device), it blends the social feel of board games with video-game mechanics and is building an open creator ecosystem with an SDK and its Board Studio software. Its hardware is deployed across tens of thousands of homes, schools, and hospitals across the US.
company.name: Board

company.description: Ilant Health is a New York City-based, AI-enabled healthcare services company delivering value-based obesity and cardiometabolic care for employers and health plans. Its clinician-led platform precision-matches each member to behavioral therapy, GLP-1 and non-GLP-1 medication, and bariatric surgery, continuously adapting treatment to improve outcomes while reducing total cost of care. Founded by Elina Onitskansky, it contracts directly with manufacturers including Lilly and Novo Nordisk to offer employers transparent medication pricing.
company.name: Ilant Health

company.description: Modelia is a Madrid-based AI platform for generating and managing visual assets for the fashion industry, turning basic garment photos into on-model imagery and campaign visuals at scale while preserving brand consistency. Its enterprise layer adds workflow control, approval processes and e-commerce integrations, and it works with brands such as Desigual, AWWG and Futbol Emotion across 26 countries. Founded in 2024, the company positions itself as the production infrastructure that bridges general-purpose AI models and industrial fashion imagery.
company.name: Modelia

company.description: Twinco Capital is a Dutch-Spanish fintech providing purchase-order finance, advancing capital to suppliers the moment a purchase order is issued rather than waiting for invoice settlement. Founded in 2016 and dual-headquartered in Amsterdam and Madrid, it uses proprietary risk models and real-time operational data to underwrite supplier performance risk in emerging markets, funding up to 60% of order value upfront and the balance on delivery. Its clients include retailers such as Mango, Vertbaudet, Lojas Renner and Centric Brands, and it has financed over $1 billion in transactions with zero credit losses.
company.name: Twinco Capital

company.description: Poindexter Labs is a London-based AI data company building high-fidelity training and evaluation datasets for frontier AI models. Through its collaborative, peer-reviewed platform Syncronus, a vetted network of Olympiad medalists, PhDs and professors produces expert reasoning data across STEM, legal, medical, finance and engineering domains. The company licenses the platform to enterprises and governments and also runs an elite in-house data-annotation service.
company.name: Poindexter Labs

company.description: BlockchainWork operates one of Vietnam's largest blockchain and Web3 talent communities, running a recruitment platform that connects over 20,000 candidates with domestic and international employers for both technical and non-technical roles. Founded in 2021 by a Vietnamese-Japanese team, it also runs an industry information portal publishing market reports and career opportunities, positioning itself as a bridge between Vietnamese blockchain talent and the wider Asian market, particularly Japan and Singapore.
company.name: BlockchainWork

company.description: Sekai is a San Francisco-based AI-native consumer platform that turns text prompts into playable, shareable mini-apps, tools, and games — positioned as a "TikTok for software." It lets everyday users instantly create, remix, and publish interactive software without traditional engineering, and has built a fast-growing creator community around AI-generated apps.
company.name: Sekai

company.description: TriOrb is a Kitakyushu-based deep-tech robotics company, originating from Japan's National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST), that develops ball-driven, omnidirectional mobile robots for the factory floor. Its TriOrb BASE platform uses spheres instead of wheels to move in any direction through narrow aisles and uneven floors that conventional AGVs and AMRs cannot navigate. The company is shifting from proof-of-concept deployments to mass production and global expansion, anchored by a U.S. office in Detroit.
company.name: TriOrb

company.description: MediElaj is the flagship AI-powered healthcare platform of Gurugram-based MedPredict Pvt. Ltd., pairing a network of smart diagnostic kiosks with a connected mobile app to bring early diagnosis and preventive screening to underserved communities. Each kiosk runs 30+ invasive and non-invasive tests (ECG, oxygen saturation, vital parameters) and targets neighbourhood clinics, workplaces, and Tier-2/Tier-3 and rural markets across India. The company holds ISO 13485 and SaMD-aligned certifications and is preparing for international expansion.
company.name: MediElaj

company.description: Simple Energy is a Bengaluru-based electric two-wheeler manufacturer founded in 2019 by Suhas Rajkumar, later joined by Shreshth Mishra and Ankit Gupta. The company designs and sells three electric scooters — Simple One, Simple OneS, and Simple Ultra — and operates roughly 70 retail outlets across nine Indian states with a 5,000-unit-per-month manufacturing base. It competes with Ather Energy, Ola Electric, River Mobility, and Ultraviolette in India's E2W market and is preparing a planned IPO targeting around $350M in FY27.
company.name: Simple Energy

company.description: Veritas Aortic Solutions is a Costa Mesa, California medical device company spun out of cardiovascular incubator inQB8 Medical Technologies in late 2025. Its flagship Transcatheter Valved Aortic Root Conduit (TVARC) is a single permanent implant combining a bioprosthetic aortic valve, ascending aortic graft, and proprietary coronary perfusion ports, designed to treat aortic root and ascending aortic diseases without open-heart surgery. The device is delivered transfemorally and is advancing toward first-in-human clinical use.
company.name: Veritas Aortic Solutions

company.description: BoreDM is a US-based vertical SaaS company offering a cloud-based platform for geotechnical and geoenvironmental borehole logging and subsurface data management. Its software lets field teams, project managers, and data analysts capture, manage, and share borehole data and generate professional subsurface reports from any device. The platform is trusted by geotechnical consultants, environmental firms, and public agencies across North America and Australia, including deployments across 18 state Departments of Transportation.
company.name: BoreDM

company.description: Mecka AI is a New York City-based startup building the data and deployment layer for physical AI, capturing and structuring real-world human motion data — collected via custom body sensors and iPhones — into training datasets for robotics and AI teams. Founded in 2025 by a team of repeat founders, it champions training robots on human-collected data rather than traditional teleoperation, and says it projects a $100M annual run rate from already-signed contracts.
company.name: Mecka AI

company.description: Circular11 is a Dorset, UK-based cleantech company, founded in 2021, that converts low-grade, hard-to-recycle plastic waste into low-carbon composite lumber for fencing, furniture, and landscaping products aimed at the construction and estates sectors. Its proprietary, machine-learning-driven manufacturing process adapts to highly variable waste streams to produce a durable, low-maintenance alternative to timber. The company tracks the plastic diverted and carbon footprint of every product through its own digital operating system.
company.name: Circular11

company.description: Unastella is a Seoul-based aerospace startup building its own small-satellite launch vehicles and rocket engines, using a kerosene and liquid-oxygen propulsion system with an electric pump in place of a traditional turbopump. Founded by former KARI engineer Jae Park, it is focused on commercial micro- and small-satellite launches, with a longer-term goal of crewed suborbital flight. It debuted its Una Express-I rocket in 2025 and is targeting orbital launches next.
company.name: Unastella

company.description: Tripo AI develops AI-powered 3D modeling tools that convert 2D images or text prompts into high-fidelity 3D models. It targets developers, designers, and creators building AR/VR, games, and other 3D content, with differentiators including AI-driven generation, auto-rigging, and API access.
company.name: Tripo AI

company.description: Spiro is an African electric vehicle (EV) company headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya, specializing in electric motorbikes and battery swapping infrastructure. The company operates across six African countries: Togo, Benin, Rwanda, Kenya, Uganda, and Nigeria, with a mission to reduce the continent's fossil fuel dependency.
company.name: Spiro

company.description: encosa is a Munich-based provider of battery energy storage systems (BESS) for commercial and industrial businesses, targeting Germany's energy-intensive Mittelstand. Founded in 2024, it runs an end-to-end model covering planning, financing, installation and operation, and combines behind-the-meter energy cost savings with front-of-meter energy trading and arbitrage. Customers can buy, rent or lease the systems and begin cutting electricity costs from the first month, with the investment typically paying back within 18 months to five years.
company.name: encosa

company.description: Invisix is an Eindhoven-based semiconductor metrology company spun out of ASML in 2025 to commercialise more than a decade of soft x-ray measurement R&D. Its non-destructive, high-throughput soft x-ray scatterometry systems use High Harmonic Generation to reconstruct the 3D internal structure of advanced logic and memory devices that optical tools can no longer resolve. The company is building metrology tools that let chipmakers inspect buried nanoscale features at high-volume manufacturing throughput.
company.name: Invisix

company.description: Lune & Wild is a London-based organic baby and children's food brand founded in 2021 by cousins Lara Rodgers, a trained chef, and Nadia Simonds. It makes chef-crafted frozen meals developed with paediatric dietitians and sourced from Soil Association-certified suppliers, aiming to raise the nutritional standard of children's food. The brand sells direct-to-consumer and through retail channels including Ocado, family clubs and nurseries.
company.name: Lune & Wild

company.description: DriveNets builds high-scale, disaggregated networking software for AI infrastructure, service providers, and cloud operators. Its Network Cloud platform layers proprietary software over standard white-box hardware to scale large AI compute clusters and telecom backbones cost-effectively, as an alternative to traditional routers from Cisco and Nvidia. The Israeli company is deployed in the core network of AT&T and other major carriers.
company.name: DriveNets

company.description: Handshake is a London-based retail technology company, founded in 2024, that builds an AI-powered platform for managing commercial agreements between retailers, wholesalers, and suppliers. Its system lets commercial, buying, and finance teams create, negotiate, track, and execute deals covering pricing, shelf space, rebates, and promotional terms in one place, replacing spreadsheets and scattered email threads. The platform is already used by online retailer Gopuff to manage supplier agreements across multiple product categories.
company.name: Handshake

company.description: Gradient Labs is an AI startup building customer operations AI agents for fintech and financial services, founded by ex-Monzo employees. Their platform automates complex customer support and compliance-heavy processes for regulated businesses, with a focus on financial institutions. The company has onboarded major European fintechs and is expanding into the US market.
company.name: Gradient Labs

company.description: Damroo is a Mumbai-based, artist-first music platform serving India's independent and regional-language musicians. It combines a B2C audio-streaming service with B2B distribution, publishing administration, royalty collection, YouTube growth and marketing support to help non-film artists build sustainable careers. Founded in 2021, it offers free and tiered paid plans and helps artists register with copyright bodies such as IPRS.
company.name: Damroo

company.description: Rovia is a Bengaluru-founded fintech building a global wealth platform for equity-rich professionals, particularly Indian employees of US-listed technology companies whose pay increasingly comes as stock grants and RSUs. Its single interface lets users transfer, diversify and reinvest equity compensation across borders, cutting the FX, tax and brokerage frictions that typically erode 6-7% of value on liquidation. The company is registered as an SEC investment adviser in the United States.
company.name: Rovia

company.description: GenerativeX is a Tokyo-based consulting firm founded in 2023 that designs and deploys AI agents for large enterprises in regulated sectors such as finance, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing. Operating on a Forward Deployed Engineer model, it supports clients end-to-end from AI strategy through production implementation and organizational change, with additional offices in New York and San Francisco.
company.name: GenerativeX

company.description: Manako Labs is a London-based Physical AI company building a Vision Agent platform that turns existing camera networks into real-time operational intelligence. Its system lets organizations across industrial, logistics, retail, and infrastructure settings detect issues, coordinate responses, and automate workflows without deploying new hardware or specialist AI teams. Manako also operates Score (Subnet 44), one of the leading subnets on the Bittensor decentralized AI network.
company.name: Manako Labs

company.description: I.W.G Inc. (founded 2020, Tokyo) is a healthtech startup tackling fragmented and incompatible medical-record systems across Asia. Its SaaS platform, DoCloud, uses AI to map and route clinical data between hospitals and clinics, making patient health data interoperable for providers. The company positions its technology as a way to ease healthcare resource shortages.
company.name: I.W.G

company.description: Startupmag (startupmag.co.uk) is a UK media outlet and data platform covering the British startup ecosystem, publishing daily funding-round news and founder insights. It maintains a widely used database tracking thousands of UK pre-seed and seed investors, serving founders, analysts and operators who want to map funding activity across the country.
company.name: Startupmag

company.description: ThIA Santé Mentale is a Montpellier-based French healthtech, founded in 2022, building AI-driven tools for coordinated psychiatric and mental-health care. Its AviPsy platform is used by hundreds of psychiatrists and psychologists across France, supporting coordinated consultations for tens of thousands of patients through assisted diagnosis, personalized treatment and remote monitoring to prevent relapse. The company also runs a community of practitioners working to widen access to mental-health care.
company.name: ThIA Mental Health

company.description: Vitrify is a São Paulo–based AI-powered fintech data platform focused on the private credit market. It centralizes and standardizes fragmented issuance and market data for CRIs, CRAs and debentures from sources such as B3, Anbima and major banks, using AI and data-structuring to deliver actionable insights.
