The platform serves institutional clients and portfolio managers with monitoring, alerts and secondary-market analytics and was founded in 2025.
company.name: Vitrify

company.description: Bayshore is a Munich-based legal-tech startup that turns regulations and internal policies into deterministic, machine-readable guardrails so AI agents can automate legal and compliance work reliably. Its B2B SaaS platform acts as a compliance front door, auto-clearing low-risk requests and escalating complex ones to humans, with auditable agents aimed at highly regulated sectors like defence, finance, energy and pharma. Founded in 2025 by lawyers and engineers building on research from Stanford.
company.name: Bayshore

company.description: Volant Aerotech is a Shanghai-based developer of high-grade commercial passenger eVTOL aircraft, specializing in research, design, and manufacturing of electric vertical take-off and landing platforms for urban air mobility. Its flagship VE25-100 is a six-seat composite-wing aircraft with a 235 km/h top speed and 200-400 km cruising range, currently in crewed flight testing with airworthiness certification targeted for 2027. The company has secured more than 1,900 preorders from domestic and overseas customers and operates a smart manufacturing and flight-test base in Zigong with a designed annual capacity of 300 aircraft.
company.name: Volant Aerotech

company.description: Sapporo-based deep-tech startup developing hybrid rocket engines and weldless propellant tanks for small satellite launchers. The company combines liquid and solid fuels in its propulsion systems to improve safety and lower cost, and is targeting mass production of engines and components for Japan's growing private space launch supply chain.
company.name: MJOLNIR SPACEWORKS

company.description: Atheni is a London-based AI adoption platform that helps organisations move beyond surface-level AI use by embedding role-specific guidance directly into employees' day-to-day workflows. Its browser-based Atheni Accelerator wraps tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Microsoft Copilot with practical prompts and measurement, and has been piloted across further education, manufacturing, financial services, and private equity.
company.name: Atheni

company.description: MokN offers the first real technical response to ID theft, ingeniously using deceptive technology. By luring attackers into believing they have an easy point of entry to the network (a fake SSL VPN, for example), the solution recovers credentials that may have been stolen before they are used. Counterattack and Phish-Back your credentials.
company.name: MokN

company.description: FLUX is a Tokyo-based enterprise AI services company that positions itself as an AI enablement partner, helping large Japanese organizations move from AI strategy through implementation to in-house capability. Its forward-deployed engineers and AI consultants work across finance, IT, telecoms, manufacturing, retail, and construction, combining bespoke AI products and agents with hiring and internalization support. Founded in 2018, FLUX has raised about ¥16B cumulatively and reported monthly revenue growing 3.1x year-on-year as of April 2026.
company.name: FLUX

company.description: Picogrid is an El Segundo defense-tech company building a vendor-neutral integration layer that connects sensors, autonomous platforms, C2 software, and edge systems across more than 100 partner systems. Its Legion API, Orion C2, and Expeditionary nodes are deployed with US and allied forces, letting operators stitch best-of-breed hardware into a unified data and control plane without rebuilding networks per mission.
company.name: Picogrid

company.description: FaceUp Technology operates a secure ethics and compliance platform combining anonymous whistleblowing, case management, an AI-powered hotline, surveys, and audit-ready reporting. The Czech-founded SaaS company serves 3,600+ organizations across 70+ countries, including Mercedes-Benz, Zendesk, Heineken, Sephora, KFC, and Vercel. It positions itself against legacy GRC providers by shifting compliance from a reactive checkbox to real-time workplace risk prevention.
company.name: FaceUp

company.description: Cloudgeni is an Oslo-based startup building deterministic AI agents that detect, remediate, and document configuration drift in AWS, Azure, and GCP environments. Founded in 2024 by ex-Microsoft engineer Davlet Dzhakishev and ex-McKinsey consultant Iuliia Petryshyn Thuen, the company targets enterprise cloud governance and counts Hydro, Havila, and IBM among its early partners.
company.name: Cloudgeni

company.description: Drafted is a San Francisco-based AI startup that turns home design ideas into floor plans and 3D layouts. Users describe a desired home and the platform generates editable plans in seconds, positioning itself between bespoke architecture and stock plans for consumers, architects, and builders.
company.name: Drafted

company.description: XCENA is a South Korea and US-based semiconductor startup building memory-centric computing chips. Its MX1 connects compute capability directly to DRAM via CXL, cutting the round trips between CPUs, GPUs, and memory that dominate AI inference cost. Founders are veterans of Samsung and SK Hynix.
company.name: XCENA

company.description: KoolLogix is a Singapore-based deep-tech company that designs heat-removal hardware for data centres, spun out of electronic-rack manufacturer ERS Industries in 2018. Co-developed with A*STAR, its HRM-series modules attack heat at the rack rather than the room, helping colocation, enterprise and supercomputer operators run higher-density AI workloads with materially lower cooling energy use.
company.name: KoolLogix

company.description: Return Helper is a Taipei-based startup providing cross-border e-commerce returns infrastructure for global merchants. The company runs a SaaS returns management platform integrated with Shopify, Amazon and TikTok, alongside a proprietary warehouse-management system and FlexForward forward-logistics service spanning 20+ overseas warehouses and 30+ carriers. Its sub-brand FlexForward channels Taiwan brands into Japan in partnership with Mitsubishi Logistics.
company.name: Return Helper

company.description: Global leader in connectomics using AI to map human brain connections from MRI scans, providing personalized treatment plans via its FDA-approved Quicktome platform.
company.name: Omniscient Neurotechnology

company.description: Pax is an AI-native public safety platform headquartered in São Paulo that connects city cameras, police records, and criminal databases into a unified real-time intelligence graph for law enforcement. Working with state and municipal governments across more than 30 Brazilian cities, the company helps police investigate vehicle thefts, armed robberies, and homicides faster, and reports cutting violent crime 27% in its first large-scale deployment. It was founded by serial entrepreneur David Peixoto, previously CEO of Nasdaq-listed edtech Arco.
company.name: Pax

company.description: EarSmartAI is an Indianapolis-based medical software company developing an AI-powered consumer diagnostic platform for pediatric ear infections. The system combines a home otoscope, mobile app, and proprietary AI to let parents capture an eardrum video and receive a likelihood assessment guiding next-step care. The company collaborates with pediatric researchers from the University of Pittsburgh and is pursuing a De Novo FDA pathway.
company.name: EarSmartAI

company.description: Orebella is a clean, alcohol-free fragrance brand founded by Bella Hadid in 2024, blending fine fragrance with essential oils and skincare-grade ingredients in bi-phase formulas. It sells through Ulta Beauty, Selfridges, Boots, and Douglas, with a footprint across 20+ countries and a #1 fragrance position at Ulta Beauty Middle East.
company.name: Orebella

company.description: Lokam.ai is an NYC-based startup building a voice-first AI platform for automotive dealerships. It integrates with DMS and CRM systems to automatically follow up with service and showroom customers via branded calls and SMS, recovering missed opportunities and surfacing churn signals to sales teams.
company.name: Lokam.ai

company.description: Reprogram Biosciences is a preclinical oncology biotech developing mRNA-based therapeutics that reprogram tumor cells in situ to activate the immune system against solid tumors. The company pairs an in vivo cell-reprogramming approach with an AI discovery engine called CellRecodeX, built on biological foundation models, to nominate and expand its pipeline. It is based in San Carlos, California and was founded in 2025 by CEO Rustam Esanov and COO Eziz Kuliyev.
company.name: Reprogram Biosciences

company.description: Motif Technologies is a South Korean AI company building large-scale foundation models from its own architecture rather than adapting foreign open-source designs. It was selected for the Ministry of Science and ICT's sovereign "Indigenous AI Foundation Model" program, where it leads a consortium co-developing a 300B-parameter reasoning LLM with partners including Seoul National University and KAIST. The company targets enterprise AI transformation and next-generation AI infrastructure.
company.name: Motif Technologies

company.description: FuriosaAI is a Korean startup specializing in artificial intelligence hardware and software solutions.
company.name: FuriosaAI

company.description: Asteromorph is a Seoul-based AI-for-science lab building Autonomous Science Systems that run the full scientific research process within a defined domain, such as uncovering unknown biological mechanisms or optimizing material synthesis. At the core sits Spacer, a foundation model that pairs a base LLM with a domain-specific add-on to generate research hypotheses and perform multi-step reasoning in biology and chemistry on its own. Its flagship Library Project runs these systems at scale to map fundamental mechanisms of life into a structured database called the Library.
company.name: Asteromorph

company.description: Buysell Standards is a South Korean fintech operating PIECE, a tokenized securities (STO) platform for fractional investment. It is one of seven fractional-investment firms vetted by the Financial Services Commission and the only multi-asset issuer among them, leading the KDX consortium of KRX-led OTC fractional trading. The company is building STO products across patent rights, corporate finance and K-content.
company.name: Buysell Standards

company.description: Moonflow is a collections management platform that automates debt collection for enterprises, banks, and fintech lenders using AI-powered voice and text agents. Founded in Peru, it lets companies stand up a collections operation in a few clicks and serves customers across Latin America, the United States, and Spain. Its client base spans operations in 25 countries and includes Kavak, Konfio, Kueski, and Banco Ripley.
company.name: Moonflow

company.description: Gray Swan AI is a Pittsburgh-based AI security company spun out of Carnegie Mellon, building adversarial testing, runtime monitoring, and red-teaming tools used by frontier labs including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta. Its platform combines Shade (automated red-teaming), Cygnal (real-time policy enforcement), and Arena, a 15,000-member red-teamer network that generates attack data for both products.
company.name: Gray Swan AI

company.description: Fether Labs is a London-based deep-tech startup spun out of Imperial College London building a smart wristband for gesture control. The device uses biomechanical sensing to detect tendon activity beneath the skin, translating finger movements into precise digital commands for AR/VR, robotics, healthcare and industrial systems without cameras or EMG.
company.name: Fether Labs

company.description: BrokerBot, operated by Tucson-based Ribera AI, builds AI teammates trained exclusively on a brokerage's own documents, policies and forms — deployed across chat, SMS, voice, web and email to resolve agent questions and enforce compliance. Since launching in early 2025 it has scaled to 240+ brokerages and roughly 30,000 agents, including independents like Baird & Warner and franchisees of Keller Williams, Coldwell Banker, Better Homes and Gardens, United Real Estate and Engel & Völkers. The company is part of the 2026 NAR REACH cohort and integrates natively with KW Command.
company.name: BrokerBot

company.description: Goshe Energy Storage is a Boulder, Colorado developer, owner, and operator of utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS), focused on acquiring late-stage projects and managing them through construction and commercial operations. The company targets the ERCOT Texas market, having raised over $460 million in total capital to support its first wave of grid-scale BESS assets.
company.name: Goshe Energy Storage

company.description: Mafer AI is a Barcelona-based enterprise software startup founded in late 2025 by Fernando Oliver Jane and Marc Montalbo Burges. Its MaferOS platform applies AI to structure decades of R&D history for formulation-driven industries — specialty chemicals, food and beverage, cosmetics, fragrances, personal care and nutraceuticals — combining per-customer proprietary models with embedded Forward Deployed Engineers in a Palantir-style enterprise model.
company.name: Mafer AI

company.description: Crimson is a London-based legaltech startup building an AI platform purpose-built for litigators handling complex disputes and arbitration. The software ingests entire case files to extract key arguments, locate evidence across pleadings and transcripts, draft documents, and build chronologies. The company says disputes currently running on its platform are collectively worth more than $40bn, and it was selected for A&O Shearman's Fuse legaltech incubator.
company.name: Crimson

company.description: Leverage Edu is a New Delhi-based study-abroad platform that helps Indian students apply to overseas universities, providing AI-led counselling, admissions support, student-loan financing, visa services, and test preparation. Founded in 2017 by Akshay Chaturvedi, it focuses on outbound corridors to the UK, US, Canada, and Australia, and reports a customer base of over 175,000 students. The company crossed Rs 375 crore in revenue in FY26 and turned EBITDA-positive.
company.name: Leverage Edu

company.description: Reactor is a San Francisco developer platform for real-time generative AI video. Founded by ex-Apple Vision Pro engineers Alberto Taiuti and Bryce Schmidtchen, the company offers an SDK and API that let developers build interactive applications powered by world models, generating video dynamically rather than via batch processing.
company.name: Reactor

company.description: H1 is a healthcare data platform connecting life-sciences and provider organizations to physician and clinical trial intelligence.
company.name: H1

company.description: YolTech Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotech company pioneering in vivo genome editing therapies. Founded in 2021, the company is advancing next-generation in vivo gene-editing therapies designed for one-time treatment, with a fully integrated platform encompassing proprietary CRISPR nucleases, base editors, and a cutting-edge lipid nanoparticle delivery system. The company operates a cGMP-compliant manufacturing facility supporting clinical supply through Phase III and early commercial scal
company.name: YolTech Therapeutics

company.description: Anveshan is an Indian D2C food brand selling minimally processed pantry staples — A2 cow ghee, wood-pressed edible oils, raw honey, beverage mixes and natural sweets — sourced through a network of village micro-entrepreneurs. Founded in 2020 by three IIT Guwahati alumni, the company combines traceability technology with traditional production methods to deliver farm-to-fork quality while paying farmers fair prices.
company.name: Anveshan

company.description: ZeroDrag is a Nagpur-based UAV electronics company building indigenous drone avionics, including autopilots, motor controllers, GNSS modules, telemetry systems and ESCs for OEMs across defence, agriculture, logistics and industrial applications. Founded in 2022, it positions itself as an Indian SovereignTech alternative to Chinese-dominated flight-control components for domestic and export markets.
company.name: ZeroDrag

company.description: Eazzy is a Gurugram-based home services and appliance lifecycle management platform connecting Indian households with trained technicians for AC and appliance installation, maintenance, repair, resale and buyback. Founded by ex-Reliance/Flipkart executive Saurabh Luthra and former Cashify COO Aksh Chauhan, the startup is building a tech-enabled, AI-augmented alternative to the fragmented, locally-run home services market across the NCR.
company.name: Eazzy

company.description: Singapore-based cross-border e-commerce platform operated through Japanese holding company Ringbull, building AI tools (Tsuro-san) for international online retail expansion.
company.name: Lingble

company.description: Corgi is an AI-native, full-stack insurance carrier built for startups, using modern infrastructure and artificial intelligence to handle underwriting, policy management, and claims so it can deliver fast, flexible commercial insurance tailored to growing companies.[2][5]
company.name: Corgi

company.description: Fonoa is a Dublin-based tax operating system that helps multinationals like Uber, Netflix, Canva, and Booking.com handle indirect tax compliance across 190+ jurisdictions, with tax ID validation in 100+ countries, real-time determination, e-invoicing, and returns on a single AI-driven platform processing over a billion transactions annually. Founded by three Uber alumni, it is repositioning fragmented enterprise tax workflows into one integrated, autonomous system.
company.name: Fonoa

company.description: AI research company
company.name: Anthropic

company.description: DeepTree is a Milan-based AI-native intelligence platform for private capital markets, unifying financial filings, ownership data, and M&A activity behind a natural-language workspace. The product serves investment banks, private equity funds, M&A boutiques and wealth managers — over 100 client institutions in its first year — to accelerate target screening, company analysis and buyer-list construction.
company.name: DeepTree

company.description: Vertically-integrated space technology company building laser communications ground stations, optical sensing systems, and in-space imaging payloads. Headquartered in Los Angeles with a 57-acre manufacturing campus in Adrian, Michigan, the company supplies NASA, the U.S. Space Force, and commercial satellite operators.
company.name: Observable Space

company.description: Luxembourg-based AI training and onboarding platform that scans existing company files to generate role-specific employee training that adapts to individual performance. Founded in 2025 by former Talkwalker executives, Deelan serves tech scale-ups and B2B teams across Europe and the US.
company.name: Deelan

company.description: Daloopa is a Series B, venture-backed company providing AI-powered fundamental data for institutional investors. Its platform sources, structures, and distributes the most complete and accurate historical financial dataset, covering nearly 4,700 public companies globally. The company is focused on accelerating decision-making for investment professionals by automating financial modeling and data extraction workflows.
company.name: Daloopa

company.description: Digital platform that helps patients find the best healthcare providers using better data and smarter financial incentives, leveraging large medical datasets to rank doctors and drive cost savings for employers.
company.name: Garner Health

company.description: Utilidata develops embedded AI software for power infrastructure, with its Karman platform delivering real-time visibility, dynamic orchestration, and control across smart meters and grid distribution equipment. Built on a custom NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano chip, Karman targets AI data centers and utilities to unlock stranded grid capacity and squeeze more usable power from existing infrastructure.
company.name: Utilidata

company.description: Triomics builds oncology-specific AI for cancer centers, automating chart prep, clinical trial matching, and tumor registry reporting against complex multi-year patient records. The platform is used by academic centers including Memorial Sloan Kettering and Yale Cancer Center, and is positioned against generic AI scribes by training models on oncology data rather than general clinical text.
company.name: Triomics

company.description: Countable Labs is a Palo Alto-based biotechnology company that has developed Countable PCR, a single-molecule PCR platform aimed at delivering higher sensitivity and quantitative accuracy than digital PCR or next-generation sequencing without requiring Poisson correction or standard curves. Its 10-color multiplexing system is targeted at cell and gene therapy, minimal residual disease (MRD) testing, biomarker validation and vaccine quality control, where rare-event detection at scale is the bottleneck.
company.name: Countable Labs

company.description: Saris builds an agentic AI workflow platform aimed at banks and credit unions, automating back-office work like lending, compliance reviews, underwriting, and customer operations while keeping a human in the loop. The company integrates with core banking software providers including Fiserv, Encompass, and MeridianLink, and says its agents can automate up to 70% of consumer, mortgage, and commercial lending tasks.
company.name: Saris

company.description: Modiqo is a San Francisco-based agentic AI infrastructure startup building a local execution layer called Rote that captures successful AI agent runs and transforms them into reusable, deterministic workflows. The platform connects agents to any API without server infrastructure or SDKs, reducing token usage and preventing repeated debugging when models or APIs update. Founded and led by CEO Chetan Conikee, Modiqo targets systems operators tasked with moving AI agents from one-off demos to production-grade systems.
company.name: Modiqo

company.description: Canals develops an enterprise AI platform that automates workflows for wholesale distributors, manufacturers, and contractors across sales order entry, quoting, accounts payable, and purchasing. The cloud-native software integrates with existing ERPs to convert unstructured inputs like handwritten notes, voice messages, and PDFs into clean digital orders. Customers include DSG, Locke Supply, and The Kendall Group, with the platform processing over 8 million sales orders and $5 billion in payables.
company.name: Canals

company.description: Rep AI builds an agentic AI operating system for ecommerce, unifying shopper-intent detection, personalized sales guidance, conversion optimization and post-purchase support inside a single platform. Founded in 2020 and led by CEO Yoav Oz, the New York-based company serves brands like OLLY, HigherDOSE, Satya Jewelry and Unclaimed Baggage.
company.name: Rep AI

company.description: Psilera is a Tampa, Florida-based biopharmaceutical company developing next-generation neuroplastogens and psychedelic-inspired therapies for neurological and psychiatric disorders. Its lead candidate, PSIL-006, is engineered to retain the therapeutic benefits of classical psychedelics while reducing hallucinogenic effects and cardiovascular risks, with first-in-human studies targeted for 2027.
company.name: Psilera

company.description: Solstice is an AI-native marketing agency for pharma brands, combining proprietary AI models with in-house medical and regulatory experts to generate, review and activate compliant commercialization content. Headquartered in New York, it serves a dozen-plus pharmaceutical companies, including several top-20 brands across oncology, immunology and metabolic diseases.
company.name: Solstice

company.description: Kubera Health builds a contract-to-payment system of record for U.S. healthcare, translating payor-provider agreements into structured rules that run continuously against claims and payment data. Its platform combines contract intelligence, payment auditing, and policy intelligence to flag underpayments and prevent disputes before they reach the appeals cycle. The NYC-based company, founded by physician Roja Garimella, partners with mid-to-large health systems including Hollywood Presbyterian Medical Center.
company.name: Kubera Health

company.description: Volt Harbor is an Ann Arbor, Michigan-based deep-tech company building modular, software-defined energy storage systems for data centers and the electric grid. Its patented MAC-BESS platform, licensed from the University of Michigan, integrates batteries, power electronics, and on-board computing into a single product class that supports both new and second-life EV battery modules across commercial, industrial, and utility deployments.
company.name: Volt Harbor

company.description: Nala is a New York-based fintech building stablecoin-powered cross-border payment infrastructure. Its consumer remittance app and B2B Rafiki platform connect 249 banks and 26 mobile money systems across 16 countries, letting enterprises move funds between the US, Europe, and emerging markets in real time. Founded in Tanzania in 2017, the company pivoted from diaspora remittances into institutional B2B rails.
company.name: Nala

company.description: Trajectory builds a platform for continual learning, letting companies post-train open-source AI models on real user interactions so products improve weekly instead of staying static between major model releases. Founded in 2025 by former Google DeepMind, Apple, OpenAI and Meta Superintelligence Labs researchers, it works with AI-native customers including Decagon, Clay and Harvey to ship narrowly-tuned models that outperform frontier labs on customer-specific tasks.
company.name: Trajectory

company.description: Blulabs is a Miami-based global supply chain, sourcing, manufacturing, and logistics partner serving airlines, hospitality groups, distributors, retailers, and Fortune 500 customers. The company operates across 10 countries and four continents through a capex-light model combining a diversified supplier base, flexible manufacturing footprint, and an emerging AI-enabled supply chain intelligence platform. Founded and led by the Garson family, Blulabs positions itself as a behind-the-scenes resilience partner for enterprises whose operations cannot tolerate supply chain disruption.
company.name: Blulabs

company.description: Developer of an agent-native security platform for safe AI adoption
company.name: Geordie AI

company.description: Kopa.ai is a Vilnius-based agentic AI platform positioned as an operating system for direct-to-consumer e-commerce teams. Its agents continuously monitor products, campaigns, customers and inventory, then execute decisions such as launching ads, reallocating budgets or generating creatives — either with approval or autonomously. The company launched publicly in December 2025 and reports €2M ARR across 2,000+ merchants, with revenue contributions from sister brand Profit Koala.
company.name: Kopa.ai

company.description: Eddy Grid develops real-time optimisation algorithms that maximise revenue from solar, wind, and battery assets by trading them on European energy markets. Founded in Utrecht in 2023, the cash-flow-positive company manages more than 500 MW for clients in the Netherlands and Belgium and is preparing a launch in Germany.
company.name: Eddy Grid

company.description: Orbital Industries is a London-based industrial technology company using AI to design and manufacture physical infrastructure for next-generation compute. Its first commercial product is a PFAS-free dielectric cooling fluid for GPUs, paired with modular data centre systems deployable in months, developed via a multi-year partnership with AWS. Founded in 2022 by former DeepMind researcher Jonathan Godwin with James Gin-Pollock and Daniel Miodovnik, the company employs 50 people across London and San Francisco.
company.name: Orbital Industries

company.description: Handshaik is a London-based AI startup building an AI-native deal origination platform that produces analyst-grade company profiles to streamline how investors and dealmakers source and evaluate opportunities. Founded by Joshua Port, the company indexed millions of company profiles during a stealth build before its 2026 pre-seed raise.
company.name: Handshaik

company.description: Itera is a San Francisco deep-tech company that has built what it calls the world's first fluid circuit board, using a glass and liquid-metal architecture that lets engineers rewire and test real electronic circuits in under a minute instead of the two-to-six weeks a traditional PCB cycle takes. It sells this as an Electronics-as-a-Service offering, assembling customers' designs with their own components at secure U.S.-based testing centers to compress hardware iteration from months into days.
company.name: Itera

company.description: Catalan edtech building an AI-powered educational platform for teachers to create personalized activities, automate assessment, and support students with safe classroom AI.
company.name: Mathew

company.description: Tokyo-based industrial DX firm providing data-driven transformation support and the Resource Cloud platform, now expanding into AI-led industry change and space.
company.name: INDUSTRIAL-X

company.description: Mykor is a Bristol-based biotech startup turning industrial and agricultural waste into low-carbon construction materials. Its biofabrication platform combines engineered mycelium strains, green chemistry and closed-loop automated manufacturing to produce insulation and prefabricated wall systems. The first product, MykoSIP, is a preassembled partition wall that the company says delivers roughly 50% carbon savings versus incumbent systems while meeting fire, acoustic and cost requirements.
company.name: Mykor

company.description: Endurance Energy is a Seattle-based deep-tech startup developing undersea geothermal power generators that tap heat from the seafloor to produce baseload renewable electricity. The company recently launched a subsea pilot with the Kingdom of Tonga and is targeting island grids and military installations as early customers.
company.name: Endurance Energy

company.description: Iconic is a Los Angeles-based tech-enabled M&A advisory firm targeting the lower-middle market of US small businesses valued between $1M and $10M. The platform combines human advisors with a proprietary AI system that automates lead qualification, generates buyer-ready deal materials (CIMs, teasers), and matches sellers with vetted institutional and individual buyers. Iconic positions itself to capture the so-called "Silver Tsunami" — an estimated 6 million baby-boomer-owned small businesses, representing roughly $5 trillion in generational wealth, expected to change hands over the next decade.
company.name: Iconic

company.description: Solstice Health is a New York-based AI platform that streamlines the medical-legal-regulatory (MLR) review process for biopharma marketing teams. Its generative-AI engine flags compliance risks early and accelerates content approval cycles, helping pharma brands push compliant campaigns to market faster. Customers include top-20 pharmaceutical companies and MM&M Top 100 agencies.
company.name: Solstice Health

company.description: Stellar Alpina is a Zurich-based deep-tech space venture developing rotating detonation rocket engines (RDREs) for in-space propulsion, alongside modular spacecraft and lunar delivery systems. Founded in early 2026 by engineers tied to Switzerland's Academic Spaceflight Initiative, the company completed its first hotfire campaign 82 days after incorporation, positioning itself among the first commercial RDRE programs in Europe.
company.name: Stellar Alpina

company.description: Funds, Inc. operates a Tokyo-based direct financial platform connecting individual investors with corporate financing opportunities through fixed-term, fixed-yield offerings. Its group spans the core Funds platform, Funds Real Estate, Funds Startups venture debt, and Taiwan-based Asia Money Fintech Company. Cumulative offerings exceed ¥110B with zero defaults to date.
company.name: Funds, Inc.

company.description: iFAST Diagnostics is a UK biotech, spun out of the University of Southampton, that builds a rapid antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) platform delivering results in under three hours from blood or urine samples — versus the 24-72 hours of current gold-standard methods. Its UKCA-certified iFAST ONE system is being placed in hospital laboratories to help clinicians prescribe the right antibiotic earlier and curb the spread of antimicrobial resistance.
company.name: iFAST Diagnostics

company.description: Fields Good is a functional cookie brand based in Austin, founded by Ashley Fields (daughter of Mrs. Fields Cookies founder Debbi Fields) and Kim Anderson. The brand sells soft-baked ready-to-eat cookies engineered around focus, protein, and sleep, blending nostalgic flavor with functional ingredients like creatine, Cognizin citicoline, and L-theanine. It sells direct-to-consumer with plans to expand into TikTok Shop, Amazon, and national retail.
company.name: Fields Good

company.description: US-Israeli defense-tech startup whose AI platform turns raw visual data — drone footage, body-cam video, traffic-camera streams — into structured, machine-readable intelligence for defense, law-enforcement, and enterprise analysts.
company.name: Airis Labs

company.description: viaim builds AI-powered workplace earbuds and recording hardware, with OpenNote as its flagship open-ear meeting headset offering real-time AI translation across 78 languages and on-device transcription. The Singapore-based firm has 1.5M+ registered users across 200 countries and is evolving from recording hardware into an office AI Agent platform.
company.name: viaim

company.description: Revolutionizing the transportation of life-saving medicines using proprietary cold chain technology, including the Ember Cube, the world’s first self-refrigerated, cloud-connected shipping box designed to reduce waste and improve patient outcomes.
company.name: Ember LifeSciences

company.description: Signos makes an FDA-cleared, over-the-counter continuous glucose monitoring app that turns biosensor data into personalized insights for weight management. The California-based company pairs Dexcom CGM hardware with an AI engine that helps non-diabetic users link food, activity, and metabolic response, and is sold direct-to-consumer through Dexcom's Stelo channel.
company.name: Signos

company.description: Tensormesh is the AI infrastructure optimization company enabling up to 10x faster inference while keeping full control of data and deployment. Founded by faculty and researchers from the University of Chicago, UC Berkeley, and Carnegie Mellon, Tensormesh commercializes state-of-the-art research to eliminate GPU waste and latency. The software captures and reuses intermediate data other systems discard, delivering breakthrough performance on infrastructure customers own and control.
company.name: Tensormesh

company.description: Develops power management solutions for AI data centers and cloud infrastructure, focusing on grid-to-core power delivery to improve efficiency and reduce losses.
company.name: C2i Semiconductors

company.description: Agentic AI platform purpose-built for the insurance industry to modernize business process outsourcing by replacing manual workflows with autonomous AI agents.
company.name: Pace

company.description:
Makers of Devin, the first AI software engineer. We are an applied AI lab building end-to-end software agents.

We’re building collaborative AI teammates that enable engineers to focus on more interesting problems and empower engineering teams to strive for more ambitious goals.
company.name: Cognition AI

company.description: RevEng.AI is a London-based cybersecurity company building a binary-native verification layer for the software supply chain. Its foundational model, BinNet, analyzes compiled executables, firmware and third-party artifacts directly — without source code — to detect hidden vulnerabilities, malicious functionality and supply-chain tampering before software ships or is deployed. The company sells into enterprise security teams and allied-government defense customers.
company.name: RevEng.AI

company.description: Thea Energy is a New Jersey-based fusion power company commercializing a planar-coil stellarator architecture, using arrays of mass-manufacturable superconducting magnets and software-defined controls in place of precision-machined 3D coils. Spun out of Princeton University and Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory in 2022, it is an awardee of the US Department of Energy's Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program and is building the Eos integrated stellarator en route to its first commercial Helios power plant.
company.name: Thea Energy

company.description: Slamcore builds spatial-intelligence software that uses a stereo camera to track the position and behaviour of autonomous vehicles inside factories and warehouses without requiring extra infrastructure. Its Slamcore Aware and Slamcore Alert products are deployed across more than 30 facilities in Europe and North America, scaling to hundreds of units in under two years. The London-based company is led by CEO Owen Nicholson.
company.name: Slamcore

company.description: AI-powered peer review platform for real estate development documents
company.name: LightTable

company.description: Capchase is a New York-based fintech operating a B2B vendor-financing platform that lets software and hardware vendors offer flexible payment terms while receiving full payment upfront. It uses AI agents for credit decisioning, quote parsing and buyer coordination, targeting the $1.3T equipment financing market. Customers include Okta, Palo Alto Networks, Barracuda Networks and Verkada.
company.name: Capchase

company.description: Cypher AI builds AI-native digital infrastructure for life science R&D, unifying experiment design, execution, analysis, and vendor coordination in a single platform for biotech, pharma, and research teams. The Cambridge, MA company aims to replace fragmented manual lab systems with an intelligent operating layer that accelerates discovery.
company.name: Cypher AI

company.description: Secretome Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company based in Plano, Texas, developing cellular therapies derived from neonatal cardiac progenitor cells (nCPCs). Its lead candidate STM-01 targets Duchenne muscular dystrophy-associated cardiomyopathy and other rare cardiomyopathies, with a preclinical pipeline including STM-21 aimed at broader cardiac inflammation and fibrosis.
company.name: Secretome Therapeutics

company.description: CodeIntegrity builds a runtime control layer that acts as a deterministic guardrail for agentic AI applications, blocking prompt-injection attacks and limiting which enterprise systems and data an autonomous agent can touch. The company was founded in the Seattle area in May 2024 by Steven Jung and Abi Raghuram, alumni of Antler's New York residency, and recently relocated headquarters to San Francisco. Its product is currently being piloted with companies in regulated industries.
company.name: CodeIntegrity

company.description: Waypoint Bio is a New York-based biotech engineering in vivo CAR-T cell therapies for solid tumors. The company is taking a portfolio approach, running parallel CAR-T clinical trials in China to move multiple programs into the clinic faster and at lower cost.
company.name: Waypoint Bio

company.description: WeRoad is a Milan-based group travel platform that organizes themed itineraries for solo Millennial and Gen Z travelers, pairing customers with a group leader and a WhatsApp pre-trip community. Since 2017 it has served 300,000+ travelers, generated €130M in 2025 revenue, and runs the WeMeet app for local in-person social events to deepen its community model.
company.name: WeRoad

company.description: Tiea Connectors is a Bengaluru-based manufacturer of high-performance interconnect and contact solutions for mission-critical applications. The company designs and produces precision connectors, contacts, and connectivity systems for electric mobility, aerospace, defence, avionics, and industrial OEMs, covering product design, rapid prototyping, tooling, material engineering, and precision manufacturing.
company.name: Tiea Connectors

company.description: ClearNote Health is a San Diego-based liquid-biopsy company developing blood-based early-detection tests for some of the deadliest cancers. Its Avantect pancreatic and ovarian tests and Virtuoso epigenomics platform — built on technology from Stephen Quake's Stanford lab — use multiomic signals and machine learning to flag disease in elevated-risk patients earlier than conventional approaches.
company.name: ClearNote Health

company.description: Olyzon is a Paris-based adtech company building an agentic decisioning layer for connected TV (CTV) advertising. Its platform deploys AI agents that qualify inventory, plan and activate media across CTV pipes (streaming, YouTube, walled gardens, OEM platforms, publisher direct), and close the measurement loop into DSPs and ad servers. Clients include Publicis, WPP, OMD, Mastercard, Audi and McDonald's.
company.name: Olyzon

company.description: Lastwall is a Canadian cybersecurity company providing identity-first security and quantum-resilient authentication for defense, government, and critical infrastructure. Founded in Fredericton, NB, it secures Zero Trust access across cloud, hybrid, and air-gapped environments and holds FedRAMP Moderate Authorization, with adoption by the U.S. Department of War and the Government of Canada.
company.name: Lastwall

company.description: Caudal Energy is an Oxford University spinout, formerly Porpoise Power, building bio-inspired oscillating-fin tidal generators. Its modular surface-mounted system targets mid-flow tidal sites with peak flows above 3 knots, expanding the addressable deployment footprint beyond the handful of extreme-flow locations conventional turbines require.
company.name: Caudal Energy

company.description: Go Swag is a Glasgow-based corporate gifting platform that helps global brands deliver branded, on-brand gifts worldwide through AI-assisted product curation, recipient management, and international warehousing across the UK, Netherlands, and US. Its customers include Meta, Netflix, Apple, ElevenLabs, and n8n.
company.name: Go Swag

company.description: Pacifico Biolabs is a Berlin-based food biotech developing scalable mycelium-fermented seafood and protein alternatives by repurposing brewery fermentation infrastructure for sustainable protein production.
company.name: Pacifico Biolabs

company.description: Baymatob (commercially branded Oli) is a Sydney-based medtech building a wireless wearable that simultaneously captures maternal and fetal physiological data across ten biosensors during labor. Its patented Predictive Maternal-Fetal Signal platform translates the readings into live clinical signals that flag complications such as postpartum hemorrhage, fetal distress and stillbirth risk hours before they emerge. The company has an FDA Breakthrough Device Designation and is running pivotal trials across seven sites in Australia and the United States.
company.name: Baymatob

company.description: SPARQ Worlds is a UAE-based startup, headquartered at the Innovation City free zone in Ras Al Khaimah, building what it calls an AI-native game engine and creator platform. Its proprietary C++ engine automates the production scaffolding of game-making — coding, asset management, networking, multi-platform publishing and monetisation — so a single creator can build and ship games while keeping control of the design. Founded by Christopher Pail and Christoffer Wilhelmsen, the company spent roughly two years and a team of 20-plus engineers building the engine before raising.
company.name: SPARQ Worlds

company.description: The Black Label is a Seoul-based South Korean music label and production house founded in 2016 by hitmaker Teddy Park, producing artists including Taeyang, Rose, and Jeon Somi. The studio created the soundtrack including the chart-topping single "Golden" for Netflix animated musical KPop Demon Hunters, and is increasingly positioned as a cross-media IP house for music, virtual artists, and film.
company.name: The Black Label

company.description: Zonova is a UK biotech developing infection-resistant materials for medical devices. Its Z-ROS platform embeds antimicrobial function directly into device polymers rather than relying on surface coatings or antibiotic release, targeting reduced device-associated infections in high-volume products such as peripheral and central venous catheters.
company.name: Zonova

company.description: Quantum Tiger is a Kolkata, India-based startup building an AI-native intelligence operating system for enterprises. Its platform ingests fragmented documents, operational reports, and intelligence feeds, then applies graph memory and contextual reasoning to turn them into unified, real-time strategic intelligence. Founded by Rana Dutta and Manas Paul, it positions itself as sovereign AI infrastructure for institutional decision-making.
company.name: Quantum Tiger

company.description: Moffett AI (Chinese name MoXin) is a Chinese AI chip startup pioneering sparse-computing architectures for inference workloads. Its self-developed Antoum chip and S-series accelerator cards have topped the global MLPerf inference benchmark three times, with the S40 reaching 2.9x the peak throughput of NVIDIA's A100. The company sells into cloud, edge, and national computing-power-network deployments across China.
company.name: MoXin AI

company.description: Tomato Jos is a Nigerian agribusiness building a vertically integrated tomato processing operation in Kaduna State, combining its own farms with a network of over 3,000 smallholder partners to feed its Kangimi paste plant. Founded in 2014 by Mira Mehta, the company targets Nigeria's domestic paste market following the federal import ban and supplies branded tomato paste across West African retail.
company.name: Tomato Jos

company.description: Mia Healthcare Technologies is a Cape Town–based e-health startup expanding access to affordable dental and orthodontic care across Southern Africa. It runs a network of mobile and fixed dental practices and locally manufactures clear aligners as a lower-cost alternative to international brands, partnering with dentists to serve patients underserved by cost, geography, or specialist availability.
company.name: Mia Dental

company.description: NavigateAI is building an AI copilot for field workers in construction and the skilled trades. Its mobile and Meta Glasses–based system offers real-time coaching, project scoping, code-and-spec quality control, and on-demand knowledge to help workers build faster with fewer defects. Founded in 2025 by Opendoor co-founder Eric Wu, the company partners with homebuilders, property managers, and trade schools to embed AI directly into the workforce.
company.name: NavigateAI

company.description: Tokyo-based deep-tech startup commercializing graphite thermal energy storage. Its G-TES boiler stores surplus renewable electricity as heat in graphite blocks and discharges it as hot water or steam, replacing fossil-fired industrial boilers. Founded in 2022 by ex-JAEA high-temperature gas reactor researchers; first commercial unit deployed in Takehara, Hiroshima in 2025.
company.name: Blossom Energy

company.description: Tonada is a Stockholm-based AI music platform that generates original, royalty-free background music tailored to a brand's sonic identity. Its engine integrates with point-of-sale, foot-traffic, weather and time-of-day signals to adapt music in real time across retail, hospitality, and nightlife venues. The company has paying customers in Scandinavia, the DACH region, and Singapore.
company.name: Tonada

company.description: Perceptic builds an AI operating system for biopharma, connecting research, development, and clinical decision-making across the full drug lifecycle. Founded by former Palantir AIP and Life Sciences team members, its Scout, PercepticOS and Atlas products are deployed at CSL and multiple top-20 pharma companies for asset triage, hypothesis testing and clinical data synthesis.
company.name: Perceptic

company.description: v4c.ai is a Scottsdale, Arizona-based data and AI services firm operating as a pure-play Databricks consulting partner. The company helps enterprises modernize data platforms and ship machine learning, generative AI, and analytics workloads, fielding 400+ certified practitioners across 150+ joint Databricks customers. Founded by Vijay Rao, the firm has grown rapidly as a specialist within the Databricks ecosystem.
company.name: v4c.ai

company.description: D-CRBN is an Antwerp-based deep-tech company that uses electrified plasma technology to convert industrial CO2 emissions and hydrocarbons into carbon monoxide and syngas — building blocks for sustainable fuels, chemicals and materials. The spin-off from the PLASMANT research group at the University of Antwerp targets decarbonisation of heavy industry, with pilots already validated in the steel and chemicals sectors.
company.name: D-CRBN

company.description: Fairdeal.Market is a Delhi-NCR-based B2B quick-commerce platform that supplies India's kirana (neighborhood) retailers with more than 1,000 SKUs delivered within 60 minutes via a dark-store-led supply chain. Founded in 2022 by brothers Prateek and Yash Bansal, it combines real-time inventory visibility, transparent pricing, and rapid replenishment to modernize wholesale procurement for small-format retail.
company.name: Fairdeal.Market

company.description: Protuoso Biosciences develops multifunctional biologics — branded MUXBODIES, fully recombinant fusion proteins that combine multiple modalities in one molecule — for cardiometabolic, oncology, and autoimmune diseases. The company pairs AI-enabled protein engineering, computational protein design, and massively parallel synthetic biology with a hub-and-spoke operating model: a shared discovery platform at the hub feeds therapeutic-area-focused spokes. Co-founded by Tim Lu and Willie Xiang and headquartered in San Francisco with R&D in Taipei.
company.name: Protuoso Biosciences

company.description: Cartesian Therapeutics is a NASDAQ-listed clinical-stage biotech developing mRNA-based cell therapies for autoimmune disease, with lead asset Descartes-08 in Phase 3 trials for generalized myasthenia gravis.
company.name: Cartesian Therapeutics

company.description: Bucketlistt operates a curated marketplace for verified adventure and experience travel across India, partnering with vetted local operators for activities such as river rafting, bungee jumping, camping, and cultural experiences. The company also runs OkGhumo, a SaaS ERP and CRM platform that helps experience-tourism operators manage bookings, operations, and finances.
company.name: Bucketlistt

company.description: Yoho is a Delhi-based direct-to-consumer footwear brand founded in 2020 by Ahmad Hushsham and Prateek Singhal, focused on comfort-led casual, formal, and performance running shoes. The company has sold over 3 million pairs through its own site and marketplaces including Amazon, Myntra, Flipkart, Ajio, Nykaa, and quick-commerce platforms such as Blinkit, Zepto, and Swiggy Instamart.
company.name: Yoho

company.description: Cable is a Sydney-based retail electricity provider for Australian small and medium businesses. It installs a smart battery at the customer site at no upfront cost and sells power on a flat 24/7 rate guaranteed for three years, charging the battery at low wholesale prices and discharging at peaks to deliver 20-40% bill savings. The company is currently serving SMEs in Sydney metro LGAs on the Ausgrid network after a six-month private beta.
company.name: Cable

company.description: Texture builds an operating system for energy utilities, unifying grid visibility and operations across distributed assets like batteries, EVs, solar and SCADA into a single layer. The platform serves utility cooperatives, OEMs and grid services companies including Vermont Electric Cooperative and sonnen, and integrates with 50+ device makers to coordinate demand response and virtual power plant programs in real time.
company.name: Texture

company.description: bitBiome is a Tokyo- and Palo Alto-based biotech building an AI-driven enzyme and strain engineering platform on top of its proprietary single-cell microbial genome database (bit-MAP/bit-GEM), which holds over 3 billion sequences mostly absent from public datasets. The company couples this with high-throughput modelling (bit-EVO) to discover and engineer microbial enzymes and pathways for industrial biotech, pharma, food and advanced biomanufacturing.
company.name: bitBiome

company.description: Certo is a Paris-based regtech building an AI compliance platform for beauty and consumer packaged goods companies. Its agents review ingredients, formulas, labelling, and marketing claims against regulations in 70+ markets including the EU, US, China, South Korea, Japan, and Latin America. Co-founders Bastien Deliège-Coste and Jean Duquenne sell into regulatory teams at beauty groups and CPG retailers.
company.name: Certo

company.description: Human Archive builds large-scale multimodal training datasets for robotics and physical AI. The startup equips gig-economy workers in India with head-mounted cameras and sensors to capture first-person video of everyday home-services tasks, then licenses that data to AI labs developing embodied models. Founded by researchers from Stanford and UC Berkeley; in Y Combinator's W26 batch.
company.name: Human Archive

company.description: P2 Science is a Connecticut-based green chemistry company that designs and manufactures plant-based specialty ingredients for personal care, flavor and fragrance, and performance materials. Co-founded at Yale's Center for Green Chemistry, it makes drop-in replacements for petroleum-derived ingredients like petrolatum and is expanding into polymers, coatings, and crop care.
company.name: P2 Science

company.description: Didit is a YC-backed identity and fraud infrastructure platform that ships KYC, KYB, AML screening, biometric verification, transaction monitoring, and wallet screening behind one API. The company serves 2,000+ customers across fintech, crypto, marketplaces, iGaming, and government use cases and is the only identity provider whose technology has been assessed by Spain's Treasury, CNMV, and SEPBLAC as equivalent to in-person verification. Dual HQ in San Francisco and Barcelona.
company.name: Didit

company.description: Adenocyte is an Israeli medical technology company developing the LINFU platform, which combines microbubble injection with low-intensity non-focused ultrasound to safely release epithelial cells for cytological analysis of the pancreas, breast, and prostate. Its FDA-cleared technology targets very early detection of pancreatic cancer in high-risk patients and is reimbursed by Medicare and commercial insurers under existing CPT codes.
company.name: Adenocyte

company.description: Phytolon is an Israeli biotech developing fermentation-derived natural food colors using engineered baker's yeast. Its first commercial pigment, Beetroot Red, received FDA approval in 2026 and targets bakery, beverage, confectionery, and supplement makers seeking clean-label alternatives to synthetic dyes. The company spans pink, red, orange, and yellow shades across the food and beverage supply chain.
company.name: Phytolon

company.description: Preventive healthcare platform that analyzes over 180 biomarkers from blood tests conducted in certified labs, using AI and medical review to provide actionable health insights, dashboards, and personalized guidance on nutrition, exercise, sleep, and more, without replacing physician consultations.
company.name: Lucis

company.description: Flexprice builds open-source monetization infrastructure for AI-native and SaaS companies, enabling usage-based, credit-based, and hybrid billing across API calls, token usage, and GPU hours. The platform offers real-time metering, entitlement gating, and revenue reporting and is self-hostable. Teams are based in New Delhi, Bengaluru, and San Francisco.
company.name: Flexprice

company.description: Otomato is a DeFi intelligence platform that ingests a user's wallet addresses and monitors on-chain positions — lending, tokens, NFTs, prediction markets — across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base and HyperEVM in real time. The product launched as a Telegram bot and now reaches over 2,000 users with iOS and Android apps released in April 2026, integrating protocols including AAVE, Pendle, Uniswap, Morpho, Euler and Hyperliquid. The operating entity is Dyment Labs, headquartered in Singapore.
company.name: Otomato

company.description: Canyon Code builds an enterprise workflow intelligence layer that sits above the model serving stack to govern, observe, and optimize multi-agent AI applications. Its dependence graph tracks how individual agents and LLM calls interact, letting enterprises set per-app and per-persona policies trading off cost, latency, and accuracy. Co-founded in 2026 by Ravikiran Gopalan (three-time founder, ex enterprise multi-agentic apps) and ML-systems researcher Prof. Aditya Akella, the company is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.
company.name: Canyon Code

company.description: CasaPerks Technologies operates CasaPerks, an AI-powered resident loyalty platform for student housing and conventional multifamily real estate, and WorksPerks, an employee recognition product for small and medium businesses. Both run on Anthropic Claude and let operators issue rewards while the engine personalizes redemption based on resident or employee behavior. The Austin-based company posted 10x revenue growth in 2025, acquired credit-reporting startup Tackle, and opened Canadian operations.
company.name: CasaPerks Technologies

company.description: Stord is an Atlanta-based commerce enablement platform that operates a vertically integrated fulfillment network, software stack, and AI layer for independent direct-to-consumer brands. With nearly 100 fulfillment facilities, more than 1,000 customers and over $15B in annual GMV processed, the company positions itself as the infrastructure that lets non-Amazon brands match Prime-grade delivery experiences.
company.name: Stord

company.description: Unified interface for large-language-model (LLM) inference, providing a single API to access hundreds of LLMs from multiple providers with features like automatic failover, unified billing, privacy controls, and edge-deployed infrastructure.
company.name: OpenRouter

company.description: Voxmind builds physics-based voice biometrics and deepfake detection technology, using a patent-pending phoneme-frequency extraction engine that analyses vocal-tract biomechanics to authenticate speakers across all languages. The London startup runs on-device with no GPU or cloud connectivity required, targeting banks, telecoms, contact centres, and hardware OEMs left exposed as Microsoft and AWS exit voice biometrics.
company.name: Voxmind

company.description:
Get the full benefits of cloud computing with a comprehensive multiphysics simulation solution in your browser | Fast, scalable, and flexible multiphysics simulation software

Get the full benefits of cloud computing with a comprehensive multiphysics simulation solution in your browser

Read more and schedule a free introductory session now at quanscient.com
company.name: Quanscient

company.description: RedGirasol is a Monterrey-based Mexican fintech and CNBV-regulated collective financing institution (IFC) operating under Mexico's Ley Fintech. For more than a decade it has connected retail and institutional investors with loans for solar-energy and other sustainable, productive projects, offering products such as Inversión Evergreen plus a secondary market that gives investors added liquidity. It positions itself as one of Mexico's leading impact-investing and green-lending platforms.
company.name: RedGirasol

company.description: Trinio is a Brazilian retail-technology startup offering a commerce orchestration suite powered by AI agents, helping retailers run omnichannel operations on a unified platform.
company.name: Trinio

company.description: Luffa is a Hong Kong-based Web3 and AI platform that bundles decentralized identity (DID), AI agents, a native multi-chain wallet, encrypted messaging, and social channels into a single "super connector," alongside a SuperBox open mini-app ecosystem and LuffaPay intent-based payments. Its products serve use cases spanning prediction markets, AI mini-games, real-world assets (RWA), the creator economy, and on-chain community governance. As of early 2026 the ecosystem reported more than 3 million global downloads, 2 million registered users, and over 150,000 daily active users.
company.name: Luffa

company.description: VendorSide is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) procure-to-pay platform that digitizes corporate procurement and supply chain management through artificial intelligence and data analytics tools. The platform provides software solutions designed for automation, predictive analytics, and cost optimization within organizational purchasing workflows. Headquartered as a technology startup, the company develops digital systems to manage corporate supply chain efficiency and tracking, while expanding its infrastructure for large-scale enterprise integration.
company.name: Vendorside

company.description: Link-Touch (BluePoint Touch / 蓝点触控) is a Beijing-headquartered Chinese developer of robotic force sensors, with a fully automated production base of more than 10,000 square metres in Guangdong. Its portfolio spans six-axis force sensors for humanoid robots, joint and general-purpose force sensors, integrated force-control solutions and composite robotic workstations, built on four in-house technology platforms covering elastomer design, embedded electronics, structural decoupling and six-axis calibration. The company is the dominant supplier of six-axis force sensors to Chinese humanoid-robot makers and counts Agibot, Xiaomi, Xpeng, Galbot, EngineAI, UBTech and Galaxea AI as customers.
company.name: Link-Touch

company.description: HeyGears is a Guangzhou-based 3D printing company specialising in high-end light-curing (stereolithography) technology with a full-stack approach to equipment, materials and software. It dominates Chinese dental 3D printing — overseas revenue accounts for over 60% of the dental business — and is extending its UltraCraft and Reflex product lines into advanced manufacturing, embodied intelligence and consumer-grade desktop printing.
company.name: HeyGears

company.description: Avrea is a Helsinki-based developer-tools startup building an AI platform that helps engineering teams test and ship software faster. Its product plugs into existing deployment pipelines and uses AI agents to surface bottlenecks like low-quality tests, stuck builds, and resource constraints. Founded in 2025 by Aiven co-founder Hannu Valtonen and Nosto co-founder Juha Valvanne, the roughly 10-person team draws from Spotify and Hoxhunt and positions itself against GitHub and GitLab as AI-generated code reshapes software delivery.
company.name: Avrea

company.description: Handle.com is a San Francisco-based software platform purpose-built for construction finance, automating payments, lien rights, waivers, compliance and invoicing for material suppliers, equipment dealers and contractors. Founded in 2018, the company now processes more than $160 billion in construction invoices for customers including Ferguson, The Home Depot, Cemex, ABC Supply and EquipmentShare.
company.name: Handle.com

company.description: allO is a Munich-based AI-native operating system for restaurants that bundles point-of-sale, payments, reservations, kiosks, delivery integrations and back-office tools into a single platform. Founded in 2020, the company serves over 1,000 active restaurant locations across Germany and is rolling out "digital employee" AI agents — starting with a voice agent for incoming calls, bookings and takeaway orders — aimed at multi-language, complex-modifier menus that legacy systems handle poorly.
company.name: allO

company.description: Polsia is an AI-native company-building platform from San Francisco that uses autonomous agents to plan, code, market, and operate businesses end-to-end. Founded by Ben Broca (also known as Ben Cera), the company runs without hired employees and reports approaching $10M ARR, positioning itself as a proof point for solo-founder, agent-operated startups.
company.name: Polsia

company.description: Backoffice is a Vilnius-based SaaS company building a unified operations management platform for hospitality businesses — restaurants, cafes, bars, and hotels. The product covers shift scheduling, payroll tracking, time logging, internal communication, document e-signing, and live labour-cost reporting, replacing the patchwork of spreadsheets and messaging apps most hospitality teams stitch together today. It is already deployed across 40+ Lithuanian venues, from Michelin-starred restaurants to late-night bars, serving around 500 users.
company.name: Backoffice

company.description: Bengaluru-based quantum security venture founded in 2023 by Richa and Vinay Hukumchand, building hybrid satellite and terrestrial systems for quantum-secure communications using Quantum Key Distribution (QKD). Develops a proprietary photonics integrated circuit (PIC) chip validated at IIT Madras and plans an in-orbit demonstration mission in 2027. Offers a QKD-as-a-service model targeting data centres, enterprise networks and space infrastructure.
company.name: Pramatra Space

company.description: Cyient Semiconductors is a Hyderabad-based fabless power semiconductor company and arm of Cyient Ltd, designing custom ASICs and analog mixed-signal silicon for data centers, robotics, automotive and industrial automation. With design centers in India, Belgium and the US, the company combines in-house IP with the recently acquired Kinetic Technologies portfolio to target power-efficiency constraints in AI infrastructure.
company.name: Cyient Semiconductors

company.description: Cybergenix Security is a Greater Noida-based AI and cybersecurity startup founded by Galgotias University students Divyansh Kumar Mishra and Prakhar Kumar Singh. The company is building a context-driven AI infrastructure that combines large language models, self-learning engines, automation frameworks, cybersecurity systems, and digital twin technology. Its product roadmap includes a system-level AI assistant with voice and contextual interaction and cybersecurity-first deployment infrastructure for adaptive, behaviour-aware AI.
company.name: Cybergenix Security

company.description: Nexco.ai is a London-based AI startup building agentic conversational co-workers for the executive search industry. Its proprietary AI agents capture compensation data, motivations, references and contract details from candidate conversations and write the resulting intelligence directly into search firms' CRMs. Founded in 2024 by Charlotte Gandell and Gareth Cokell, the company augments rather than replaces consultants, freeing up to 40 hours per search for advisory work.
company.name: Nexco.ai

company.description: abcoffee is a Mumbai-based grab-and-go coffee chain operating more than 90 compact takeaway outlets across Mumbai, Delhi-NCR and Bengaluru. The startup focuses on fast, technology-led daily coffee consumption with app-based ordering and subscription plans, and has extended its lineup with matcha and protein-coffee drinks under its Procaff brand.
company.name: abcoffee

company.description: Tequipy is a Polish startup founded by former Revolut employees that provides an end-to-end IT equipment management platform tailored for remote-first companies. It streamlines the entire lifecycle of IT assets including procurement, configuration, delivery, maintenance, and secure recovery, supporting over 100 clients globally with fast device deployment and a pay-as-you-go pricing model.
company.name: Tequipy

company.description: Yes Madam is a Gurugram-based at-home beauty and wellness platform that brings salon and spa services to customers across 55+ Indian cities. Founded in 2016, the company connects more than 12,000 women service professionals to households and fulfils roughly 300,000 bookings a month while remaining bootstrapped and profitable until its 2026 Series A.
company.name: Yes Madam

company.description: StrainX Bioworks is a Bhopal-headquartered Indian biotech startup using synthetic biology and precision fermentation to manufacture alternative proteins at scale. Founded in 2023 by IIT Delhi alumni Akshay Mittal and Alok Malaviya, the company engineers microbes to produce ingredients for food, nutrition, materials and cosmetics, anchored by India-based bio-manufacturing capacity. It has secured US commercialisation approval and operates a 10,000-litre fermentation facility in Bhopal with an R&D lab in Bengaluru.
company.name: StrainX

company.description: Copenhagen-based AgTech company building AI camera sensors that retrofit onto tractors and sprayers to identify weeds plant-by-plant and apply herbicide only where needed. The system claims up to 90% herbicide reduction and 30% fertilizer savings for typical farms, and has mapped more than 200,000 hectares across Europe. PerPlant has 15 employees and operates in 12 countries including Denmark, the UK, Netherlands, Poland and Spain.
company.name: Perplant

company.description: RESONIKS is a Dutch-Finnish deeptech company (founded 2022, formerly SUPPLYZ) that builds AI-powered acoustic resonance testing for non-destructive quality control in manufacturing. Its systems strike a metal part, capture the resulting vibrations with a microphone, and use AI to detect internal defects such as cracks and air pockets that conventional methods miss. The company targets automotive and mechanical-engineering manufacturers, operates from The Hague, Tampere and Munich, and won the VDMA Startup Award 2025.
company.name: RESONIKS

company.description: TIST Media is a Mumbai-based creator economy platform that connects brands and creators through structured partnerships, payments and campaign management tools. Founded by Tarun Nathani, the company pivoted from fashion and beauty production toward broader workflow infrastructure aimed at standardising creator-brand collaborations across India and international markets.
company.name: TIST Media

company.description: Robbin is a Brazilian fintech building AI-native trade credit and payment infrastructure for retail supply chains. The platform lets manufacturers issue co-branded virtual credit cards to their reseller networks, financing instalment B2B purchases on Brazil's Pix instant-payment rails while Robbin assumes the credit risk. Founded in 2023 by ex-Itaú BBA, XP and OpenCo operators, it pairs ERP, open finance and credit-bureau signals with AI agents (notably Robbinson, a WhatsApp-based credit approver) to underwrite SME retailers in real time.
company.name: Robbin

company.description: PlantSwitch makes compostable food-service products — cups, cutlery, bowls — engineered to feel and function like petroleum plastic but break down naturally within about ten weeks. The Sanford, North Carolina company converts agricultural waste such as rice hulls and wheat straw into a drop-in bioplastic resin sold to 100+ customers including Taylor Farms, Starlink, Live Nation, and SoFi Stadium.
company.name: PlantSwitch

company.description: Lume Music operates Lume, a New Zealand-built digital music platform that sells full albums as a one-off purchase rather than offering subscription streaming. Each release bundles the full tracklist alongside artist-curated extras such as demos, live recordings, lyrics, behind-the-scenes footage and photography, with artists and rightsholders keeping roughly 80% of net revenue. The company plans a New Zealand-only launch in June 2026 with a curated slate of local artists before expanding to Australia and other markets.
company.name: Lume Music

company.description: FACTORY X is an Aichi-based Japanese manufacturing-tech startup developing an "Inventory Strategy Model" that helps factories decide how much stock to hold strategically rather than reflexively minimizing it. Its software structures production, financial, and risk data to calculate the inventory level that maximizes value within a given budget, giving executives and shop floors a shared logic for inventory decisions. Early proofs of concept are underway with major Japanese automotive parts manufacturers, with some advancing toward full deployment.
company.name: FACTORY X

company.description: Squid is a Zug-based cross-chain platform that connects blockchain networks, liquidity sources and applications to move digital assets across crypto in a single transaction. Its stack spans the Squid Intents settlement protocol, a routing and aggregation engine, developer tools (SDK, API, widgets) and consumer-facing apps reaching 100+ networks including Ethereum, Bitcoin, Solana, Cosmos and the XRP Ledger. Since its January 2023 launch it has routed over $6B in volume across 4M+ transactions for more than 1M users, and it acts as the official bridge partner for the XRP Ledger.
company.name: Squid

company.description: Retro Biosciences is a San Francisco-based longevity biotech aiming to add ten healthy years to human lifespan by targeting the underlying drivers of aging. Its programs span small-molecule autophagy activators, autologous iPSC-derived hematopoietic cell therapy, partial cellular reprogramming, and AI-enabled protein engineering. The company is operating its lead candidate RTR242 in a first-in-human Phase I trial and runs in-house cGMP cell therapy manufacturing.
company.name: Retro Biosciences

company.description: Nuvolog is an Istanbul-based logistics SaaS founded in 2024 by Abdullah Cansu and Serhat Levent Yavaş. Its Transportation Management System (TMS) digitizes shipping operations, carrier management, route planning, cost tracking and supply chain workflows on a single modular platform, serving customers such as Trendyol, Norm Holding, Yiğit Akü, Teknorot and Standard Profil. The company is a 2025 Turkish Airlines Terminal accelerator graduate and reports 4x year-over-year growth.
company.name: Nuvolog

company.description: Ordermentum is a Sydney-based wholesale ordering and payments platform connecting Australian hospitality venues with food and beverage suppliers. The platform integrates ordering, payments and reconciliation in a single system, and has layered AI tools for demand forecasting, automated reordering and intelligent product recommendations. It merged with Melbourne marketplace Foodbomb in 2023 and supports tens of thousands of pubs, restaurants and cafes nationally.
company.name: Ordermentum

company.description: Austrian cleantech startup developing the Road Energy Production System (REPS), a patented mechanical converter embedded in roads that harvests kinetic energy from vehicle traffic and turns it into clean electricity for ports and other infrastructure.
company.name: REPS

company.description: Benefitbay Inc. is a health tech startup specializing in end-to-end Individual Coverage Health Reimbursement Arrangement (ICHRA) administration, enabling employers to model, deploy, and oversee ICHRA plans with compliance tools and employee support, focusing on flexible healthcare solutions.
company.name: Benefitbay Inc.

company.description: Kordata Dynamics is a Bakersfield, California-based clinical trial innovation company spun out of UK neural engineering firm BIOS Health. It develops an AI platform for precision clinical trials that integrates BIOS NeuroTune dosing technology with real-time neural data, targeting neurological diseases such as Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, epilepsy and other chronic conditions. The startup operates a decentralized trial model designed to extend research infrastructure beyond elite academic centers into regional medical markets.
company.name: Kordata Dynamics

company.description: Gabit is an Indian wearable healthtech and wellness platform founded in 2022 by former Zomato co-founder Gaurav Gupta and former SkillTap founder Arpana Shahi. Its flagship titanium smart ring tracks 150+ health markers across sleep, recovery, activity, stress and nutrition, paired with an AI coaching app and a vertically integrated nutrition and skincare product line. The company acquired Sweden-based nutrition brand Näck in December 2025 to deepen its supplements play.
company.name: Gabit

company.description: Issuer and operator of the Japanese yen-pegged stablecoin JPYC, supporting Avalanche, Ethereum, and Polygon for financial and Web3 ecosystems.
company.name: JPYC Inc.

company.description: Trajektory is a Chicago-based sports sponsorship analytics platform that consolidates fragmented measurement data into a single intelligence system used by brands, agencies, teams, and leagues. The company supports organizations representing more than $180B in annual sponsorship value and counts more than two-thirds of the NHL among its clients. Founded in 2019, it focuses on currency-grade valuations and reporting to help rights-holders and sponsors quantify the impact of their deals.
company.name: Trajektory

company.description: HiDream.ai (智象未来) is a Hefei-based generative AI startup founded in 2023 by former JD.com VP Tao Mei, focused on native multimodal foundation models for image, video, text, and audio generation. The company released HiDream-O1-Image-Pro, a 200B+ parameter image model on its Unified Transformer (UiT) architecture, and operates a Model+Agent stack with three commercial products (HiBurst marketing, ZanZan film creation, vivago social).
company.name: HiDream.ai

company.description: Roboxi is a Stavanger-based Norwegian robotics company building autonomous systems for airport airside operations. Its multifunctional robotic platform handles foreign object debris detection and collection, runway and taxiway surface inspection via high-speed 3D laser scanning, photometric inspection of airfield lighting, and wildlife/bird deterrence. Developed in cooperation with Avinor and Norwegian aviation authorities, the technology is deployed or piloted at airports including Schiphol, Frankfurt, Florence, Dallas and Stavanger.
company.name: Roboxi

company.description: Harmoney is a Ghent, Belgium-based software company providing a modular counterparty risk management platform for banks, insurers, investment funds, wealth managers, and other regulated enterprises. Its software orchestrates KYC, AML, MiFID, PEP, UBO, ESG, DORA, and third-party risk workflows across the full counterparty lifecycle.
