Founded in 2016, Harmoney serves more than 70 financial institutions across seven countries, including Belfius, Baloise, and Ayvens.
company.name: Harmoney

company.description: Memed is a Brazilian healthtech that runs the country's leading digital medical prescription platform, used by roughly 150,000 doctors a month and generating around 100 million e-prescriptions a year. Beyond prescriptions, it is building AI-driven clinical tools — drug-interaction and dosage alerts, patient guidance, and integrations with electronic health records and hospital systems — to become core infrastructure for digitizing Brazilian healthcare. The company reached breakeven in mid-2025, largely by helping the pharmaceutical industry communicate with physicians, and holds about a 60% share among doctors who prescribe digitally.
company.name: Memed

company.description: Secai Marche is a Tokyo-headquartered company founded in 2018 that operates a B2B farm-direct fresh-food platform across Japan, Malaysia, and Singapore, connecting farmers and producers directly with restaurants, hotels, cafes, and retailers. Its technology combines demand forecasting, order and warehouse management, and cold-chain logistics to streamline the Southeast Asian fresh-produce supply chain. The company is now extending the platform into embedded finance, adding integrated payments, invoicing, and settlement for its buyers and suppliers.
company.name: Secai Marche

company.description: Giva is a Bengaluru-based omnichannel jewellery brand founded in 2019 by Ishendra Agarwal, known for affordable silver pieces and now expanded into gold and lab-grown diamonds. The company runs about 210 stores across 25 Indian cities alongside its website and app, and reported INR 518 crore in FY25 operating revenue, up 89% year-on-year.
company.name: Giva

company.description: siliXon is a UK startup building generative AI that turns natural-language prompts into complete printed circuit board designs — schematics, component selection, layout, BOM and Gerber files. Founded by University of Cambridge engineers Mihai Mesteru and Ngoc Bach Nguyen, it pitches text-to-PCB as a way to compress hardware iteration cycles and help Europe localise electronics manufacturing.
company.name: siliXon

company.description: Dealinka is a French circular-economy platform that helps companies redistribute non-food unsold inventory and dormant stock to a network of nonprofits rather than destroying it. Its algorithm matches corporate supply with vetted association needs and handles the full logistics, traceability and tax-deduction paperwork. Founded in 2023 in Valenciennes by Alexis Raspilair and Ramil Alvarez, the company serves over 1,200 partner associations and reports more than 800 completed operations.
company.name: Dealinka

company.description: FundCanna is a San Diego-based specialty finance platform that provides working capital, equipment, vendor, dispensary, and inventory loans to cannabis operators across the United States. Founded by Adam Stettner, the company underwrites the full cannabis supply chain — multi-state operators, manufacturers, distributors, and retailers — and also runs ReadyPaid, a buy-now-pay-later product aimed at the multibillion-dollar pool of delinquent receivables in cannabis wholesale. It has originated more than 5,000 transactions and deployed over $250M since launch.
company.name: FundCanna

company.description: Iceye is a Finnish spacetech company specializing in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite technology for Earth observation. It operates the world's largest SAR satellite constellation, providing high-resolution radar imagery and data for sectors such as defense, insurance, disaster response, maritime monitoring, and finance. The company has a global presence with over 700 employees and offices in Europe, the US, Asia, and the Middle East.
company.name: Iceye

company.description: Palenca is a Mexico-based income and employment verification platform for financial institutions, surfacing real income data — including from informal work, gig, and multi-job earners — so banks and fintechs can extend credit to historically underserved borrowers across Latin America.
company.name: Palenca

company.description: LionsBot International is a Singapore-based robotics company building autonomous floor-cleaning robots — the R3, R12 and newer R5 — for commercial environments such as airports, malls and large facilities. Its robots are deployed through facility-services partners including Germany-based WISAG and Austria-based Reiwag, and via distribution partners in North America. The company has raised more than $60M to date and operates a Singapore manufacturing facility.
company.name: LionsBot International

company.description: Israeli food-tech (formerly Plantish) producing 3D-printed whole-cut plant-based seafood from soy and mycoprotein. Salmon is in distribution across 100+ US restaurants and major retailers via KeHE, UNFI and Sysco, and the company has added a whitefish fillet to its lineup. Its plug-and-play manufacturing platform has cut production costs roughly 80%, and Oshi is preparing a 686-door US retail rollout and expansion into Latin America.
company.name: Oshi

company.description: Core42 is a UAE-based subsidiary of G42 that builds sovereign cloud and AI infrastructure for enterprise, government and hyperscale customers. It operates large-scale data centres and the G42 Intelligence Grid, with a European base in Dublin and active expansion across the US, Italy and France.
company.name: Core42

company.description: Sally is a Tokyo-based experiential entertainment startup built around murder-mystery games. It runs the UZU voice-chat app and madamis.jp portal, operates the UZU TOKYO and MyMy permanent theaters via Uzu Production, and develops original IP with major Japanese publishers and commercial-property partners. It opened a Korean branch in 2025 to extend the platform across Asia.
company.name: Sally

company.description: Starlab Space is a U.S.-led joint venture developing a next-generation, AI-enabled commercial space station intended to succeed the International Space Station in low-Earth orbit. The venture is led by Voyager Technologies (NYSE: VOYG) with Airbus, Mitsubishi Corporation, MDA Space, Palantir, and Space Applications Services as JV partners, targeting microgravity research, government, and commercial customers post-ISS.
company.name: Starlab Space

company.description: Fresha is a London-based AI-powered marketplace and business management platform for the beauty and wellness industry, used by more than 130,000 salons, spas, and self-care businesses across the UK, Australasia, the GCC, North America, Europe, and South East Asia. The platform combines appointment booking, payments, and embedded financial-services tools, processing over 35 million bookings per month and $15B+ in annual GMV. Founded in 2015 by William Zeqiri and Nicholas Miller, Fresha is profitable and has raised $285M to date.
company.name: Fresha

company.description: Patina is a fragrance technology startup applying molecular design and machine learning to engineer new scent molecules. The company is building a foundation model called Sense1 that simulates olfactory receptors, aiming to give perfumers and flavorists a universal palette of primary scent ingredients. Founded in 2024 by artist-perfumer Sean Raspet and engineer Laura Sisson, it works with fragrance houses and fashion brands on custom scents and synthetic replacements for scarce natural ingredients.
company.name: Patina

company.description: Jia is a fintech company building a financial operating system for small businesses in emerging markets, with primary operations in the Philippines. The platform combines working capital (invoice financing advanced in under 24 hours via its proprietary Ossicone AI credit model), a no-fee interest-bearing business account, and cash-flow tools. Jia connects local SMEs to global capital through on-chain lending pools on Base, Stellar, Arbitrum, Celo, and Polygon, and enables stablecoin-based cross-border payments.
company.name: Jia

company.description: K25.ai is an APAC-focused AI-powered watch-to-predict platform that blends live-streaming content, creator communities, and AI-assisted event generation into consumer-facing prediction markets across permitted Asia-Pacific markets (Thailand, Singapore, Japan and others, excluding mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau and the US). Its AI layer supports market generation, live-content understanding, outcome monitoring and resolution workflows around sports, esports, entertainment and creator events. The company is led by founder Andy Cheung, former COO of OKX and former CEO of Groupon Hong Kong.
company.name: K25.ai

company.description: Aboard is a Southern California-based startup applying automotive-grade engineering to the travel trailer category. Its first product is an extended-range electric (EREV) travel trailer that integrates energy independence, vehicle-level towing, automated comfort systems, and high-precision body manufacturing into a single platform, with configurations starting at $80,000. Founded by Jiangtao Lyu, the company is preparing for mass production and a formal public launch at Outside Days in Denver.
company.name: Aboard

company.description: Vetir operates an AI-powered luxury wardrobe operating system that helps high-value consumers shop, get dressed, and manage their wardrobes. The platform combines a digital closet, image and video search, photorealistic try-on, and a CRM-like clienteling layer for private stylists and luxury brands. Founded by Kate Davidson Hudson, it serves both consumers via iOS and web, and enterprise partners across luxury fashion and retail.
company.name: Vêtir

company.description: MOTO Pizza is a Seattle-based Detroit-style pizza chain founded in 2021 by Lee Kindell and Nancy Gambin, known for blending Filipino-influenced toppings with in-store robotic assembly. The company operates locations across West Seattle, Belltown, Edmonds, Pioneer Square and at T-Mobile Park, and is now spinning out a dedicated robotics venture, Motobotics, to build pizza-making robots in-house.
company.name: MOTO Pizza

company.description: The Path is an AI therapy platform—formerly branded Mental—offering personalized, 24/7 mental wellness coaching across iOS and Android. Built on therapy-specific foundational models, it provides users with AI therapists, customized homework, and crisis-handoff safety features designed to bridge the gap between everyday emotional support and clinical care.
company.name: The Path

company.description: CVRD Health (pronounced 'covered') is a Miami-based benefits compliance platform built specifically for federal government contractors. The product gives contractors real-time visibility into Service Contract Act and Davis-Bacon fringe-benefit obligations, pairs ICHRA-based individualized coverage with dedicated member advocates, and helps recover fringe dollars that would otherwise erode contractor margins.
company.name: CVRD Health

company.description: Muybridge is an Oslo-based imaging technology company building a software-defined camera platform for live broadcast and virtual production. Its system replaces traditional broadcast hardware with distributed 4K sensor arrays and real-time GPU compute that reconstruct a volumetric view of the scene, letting operators generate and move between virtual camera angles on the fly. The platform targets sports and live-event broadcasters seeking spatial, replayable footage instead of fixed single-lens coverage.
company.name: Muybridge

company.description: Clouted is a Los Angeles-based marketing technology startup building a Distribution Intelligence platform for consumer and entertainment brands. Its AI agents ingest a brand's strategy and content library to plan and execute viral campaigns across clipping, UGC, fan pages, influencer seeding, and performance ads, learning from results to compound effectiveness over time.
company.name: Clouted

company.description: IrisGo builds an on-device AI desktop companion for AI PCs that watches a user work, learns repetitive workflows in a single demonstration, then re-executes them autonomously. The system blends local NPU processing for privacy with optional cloud calls for heavier tasks, and ships skills for email drafting, document summarization, invoice processing and developer assistance. The company has lined up Acer as its first OEM pre-install partner and targets knowledge workers as its primary audience.
company.name: IrisGo

company.description:
Deploy generative AI models, large-scale batch jobs, job queues, and more on Modal's platform. We help data science and machine learning teams accelerate development, reduce costs, and effortlessly scale workloads across thousands of CPUs and GPUs.

Our pay-per-use model ensures you're billed only for actual compute time, down to the CPU cycle. No more wasted resources or idle costs—just efficient, scalable computing power when you need it.
company.name: Modal

company.description: August Robotics is a Hong Kong–headquartered robotics automation company building modular autonomous robot fleets for construction and large-scale industrial workflows. Its drilling robots, deployed in partnership with Stanley Black & Decker's DEWALT brand, compress schedules on hyperscale AI data center construction sites in the US and Europe, while its earlier Lionel platform automates floor-marking for exhibitions and large indoor venues at scale. Founded in 2017 by Alex Wyatt, the company operates seven hubs across the US, Asia, Australia and Europe.
company.name: August Robotics

company.description: Tokyo-based startup providing visualization, management, and optimization of corporate computing resources for AI workloads.
company.name: Morgenrot

company.description: Develops and provides conversational voice AI SaaS 'IVRy' that automates phone responses 24/7, analyzes call data, and improves operational efficiency and customer experience.
company.name: IVRy, Inc.

company.description: Cycles is a Toronto-based crypto infrastructure startup building a privacy-preserving multilateral clearing network for on-chain finance. Spun out of Informal Systems and led by Cosmos co-founder Ethan Buchman, the company uses zero-knowledge proofs and trusted execution environments to net OTC obligations and stablecoin payments without moving collateral. Its first products are Cycles Prime for institutional trading firms and Cycles Pay for individuals and businesses.
company.name: Cycles

company.description: Synakis is a Toronto-based biotech startup developing biomaterial-based therapies for ocular diseases including retinal detachment and glaucoma, originating from research at the University of Toronto.
company.name: Synakis

company.description: Shatterdome Energy is a San Francisco-based AI-driven energy trading and intelligence startup, emerging from stealth in May 2026 to build an optimization platform for battery storage assets in wholesale power markets.
company.name: Shatterdome Energy

company.description: Doozy Robotics is a Singapore-based Physical AI company building an autonomous industrial workforce that combines an Industrial Super Humanoid with autonomous mobile robots and forklifts, coordinated by its proprietary Eywa-OS orchestration layer. The startup sells its stack via a Robot-as-a-Service subscription model and has live deployments across Asia and the US with customers including Daimler, Carrier and VitaQuest.
company.name: Doozy Robotics

company.description: Efferon is an Austrian biotech company that designs multimodal hemoadsorption cartridges used to strip endotoxins and inflammatory cytokines from the bloodstream, primarily as an ICU therapy for sepsis and septic shock. Its product line spans Efferon LPS for adult patients and Efferon NEO for pediatric and neonatal use, with the technology approved and deployed across more than 40 countries. Manufacturing sits in Estonia and Latvia, with commercial offices in Austria and the United States.
company.name: Efferon

company.description: ClearOps is a Munich-based enterprise SaaS company building an AI-powered after-sales operating system for industrial OEMs, dealers, and service partners. The platform aggregates data across service networks to enable predictive parts planning, real-time service coordination, and demand forecasting for customers such as AGCO, Terex, Jungheinrich, and Lippert.
company.name: ClearOps

company.description: Sparq is an AI-native game engine and creator platform headquartered at Ras Al Khaimah's Innovation City free zone. Its proprietary C++ engine handles coding, asset generation, networking, publishing, and monetization while creators keep full control over gameplay and game design, aimed at opening real game development to the 250 million-strong content-creator economy. Co-founded by CEO Christopher Pail and COO Christoffer Wilhelmsen, the company spent two years and roughly $2.5M of founder capital building the engine before opening to outside investors.
company.name: Sparq

company.description: Stride is a Vietnam-based climate-tech platform offering rooftop solar financing to households and small businesses, letting customers pay for solar installations through flexible monthly plans instead of large upfront costs. The company also runs a network of vetted installers and digital project-management tools to maintain quality across decentralised projects across Southeast Asia.
company.name: Stride

company.description: Hark is an AI lab founded by Figure AI CEO Brett Adcock, building a personal intelligence platform and bespoke hardware that aims to serve as a universal interface to AI assistants. The company is developing multimodal models that integrate with existing services, and operates an Nvidia B200 GPU data center to train them.
company.name: Hark

company.description: Scapia is a Bengaluru-based travel fintech that issues a co-branded rewards credit card in partnership with Federal Bank and operates a travel booking app for hotels, flights, buses and trains. The platform also offers AI-powered visa services and curated travel experiences and launched commercially in mid‑2023.
company.name: Scapia

Founded in 2023, Pivot operates in more than 25 countries with enterprise customers including DoorDash, Lemonade, and Flix, and reportedly handles roughly €2.5 billion in invoices each year.
company.name: Pivot

company.description: Imperagen is a Manchester-based techbio company that engineers enzymes using a closed-loop platform combining quantum physics simulations, problem-specific AI models, and highly automated wet-lab robotics. company.name: Mozart

company.description: Providing automated, regulated, and affordable financial advice to mass-market users underserved by traditional wealth managers.

company.description: NanoCo is the Israeli startup behind NanoClaw, an open-source platform for running AI agents securely. company.name: NanoClaw

company.description: Contrario is a San Francisco-based AI-native recruiting platform that pairs expert recruiters with AI agents to handle sourcing, scheduling, and follow-ups for high-growth companies. company.name: Contrario

company.description: AI risk platform for fraud detection, compliance, and credit underwriting using device intelligence, behavior biometrics, and machine learning.

company.description: Sağlık sektöründe yapay zeka dönüşümünü hedefleyen SaaS girişimi; tıbbi görüntüleme alanında yapay zeka destekli çözümler sunar, dijital patoloji, CT, MR, ultrason ve X-ray analizleri yapar.

company.name: Draiven

company.description: Finance operations platform that consolidates bookkeeping, payroll, FP&A, reporting, and compliance into a single system anchored by a native general ledger and AI agents. company.description: Country Delight is an Indian tech-enabled fresh food delivery service focused on quality and timely delivery.

company.description:
At Commure, our mission is to simplify healthcare. We have bold ambitions to reimagine the healthcare experience, setting a new standard for how care is delivered and experienced across the industry. Our growing suite of AI solutions spans ambient AI clinical documentation, provider copilots, autonomous coding, revenue cycle management and more — all designed for providers & administrators to focus on what matters most: providing care.

Healthcare is a $4.5 trillion industry with more than $500 billion spent annually on administrative costs, and Commure is at the heart of transforming it. We power over 500,000 clinicians across hundreds of care sites nationwide – more than $10 billion flows through our systems and we support over 100 million patient interactions. With new product launches on the horizon, expansion into additional care segments, and a bold vision to tackle healthcare's most pressing challenges, our ambition is to move from upstart innovator to the industry standard over the next few years.

Commure was recently named to Fortune’s Future 50 list for 2025 and is backed by world-class investors including General Catalyst, Sequoia, Y Combinator, Lux, Human Capital, 8VC, Greenoaks Capital, Elad Gil, and more. Commure has achieved over 300% year-over-year growth for the past two years and this is only the beginning. Healthcare's moment for AI-powered transformation is here, and we're building the technology to power it. Come join us in shaping the future of healthcare.
company.name: Commure

company.description: Mediads is a Paris-based adtech startup operating in the social publishing space, letting brands amplify their existing Meta, TikTok and LinkedIn social campaigns through a network of more than 250 premium publishers such as Euronews, Brut, Le Figaro and Ziff Davis. Advertisers keep their creative, targeting and tracking, while distribution shifts to trusted media partners to lift conversion through editorial authority. Founded in 2022 by Alfred Cardinal and Stephane Labrouche, the company has been bootstrapped and profitable, claiming 100% annual growth and 500+ advertisers served.
company.name: Mediads

company.description: NEX Health Intelligence is an Imperial College London spinout building an AI platform that detects and predicts the spread of antimicrobial-resistant infections inside hospitals. Its software is already deployed across NHS trusts and an overseas military hospital, covering more than 40,000 patient admissions with measurable drops in surveillance testing and case-review time. Co-founded by Imperial PhD Dr Ashleigh Myall and Oxford clinician Dr Chang Ho Yoon.
company.name: NEX Health Intelligence

company.description: Voker is an agent analytics platform that helps AI product teams monitor and improve conversational AI agents in production. Its SDK transforms agent interactions into structured dashboards covering usage, intents, knowledge gaps and quality, so product managers, designers and executives — not just engineers — can measure how agents perform with real users. Backed by Y Combinator (S24) and FundersClub, with customers including Dutch.com and Lightfield.
company.name: Voker

company.description: ONO is a Bengaluru-based agritech startup, founded in 2021 by Rama Rao Kancharapu and David Pokuri, that runs a data and AI-powered platform for post-harvest agricultural supply chains and Agri-SME lending in India. The company provides market intelligence, pricing data, logistics support, and formal credit access to farmers, traders, transporters, and buyers across 12 states and over 125 districts. ONO has acquired a significant stake in an NBFC to deepen its direct lending operations.
company.name: ONO (Agritech)

company.description: Feldwerke is a Munich-based cleantech company developing and installing open-field agri-photovoltaic systems that pair single-axis solar trackers with active farming on the same parcels. Founded in 2023 by Nicolai Reiners, Nils Kuchenbuch and Marco Mielenz, the firm has installed more than 20 MW to date — including the largest agri-PV plant in southern Germany at Oberndorf am Lech — and is developing a pipeline of roughly 50 projects totaling over 400 MW with an ambition of 1 GW by 2030.
company.name: Feldwerke

company.description: GEEIQ is a London-based virtual worlds intelligence platform that gives brands cross-platform analytics across Roblox, Fortnite, ZEPETO and other virtual worlds. Its software combines proprietary cross-platform data with category expertise so brands such as Walmart, L'Oréal, Gucci, NASCAR and Warner Bros. Discovery can benchmark activations and measure performance consistently. Customers pay via subscription with managed services embedded, positioning GEEIQ as the independent measurement layer for brand investment in virtual worlds.
company.name: GEEIQ

company.description: Aryze is a Copenhagen-founded fintech providing stablecoin infrastructure as a managed service to licensed issuers of stablecoins and tokenised real-world assets. Its single-stack platform combines issuance, wallet, ledger, custody, and bank-to-bank fiat on- and off-ramps so issuers no longer need to stitch components together from separate vendors. Builders deploy products under their own or a partner licence via a self-serve layer, while banks and regulated institutions consume the same stack as a managed service.
company.name: Aryze

company.description: Cosmon builds an AI toolchain for advanced engineering — agents that assist mechanical, electrical, thermal, and chemical engineers across CAD, CAE, and PLM workflows. The platform aims to automate repetitive design-to-production tasks, accelerate simulation and analysis, and let small teams ship hardware faster. Founded in 2025 by former Expedock CTO Rui Aguiar, the company markets itself as a Cursor-style copilot for hardware engineering.
company.name: Cosmon

company.description: CircuitHub operates automated, software-driven factories for on-demand electronics manufacturing. Its Grid platform uses robotics, computer vision and AI to turn uploaded design files into printed circuit boards in days, supporting batch sizes from a single prototype to 10,000 units. The company targets high-mix production for hardware teams building robotics, satellites and autonomous systems.
company.name: CircuitHub

company.description: GymNation is the GCC's largest low-cost gym operator, founded in Dubai in 2018 by Loren Holland, Frank Afeaki, and Ant Martland. The company runs close to 50 locations across the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain, serving more than 200,000 members with affordable mass-market fitness access. It operates a proprietary technology stack covering pricing, operations, and AI-driven member experience.
company.name: GymNation

company.description: Blank Bio is an applied AI research lab developing RNA foundation models that learn from tumor transcriptomes to improve patient-level prediction in oncology. The company is a YC S25 graduate and collaborates with pharmaceutical and diagnostic partners on biomarker discovery, clinical trial design, and long-read RNA-seq applications.
company.name: Blank Bio

company.description: B-SIDES is a New York-based snack startup making crunchy puffs from upcycled ingredients—oat flour from oat milk production and corn from grits milling. Founded in 2024 by former Goldman Sachs private wealth advisor Yousuf Ahmed, the products are free from the top nine allergens and offer complete plant-based protein. The brand is expanding from direct-to-consumer into retail with distribution targeting 1,500+ doors across the Northeast.
company.name: B-Sides

company.description: Hellbender is a Pittsburgh-based physical AI infrastructure company building integrated edge perception platforms — combining proprietary hardware with edge-native AI to deliver real-time vision, reasoning, and decision-making for autonomous and embedded systems. Its U.S.-manufactured camera lines (Stereo, Vine, Tadpole) target mission-critical use cases across energy, logistics, agriculture, manufacturing, and healthcare. The company positions domestic engineering and onshore production as a hedge against cloud-dependent architectures and global supply-chain fragility.
company.name: Hellbender

company.description: Benji operates a universal API for loyalty partnerships, letting brands across travel, hospitality, retail, food, and fintech connect their loyalty programs through a single integration for earning, redemption, point transfer, and co-acquisition flows. Dubbed the "Plaid for loyalty," the platform compresses integration timelines from months to days versus traditional in-house builds that can cost 7-8 figures and take over a year. Founded by Nick Anastasiades, Jon Elron, and Arik Gaisler — previously the team behind 2ndKitchen, acquired by REEF Technology in 2021 — Benji counts JetBlue, Cook Unity, 1-800-Flowers, and Chip City among the loyalty programs on its network, representing 50M+ combined active members.
company.name: Benji

company.description: Exhibitly is a Ghent-based EventTech startup that layers AI-driven personalisation on top of existing B2B event websites. Visitors enter their role and company to instantly receive a tailored mini-site of the most relevant sessions, speakers and exhibitors, which the company says lifts click-through to registration to around 30%. Founded in 2025 by Hendrik Franck and Brent Coppens, the platform serves organisers including Informa, Easyfairs, Hyve Group and the Dubai World Trade Centre across Europe, the US, the Middle East, Africa and Asia.
company.name: Exhibitly

company.description: Stilta is a Stockholm-based legal-tech startup building an agentic AI platform for patent and intellectual-property work. Its software runs a network of AI agents that perform prior-art search, claim charting and litigation-grade analysis from a patent number, helping law firms and in-house IP teams monetize and defend portfolios. Founded in December 2025 by four former McKinsey engineers, the company is part of Y Combinator's W26 batch and counts AmLaw 100 firms and Fortune 500 in-house IP teams among its early customers.
company.name: Stilta

company.description: TerraByte AI builds a multi-sensor geospatial foundation model that ingests optical, synthetic aperture radar, thermal and hyperspectral satellite imagery and turns it into natural-language-queryable insights through its "Earth Search Engine." The Seattle- and San Francisco-based startup, founded in 2025 by former Microsoft researchers, targets enterprise customers in insurance, utilities, emergency response and logistics, with plans to run the model on the ground as well as on-orbit for low-latency monitoring.
company.name: TerraByte AI

company.description: Aquanta Vision is a Tulsa, Oklahoma-based software startup founded in 2023 that develops optical gas imaging (OGI) and thermal imaging applications for methane leak detection. Its patented, physics-based signal-processing algorithm runs as an add-on app — including the NETxTEN product — that adds automated detection to drone-mounted, fixed and handheld OGI cameras without requiring new hardware. The company has participated in the Chevron Studio, Rose Rock Bridge, Shell GameChanger and Innosphere accelerator programs.
company.name: Aquanta Vision

company.description: Rely is an AI-native diligence platform for multifamily real estate, ingesting entire data rooms — leases, vendor contracts, utility agreements and financials — and turning them into structured, source-linked data so acquisition and transition teams can audit thousands of files in hours instead of weeks. Founded in 2025 in Portland, Maine by former PropTech operators George Matelich and David LoBosco, the company targets operators, lenders and servicers across the multifamily sector with early customers including Cardinal Group, Preiss and Olympus.
company.name: Rely

company.description: ProEncia Biotechnology is a Minnesota-based biopharma startup developing patent-pending, non-invasive treatments for periodontal disease, including gingivitis and periodontitis. Co-founded in 2022 by Dr. Tarun Ghosh and Dr. Thomas Hoover, the company is building a pipeline of prophylactic and therapeutic oral-care products targeting more than 100 million U.S. patients affected by chronic gum disease.
company.name: ProEncia Biotechnology

company.description: Atlo is a Copenhagen-based startup building an AI-powered B2B wholesale platform for interior design and lifestyle brands. The product predicts reorder needs, automates retailer follow-ups, and surfaces real-time sell-in performance data for brands managing hundreds of retailer relationships.
company.name: Atlo

company.description: Viktor is a Warsaw-based AI startup that embeds an agentic "virtual coworker" inside Slack and Microsoft Teams, where team members can chat it to pull reports, build internal apps, or run workflows across tools like Google Drive, Notion, Airtable, Shopify and Meta Ads. Founded by former Meta engineers Fryderyk Wiatrowski and Peter Albert, the product reached a $15M annualized revenue run rate within roughly three months of its public launch.
company.name: Viktor

company.description: Status is an interactive social media network that lets users step inside scenarios rather than scroll a passive feed. The company positions itself as a gamified alternative to traditional social platforms, blending entertainment and social networking.
company.name: Status AI

company.description: Unframe is a Cupertino-based enterprise AI startup founded in 2024 by Shay Levi and Larissa Schneider. The platform delivers turnkey AI deployments for enterprises and reached over $100M in total contract value within 12 months of launch.
company.name: Unframe

company.description: Cagent Vascular is a Wayne, Pennsylvania medical device company developing the Serranator SONIC IVL (intravascular lithotripsy) system for treating peripheral artery disease. The company recently reported its first-in-human Serranator SONIC cases ahead of the global REMODEL II pivotal trial.
company.name: Cagent Vascular

company.description: Nourish is addressing the root cause of the chronic disease crisis through AI-powered, insurance-covered virtual nutrition care. We are tackling one of the most important problems in the world — our broken healthcare system. We are building a digital health system that is patient-friendly, prioritizes lifestyle change, delivers outcomes, and lowers costs.
company.name: Nourish

company.description: CRACI is a Helsinki-based cybersecurity startup automating software supply chain compliance for the EU Cyber Resilience Act. Its platform plugs into CI/CD pipelines to generate continuously-updated SBOMs, track vulnerabilities across deployed product fleets, and automate the documentation, lifecycle management, and ENISA incident reporting the CRA mandates. The company targets software vendors selling digital products into the EU that must meet stricter security and traceability standards from September 2026.
company.name: CRACI

company.description: Leadbay is a San Francisco-based B2B sales intelligence platform that uses a proprietary AI inference model to surface small and mid-sized businesses with sparse digital footprints — sectors like construction, manufacturing, hospitality, and retail. Its product scores prospects against a learned ideal customer profile, runs automated qualification, and pushes ranked lists into CRM and outreach tools.
company.name: Leadbay

company.description: Scope is a London-based AI workflow platform built for the Testing, Inspection and Certification (TIC) industry. The product helps field inspectors capture findings on-site, surface relevant historical context, automate admin work and generate compliant reports in minutes instead of hours. By cutting reporting time and lifting inspection capacity, Scope lets TIC firms scale audits without sacrificing quality or expert judgement.
company.name: Scope AI

company.description: Armada is a full-stack edge infrastructure company that delivers compute, storage, connectivity, and AI/ML capabilities to remote and rugged industrial environments, enabling organizations to operate at the edge without compromise. They specialize in mobile, modular AI data centers designed for fast, flexible deployment in challenging locations, supporting AI training and inference at the edge.
company.name: Armada

company.description: Pluang is an Indonesia-based multi-asset wealth and trading platform that lets retail investors access gold, mutual funds, US stocks, ETFs, options, crypto, crypto futures, and (as of May 2026) local Indonesian equities, with minimum tickets as low as roughly $0.50. Founded by Claudia Kolonas and Richard Chua, the Jakarta-headquartered company has grown to more than 13 million registered users, achieved profitability in 2025, and expanded into the Philippines, positioning itself as one of the most downloaded retail trading apps in Indonesia.
company.name: Pluang

company.description: Rheaplant is a San Francisco-based startup, founded in 2025, that produces high-value botanical and nutraceutical ingredients through plant cell culture instead of field-grown crops. It grows plant cells in modular bioreactors under controlled conditions, betting on a food-grade, scale-out approach to reach unit economics that conventional biopharma-grade systems cannot. The company sells primarily B2B to food, supplement and nutrition makers seeking consistent, year-round supply of compounds such as ashwagandha, rhodiola and ginseng.
company.name: Rheaplant

company.description: Vital Signals is a San Francisco-based health innovation company building consumer-facing technology for blood pressure tracking and long-term cardiovascular care. Founded by tech veteran Tom Moss, it is developing a direct-to-consumer device and software platform that aims to replace episodic cuff readings with continuous, behavior-aware insights for managing hypertension.
company.name: Vital Signals

company.description: MiRus is a privately-held medical device company headquartered in Marietta, Georgia, developing biomaterials, implants and procedural solutions for cardiovascular, structural heart, spine and orthopedic conditions. Its lead cardiovascular program is the SIEGEL balloon-expandable transcatheter aortic valve, which uses a proprietary nitric-oxide-coated rhenium alloy frame and is delivered through an 8 French sheath roughly half the size of currently marketed TAVR sheaths.
company.name: MiRus

company.description: Cigno is a Swiss startup building AI infrastructure purpose-built for the management consulting industry. Its platform automates the consulting workflow end to end — from research to recommendation delivery — using a proprietary reasoning model and specialized agents that produce structured, sovereignty-aware analyses aligned with consulting standards. Cigno targets independent consultants and firms, with customizable instances connected to each firm's own data and methodologies.
company.name: Cigno

company.description: Instalimb is a Tokyo-based deeptech startup that uses AI and 3D printing to manufacture low-cost prosthetic legs and orthotics. Since 2017 the company has delivered over 9,000 3D-printed prosthetics through clinics in the Philippines and India, and licenses its 3D design and manufacturing solution to public agencies and NGOs including ALIMCO and BMVSS Jaipur Foot. The company also produces its own 3D printers and 3D-CAD software for prosthetic socket design.
company.name: Instalim

company.description: CoreWeave is a GPU-native cloud provider purpose-built for AI training and inference at scale. Founded in 2017, the company operates infrastructure optimized around modern AI stacks with tightly integrated networking, storage, and orchestration systems. CoreWeave serves AI labs, startups, and large enterprises needing burstable high-throughput compute without the capex of building GPU farms. The company completed its public listing on Nasdaq in March 2025 under ticker CRWV.
company.name: CoreWeave

company.description: Salt & Fiber is a Malmö-based climatetech materials startup developing biodegradable textile yarns from beach-cast seagrass. Its process turns a waste stream that coastal municipalities normally landfill into plant-based fibres that need no fresh water, fertilisers, or land and shed no microplastics. The company runs pilot production with a Swedish municipal supply agreement and is part of the Älmhult x IKEA incubator, targeting commercial market entry between 2027 and 2028.
company.name: Salt & Fiber

company.description: Kalpi is a Hyderabad-based fintech building a rule-based systematic investing platform for retail and institutional investors. It lets users build, backtest, automate and execute quantitative strategies across equities, ETFs and mutual funds through Kalpi.ai for retail and KalpiQuant.com for PMS firms, AIFs, RIAs, brokers and family offices.
company.name: Kalpi

company.description: BirdyChat is a Riga-based professional messaging platform that gives external business communication a dedicated home, replacing the WhatsApp and SMS threads workers use with clients, suppliers and freelancers. It uses work email as the username, organises chats into threads and contact lists, and last year became the first app to ship WhatsApp interoperability under the EU Digital Markets Act. The app runs on iOS, Android and macOS and has built a 50,000-person waitlist across Europe.
company.name: BirdyChat

company.description:
Legal professionals spend up to 70% of their time on repetitive, low-value tasks. Lexroom.ai provides them with the superpowers to change that.

Our AI-powered platform enables lawyers to transform hours of time-consuming legal research into seconds, generating high-quality drafts and practical answers backed by trusted, institutional legal sources. Designed in collaboration with top-tier law firms (GOP, Ichino Brugnatelli, GPBL), Lexroom.ai seamlessly integrates into the daily workflow of lawyers, helping them focus on strategic, value-adding activities.

Available today for Civil Law, Commercial Law, Privacy, Banking, Labour Law.
Coming soon for Tax, Public, Criminal, Family and IP Law. 
Lexroom.ai is redefining the way lawyers work—one regulation at a time. 🚀
company.name: Lexroom.ai

company.description: Vortex Imaging is an Israeli MedTech company developing a cloud-based computational ultrasound platform that produces standardized 3D volumetric images using GPU-powered reconstruction. The system aims to bring CT/MRI-grade visualization to point-of-care ultrasound while reducing operator variability and the learning curve associated with conventional scanners. Initial clinical focus targets urology and nephrology applications.
company.name: Vortex Imaging

company.description: SponsorCX is a SaaS sponsorship management platform that helps properties and brands manage sponsorships across sports, entertainment, events, and other industries. Its product offers inventory management, sales pipeline, agreements, fulfillment tracking, dashboards and reporting, a mobile app, and integrations with CRMs and finance tools. The platform targets teams, venues, universities, and event organizers and emphasizes replacing spreadsheets with centralized workflows. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Lehi, Utah.
company.name: SponsorCX

company.description: Bunch is a Berlin-based AI-native fund operations platform for private markets, providing integrated software for venture capital and private equity managers across the fund lifecycle including investor onboarding, capital calls, fund accounting, reporting, compliance and tax. Founded in 2021, it supports over 150 asset managers and 12,000+ LPs across Europe with offices in Berlin, London, Amsterdam and Luxembourg.
company.name: Bunch

company.description: MokSa.ai develops AI-powered video intelligence software that layers on top of existing security cameras to detect shoplifting, employee fraud, and other loss events for retail and commercial operators. The Troy, Michigan-based startup, a Techstars 2023 alum, has expanded from pre-seed-stage pilots into broader retail deployments.
company.name: MokSa.ai

company.description: Tomorrow.io is a weather intelligence and climate security platform that combines next-generation space technology, generative AI, and proprietary weather modeling to deliver forecasting and decision-making capabilities for managing weather resiliency.
company.name: Tomorrow.io

company.description: Searchable offers a tool that gives marketing teams a real-time view into how their brand is being recommended by AI models, helping brands understand and optimize their presence in AI-powered search environments.
company.name: Searchable

company.description: Blink is a Boston-based mobile-first employee experience platform built for frontline workers in restaurants, retail, hospitality, transportation, healthcare, and manufacturing. The app combines internal communications, scheduling, shift swapping, and payroll self-service with an AI workforce-insights layer called Blink IQ that tracks sentiment, engagement, and turnover risk. Customers include McDonald's, Domino's, Shake Shack, Dollar Tree, Booking.com, and easyJet.
company.name: Blink

company.description: Cellogen Therapeutics is a Noida-based biotech founded in 2021 developing cell and gene therapies using CAR-T technology to treat cancers and blood disorders. Its lead candidate is a bispecific, dual-antigen CAR-T designed to target two cancer markers and reduce relapse risk, with a stated goal of bringing CAR-T pricing down from roughly $500K-700K to $60K-70K. The company operates a GMP-compliant facility in Noida with in-house CAR design and lentiviral vector manufacturing and is preparing Phase I trials in collaboration with Christian Medical College Vellore.
company.name: Cellogen Therapeutics

company.description: Sigma is a San Francisco-based cloud analytics company whose warehouse-native platform pairs a spreadsheet interface with SQL, Python, and native AI so business and technical teams can build apps, agents, and live dashboards directly on cloud data warehouses like Snowflake and Databricks without moving data. Its customer base of more than 2,000 enterprises includes AMD, Duolingo, Colgate-Palmolive, and JPMorgan Chase, and recent launches such as Sigma Agents and Sigma MCP Server have pushed the company into the agentic analytics category.
company.name: Sigma

company.description: Gradiant is an MIT-founded, Boston-headquartered water technology company providing end-to-end industrial water and wastewater solutions to sectors including semiconductors, data centers, pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, mining, and energy. Its proprietary technology stack, paired with an AI-driven digital platform, helps customers secure water sourcing, maximize reuse, minimize discharge, and lower energy use. The company has emerged as one of the fastest-growing players in the water industry on the back of AI infrastructure and advanced manufacturing demand.
company.name: Gradiant

company.description: Accro Bioscience (also operating as Accropeutics) is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral small-molecule therapeutics for inflammatory and autoimmune diseases by targeting regulatory cell death pathways. Its lead asset AC-101, a selective RIPK2 inhibitor for moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis, has completed Phase I and Phase Ib/IIa studies and holds U.S. FDA IND clearance for Phase II. The company is headquartered in Suzhou, China with U.S. operations.
company.name: Accro Bioscience

company.description: Fragile is a San Francisco-based platform that helps consumer brands launch and operate hardware subscription programs, bundling the financing, capital and operational infrastructure needed to turn one-time hardware sales into recurring revenue. Founded in 2022 by CEO Zachary Wynegar, the company targets the hardware-as-a-service category and works with brands that want to offer their devices on a subscription basis without building the underlying financing stack in-house.
company.name: Fragile

company.description: Full-Life Technologies is a fully integrated global radiotherapeutics company with operations in Belgium, Germany, and China. The company focuses on radiopharmaceutical research, development, production, and commercialization, aiming to deliver clinical impact for patients through innovative treatments.
company.name: Full-Life Technologies

company.description: AEON is a Hong Kong-based settlement layer designed for the agentic economy, enabling autonomous AI agents to execute verifiable on-chain transactions and pay real-world merchants. The company combines protocols like x402, ERC-8004 and MCP with its Protocol Kernel x402 Stack and Trust Hub to deliver atomic settlement for Agent-to-Agent and Agent-to-Merchant flows. AEON partners with Coinbase's x402 protocol and BNB Chain, and reports more than 2 million users with 30 million monthly transactions.
company.name: AEON

company.description: Dust builds custom AI assistants for enterprises that integrate with leading language models like OpenAI's GPT, Google's Gemini, Anthropic, and Mistral AI, connecting to company data in tools like Slack, Notion, and Google Drive to enhance productivity in customer support, sales, code management, and more.
company.name: Dust

company.description: Our efficient systems-level AI infrastructure enables a tenfold improvement in both training and inferencing of the largest generative models. Leveraging these capabilities, Decart is able to train its own foundational generative interactive models and make it accessible for everyone in real-time.
company.name: Decart.AI, Inc.

company.description: Kin Health builds a consumer-facing AI notetaker that records doctor visits, transcribes them through specialized medical models, and turns the conversation into a clinical narrative and patient-friendly summary with next steps. Founded by physicians Arpan and Amit Parikh with Kyle Alwyn, the free app is positioned as a portable health graph that travels with the patient across providers and EHRs, monetizing through referrals to specialists and labs.
company.name: Kin Health

company.description: Brami is a San Diego-based food brand producing protein-fortified pasta and lupini bean snacks. It combines Italian durum wheat semolina with high-protein lupini beans, positioning itself as a healthier alternative in the everyday pasta category.
company.name: Brami

company.description: WineView builds wine club subscription management software that integrates with restaurant and winery point-of-sale systems including Toast. The platform handles enrollment, fulfillment, compliance, and customer-side subscription management for wine clubs across roughly 40 US states.
company.name: WineView

company.description: EYST Technology is a Tunis-based insurtech founded in 2022 by Marwen Amamou. Its SaaS platform lets insurers settle claims instantly by issuing virtual bank cards loaded with the reimbursement amount, integrated directly into the insurer's mobile app or web portal so policyholders can spend without paying upfront. The product layers spending controls, real-time transaction tracking, and fraud scoring on top of the payout flow.
company.name: EYST

company.description: Hypershell is a Shanghai-based consumer-grade exoskeleton company founded in 2021 by Sun Kuan, designing lightweight (~1.8 kg) AI-driven lower-body exoskeletons for outdoor recreation, hiking and mobility assistance. Its X Series uses a proprietary single-motor Omega architecture and AI MotionEngine algorithm to offset roughly 30% of carried weight, has shipped to over 70 countries, and ranks as the top-selling consumer exoskeleton brand on JD.com in China and Amazon in the US and Europe. The company operates offices in Shanghai and Shenzhen with a team of nearly 200, more than 70% of whom are in R&D.
company.name: Hypershell

company.description: eVoost AI is an Abu Dhabi-based proptech startup building an AI-powered platform for residential real estate sales, using predictive analytics, behavioral data, and automation to help developers forecast buyer demand, optimize pricing, and run sales campaigns before construction is complete. Founded in 2024 by Cristian Pastrana and Koh Onozawa (former co-CEO of crypto exchange Bit2Me), the company says it manages more than $3.5 billion in real estate assets under commercial agreements across Europe, the UAE, and the United States, with operating teams in Abu Dhabi, Madrid, and Gijón.
company.name: eVoost AI

company.description: Calibre is a London-based AI startup building bespoke agents and software for the $200 billion testing, inspection and certification (TIC) industry. Its first product, AuditorOS, automates documentation review, report writing and standards cross-referencing for independent auditors, and it also deploys enterprise-grade agents inside large TIC organizations and accreditation bodies. The company was founded by Gautham and Steve Thomas, who previously spent three years at Palantir deploying enterprise AI across more than 20 industries.
company.name: Calibre

company.description: Cosmico is a Milan-based future-of-work holding company operating across Italy and Spain with a network of more than 35,000 digital professionals and 300+ clients. Its ecosystem spans Talent-as-a-Service for digital professionals, a creator-economy agency (Flatmates), community-based employee engagement, and AI-augmented team design. The group is pursuing a multi-vertical M&A strategy targeting adjacent future-of-work segments.
company.name: Cosmico

company.description: Greenpixie is a UK-based energy SaaS startup that helps Fortune 1000 enterprises reduce the carbon, water, and cost impact of cloud and AI infrastructure. Its FinOps and GreenOps tooling integrates with major cloud providers to terminate zombie resources, optimise AI model usage, and route workloads to low-carbon regions. Customers include Mastercard.
company.name: Greenpixie

company.description: LawX is a Berlin-based legaltech startup building an AI-driven operating system for the back office of law firms and notaries, automating case management, workflow handling, document processing, contact and calendar management, and billing. Founded in 2025 by Dr Norman Koschmieder with Sara Brinkmann and Torben Rabe, the platform launched in November 2025 and targets the administrative half of legal work that has historically been left to fragmented legacy software. The company positions itself alongside research and drafting tools as a separate operating layer for legal operations across the German and broader European market.
company.name: LawX

company.description: Cimento AI is a Salt Lake City-based cybersecurity startup that builds an AI-native human risk management platform. The system creates a living risk profile for every employee by integrating with email, identity providers, cloud services, and existing security tools, then runs realistic social engineering simulations across email, SMS, and voice. When risky behavior is detected, it delivers short, contextual interventions in the moment to coach employees and continuously refine its risk model.
company.name: Cimento AI

company.description: Peekabox is a Dubai-based surplus food marketplace that lets restaurants, cafes, bakeries and grocery stores sell unsold but fresh food at 50-70% discounts through reservable surprise boxes picked up in-store. Founded in 2025 by brothers Hasan and Omair Sarwar, the app launches with 1,000+ stores across 40+ F&B and grocery brands including Carrefour, Costa, Tim Hortons, Eataly and Union Coop. The company targets two regional pain points at once: the UAE wastes over $3.5B of food annually while household budgets are squeezed by rising costs.
company.name: Peekabox

company.description: Japanese medical technology startup developing LIPUS-Brain, a low-intensity pulsed ultrasound therapy for early-stage Alzheimer's disease. Spun out of Tohoku University, the device has been designated a Breakthrough Medical Device by Japan's PMDA and is advancing through pivotal clinical trials.
company.name: Sound Wave Innovation

company.description: Tokyo-based developer of Henry, a cloud-based core hospital information system and electronic medical record platform targeting Japanese hospitals ahead of the regulatory push for full EHR adoption.
company.name: Henry (Japan)

company.description: Japanese mobility startup providing subscription-based eco-friendly vehicles to ride-hailing drivers in Southeast Asia, using proprietary credit-scoring data to lower entry barriers.
company.name: movus technologies

company.description: Aimirim is a Brazilian deeptech founded in Uberlândia that combines field hardware with software to digitalize industrial operations. Its Tupana platform delivers real-time automation, digital twins, IoT sensor management, predictive control and data analytics across agriculture, mining and pulp sectors, integrating with existing legacy equipment. Clients include Raízen, British American Tobacco, Mondelēz and Agropalma, and the company already serves customers in Brazil, the United States, Europe and Asia.
company.name: Aimirim

company.description: Trackk is a Mumbai-based trading-first platform building a stock trading app aimed at young Gen Z investors, offering single-screen trading, personalized insights, and behavioral analytics. The company received SEBI approval to operate brokerage services on the NSE and BSE.
company.name: Trackk

company.description: Owners ID is a Bengaluru-based recovery-tech startup building a privacy-first QR identity layer for physical belongings — luggage, pets, vehicles, keys, and personal valuables — so finders can instantly contact the owner without exposing private phone numbers. Founded in 2025 by Jesintha Louis (previously G7 CR Technologies, acquired by Noventiq), the company sells smart QR tags and stickers through ownersid.com and is positioning at the intersection of digital identity, recovery, and consumer safety technology.
company.name: Owners ID

company.description: Nord Quantique is a Sherbrooke, Quebec quantum computing hardware developer building fault-tolerant machines that perform error correction at the qubit level using microwaves in supercooled cavities. The approach is designed to use far fewer qubits than competing architectures, producing smaller and more energy-efficient systems that the company aims to install in standard data centres. Spun out of the Institut quantique at l Universite de Sherbrooke in 2020, Nord Quantique is one of 11 companies advancing in the U.S. DARPA Quantum Benchmarking Initiative.
company.name: Nord Quantique

company.description: Legend of Toys is an Indian direct-to-consumer toy brand building a character-driven play universe around premium remote-control vehicles (drift cars, high-speed cars, off-road trucks) sold alongside narrative storylines. Founded in 2024 and headquartered in Bengaluru, it differentiates with a "repair-not-replace" after-sales model offering free lifetime service, targeting both children and the adult "Kidult" collector segment.
company.name: Legend of Toys

company.description: Simple Energy is a Bengaluru-based electric two-wheeler manufacturer founded in 2019 by Suhas Rajkumar, later joined by Shreshth Mishra and Ankit Gupta. The company designs and sells three electric scooters — Simple One, Simple OneS, and Simple Ultra — and operates roughly 70 retail outlets across nine Indian states with a 5,000-unit-per-month manufacturing base. It competes with Ather Energy, Ola Electric, River Mobility, and Ultraviolette in India's E2W market and is preparing a planned IPO targeting around $350M in FY27.
company.name: Simple Energy

company.description: Luel operates a global contributor network that turns everyday life into rights-cleared multimodal training data for frontier AI labs. Founded in late 2025 and part of Y Combinator W26, the company runs a marketplace that pairs custom-spec datasets with hundreds of thousands of vetted contributors across nearly 100 countries.
company.name: Luel

company.description: Nof1 is an AI research lab building frontier models trained for autonomous trading in real financial markets. Its flagship Alpha Arena pits leading models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI and Alibaba against each other with live capital, and the team plans to launch a consumer platform of coding agents for markets. Leadership has roots at DeepMind and Renaissance Technologies.
company.name: Nof1

company.description: Elephant Company is a Berlin-based AI-powered training and communication platform built for frontline and deskless workers. Its product suite — Field App, AI assistant, AI Coursebuilder, and Elephant Hub — turns internal know-how into interactive micro-learning and provides context-aware answers in the workflow. Founded in 2022, the company targets the 70%+ of the global workforce in blue-collar roles who have historically lacked access to digital training tools.
company.name: Elephant Company

company.description: Harken Sweets is a New York City-based confectionery startup making plant-based, clean-label chocolate candy bars sweetened with whole-food dates instead of refined sugar. Founded in 2022 by Katie Lefkowitz, its products deliver up to 14g of prebiotic fiber per serving and are stocked nationally at Walmart, Whole Foods Market, Costco, Kroger, Sprouts, Albertsons, and Wegmans.
company.name: Harken Sweets

company.description: XbotGo (Shenmou Yuanzhi), operated by Blink Tech Inc., develops AI-powered sports cameras that automate motion capture, multi-camera coverage and highlight editing for amateur and youth athletics. Its Falcon device is a standalone 4K dual-lens AI camera with on-device tracking, and the company also ships smartphone-based AI gimbals. Distribution spans direct-to-consumer plus retail partners including Best Buy and youth-sports platform TeamSnap.
company.name: XbotGo

company.description: OpenHeart is a Tokyo-based startup developing TAVIO, a 3D and 4D spatial platform that converts smartphone photos and videos into photorealistic, interactive 3D scenes via its proprietary TAVIO ENGINE. The company offers a Platform API, a B2B dashboard (TAVIO BIZ), a VR app (TAVIO DIVE), and a 3D photo studio service (TAVIO STUDIO, including a permanent location in Asakusa), serving real estate, tourism, retail, and entertainment use cases. Founded by CEO Koji Watanabe, OpenHeart targets the democratization of spatial computing and consumer SNS-style 3D memory sharing.
company.name: OpenHeart

company.description: London-based upskilling platform that helps employers close AI, data, and digital skills gaps across their workforce via apprenticeships, AI coaching (Atlas), and outcome-tracked training. Founded by Euan Blair, it counts Babcock, The AA, Capita, and Addison Lee among customers and acquired Berlin-based StackFuel in January 2026.
company.name: Multiverse

company.description: Chromie Health is an NYC-based developer of AI-powered scheduling and operations software for hospitals. Its flagship product, Chromie Dispatch, is an SMS-first AI agent that automates internal nurse shift coverage—matching open shifts with qualified staff via text in under five minutes, with no IT integration or patient-data exposure. Co-founded in 2024 by CEO Douglas Ford, CTO Scott Tisoskey, and CMO Dr. Patricia Ford, the company has filled more than 35,000 last-minute shifts and has a waitlist of 750 healthcare facilities.
company.name: Chromie Health

company.description: Rapido is a Bengaluru-based ride-hailing and mobility platform operating across more than 400 Indian cities. The company offers bike taxis, auto rickshaws, cabs, parcel deliveries, flight bookings, and food delivery, positioning itself as an affordable mass-mobility platform built around a network of independent captains.
company.name: Rapido

company.description: Fresh People is a Valencia-based HR-tech company that combines AI and leadership methodologies to help large organisations scale talent management. Its flagship product, Booster, is an AI-native leadership copilot that integrates into managers' day-to-day tools to set goals, give feedback, and turn corporate strategy into team-level execution. Founded in 2020, it serves clients including Acciona, LaLiga and Kave Home and reports a 45% lift in goal achievement and 23% reduction in attrition.
company.name: Fresh People

company.description: Tokyo-based provider of an all-in-one operating platform for lodging facilities, combining property management, channel manager, and booking engine functions for Japanese hotels and ryokans.
company.name: Check Inn

company.description: CLEATUS is an AI platform for U.S. government contractors that automates the full find-bid-win workflow for small and midsize businesses. Its agents source opportunities across SAM.gov and tens of thousands of state, local, education, and defense sources, then plan, write, and submit proposals against the actual evaluation criteria. The company has supported 500+ contractors targeting a roughly $2.7T federal + SLED procurement market.
company.name: CLEATUS

company.description: Roxberry Naturals is a Columbus, Ohio-based beverage startup founded in 2022 that markets itself as the first modern soda built specifically for kids. Its drinks contain 5 grams of sugar per 7.5oz aluminum can, are made with real fruit and vegetable ingredients, and carry no artificial flavors, sweeteners or dyes. Flavors include Pink Lava Strawberry Lemonade, Ocean Potion Fruit Punch, and Galaxy Gulp Citrus, distributed in Walmart, H-E-B, Kroger, and Meijer.
company.name: Roxberry

company.description: RSA Cross-Border (RSA XB) is a Dubai-based, software-first cross-border logistics startup spun out of RSA Global in May 2026. It supplies freight forwarders, consolidators, and 3PLs with a modular technology platform and AI tooling to launch and scale their own end-to-end air-freight, customs, and last-mile networks, with initial focus on India-to-UK, India-to-Europe, and India-to-GCC e-commerce corridors.
company.name: RSA Cross Border

company.description: Orasio is a Paris-based defence and public-safety startup building AI-powered video intelligence software that analyses camera feeds in real time to detect events like weapons, fires, or anomalies. The company targets European local authorities, private security operators, and military forces with a sovereign, GDPR- and AI-Act-compliant alternative to US, Israeli, and Chinese surveillance platforms, supporting on-premise, edge, and cloud deployments. It was founded in early 2025 by PayFit cofounder Florian Fournier with CTO Arnaud Delaunay and COO Fabio Gennari.
company.name: Orasio

company.description: Agricultural biotechnology company developing disease-resistant crops through proprietary gene editing platform that resurrects native resistance genes from wild relatives and heritage varieties to provide sustainable alternative to chemical crop protection.
company.name: Resurrect Bio

company.description: Elvy is a Swedish energy company offering an all‑in‑one home energy subscription that bundles solar panels, heat pumps, battery storage, installation, maintenance and AI-driven optimisation to lower household energy costs and emissions.
company.name: Elvy

company.description: Sprouts.ai is a Palo Alto-based B2B AI startup building Revenue Agents on top of a proprietary go-to-market data layer that runs natively inside Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, and LLMs like Anthropic Claude. The platform discovers ICP-qualified accounts, enriches contacts, surfaces buying signals, and automates multi-channel outreach to replace fragmented sales tech stacks. Founded in 2023 by Karan Chaudhry, Kapil Chaudhry, and Avinash Nagla, customers include Hewlett Packard, Razorpay, HighRadius, and Udemy.
company.name: Sprouts.ai

company.description: Arkeus develops AI-powered sensing and perception systems that serve as the eyes and brains for autonomous defense platforms. Its flagship Hyperspectral Optical Radar captures multi-layer visual data for real-time onboard AI detection, classification, and tracking in degraded or contested environments. The Melbourne-founded company sells into the U.S. Department of War, the Australian Department of Defence, and major drone manufacturers including AeroVironment, Textron, Tekever, and Boeing subsidiary Insitu.
company.name: Arkeus

company.description: Danish biotech developing an enzyme-based dietary supplement that breaks down alcohol in the stomach before it enters the bloodstream, targeting ALDH2 deficiency. Uses liposomal encapsulation to protect enzymes from stomach acid. Founded in 2020 out of student innovation hubs at UCPH and DTU, with a UK therapeutic subsidiary and initial market plans in Singapore and South Korea.
company.name: Alcolase

company.description: Beepay is a Brazilian retailtech and fintech that develops autonomous checkout technology, letting shoppers scan products with their phones or self-service kiosks instead of waiting in line at a staffed register. The platform integrates payments and identification in a single flow for physical stores, condominium minimarkets, hotels, and corporate spaces. Founded by Renata Costa and headquartered in Florianópolis, Beepay serves around 170 clients and is investing in AI-driven in-store personalization.
company.name: Beepay

company.description: BlueWind Medical develops the Revi System, an FDA-cleared implantable tibial neuromodulation (ITNM) device for urgency urinary incontinence. The miniature implant is placed near the ankle in a single outpatient procedure and is activated by a battery-operated external wearable that delivers wireless stimulation to the posterior tibial nerve. Pivotal OASIS three-year data showed 95% patient satisfaction at 36 months.
company.name: BlueWind Medical

company.description: Onramp is an Austin-based bitcoin-centric financial services platform built on Multi-Institution Custody (MIC), a model that distributes key control across multiple regulated custodians (Onramp, BitGo, Coincover, Tetra Trust) to eliminate single-custodian risk while preserving on-chain verifiability. Its Onramp Finance product unifies bitcoin brokerage, MIC-secured custody, cash accounts with rewards up to 5%, a spending card, bitcoin IRAs, and gold access in one account, serving individuals, RIAs, businesses, and institutional clients with over $1B in assets under custody.
company.name: Onramp Bitcoin

company.description: Ouinex is a regulated crypto and multi-asset derivatives exchange that lets users trade spot crypto, perpetuals, forex, equities and commodities derivatives from a single account, using digital assets as collateral. It runs on a proprietary No-CLOB execution model that hides order books from market makers to curb front-running and stop-hunting, and is funded by its own community of retail and professional traders rather than venture capital. Founded in 2022 and live since December 2025, the platform has crossed 40,000 downloads and 10,000 monthly active users ahead of its native $OUIX token launch.
company.name: Ouinex

company.description: Flick is a San Francisco Bay Area generative-AI filmmaking platform that lets creators build short films via a chat-and-canvas interface that orchestrates models including Google Veo 3, Nano Banana, ByteDance Seedance, and Midjourney. Founded in early 2026 by ex-Instagram engineer Ray Wang and award-winning filmmaker Zoey Zhang, the YC Fall 2025-batch startup focuses on character consistency and a storyboard-style UI aimed at lowering the barrier to film production.
company.name: Flick

company.description: Nectar Social is a Palo Alto-based agentic marketing platform that unifies social intelligence, community management, creator workflows, and conversational commerce in one system. Its autonomous Nectar Agent operates in real time across DMs, comments, and group chats on Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, Reddit, and X — with brand voice and approval workflows owned by the customer team. Customers include e.l.f. Beauty, Babylist, Figma, Graza, and Liquid Death.
company.name: Nectar Social

company.description: WIRobotics is a Seoul-based robotics company developing wearable assistive robots and humanoid systems built on real-world human-movement data. Its WIM walking-assist wearable has surpassed 3,000 cumulative units across Korea, Europe, China, Türkiye, and Japan, while its ALLEX humanoid platform targets human-level manipulation through Physical AI. The company collaborates with NVIDIA and AWS and was selected for the Physical AI Fellowship.
company.name: WIRobotics

company.description: Novella is an AI-native wholesale insurance brokerage targeting the U.S. excess and surplus (E&S) market, using vertical AI agents to automate placement, underwriting analysis, billing, renewals, inspections, and policy endorsements. Founded in 2024 and headquartered in New York with an R&D center in Tel Aviv, it is licensed in all 50 states and has appointed close to 100 specialty carriers and MGAs.
company.name: Novella

company.description: PANTA is a London-based SaaS platform serving the global index industry, providing an end-to-end Index Operating System that automates index design, calculation, rebalancing, reporting and compliance workflows for asset managers, index providers and data platforms. Founded by Tobias Sproehnle and Mark Pralle, both alumni of Markit and Morningstar, PANTA targets a sector that underpins more than £9.1 trillion in UK assets under management and over $120 trillion globally yet still relies heavily on legacy systems and manual workflows.
company.name: PANTA

company.description: Iceotope is a UK-based provider of precision liquid cooling technology for AI infrastructure, high-performance computing and edge data center deployments. Its chassis-based system replaces traditional air cooling with liquid-based thermal management, enabling rack densities beyond the limits of air or direct-to-chip cooling while reducing energy and water use. Founded in 2005 in Sheffield, the company holds more than 200 granted and pending patents.
company.name: Iceotope

company.description: CREW Carbon is a Brooklyn-based water and climate-technology company that helps wastewater utilities optimize biological treatment by smart-dosing strategically-sourced alkaline minerals such as calcium carbonate. The process improves pollutant removal, settleability, and plant capacity while permanently sequestering CO2 in measurable form for carbon-removal credits. CREW has commercial deployments at nearly ten facilities across the US and Europe and offtake agreements with buyers including Stripe, Google, JP Morgan, and Autodesk via Frontier.
company.name: CREW

company.description: GovWell builds an AI-native operating system for local government, replacing legacy permitting and licensing software at over 130 municipalities and counties across 34 states. Its AutoCheck engine reviews permit applications for code compliance in seconds and handles the full operational lifecycle including inspections, fee collection, and notices. The company plans to expand from permitting into the broader stack of municipal operations such as public works, benefits, and licensing.
company.name: GovWell

company.description: Degron Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing molecular glue degrader (MGD) medicines that target previously undruggable proteins across oncology, immunology and metabolic disease. Its proprietary GlueXplorer platform has produced more than 10,000 differentiated MGD compounds, and its lead program DEG6498 — a first-in-class oral HuR degrader — is in Phase 1 dose escalation for advanced solid tumors. Founded in 2021, the company is headquartered in Shanghai with offices in Hangzhou and San Diego, and partners with Takeda on additional MGD programs.
company.name: Degron Therapeutics

company.description: Butterfly Medical is an Israel-based medical device company developing a minimally invasive, non-surgical treatment for benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). Its flagship Butterfly Prostatic Retraction Device is an implantable, fully reversible solution that mechanically retracts the prostate's lateral lobes to restore natural urinary flow without cutting, burning or removing tissue. The device holds CE Mark approval in Europe and is currently under U.S. FDA review following completion of a pivotal trial.
company.name: Butterfly Medical

company.description: Stitch builds a cloud-native operating system for modern financial institutions, replacing fragmented legacy core systems with a unified stack across lending, cards, payments and ledgers. Headquartered in Riyadh, it serves banks, lenders and exchanges across the GCC, Africa and Southeast Asia.
company.name: Stitch

company.description: Zerops is a Prague-based developer-first Platform-as-a-Service that runs on its own bare-metal hardware across Europe and the United States, eliminating the gap between development and production environments. Its unified cloud environment uses full Linux containers with over 15 built-in services, plus a Zerops Control Panel that lets AI coding agents like Claude, Codex, and Gemini build and debug directly inside live infrastructure. The team claims operational and storage costs up to four times lower than legacy AWS-rented PaaS competitors such as Render, Fly.io, and Railway.
company.name: Zerops

company.description: Urologic Health is a clinical-stage medical device company developing the niu System, a non-invasive, catheter-free urodynamic testing platform that converts bladder activity into clinically meaningful diagnostic data. It targets diagnosis of overactive bladder, urinary retention, incontinence, and BPH, and is preparing for FDA 510(k) submission and US commercialization. Co-founded by Adam Yaacov (CEO) and Dr. Noam Omer, the company was incubated at the MEDX Xelerator venture studio (now EdgeLabs) and operates as part of the Edge Medical Ventures portfolio with teams in the US and Israel.
company.name: Urologic Health

company.description: equipifi is a Scottsdale-based fintech that provides a white-labeled Buy Now, Pay Later and installment-lending platform embedded directly into the digital banking apps of banks and credit unions. Its checkout engine integrates with institutions' deposit data to offer personalized split-payment options under the bank's own brand, positioning equipifi as infrastructure for institutions rather than a consumer-facing lender.
company.name: equipifi

company.description: Optura is a Nashville-based enterprise healthcare platform that quantifies Return on AI Investment (ROAI). It maps a healthcare organization's fragmented data into a unified knowledge layer, scores and prioritizes AI use cases against cost and readiness, converts top priorities into healthcare-trained AI agents built from existing SOPs, and tracks projected versus realized value in real time. Customers include Independence Blue Cross, Prime Therapeutics, and Ardent Health.
company.name: Optura

company.description: Graphon AI builds a pre-model intelligence layer for enterprise AI, using graphon functions to map relationships across video, audio, documents, images, and structured databases before data reaches a foundation model. The San Francisco company emerged from stealth in May 2026 with deployments at South Korean conglomerate GS Group spanning convenience-store customer-movement analytics and construction-site CCTV safety analysis.
