Founders and advisors come from Amazon, Meta, Google, Apple, NVIDIA, NASA, MIT, and UC Berkeley.
company.name: Graphon AI

company.description: CREATE Medicines is a clinical-stage biotech pioneering in vivo immune programming via a proprietary mRNA-LNP platform that engineers T, NK, and myeloid cells directly inside the body. Its pipeline targets autoimmune disease and oncology, with lead programs CRT-402 (CD19 in vivo CAR-T), a dual CD19xBCMA CAR, and MT-303 in hepatocellular carcinoma. The Cambridge-based company has dosed more than 50 patients across its in vivo CAR clinical programs, the largest clinical dataset in the field.
company.name: CREATE Medicines

company.description: Wirestock operates a multimodal data platform that supplies ethically-sourced training datasets — images, video, 3D models, design assets and spatial data — to leading AI foundation model labs. The company pivoted from creator stock-photo distribution in 2023 and now coordinates more than 700,000 contributing artists, photographers, videographers and designers worldwide. It reports a $40M annual run-rate and has paid out roughly $15M to contributors.
company.name: Wirestock

company.description: Fasset operates a stablecoin-powered digital banking platform aimed at emerging markets, offering an Islamic-finance-aligned super app for receiving money, investing, earning yield, and making cross-border payments.
company.name: Fasset

company.description: Shyld AI develops AI-driven agents and an autonomous UV-C disinfection system for hospitals, automating infection control and operating-room workflows through multimodal sensors and onboard processing. The Sunnyvale-based startup, founded in 2020 by brothers Mohammad and Morteza Noshad, holds three patents in medical hygiene and cites a Stanford peer-reviewed study showing material reduction in surface contamination.
company.name: Shyld AI

company.description: GridCARE develops physics-based AI software that helps utilities and AI data center operators identify latent capacity on existing power grids, compressing interconnection timelines from years to months. Founded at Stanford's Doerr School of Sustainability, its Energize platform models grid congestion, outages, weather and demand variability in real time to activate underutilized megawatts for AI factories.
company.name: GridCARE

company.description: Synthetic is a San Francisco-based startup building an autonomous AI bookkeeping service for software, SaaS, and AI startups. The product connects directly to a customer's banks, payroll, billing, and inboxes and produces clean accrual-basis books with no human bookkeepers in the loop, priced at roughly a quarter of a human-staffed service. Founded by Ian Crosby, who previously co-founded Bench Accounting and scaled it to tens of thousands of customers.
company.name: Synthetic

company.description: Furientis is a Los Angeles defense technology startup developing cost-effective, ship-based interceptor missile systems engineered for high-volume manufacturability. Founded in 2025 by ex-Virgin Orbit and Castelion engineers, the company aims to close the cost-versus-scale gap exposed by recent Red Sea operations, where multi-million-dollar interceptors were spent on inexpensive drone threats. Furientis runs a 9,000-square-foot facility, operates under multiple U.S. Navy and Army partnerships, and is a Tactical Missile Innovation Challenge finalist alongside Anduril and Kratos.
company.name: Furientis

company.description: DDD Invoices is a Ljubljana-based API-first compliance platform that lets SaaS vendors, ERPs, accounting tools, and marketplaces issue, receive and archive tax-compliant e-invoices across 30+ jurisdictions through a single integration. The company connects business systems to government tax portals and Peppol-style real-time reporting networks, using proprietary AI-driven logic to convert standardized invoice data into local formats, validate against current rules, and route filings to the correct authority. It serves software firms including Access Group, Zenoti, Logitude and WheelSys and offers native integrations with Stripe, Shopify, Chargebee and Bitrix.
company.name: DDD Invoices

company.description: Extraordinary Money (XMO) is a Melbourne-based AI-native consumer finance startup applying for Australian Financial Services and Credit Licences. The company is building a predictive financial model that uses agentic AI to anticipate consumer financial decisions rather than reviewing past spending. It was founded in 2026 by former Up Bank chief product officer Anson Parker and payments executive Sam Mendelsohn.
company.name: Extraordinary Money

company.description: Graphcore is a Bristol, UK semiconductor company that designs Intelligence Processing Units (IPUs), chips purpose-built for AI and machine-learning workloads as an alternative to GPUs. Founded in 2016, it was acquired by SoftBank Group in 2024 and now develops silicon for large-scale AI and AGI infrastructure within SoftBank's compute ecosystem alongside Arm.
company.name: Graphcore

company.description: Digital Asset Clearing Center (DACC.HK) is a Hong Kong-based financial market infrastructure provider building compliant clearing, settlement, custody, and record-keeping services for tokenized assets. It offers Clearing-as-a-Service to financial institutions, connecting blockchain networks with traditional payment rails such as China's Cross-Border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) and integrating stablecoins, tokenized-deposit rails, KYC/AML, and DLT wallets with established banking and exchange services.
company.name: Digital Asset Clearing Center

company.description: Recursive Superintelligence is a frontier AI lab pursuing recursive self-improvement — building systems that analyze their own performance and iterate on their own architectures with minimal human intervention. Co-founded by Richard Socher (former Salesforce chief scientist, You.com founder) and UCL/DeepMind researcher Tim Rocktäschel, the team of under 30 splits between London and San Francisco and draws talent from Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Meta. Its stated focus is "AI that improves AI" via open-ended algorithms, with the long-term aim of accelerating scientific discovery.
company.name: Recursive Superintelligence

company.description: Fakto is a Paris-based AI platform for cost intelligence that helps large enterprises capture financial value lost between negotiated supplier contracts and what is actually invoiced. Its agents continuously ingest contracts, pricing grids and invoices to flag billing errors, missing rebates, SLA breaches and volume opportunities. The company targets groups with €500M+ revenue in construction, retail, logistics and manufacturing.
company.name: Fakto

company.description: Violet Therapeutics is a Cambridge, Massachusetts-based preclinical biotech developing small-molecule therapies for neurodegenerative diseases, with a lead program targeting the EphB3 receptor to preserve and repair synapses. Its CONNECT platform maps brain cell-to-cell signaling networks to identify glial-mediated targets implicated in Alzheimer's and related cognitive disorders. The company was founded out of a collaboration between Mass General Brigham Ventures and Brigham and Women's Hospital researchers.
company.name: Violet Therapeutics

company.description: Mind Robotics is an industrial AI and robotics startup building AI-powered robots for manufacturing and industrial applications. The company develops systems focused on dexterity, adaptation, and physical reasoning to automate factory floors, logistics, and supply chains, prioritizing practical, non-humanoid robot designs. It leverages operational data from automotive manufacturing partners to train and deploy robots tailored to real-world industrial tasks. Headquarters are reported in Palo Alto, with operational ties to multiple manufacturing sites.
company.name: Mind Robotics

company.description: Mantle8 is a French geoscience company pioneering natural hydrogen exploration. The company combines geology, geophysics and geochemistry data to create predictive multiphysics imaging models (HOREX®) for detecting and characterizing natural hydrogen systems. It targets energy and clean-tech markets, aiming to industrialize its technology platform and enable faster, larger-scale exploration. Mantle8 is headquartered in Grenoble and builds on the region's established geoscience expertise.
company.name: Mantle8

company.description: Develops chips for AI applications using in-memory compute to run large language models faster and more efficiently than GPUs.
company.name: Fractile

company.description: Anduril Industries is a defense technology company developing advanced AI-powered systems and hardware for military applications, including augmented reality helmets and autonomous systems.
company.name: Anduril Industries

company.description: Outmarket AI is a San Francisco-based intelligence platform designed to transform the commercial insurance industry by integrating AI-powered insights and automation directly into brokers' and carriers' workflows to improve productivity, streamline operations, and uncover revenue opportunities.
company.name: Outmarket AI

company.description: Twin Prime is a US-based frontier AI lab developing multi-modal models that natively reason across sensor data for defense and national security operations. Founded in 2025 by George Lentzas, Stephane Sezer, Drew Calcagno, and Michael Leite-Garcia, the team draws on backgrounds spanning Hudson River Trading, Google Research, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, and US/European defense institutions. Its models aim to compress the perception-to-decision layer for edge deployment in high-stakes environments.
company.name: Twin Prime

company.description: NinaMED is a Melbourne, Australia-based medical device company developing the NiNA System, a non-invasive, at-home neuromodulation wearable for overactive bladder (OAB). The company holds an exclusive global license from Toronto-based EBT Medical for technology that activates the saphenous nerve to reduce OAB symptoms without drugs or surgery. NinaMED is preparing a clinical pathway toward an FDA Investigational Device Exemption while building Australian operations.
company.name: NinaMED

company.description: Xpanner builds an Automation-as-a-Service platform for the construction industry, retrofitting customer-owned heavy equipment with its X1 Kit hardware and software to enable autonomous piling, material handling, trenching and grading. Customers subscribe to task-specific automation licenses rather than replacing machines, with software updates expanding capabilities over time. Founded in South Korea in 2020 and headquartered in Santa Fe Springs, California since 2023, customers include Mortenson, Black & Veatch and QCells.
company.name: Xpanner

company.description: Envoke is a Leeds-based healthtech company that builds virtual simulation software replicating complex medical laboratory equipment for remote operator training, troubleshooting, and competency tracking. Its platform serves healthcare providers and equipment manufacturers (Bio-Rad, ThermoFisher Scientific, PacBio, Haemonetics), reducing field service call-outs by 15-20% and capturing real-time competency data. Founded in 2019 by Stuart Warrington, the company employs 16 staff across the UK and US and counts the Mayo Clinic and Hawaii national blood laboratories among its customers.
company.name: Envoke

company.description: Vienna-founded global hydration brand that pioneered the Microdrink — sugar-free, fruit-and-plant-extract cubes that dissolve in water. Now a full ecosystem spanning hydration cubes, caffeine and electrolyte variants, reusable bottles and filtration, generating roughly €150M annual revenue across more than 20,000 retail outlets and ~50 dedicated stores in Europe, the US, Japan and Australia. Profitable and decade-old in 2026.
company.name: waterdrop

company.description: Avant Genomics is a University of Virginia spin-out developing the Avant Source platform, a fully-automated liquid biopsy sample preparation system for precision cancer detection. The technology consolidates more than 50 manual workflow steps into two, improving cfDNA yield by up to 70% versus semi-automated tools. Founded by Rachelle Turiello and Renna Nouwairi, the company is based in Charlottesville, Virginia and holds exclusive IP from UVA.
company.name: Avant Genomics

company.description: Urology-focused medical device company developing the FloStent System, a non-surgical, fully reversible, first-line device therapy for men with lower urinary tract symptoms caused by benign prostatic hyperplasia.
company.name: Rivermark Medical

company.description: UroMems develops implantable mechatronic technology to treat stress urinary incontinence. Its UroActive system is a smart automated artificial urinary sphincter currently in pivotal clinical trials in the U.S. and France.
company.name: UroMems

company.description: SuperMeat is an Israeli food-tech developing cultivated chicken using cell-cultured meat technology, with plans to scale commercial production through a Switzerland launch.
company.name: SuperMeat

company.description: Visionary (Viseneri) is a Korean spatial-intelligence software startup building PRISM, a data operating system that automatically processes and structures unstructured LiDAR and camera data for autonomous-driving and mobility AI training. The platform claims to cut training-dataset build time by up to 90% via a proprietary Data Flywheel architecture, and is being deployed in R&D collaborations with Hyundai Motor, 42dot, HL Klemove, KAIST, and Seoul National University.
company.name: Visionary

company.description: Champ AI builds enterprise AI agents that automate operations workflows across browsers, document processing and phone calls, helping teams in healthcare, insurance, finance and logistics scale without adding headcount. Founded in 2024 by three former Instacart engineers, the company emerged from stealth in May 2026 with paying customers across logistics, healthcare and e-commerce.
company.name: Champ AI

company.description: FusionAP is a Penang-based advanced semiconductor packaging startup building a geopolitically-neutral OSAT platform for 2.5D, 3D and beyond packaging. Founded in 2025 by senior Intel and TSMC veterans led by CEO Ooi Teng Chow, the company is a founding member of the Malaysia Advanced Packaging Consortium and is targeting customers among global design houses and foundries.
company.name: FusionAP

company.description: Hacktron is a San Francisco-based cybersecurity startup founded by competitive hackers (DEF CON CTF champions) building an AI-powered platform that runs attacker-style security tests on every code change rather than relying on quarterly manual penetration tests. Its system continuously identifies exploitable vulnerabilities in pull requests, reduces false positives versus traditional scanners, and assists developers with remediation; the team has publicly disclosed flaws in widely used software including OAuth2 Proxy and frameworks tied to Vercel and Cloudflare.
company.name: Hacktron

company.description: Autoimmunity BioSolutions (ABS) is a Houston-based biotechnology company developing a personalized, genetically-guided immuno-corrective therapy that aims to normalize elevated levels of soluble IL7 receptor (sIL7R), a key driver of severity and poor treatment response across autoimmune diseases. Its lead indication is rheumatoid arthritis, with additional applicability to lupus nephritis and type 1 diabetes. ABS targets the roughly 50% of patients carrying a prevalent SNP that elevates sIL7R, a distinct mechanism from conventional immunosuppressive standards of care.
company.name: Autoimmunity BioSolutions

company.description: Instafix is a Gurugram-based on-demand smartphone repair startup founded in 2025 by ex-Blinkit operators Aniket Kale and Chetan Chauhan. Certified technicians repair phones at a customer's doorstep within roughly 30 minutes, with up to 12-month warranties and a 4.7-star rating. The company is building a broader consumer-electronics repair platform on top of the smartphone wedge.
company.name: Instafix

company.description: The EleFant is a Mumbai-based toy subscription startup that operates a circular play platform for children aged 0-12. Families subscribe to curated toys and books from 90+ brands through monthly plans (starting at Rs 599), returning items after use to swap for new ones. The company runs a Franchise-Invested, Company-Operated (FICO) model across 125+ partners in 18 Indian cities and was featured on Shark Tank India.
company.name: The EleFant

company.description: e4n is an operator-led, AI-powered platform building a U.S. managed services and cybersecurity rollup. The company acquires and partners with strong local IT businesses and helps them scale through practical AI automation, shared operating capabilities, and disciplined commercial execution. Co-founded in 2026 by Hervé Tessler and Lucas Fischer.
company.name: e4n

company.description: Cinder builds a trust-and-safety operations platform that helps high-growth tech companies detect, investigate and respond to digital threats such as fraud, AI prompt abuse, and non-consensual imagery. The product centralises policy enforcement and trains AI agents on each customer's rulebook so moderation decisions can happen in real time at scale. Customers include OpenAI, Spotify, Depop and Black Forest Labs; the founding team came out of Palantir, Meta, the CIA and U.S. counterterrorism units.
company.name: Cinder

company.description: NVision is an Israeli-German quantum technology company for healthcare, headquartered in Ulm, Germany and founded in 2015. Its POLARIS platform uses parahydrogen-induced polarization to boost MRI signals of metabolic agents by over 10,000x, enabling real-time metabolic imaging on standard MRI systems. The company is expanding from quantum sensing into quantum computing via its Photonic Integrated Quantum Circuits (PIQC) platform built on organic molecule-based qubits.
company.name: NVision Imaging Technologies

company.description: DataMission.ai is an AI-native operating platform built for local governments. The product combines real-time data integration with municipal workflows and runs a multi-agent system across finance, public safety, housing, infrastructure, 311 operations, and economic development to help mid-size cities make faster, evidence-backed decisions. Headquartered in New York and founded by Sloan Gaon, the company is onboarding pilot cities ahead of a Q3 2026 commercial launch and targets municipalities serving 50,000 to 500,000 residents.
company.name: DataMission.ai

company.description: Secludy is a San Francisco-based privacy-tech company building a control layer for enterprise AI in regulated industries. Its platform generates differentially-private synthetic data that mirrors original datasets, letting banks, payments firms and fintechs train, fine-tune and evaluate AI models without exposing real customer records. Deployments run inside the customer cloud and target GDPR, CCPA and HIPAA compliance.
company.name: Secludy

company.description: Origin Lab operates a marketplace that connects video game studios with AI labs building world models, converting licensed game assets and playthrough data into training datasets. Lightspeed-backed and founded by Anne-Margot Rodde, the startup sits between major AI labs sourcing physical-world training data and gaming companies looking to monetize existing digital assets. Early customers include AMI Labs and Fei-Fei Lis World Labs.
company.name: Origin Lab

company.description: NYC-based AI-powered payer intelligence company helping health systems engage with payers.
company.name: Anomaly Insights

company.description: Hint is an AI-native home management platform co-founded by Martha Stewart, Yih-Han Ma, and Kyle Rush. The Charlotte-based company combines property data, expert guidance, and AI agents to help homeowners proactively manage maintenance, insurance, and major decisions across the lifecycle of their home. Hint launches publicly on desktop and iOS in summer 2026.
company.name: Hint

company.description: NYC-based platform combining physician credentialing with a direct-to-facility shift marketplace, eliminating staffing-agency intermediaries. Five modular AI agents automate recruiting, onboarding, credentialing, staffing, and compliance, cutting onboarding from ~90-120 days down by roughly 45 days. Used by ~5,000 physicians, CRNAs, NPs, and PAs across the US.
company.name: Saile

company.description: Nivasa Finance is a Bengaluru-based secured-credit distribution and fulfilment platform serving underserved housing-loan and Loan Against Property borrowers across non-metro India. Founded in 2025 by Samit Shetty (ex-CEO Navi Finserve, founder Chaitanya India Fin Credit) and Hitesh Saraf, the company combines remote onboarding and assessment with on-ground field execution, working with 10+ lending partners across banks, SFBs, HFCs and NBFCs. It has piloted in Mysore and Mandya districts, facilitating hundreds of loans worth over INR 20 crore, and is exploring an NBFC license.
company.name: Nivasa Finance

company.description: Dolfin is a Barcelona-based AI-native platform for sales compensation management. It helps RevOps, finance, and compensation teams design, operate, and adapt commission plans across CRM, ERP, and HRIS systems — replacing spreadsheets and consulting-heavy implementations. Founded in 2023 and SOC 2 certified, Dolfin serves mid-market through enterprise organisations generating over $1B in annual revenue.
company.name: Dolfin

company.description: Asterix Health is a London-based healthtech startup operating a remote workforce platform that connects NHS GP surgeries with GMC-registered doctors working from countries including Australia, Malaysia, and India. The platform handles telephone consultations, triage, and administrative clinical work, integrating with EMIS and SystmOne electronic patient record systems to ease primary care workforce shortages. Founded in 2024 by Julian Titz and Max Thilo, it already supports NHS practices serving roughly 250,000 patients.
company.name: Asterix Health

company.description: Gyver is a Brescia-based Italian startup building AI-powered workforce infrastructure for skilled trades, starting with electricians. Its conversational platform digitally replicates word-of-mouth referral hiring at scale, matching electricians with industrial and energy-sector employers, with a roadmap into upskilling, learning and productivity tools (electrical design, PLC workflows).
company.name: Gyver

company.description: AI-based platform that builds and delivers complex enterprise software using an organization own design systems and internal documentation, used by EUROCONTROL to modernize air traffic management software for European aviation.
company.name: DesignVerse

company.description: Anaut develops surgical-vision AI for laparoscopic and robot-assisted surgery. Its flagship product EUREKA α, a programmable medical device approved by Japan's MHLW in April 2024, highlights connective tissue and dissection planes in real time on a secondary monitor to help surgeons identify anatomical landmarks. The company is led by founding CEO and surgeon Dr. Nao Kobayashi, ships the EUREKA platform across Japanese hospitals, and is rolling the technology out internationally through its 2025-founded US subsidiary Anaut America Inc. and a planned European entity.
company.name: Anaut

company.description: TruKKer is MENA and Central Asia's largest digital freight network, connecting over 60,000 transporters with more than 1,200 enterprise clients across nine countries including Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Bahrain, Jordan, Turkey, and Kazakhstan. Its proprietary platform digitizes road freight with real-time booking, intelligent dispatching, capacity optimization, KYC, contract management, and payments for sectors spanning FMCG, petrochemicals, metals, construction, and retail. Backers include Investcorp, Mubadala, ADQ, and STV.
company.name: TruKKer

company.description: NeptuneZero is a Greek maritime-technology startup, based in the Piraeus/Athens area, that automates vessel reporting and fleet-performance optimisation for shipping companies. Its modular platform pairs onboard hardware and edge computing with cloud-based AI analytics to capture and validate operational data, generate daily noon reports, and surface real-time fleet insights. The system targets fuel and emissions reduction and helps owners manage decarbonisation compliance under EU ETS, FuelEU Maritime, the IMO DCS and CII frameworks.
company.name: NeptuneZero

company.description: Odd Meter is an independent game development studio behind the critically acclaimed narrative adventure INDIKA. Founded in 2017, the team relocated most of its operations to Almaty, Kazakhstan during INDIKA's production. The studio focuses on deeply authored, narrative-driven games and is now developing its next, unannounced project.
company.name: Odd Meter

company.description: SiteVue is a Nashville-area startup founded in August 2025 by Andrew Jebasingh that uses AI-powered video analytics and smart cameras to monitor workforce productivity and safety in factories, warehouses and construction sites. Its computer-vision platform interprets activity on the floor in real time and surfaces cycle-time, downtime and near-miss metrics through dashboards aimed at operations leaders.
company.name: SiteVue

company.description: Happl is a London-based, AI-native global benefits operating system that lets multinational employers manage core, flexible and insurance benefits across 160+ countries from a single platform. The product pairs a configurable rules engine with HRIS and payroll integrations to automate compliance-heavy workflows for mid-market and enterprise customers including Moelis, Kainos and Hootsuite.
company.name: Happl

company.description: n8n is a Berlin-based workflow automation platform founded in 2019 that integrates AI capabilities with business process automation. It enables technical teams to build workflows via a visual drag-and-drop interface or custom code, connecting to any application, API, or data source. The platform supports self-hosting or cloud deployment and is released under a fair-code license with an active community contributing templates and workflows.
company.name: n8n

company.description: AI-native sales platform that combines AI agents with human sales professionals to automate sales workflows including prospecting, outreach, CRM, and meeting notes for early-stage startups.
company.name: Monaco

company.description: Photonic is a Vancouver-based quantum computing company building silicon-based, photon-connected qubits under a proprietary Entanglement First architecture that combines computation and networking on the same hardware. Founded in 2016 and led by CEO Don Mattrick with Chief Quantum Officer Stephanie Simmons, the company is one of three Canadian quantum firms participating in DARPA's Quantum Benchmarking Initiative Phase 2. Photonic operates across Canada, the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Middle East with strategic partnerships with Microsoft, Bell, RBC, and TELUS, targeting commercial-scale fault-tolerant quantum computers connected through standard telecom networks.
company.name: Photonic

company.description: Stream is a Saudi Arabia-based fintech company focused on automating billing and payments to simplify and modernize B2B payment processes across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.
company.name: Stream

company.description: BranchLab is a New York-based data science company developing advanced AI software that enables healthcare and pharmaceutical brands to reach consumers in a privacy-compliant way. Their HealthGraph™ database aggregates anonymized data from over 300 million US residents and 2.1 million healthcare providers, supporting privacy-safe healthcare advertising solutions for TV.
company.name: BranchLab, Inc.

company.description: Charms is the consumer app from Charms Interactive, Inc. that lets creators build AI characters with persistent reasoning memory, distinctive voice and personality, and an onchain economy that runs alongside them on Base. Each public character can launch a USDC-paired market via the platform's Clanker Custom integration and accrue fees into its own treasury, which helps fund inference, model upgrades, and community rewards. By private beta the platform had reached 6,000 users and over $1M in trading volume; it ships on iOS, web, and through World App and Base App.
company.name: Charms

company.description: New York-based healthcare technology company formerly known as Tandem, building an AI-powered network that automates insurance authorization, financial assistance and fulfillment routing for prescription drugs across providers, pharmacies, payers and biopharma.
company.name: Forus

company.description: SwishX is a Bengaluru-based agentic AI platform built for pharmaceutical and medtech commercial operations across emerging markets. Its four products — Tender IQ, Contract IQ, Marketing IQ, and Channel IQ — automate tender bidding, hospital rate contracts, HCP marketing, and channel engagement using autonomous AI agents and a registry of 800,000+ certified healthcare professionals. The company serves 39 enterprise clients including 12 pharma firms and is targeting USD 5M ARR by FY27.
company.name: SwishX

company.description: Exaforce is an AI-native security operations platform that uses agent-based AI (branded Exabots) and real-time reasoning to detect and respond to cyberattacks as they unfold. Founded around 2022 and led by CEO Ankur Singla, the company replaces manual SOC triage with continuous threat investigation across logs, identity, and endpoint telemetry, claiming roughly 90% reduction in analyst workload. Reported customers include Replit and Guardant Health; team grew to 130+ employees through 2026 as the company positions against AI-augmented threats targeting enterprise software.
company.name: Exaforce

company.description: Vector is a Boston-based B2B advertising platform that lets demand-gen and ABM teams build ad audiences at the contact level by named buyer, using intent signals such as site visits, ad clicks, and competitor research rather than firmographic filters. The platform de-anonymizes ad clickers and ties them back to campaign performance, helping marketers cut wasted spend and prove pipeline impact. With the May 2026 Series A, the company is layering AI on top of that data — including Vector MCP, an LLM-facing interface that surfaces campaign and buyer-activity answers inside tools like Claude and ChatGPT.
company.name: Vector (B2B ads)

company.description: Franq is a Brazilian fintech operating a platform for independent personal bankers, aggregating 150+ financial products (real-estate financing, loans, consortia, insurance, home-equity credit) from 50+ partner banks and fintechs including Itaú, Santander, Bradesco, Inter and C6 Bank. Founded in 2019 in Florianópolis by Paulo Silva, the company has onboarded 10,000+ banking professionals and ended 2025 with $423M in originated transactions, reaching operational break-even.
company.name: Franq

company.description: Oishii (oishii.com) is a high-end, New York-based vertical farming company that revolutionized commercial indoor agriculture by growing premium, pesticide-free, Japanese-varietal strawberries (like the Omakase Berry) year-round, expanding to vertical farming technology in the US.
company.name: Oishii

company.description: AI-first procurement operating system for healthcare, connecting pharmacies, distributors, hospitals, and pharmaceutical suppliers across the MENA region. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Riyadh, Aumet processes over $1B in annual GMV and runs a three-tier product suite (Pulse, Chain, Enterprise) deployed across Jordan, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia.
company.name: Aumet

company.description: 9amHealth is a San Diego-based virtual specialty care company founded in 2021 that contracts with large enterprise employers to manage cardiometabolic conditions including diabetes, obesity, hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Members get 24/7 access to endocrinologists, obesity medicine specialists and clinical pharmacists alongside at-home lab testing, GLP-1 medication management and AI-enabled clinical workflows. The company is extending its platform into additional high-cost chronic conditions that account for up to 70% of employer pharmacy spend.
company.name: 9amHealth

company.description: Technology-driven lender and financial management platform for multi-unit franchise operators, providing financing, charge cards and AI-enabled CFO tools for QSR and independent brand operators.
company.name: Exponent Financial

company.description: AI compute aggregation platform building an independent grid by buying excess compute capacity from data center operators and reselling it to startups, universities and research labs.
company.name: Amp PBC

company.description: Casimir is building semiconductor chips that harvest energy from quantum vacuum fields to produce continuous electrical power with no batteries, cords, or charging. The company's 5mm x 5mm MicroSparc chip targets ultra-low-power electronics — tire-pressure sensors, wearables, embedded IoT — with a roadmap toward consumer electronics, mobility, and grid-scale energy. Founded by former NASA advanced-propulsion researcher Dr. Harold "Sonny" White and incubated at the Limitless Space Institute.
company.name: Casimir

company.description: Coworked builds Harmony, an agentic AI project manager that handles enterprise coordination, follow-through, and admin work across the systems teams already use — email, chat, voice, Jira, Microsoft Project, Smartsheet, ServiceNow, and Salesforce. The Boston startup targets the structural gap PMI projects between project-management demand and supply by adding AI agents that operate as remote coworkers inside PMO and transformation teams.
company.name: Coworked

company.description: Osero is a stablecoin yield infrastructure project incubated by Stablewatch and Soter Labs. Its three products — Earn, App, and Foundry — let wallets, neobanks, and asset managers embed the Sky Savings Rate and bring on-chain yield products to market with a Basel III-inspired risk framework.
company.name: Osero

company.description: gaiia is an AI-native operating system for Communications Service Providers, replacing fragmented legacy OSS/BSS tools with a single platform spanning billing, provisioning, account management, field service, and workflow automation. Founded by the team behind Canadian ISP oxio, the Montreal-headquartered company brings new operators live in 4 to 6 months versus the 12 to 18 month industry standard.
company.name: gaiia

company.description: Infex Therapeutics is a UK clinical-stage biotech focused on novel anti-infective therapies for drug-resistant infections and other critical-priority pathogens. Its lead programme RESP-X is a first-in-class anti-virulence monoclonal antibody in-licensed from Shionogi targeting Pseudomonas aeruginosa in non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis (NCFB) patients, with the company also advancing MET-X (a metallo-beta-lactamase inhibitor partnered with Venus Remedies for India) and a preclinical BamA inhibitor programme with Justus-Liebig-University Giessen. Headquartered at Alderley Park in Cheshire and led by CEO Dr. Peter Jackson and Chair Jon Moulton, Infex builds its differentiated pipeline through in-house discovery, acquisition and in-licensing.
company.name: Infex Therapeutics

company.description: Roadrunner is a San Francisco-based AI-native CPQ (configure, price, quote) software company building a natural-language quote-to-cash workflow for enterprise sales teams handling complex pricing models. Cofounded in 2025 by Joubin Mirzadegan, Ajay Natarajan, and Eugene Shao, the company is Kleiner Perkins first incubation since Glean and is targeting the prompt-quote-approve layer over legacy CPQ systems.
company.name: Roadrunner

company.description: IREN (NASDAQ: IREN) is a vertically integrated AI Cloud provider operating large-scale data centers and GPU clusters for AI training and inference. Its platform is underpinned by an expansive portfolio of grid-connected land and renewable power across the U.S. and Canada, originally built up under the Iris Energy name before the February 2024 rebrand. The company designs and deploys direct-to-chip liquid cooling and serves AI-native and enterprise customers from bare metal through Kubernetes.
company.name: IREN

company.description: Tolemy Bio is a UK-Spanish biotech building Orbit, an AI-native virtual control panel for cell biology that unifies fragmented lab data, instruments, and software into a single platform. The company combines virtual cell models and AI research agents to help cell and gene therapy developers simulate biological responses and accelerate biopharma R&D. Founded in 2026 by Alex Ward and Caelan Anderson, Tolemy operates between Cambridge, UK and Barcelona, Spain.
company.name: Tolemy Bio

company.description: Knit Health is a San Francisco-based health-tech company that builds AI infrastructure for healthcare delivery. Spun out of UC Berkeley in 2025, the company develops a Large Clinical Behavior Model (LCBM) trained on Truveta EMR data from over 130 million patients across 30 U.S. health systems, using deep reinforcement learning, causal inference, and behavioral cloning to capture how clinicians actually triage, route, and discharge patients. The platform sits beneath routing, scheduling, and care-team decisions to help health systems improve patient flow and quality.
company.name: Knit Health

company.description: Judgment Labs builds infrastructure that turns AI agents production data — reasoning traces, tool calls, retries, memory — into evals, alerts, and continuous-improvement loops. Its platform analyzes the full agent trajectory rather than just final outputs, surfacing failure patterns so teams can ship targeted fixes. Founded by three childhood friends (Alex Shan, Andrew Li, Joseph Camyre), the San Francisco company already powers monitoring at a growing roster of agent-native startups.
company.name: Judgment Labs

company.description: Cerevance is a Boston-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing cell type-specific therapies for neurodegenerative diseases and obesity. Its proprietary NETSseq (Nuclear Enriched Transcript Sort sequencing) platform identifies drug targets from human brain samples expressed in specific cell types. Lead candidate solengepras (CVN424) is a once-daily, oral GPR6 inhibitor in Phase 3 development for Parkinson disease motor fluctuations, with topline ARISE data expected end of Q3 2026.
company.name: Cerevance

company.description: Greenboard is an AI-native compliance platform for financial institutions, founded in 2023 by Dave Feldman and Ed Schembor in New York. Its unified system replaces fragmented legacy tools for employee compliance, communications supervision, marketing review, third-party oversight, and securities recordkeeping (17a-4). More than 500 RIAs, broker-dealers, and asset managers run their compliance programs on the platform, with the recently launched GreenboardGo adding a conversational AI layer that resolves employee compliance questions and routes edge cases to in-house experts for sign-off.
company.name: Greenboard

company.description: Milan-based AI technology company building an operating layer that lets small and medium-sized businesses run marketing, sales, and operations through agentic automation. Its proprietary stack — Jooice for marketing, Groow for AI agents, and Welpy for content governance — combines existing tools into one execution layer. Founded in 2017, the company reports ~$18M revenue with ~$3M EBITDA in 2025 and operates across Italy, Spain, the US, and the UAE.
company.name: Webidoo

company.description: Star Catcher Industries is a Jacksonville, Florida-based aerospace startup building the first power grid in space, using optical power beaming to deliver concentrated solar energy on demand to satellites and other spacecraft. The company's approach is designed to lift the power ceiling on existing satellite hardware — claiming up to 10x more available power with no retrofit or custom receiver — addressing what its team calls the binding constraint on space connectivity, computing, sensing and security workloads. Founded in 2024 by CEO Andrew Rush, Star Catcher has set a world record for optical power beaming, completed an on-orbit subsystem demonstration, and serves both commercial space operators and U.S. national security customers.
company.name: Star Catcher Industries

company.description: Kleva is a Mexican fintech founded in 2025 that builds autonomous AI agents to automate debt collection for banks, lenders, and fintechs across Latin America. Its agents handle contact-center collection workflows end to end, calling debtors, negotiating, and executing recovery in Spanish and Portuguese. The company says the platform lifts debt recovery rates by up to 25% while cutting operational costs by as much as 70% compared with traditional contact centers.
company.name: Kleva

company.description: Qurie is a Freiburg-based Fraunhofer IPM spin-off building solid-state electrocaloric refrigeration systems that cool without compressors or chemical refrigerants. Its globally patented active electrocaloric heat pipe pumps heat at high frequencies for theoretically over 80% efficiency, positioning the technology as an F-Gas-Regulation-compliant alternative to traditional HVAC. The company is targeting industrial enclosure cooling first, with plans to extend into commercial refrigeration, medical, electronics and automotive thermal management.
company.name: Qurie

company.description: Iterative Health develops AI-powered precision medicine solutions for gastroenterology and hepatology, using machine learning and computer vision to analyze endoscopic images and accelerate clinical research. Its products include SKOUT for AI polyp detection and a site network and tooling to optimize clinical trial enrollment and execution for sponsors and providers. The company focuses on improving colorectal cancer screening and treatments for inflammatory bowel disease by embedding research into community care; it was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
company.name: Iterative Health

company.description: DISA Technologies is an innovative materials liberation company based in Casper, Wyoming, specializing in patented High-Pressure Slurry Ablation (HPSA) technology that enhances mineral recovery and environmental remediation. Their technology optimizes resource use, disrupts mining and reclamation practices, and isolates a wide range of geologic materials including precious and industrial metals, hydrocarbons, and abandoned uranium mine material.
company.name: DISA Technologies

company.description:
Our mission is to empower exceptional lawyers. 

We’re building the world’s first truly collaborative AI for legal professionals: a workspace for boundless collaboration between lawyer ingenuity and machine intelligence. 

We're serving 400+ clients across the globe and are backed by ICONIQ, General Catalyst, Benchmark, Redpoint, Y Combinator, and other top investors.
company.name: Legora

company.description: Opalia is a Montreal-based food-tech startup developing cell-based dairy by cultivating immortalised bovine mammary cells in proprietary modular bioreactors to produce whole milk and downstream products such as butter, cream, cheese and yoghurt without the cow. Founded in 2020 by CEO Jennifer Côté and CTO Lucas House, the company sells its serum-free cell-cultivated milk as a B2B ingredient that drops into existing dairy supply chains, with a first commercial supply agreement signed with Dutch dairy giant Hoogwegt and regulatory submissions underway in Canada and the United States.
company.name: Opalia

company.description: Shanghai-based embodied AI company founded in May 2025, developing a universal robot intelligence platform that pairs 3D world models with reinforcement learning for manipulating transparent, reflective, and deformable objects. Its flagship Sudo R1 demonstrates simulation-trained dexterity in unscripted real-world tests. Chief technical advisor Su Hao co-created ImageNet and pioneered PointNet at Stanford and concurrently leads Fudan University Institute of General Physical Intelligence.
company.name: Sudu Technology

company.description: Beijing-based GigaAI (also known as Jijia Vision / 极佳视界) develops world models and embodied foundation models for general-purpose robotics, including the GigaWorld and GigaBrain (Vision-Language-Action) model families. Its self-developed humanoid robot Maker H01 targets automotive, 3C electronics, warehousing and home-service applications, with a stated 2026 delivery goal of 1,000 units.
company.name: GigaAI

company.description: Suzhou-based supplier of core components and full-machine solutions for embodied intelligence and industrial robotics, founded in 2015. Linkhou (Linghou) develops visual-perception modules, motion-control parts, mobile chassis (e.g. TX-S2 wheeled chassis) and humanoid arms, and has helped customers ship embodied robots at the tens-of-thousands scale. Production bases in Wujiang (Suzhou) and Jiaxing back its push into mass-production of components for humanoid and industrial robots, with a 260+ R&D team and 200+ patents.
company.name: Linkhou Robotics

company.description: Casa is a subscription-based home maintenance platform that pairs an AI-driven concierge with a network of vetted handymen to handle ongoing repairs, projects, and proactive upkeep for homeowners. Founded by former Uber and CloudKitchens operators Michael York and Michael Mizrahi, the company emerged from two years of stealth in April 2026 with the long-term goal of building what its team calls "the autonomous home."
company.name: Casa

company.description: Highway Data Systems (HDS) is a Glasgow-based provider of automated quality assurance technology for the road construction and asphalt industries. The company combines proprietary hardware, embedded electronics and software to replace manual, labour-intensive and safety-critical testing across asphalt production, haulage and laydown. HDS serves major UK contractors including Eurovia, Holcim and Tarmac and is scaling its presence in the US market.
company.name: Highway Data Systems

company.description: Ubiqedge is a Mumbai-based deeptech startup building a full-stack AIoT operating system for industrial infrastructure, combining its KLEON hardware platform with the SAMASTH cloud software layer. The company enables real-time monitoring, control and optimisation of assets across water systems, solar energy, air quality and construction operations, and is the largest player in groundwater digitisation in India with more than 23,000 borewells digitised. Ubiqedge has been profitable since its first year of operations and is a member of the NVIDIA Inception Program.
company.name: Ubiqedge

company.description: Pickleball Inc. is the US-based parent company of Major League Pickleball (MLP) and the PPA Tour, the two largest professional pickleball organizations. Following its May 2026 capital raise, it consolidates pro leagues, retail (Pickleball Central), tournament software (PickleballTournaments.com), court infrastructure (Just Courts), and media platforms (Pickleball.com, Pickleball TV) under a single ecosystem. Its combined business verticals generated more than $140 million in 2025 revenue.
company.name: Pickleball Inc

company.description: CMBlu Energy is a German deep-tech company developing SolidFlow, a non-lithium long-duration energy storage system built from non-flammable, water-based electrolytes and earth-abundant materials. Its multi-hour batteries are aimed at utilities, industrials, and AI data centers needing dependable overnight capacity, with manufacturing scaling at an automated gigafactory in Alzenau and additional sites planned in Greece and the United States. The company employs over 250 people, including more than 150 scientists and engineers.
company.name: CMBlu Energy

company.description: Kigen is a Cambridge-based provider of eSIM, iSIM and Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP) security technologies for IoT and enterprise connectivity. Originally spun out of Arm in 2020, the company supplies GSMA-certified eSIM platforms with interoperability across 200+ terrestrial and satellite networks, and increasingly delivers its OS licensing, device management and lifecycle services via SaaS. It targets manufacturers and enterprises that need long-term secure connectivity, regulatory readiness and at-scale firmware updates across cellular IoT deployments.
company.name: Kigen

company.description: Fun is a New York-based payments infrastructure company that builds unified deposit, withdrawal, and settlement rails spanning fiat currencies and digital assets. Its API-first money layer powers high-volume consumer platforms including Polymarket, Lighter, and Aave, processing over $18 billion in annual transaction volume across more than 100 countries. Founded in 2022 by Stanford dropout Alex Fine, the company positions itself as a neutral bridge between banks, stablecoins, and onchain markets for fintechs adopting tokenized money.
company.name: Fun

company.description: UK defence-tech company building solar-powered autonomous surface vessels (Scout) and a cloud-based command platform (Tether) for persistent maritime monitoring. Targets defence, anti-submarine warfare, subsea infrastructure protection, border security and ocean data customers across Europe and globally.
company.name: Online Oceans

company.description: Standard Intelligence is a San Francisco-based AI research lab building foundation models optimized for computer use — agents that interact with software through its graphical interface. Its FDM-1 model is trained on video footage rather than annotated screenshots, using an inverse dynamics model to auto-generate captions, and ships with a video encoder the team says is roughly 100x more efficient than comparable alternatives. The six-person team is pursuing what it calls aligned artificial general intelligence by scaling agentic capabilities through video pretraining.
company.name: Standard Intelligence

company.description: Techcyte builds an AI-powered digital pathology platform that unifies anatomic and clinical pathology workflows under its Fusion product, integrating with lab systems and supporting cytology, hematology, microbiology, parasitology, and frozen-section diagnostics. Founded in 2013 and based in Orem, Utah, the company serves human, veterinary, and environmental laboratories worldwide through partnerships with scanner makers, AI developers, and reference labs including BD, Mayo Clinic, ARUP, and Zoetis.
company.name: Techcyte

company.description: Chance Studios is a Los Angeles-based superapp for trading card game (TCG) collectors, launching first as a Pokemon TCG mystery bag and vault platform with social and gamified layers on top. Founded by CEO Jun Park alongside cofounder Arvin Dabiri (whose GoatedPullz brand has spent five years in Pokemon TCG buying, selling, and vending), the company is building products like Chance Gacha, Chance Country Club, and Chance Predict to turn collecting into a multiplayer experience with instant liquidity, transparent transactions, and community-driven engagement.
company.name: Chance Studios

company.description: Moleculent is a Stockholm-based life sciences technology company developing a functional profiling platform that maps cell-to-cell communication directly in intact human tissue. Its proprietary Proximity Ligation Assay detects cell-cell interactions at high-plex while enabling cell typing through individual protein detection, and the company is building an automated instrument to scale these workflows for translational research labs. Moleculent currently makes its platform available to selected academic and biopharma partners through Techstart, an early access program ahead of full commercial launch.
company.name: Moleculent

company.description: Blomma is an AI career coaching platform aimed at extending the kind of structured, ongoing professional support traditionally reserved for executives to a much broader workforce. Its system blends user goals, memory, and contextual signals (calendars, notes, resumes, performance reviews) to coach users through feedback, high-stakes conversations, priority shifts, and leadership transitions, and produces concrete artifacts like review drafts and communication frameworks. The company launched out of stealth in April 2026 with a $25/month consumer subscription and was founded by former Pinterest and Canva executive Silvia Oviedo Lopez with Button co-founder Siddhartha Dabral.
company.name: Blomma

company.description: Illuminant Surgical is a Los Angeles-based medical device company developing Skylight, a projection-based clinical guidance system that overlays a patient's internal anatomy directly onto the body during procedures, eliminating the need for surgeons to switch between monitors or wear AR headsets. The platform combines two proprietary technologies — SkinMatch, a non-invasive computer-vision tracking system using adhesive markers, and LightScreen, a depth-adaptive projector — to deliver millimeter-level accuracy. Initially designed for spine surgery and cancer biopsies, the system is built to extend across image-guided interventions, with founding leadership drawn from Stanford and prior medtech and Apple sensing engineering experience.
company.name: Illuminant Surgical

company.description: Gymkhana Fine Foods is a premium Indian retail food brand born from the two-Michelin-starred Gymkhana restaurant in London Mayfair, run together with JKS Restaurants. The company sells restaurant-developed simmer sauces (Butter Masala, Goan Coconut Curry, Korma, Tikka Masala, Madras) and tandoori marinades through Whole Foods, Waitrose, Sainsburys and Ocado in the UK and is launching nationally in the US via an exclusive Whole Foods Market partnership. Nielsen data shows the brand as the leading growth driver across UK ambient sauces over a 26-week period, with distribution in more than 1,000 doors across UK retail.
company.name: Gymkhana Fine Foods

company.description: Greatly Health is a New York City-based healthtech company delivering integrative oncology at scale through a virtual platform that layers evidence-based supportive care — exercise, nutrition, mind-body interventions, symptom tracking and live group sessions — onto standard cancer treatment. The clinical model originated at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, where its co-founders ran randomized trials showing meaningful improvements in survival, hospitalization rates and treatment adherence. The company partners with health plans, oncology providers and life sciences companies to make integrative cancer care broadly accessible and reimbursable rather than confined to top academic centers.
company.name: Greatly Health

company.description: Kimbal is an Indian smart grid and smart metering company that builds Made-in-India advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) for utilities, AMISPs, and industrial customers. Its product stack spans the Vayu RF-mesh communications network, the Vaani cloud platform, and the Kimbal Edge Intelligence (KEI) edge-computing platform, with over 10 million AMI endpoints supported across deployments. Founded in 2011, the company operates from Delhi with engineering and commercial offices in Bengaluru, Singapore, Seoul, and Australia.
company.name: Kimbal

company.description: auryx is a Cambridge-based healthtech company building a platform that turns the microphones already inside everyday earbuds and other consumer devices into continuous health monitors. By analysing acoustic signatures from the heart, lungs and blood flow, its software extracts vital signs such as heart rate, respiratory rate, cardiac output and blood pressure without requiring additional sensors. The company spun out of more than a decade of University of Cambridge research at the intersection of machine learning and audio diagnostics.
company.name: auryx

company.description: Synaps is a Vienna-based startup building an AI-native, browser-based design canvas for architects that combines real-time collaboration with prompt-driven floorplan drawing and rendering. Founded by Albanian-born entrepreneurs, it positions itself as a challenger to AutoCAD and Revit by compressing rendering time and replacing fragmented desktop CAD tooling.
company.name: Synaps

company.description: Creati Gen AI is a San Francisco-based AI creative-tools company that develops conversational video-editing software for creators, small businesses and marketing teams. Its flagship product Creati Studio grew to over 20 million users and roughly $15M ARR within its first year, and its newest product Buzzy lets users refine generated or filmed video clips using natural-language commands. The company targets the global SMB and creator market, with parent organisation "Perceptual Leap" founded in 2021.
company.name: Creati Gen AI

company.description: Versana is a New York-based fintech building a real-time data and digital infrastructure layer for the broadly syndicated loan and private credit markets. Its platform ingests loan data directly from administrative agents' golden-source ledgers and standardises it for lenders, investors and service providers, replacing manual reconciliation and email-based workflows. Backed by major US and European banks alongside private credit and ratings investors, Versana has raised over $125 million and now covers more than $4.1 trillion in active facility notional value.
company.name: Versana

company.description: pmtbox is a Utah-based Enterprise Commerce Platform that consolidates payments, risk intelligence, and transaction data into a single system of record for merchants. Founded in 2024 by Taylor Bradshaw, Wayne Hamilton, and Pete Kledaras, it serves roughly 1,300 customers in verticals where payment complexity, fraud, and chargeback exposure are highest. The platform aims to replace fragmented point-solution stacks so operators control their data and lower the true cost of payments.
company.name: pmtbox

company.description: ALP Bio is a Swiss biotech building an immunogenicity-intelligence platform for antibody and biologics development. The Schlieren-based company combines human tonsil-derived immune organoids with generative AI to predict and reduce anti-drug antibody (ADA) risk earlier in antibody discovery, aiming to surface immunogenicity signals before clinical trials. Founded in 2025, it partners with pharma and biotech teams on lead-candidate screening, ADA risk stratification and sequence redesign.
company.name: ALP Bio

company.description: Spiral Hydrogen is an Estonian-Dutch deep-tech company developing a centrifugal, bubble-free alkaline electrolyzer aimed at making green hydrogen cheaper than fossil-based hydrogen. By rotating the electrolyte and routing gases through hydrophobic porous electrodes, the system removes the bubble formation that limits conventional electrolyzers, pushing efficiency above 90% and simplifying the balance of plant. Founded in 2024 in Tartu, the company is moving from lab prototypes to an industrial pilot at the Port of Rotterdam in partnership with SwitcH2, targeting feedstock markets like ammonia, refining, methanol, and steel.
company.name: Spiral Hydrogen

company.description: Netomi is a San Francisco-based enterprise agentic AI platform for customer experience, deployed by global brands including Delta Air Lines, United Airlines, MetLife, Paramount, DraftKings, NBA, and Ingram Micro across chat, email, and voice channels. Founded by CEO Puneet Mehta, the company builds production-grade AI agents engineered for high-stakes, regulated environments operating at massive concurrent scale. Netomi was featured in an OpenAI case study on scaling agentic systems into the enterprise.
company.name: Netomi

company.description: JuliaHub develops Dyad, an AI-native engineering platform that combines autonomous agents with physics-based simulation and Scientific Machine Learning to design and validate industrial systems across aerospace, automotive, HVAC, semiconductors, energy, and utilities. Founded in 2015 by the creators of the open-source Julia programming language at MIT, the Cambridge, Massachusetts company counts Fortune 100 enterprises and government researchers among its customers and positions itself as a Simulink alternative for the emerging Physical AI stack.
company.name: JuliaHub

company.description: StudentCrowd is a UK-based student accommodation data and review platform that verifies user reviews via active university email addresses, combining authentic peer insight with structured property profiles for purpose-built student accommodation (PBSA). It serves students researching where to study and live as well as the UK's largest PBSA operators, with coverage of the full UK PBSA market across hundreds of thousands of beds and tenancy types. Founded by Paul Humphreys and Pete Sisson, the company is now extending its model into mainland Europe and the United States as AI-powered search increases demand for trusted, structured housing data.
company.name: StudentCrowd

company.description: XO Market is a permissionless, on-chain prediction-market platform where any user can spin up a market, set its parameters and fees, and let others trade on the outcome — positioning itself as the "YouTube of prediction markets" against curated incumbents like Kalshi and Polymarket. Built on Celestia, the platform is in mainnet beta with more than 600 user-created markets, 30,000+ users, and over $150M in trading volume since launch. The team is rolling out XO Vaults, a pooled-liquidity product targeting 8-10% annual yield to let anyone act as a market maker.
company.name: XO Market

company.description: Ctruh is a Bengaluru-based deep-tech startup building infrastructure for the spatial internet, providing an AI-led 3D and XR platform that lets brands generate immersive web experiences. Founded by Vinay Agastya, the company plans US and UAE launches in 2026.
company.name: Ctruh

company.description: Photon is a New York-based healthcare technology company building end-to-end prescription infrastructure that connects prescribers, pharmacies, and patients. Its platform pairs a digital prescribing and routing engine with a multi-pharmacy network and a consumer-facing marketplace that surfaces real-time pricing and stock at the moment a prescription is written. Founded in 2021, Photon supports prior authorization, clinical decision support, and in-house pharmacy workflows for direct-to-consumer health brands and hospital systems.
company.name: Photon

company.description: Groove Quantum is a Delft-based quantum computing startup, spun out of QuTech in 2024, developing scalable germanium spin-qubit processors. Its architecture uses CMOS-compatible semiconductor manufacturing so quantum chips can be produced through the same foundries that make modern CPUs and GPUs. The company is led by CEO Anne-Marije Zwerver and CTO Nico Hendrickx, and is targeting a path from its 18-qubit prototype toward million-qubit systems.
company.name: Groove Quantum

company.description: BioOrbit is a London-based space-biotech company developing in-orbit pharmaceutical manufacturing for high-value biologics. Its compact modular hardware (BOX) uses microgravity to crystallise protein-based drugs more precisely than is possible on Earth, with the goal of reformulating intravenous biologics — including cancer therapies — as subcutaneous, self-injectable treatments. The company is building toward industrial-scale in-orbit production and is engaged with the UK Space Agency, NHS, MHRA, CAA and Regulatory Innovation Office on the regulatory pathway for space-manufactured medicines.
company.name: BioOrbit

company.description: Featherless.ai is a Singapore-headquartered serverless inference platform that gives developers and enterprises one-API access to 30,000+ open-source AI models across language, vision, and audio. It positions itself as a neutral, hyperscaler-agnostic layer for open-source AI, with infrastructure in the US and EU and a hardware-diversity strategy that runs popular models natively on AMD ROCm. Founded by the team behind the RWKV open-source architecture under the Linux Foundation, the company offers flat-rate pricing as an alternative to per-token billing from incumbent inference clouds.
company.name: Featherless.ai

company.description: Mosaic SoC is a Swiss semiconductor startup designing dedicated perception chips that bring real-time spatial intelligence to energy-constrained devices such as smart glasses, AR wearables, and smartphones. Its proprietary multi-core architecture is engineered to maximize performance per watt, enabling always-on visual and positional sensing without the bulk and battery cost of repurposed application processors. Founded in 2024 by ETH Zurich PhDs Alfio Di Mauro and Moritz Scherer, the company pairs custom silicon with an integrated AI deployment toolchain that ODMs build their products on top of.
company.name: Mosaic SoC

company.description: Oolka is India's first agentic AI credit management platform, helping users track credit scores, improve financial health, and access personalized credit solutions. Founded in 2023 by Utkrishta Kumar, former Chief Business Officer at Meesho, the platform leverages AI agents to automate credit management workflows and democratize financial access for Indian consumers.
company.name: Oolka

company.description: Cirql One builds a Business Context Layer that extracts the workflows, pricing rules, approval logic, and decision structures embedded in enterprise systems like SAP and formalizes them into reusable, governed assets that AI applications, analytics teams, and autonomous agents can act on directly. Founded in January 2025 in Heidelberg by SAP veterans Gregor Stoeckler, Stefan Hockenberger and Tara von Metzen, the company is already in production with enterprise customers including dm.
company.name: Cirql One

company.description: Quick-service restaurant chain operating a portfolio of brands (Wow! Momo, Wow! China, Wow! Chicken, Wow! Kulfi) with 700+ outlets focused on fast-casual food and FMCG expansion.
company.name: Wow! Momo

company.description: Aidoc develops AI-powered clinical decision support tools that assist physicians in radiology, cardiology, neurovascular, vascular, and oncology care. Their platform integrates AI into clinical workflows to improve diagnosis, treatment, and patient outcomes, supporting over 45 million patients annually across more than 150 health systems worldwide.
company.name: Aidoc

company.description: Hightouch is a leading data and AI agent platform for marketers that enables companies to activate their data warehouse to power personalized marketing and business operations. It offers AI Decisioning, an agentic AI product for lifecycle marketing that automates personalized messaging, timing, and channel decisions for individual customers, helping marketers execute faster and with more control.
company.name: Hightouch

company.description: Firestorm Labs is a California-based manufacturer specializing in unmanned aerial systems (UAS) that leverages expeditionary additive manufacturing and proprietary 3D-printed, interconnected, and interchangeable airframe components to enable rapid drone production at the edge.
company.name: Firestorm Labs

company.description:
Generative AI for Finance. 

Contact us at sales@rogo.ai or visit us at rogo.ai to learn more.
company.name: Rogo

company.description: ROSI is a French cleantech company developing high-value photovoltaic module recycling technology that recovers silver, silicon, copper, aluminium and glass from end-of-life solar panels for the European circular-economy supply chain.
company.name: ROSI

company.description: Dholakia Lab Grown Diamond (DLGD) is a Surat-based, vertically integrated lab-grown diamond and jewellery manufacturer that grows, cuts, polishes, designs, and distributes LGDs to over 100 retailers across 25+ countries. It is the lab-grown diamond arm of the 33-year-old Hari Krishna Group and runs proprietary diamond-growing, robotic manufacturing and AI-assisted design lines powered by renewable energy. Beyond jewellery, DLGD is developing precision single-crystal diamonds for optical, quantum, thermal, semiconductor and defence applications.
company.name: Dholakia Lab Grown Diamond

company.description: Volant Aerotech is a Shanghai-based developer of high-grade commercial passenger eVTOL aircraft, specializing in research, design, and manufacturing of electric vertical take-off and landing platforms for urban air mobility. Its flagship VE25-100 is a six-seat composite-wing aircraft with a 235 km/h top speed and 200-400 km cruising range, currently in crewed flight testing with airworthiness certification targeted for 2027. The company has secured more than 1,900 preorders from domestic and overseas customers and operates a smart manufacturing and flight-test base in Zigong with a designed annual capacity of 300 aircraft.
company.name: Volant Aerotech

company.description: Manifest is an AI-powered operational intelligence platform that automatically captures how time, effort and workflows move across an advertising agency's teams, internal tools and AI assistants, replacing timesheet-based reporting with a real-time view of how work is created and what it costs. Founded by Freddie McKenzie (CEO), Henry Collinson (CPO) and Tom Reid (CTO), the trans-Tasman startup is launching with a cohort of agencies across Sydney, Melbourne and Auckland and is preparing for global expansion. The platform pitches itself as the operating system agencies need as generative AI compresses creative production cycles and decouples value from billable hours.
company.name: Manifest

company.description: EnteroBiotix is a Glasgow-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing full-spectrum microbiome therapeutics for gut-health indications. Its lead candidate, EBX-102-02, is an orally delivered investigational microbiome therapy advancing into Phase 2b development for Irritable Bowel Syndrome with Constipation (IBS-C). The company is building a pipeline targeting high-value gastrointestinal conditions where unmet need has persisted for decades.
company.name: EnteroBiotix

company.description: Kibu is a Washington, DC-based human network and app-based platform that cryptographically binds real human identity to verified interactions, enabling trusted communication and authorization in the AI era. The platform uses biometric-protected devices, liveness testing, and cryptographic proof to verify users and build networks of confirmed contacts for high-stakes work. Customers include financial institutions, family offices, government agencies, defense contractors, and security consultants coordinating sensitive work across organizational boundaries.
company.name: Kibu

company.description: Tacalyx is a Berlin-based biotech developing antibody-based cancer therapies aimed at Tumour Associated Carbohydrate Antigens (TACAs), glycan structures overexpressed on tumour cells but largely absent from healthy tissue. Spun out of the Max-Planck-Institute of Colloids and Interfaces in 2019, the company runs a proprietary discovery platform that generates high-affinity antibodies against historically undruggable carbohydrate targets and uses them to build antibody-drug conjugates and other modalities for solid tumours. Its lead programme TCX-201 is an ADC selected as the first clinical candidate, with a planned clinical trial application in 2027.
company.name: Tacalyx

company.description: Aigentsphere is a Sydney-based enterprise AI governance platform that gives organisations a unified control layer to register, monitor, and manage autonomous AI agents across functions like customer service, operations, and internal productivity. The platform tracks performance and costs in real time, enforces policies, and automatically generates compliance reporting, and is designed to be agnostic to the underlying AI models. Co-founded by former Optus CEO Kelly Bayer Rosmarin and CEO Quinton Anderson, it is already piloted by enterprises in insurance, media, legal, and financial services.
company.name: Aigentsphere

company.description: Bug Bounty Switzerland (BBS) is a Lucerne-based cybersecurity firm that delivers continuous, AI-driven security testing through its Cyber Resilience Shield subscription platform, orchestrating more than 16,000 vetted ethical hackers alongside offensive AI agents. Founded in 2020 by Sandro Nafzger, Florian Badertscher and Lukas Heppler — and built on Switzerland's first bug bounty programmes from 2015 — the bootstrapped, profitable company serves over 50 enterprise customers across financial services, defence, critical infrastructure and the public sector, including the Swiss National Cyber Security Centre.
company.name: Bug Bounty Switzerland

company.description: definity is a Chicago-based agentic data engineering platform that embeds AI agents inside enterprise lakehouse and Spark pipelines. Its in-motion runtime intelligence helps data teams cut platform cost, prevent pipeline incidents before they impact production, and validate code or upgrade changes automatically.
company.name: definity

company.description: viteSicure is a Milan-based digital insurance broker focused on making term life and protection policies accessible online in Italy. Founded in 2021 by Eleonora Del Vento and Alessandro Turra, the company runs a fully digital, plug-and-play platform for embedded insurance in the life-protection segment, and reached break-even with positive EBITDA at year-end 2025.
company.name: viteSicure

company.description: Dreambase is an Austin-based AI analytics platform that lets product, engineering and operator users query their Postgres database directly, generating dashboards, analyses and insights without staffing a dedicated data team. The product is built Supabase-native — connecting to a Supabase project to surface answers in seconds — while remaining usable on any Postgres database underneath. Founded in 2024 by Andy Keil and Kyle Ledbetter, the company is positioning itself as the analytics layer for the Postgres-native generation of software companies.
company.name: Dreambase

company.description: Vikra Ocean Tech is a Chennai-based deeptech startup developing an indigenous, modular ocean robotics ecosystem. Its hardware stack spans deep-water ROVs, autonomous surface vessels, amphibious crawling robots, and AI-enabled imaging and communication systems, all unified under a single control platform. The company targets defence, subsea inspection, hydrographic surveys, and disaster response, aiming to reduce India's reliance on imported underwater robotics.
company.name: Vikra Ocean Tech

company.description: Prithu is a Gurugram-based climate technology startup building a full-stack carbon removal platform rooted in Indian smallholder agriculture. It combines regenerative farming, biochar, agroforestry, and biogas interventions with a blockchain-powered digital MRV system that uses satellite imagery, soil sampling, and machine learning to issue verifiable carbon credits aligned with Verra, Gold Standard, and Puro. Founded in 2024, the company has onboarded over 35,000 farmers across 30,000 hectares and is targeting 20 million tonnes of CO2e sequestration by 2030.
company.name: Prithu

company.description: Rocsys is a Dutch-American robotics company that builds hands-free charging and depot-service systems for electric and autonomous vehicles. Its overhead rail-mounted robotic arm uses AI-driven computer vision and soft robotics to plug, charge and unplug EVs without human intervention, originally proven in port and heavy-truck operations and now scaling into robotaxi fleets. The company is dual-headquartered in Rijswijk, Netherlands and Portland, Oregon, and holds 130+ granted patents and pending applications.
company.name: Rocsys

company.description: Misti AI is a London-based startup building "Physical Observability" software that turns existing industrial camera feeds into real-time operational intelligence. Its memory-based vision-language models run at the edge to flag unsafe behaviour, missing PPE, equipment issues, and process deviations across heavy-industry sites, with early deployments in mining and energy operations in Peru. The company is part of the NVIDIA Inception Program and operates across the UK and Latin America.
company.name: Misti AI

company.description: General Analysis is a San Francisco-based AI security company that helps enterprises safely deploy autonomous agents through context-aware adversarial red-teaming, vulnerability scanning, and runtime guardrails. Founded in 2025 by ex-Cohere/NVIDIA, Harvard, and Caltech researchers, it already serves enterprise customers in support and finance whose products and workflows reach hundreds of millions of users.
company.name: General Analysis

company.description: OpenObserve is a Menlo Park, California-based provider of an open-source observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, and real user monitoring in a single interface. Built on an S3-native Parquet architecture that separates compute from storage, the platform targets significantly lower operating costs than legacy stacks like Datadog or the ELK stack while supporting petabyte-scale telemetry. Founded in 2022 by Prabhat Sharma, the company is now layering AI-native capabilities on top — autonomous AI SRE, anomaly detection, and LLM observability — under what it calls Observability 3.0.
company.name: OpenObserve

company.description: Chord is a New York-based AI platform for modern commerce operations, founded in 2021 by former Glossier executives Bryan Mahoney and Henry Davis. The platform unifies a brand's data, business rules, and historical decisions into a "context graph" that lets AI agents run core ecommerce workflows in real time rather than producing static dashboards. Customers include Mr. Beast, Ritual, Ruggable, Rodan + Fields, and Blue Bottle Coffee, and the platform serves mid-market and enterprise commerce teams that collectively represent more than $1 billion in annual revenue.
company.name: Chord

company.description: Squads is a New York-based financial infrastructure company building on Solana. It operates Squads Multisig, a smart-account protocol that secures over $10B in assets across the Solana ecosystem, and Altitude, a stablecoin-native financial operating system that gives businesses multi-currency accounts, corporate cards, global payments, yield on balances and a CFO toolkit. The platform routes settlement on Solana 24/7 while connecting into traditional banking rails through a network of licensed stablecoin PSPs.
company.name: Squads

company.description: Counterpart is a US specialty InsurTech building Agentic Insurance for management and professional liability in the AI era, combining deep underwriting expertise with AI-driven risk management and claims tooling for small and mid-sized businesses.
