The company underwrites through a 2,800-broker network supported by four A-rated carriers and operates its own paper via Counterpart Insurance Company. Founded by CEO Tanner Hackett, it positions itself as the first carrier purpose-built for AI-era employment, content, and operational liability risks.
company.name: Counterpart

company.description: Certifyde is a New York-based AI adoption platform for modern businesses. Its flagship Productivity Companion is a browser extension that overlays existing tools like Salesforce, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Google Workspace, delivering role-specific AI guidance, reinforcing usage habits, and issuing verified AI fluency credentials. A complementary Academy product gives organizations a centralized hub for compliance training and custom certification programs, with active engagements spanning the NYC Department of Transportation, nationwide biomedical labs, and the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System.
company.name: Certifyde

company.description: Vivacta Biotechnology (Shanghai) is a clinical-stage biotech developing in vivo CAR-T cell therapies for cancer and autoimmune disease. Its lead program, GT801, reprograms immune cells inside the patient rather than via traditional ex vivo manufacturing, with early human data presented at ASH 2025 covering hematological malignancies and autoimmune indications. The company is headquartered in Shanghai and is backed by global healthcare and Chinese growth investors.
company.name: Vivacta Bio

company.description: All3 is a UK-based deep-tech company building an integrated platform for construction automation, combining AI-driven design software, robotic factories that manufacture custom structural components off-site, and an autonomous legged robot called Mantis that handles on-site assembly. Founded by repeat entrepreneurs Rodion Shishkov and Slava Bocharov (previously co-founders of Russian rapid-grocery unicorn Samokat), the company targets up to 30% cost savings and 50% timeline reductions versus traditional construction, with first commercial deployments in Germany. R&D is run from London and Belgrade, with operational offices in Berlin and Zug.
company.name: All3

company.description: Axoft is a Cambridge, Massachusetts neurotechnology company developing implantable brain-computer interfaces (iBCIs) built on its proprietary Fleuron material, which is engineered to be roughly 10,000x softer than the polyimide used in conventional neural implants. The company was co-founded in 2021 by Paul Le Floch, Tianyang Ye, and Harvard professor Jia Liu, and has implanted its devices in 11 patients across early clinical studies aimed at improving prognosis and communication for people with neurological disorders. Axoft also commercializes Fleuron for biomedical applications under an exclusive licensing agreement with Stanford University.
company.name: Axoft

company.description: Pursuit is an AI-powered platform that helps companies find and win government contracts across the U.S. state, local, and education (SLED) market. Its systems continuously ingest public data from roughly 11,000 SLED entities — budgets, contract registers, FOIA records, and RFPs — and surface buying signals 6 to 18 months before procurements go public, so vendors can engage decision-makers before formal solicitations drop. Founded in 2023 by Wisely alums Mike Vichich and Brandon Max, the Ann Arbor company counts Adobe, Samsara, Datadog, Granicus, CentralSquare, and Tyler Technologies among its customers.
company.name: Pursuit

company.description: Optimized Electrotech is an Ahmedabad-based deeptech company that designs and manufactures indigenous electro-optic surveillance and imaging systems for India's defence and space sectors. Founded in 2017 by Sandeep Shah and Dharin Shah, it supplies the Indian Army, Indian Navy, Bharat Electronics, Bharat Dynamics and ISRO, and is now extending its AI-native sensing platforms from ground deployments to satellite constellations. Multiple iDEX wins (including ADITI) and four patents anchor its position at the intersection of defence, space and AI.
company.name: Optimized Electrotech

company.description: Udora, formerly Flowwow, is a Dubai-headquartered gifting marketplace that connects customers with independent local florists, confectioners and artisan makers across more than 50 countries and 1,500 cities. The platform gives small sellers digital infrastructure for orders, marketing and customer access, with strongholds in the UAE and rapidly growing share in European markets such as Spain, Germany, Poland and the UK. The 2026 rebrand separates the international business from its former Russian operations so the company can focus entirely on global expansion, deeper localisation and AI-powered gift personalisation.
company.name: Udora

company.description: Crazy Mountain is a Los Angeles-based non-alcoholic beer brand co-founded by George Clooney, Rande Gerber, and Mike Meldman — the trio behind Casamigos tequila — and led by CEO Steve Fechheimer, formerly of New Belgium Brewing. The brand launched with two SKUs (Original and Lime) targeting mainstream beer drinkers seeking moderation, and is sold direct-to-consumer via crazymountain.com with a U.S. retail rollout planned through 2026.
company.name: Crazy Mountain

company.description: Blockworks is a New York-based crypto data, research, and intelligence platform aiming to become the institutional standard for digital asset analytics. The company operates Blockworks Research and Blockworks Intelligence, delivering on-chain data, governance tracking, and market analysis to hedge funds, family offices, and institutional crypto investors. After shuttering its news division in late 2025, Blockworks has refocused on building a software and data infrastructure stack — positioning itself as a Morningstar-style reference for tokenized assets — and runs the Digital Assets Summit institutional conference series.
company.name: Blockworks

company.description: Fence builds internet-native infrastructure for asset-backed finance, replacing legacy trust and agency providers (verification, calculation, collateral, and paying agents) with a single smart-contract platform that verifies underlying assets, enforces credit agreements, and moves cash and collateral programmatically in real time. The platform supports warehouse facilities, securitizations, and forward flow agreements across asset classes including invoice financing, BNPL, consumer and auto loans, mortgages, credit-card receivables, and Earned Wage Access, and is used by financial institutions including BlackRock, Fortress, i80 Group, and BBVA.
company.name: Fence

company.description: Primogene is a Leipzig-based biotechnology company founded in 2023 that uses a multi-enzyme platform to manufacture complex bioactive molecules — including human milk oligosaccharides such as DSLNT and LNDFH I — at industrial scale, identical to their natural counterparts. The platform sidesteps cost and complexity limits of conventional fermentation, and supplies functional ingredients for infant nutrition, adult and senior health, personal care, and pharmaceutical raw materials.
company.name: Primogene

company.description: Scout AI is a frontier AI lab building Fury, a Vision-Language-Action foundation model for defense robotics that translates commander intent into coordinated autonomous action across drones and unmanned ground vehicles. The company sells a software intelligence layer rather than the vehicles themselves, with early use cases focused on automated logistics, reconnaissance, and command-and-control of mixed unmanned fleets. Founded in 2024 by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis, Scout AI works with the U.S. Army, DARPA, and other Department of Defense customers from its Sunnyvale, California base.
company.name: Scout AI

company.description: Cleo Labs is a Paris-based RegTech startup that automates international product regulatory compliance through an AI-powered platform. Founded in 2023 by Naomie Halioua and Anaëlle Guez, the company's proprietary MARIA (Multi-Agent Regulatory Intelligence Architecture) continuously monitors more than 25,000 regulatory authorities across 106 countries and generates per-product, per-market compliance checklists, with each output validated by legal experts in a human-in-the-loop workflow. Early pilot users include Decathlon.
company.name: Cleo Labs

company.description: Hypervision Surgical is a King's College London spin-out developing real-time hyperspectral imaging for surgery. Its Hyperspectral Intelligence platform combines proprietary on-chip spectral sensing with cloud-based AI analytics to give surgeons quantitative tissue insights beyond what conventional cameras can perceive, with the goal of integrating into laparoscopic, robotic, microscopic, and endoscopic surgical platforms. Its commercial system HYPERSNAP is UK-certified and FDA-cleared for open and minimally invasive general surgery.
company.name: Hypervision Surgical

company.description: FORMAS.AI is a Singapore-based AI-native design platform for architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC), giving architects and designers a single workspace to sketch, render, iterate, and present rather than stitching together fragmented point tools. The platform orchestrates more than 60 specialist AI models — including Google Gemini and Luma — to preserve a designer's creative intent at every step. Since its public beta in November 2025, users have generated over 500,000 designs across 135 countries through organic inbound demand.
company.name: FORMAS.AI

company.description: SPREAD is a Berlin-based industrial AI company building an Engineering Intelligence Platform that unifies product data from PLM, ERP, requirements management and field-service systems into a single navigable ontology that engineers and AI agents can query. Its Product Twin offering is used by automotive and defense OEMs including Volkswagen, Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Audi, Daimler Truck and Rheinmetall to accelerate development cycles, troubleshoot engineering defects and reduce warranty risk. Founded in 2019 by Philipp Noll and Robert Göbel, the company positions itself as Europe's challenger in AI-native infrastructure for complex, software-defined hardware.
company.name: SPREAD AI

company.description: Mumbai-based sports-tech startup providing online streaming, AI-driven production, and grassroots sports talent discovery. Streams over 500,000 matches across 30+ countries reaching 100M+ viewers, with growing traction across North America, Europe, the Middle East and Australia. Founded 2019.
company.name: SportVot

company.description: Mobile-first trading platform offering professional-grade tools for individual traders, including futures and options, stocks trading, single-screen experience, intuitive charts, AI-driven insights, educational content, real-time alerts, and simple interface for everyday investors.
company.name: Sahi

company.description: B2B SaaS platform providing logistics planning and route optimisation for mid-to-large enterprises in CPG, retail, courier services, dairy, and e-commerce sectors, using AI, operations research, machine learning, and big data to reduce costs by up to 20%.
company.name: Mojro

company.description: Iantrek is a pioneer in bio-interventional ophthalmic surgery (BIOS), focusing on minimally invasive glaucoma surgery solutions.
company.name: Iantrek, Inc.

company.description: Parallel Web Systems is a Palo Alto-based AI startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal in 2023. It develops a cloud-based deep research API that enables AI agents to perform real-time, large-scale web research and synthesis, outperforming leading AI models like GPT-5 on complex web-centric tasks.
company.name: Parallel Web Systems

company.description: Marloo is an AI assistant for financial advisers, designed to streamline financial advisory processes.
company.name: Marloo Limited

company.description:
With decades of experience in photonics design, our executive and engineering teams are delivering the world’s first open silicon photonics platform with integrated lasers.

You can improve the performance, power efficiency, and reliability of your design using the OpenLight platform for datacom, telecom, LiDAR, healthcare, HPC, AI, and optical computing applications. With over 200 patents and technology adoption by companies across industries, we’re bringing optical solutions to places they’ve never been before, and we’re enabling technologies that weren’t previously possible.
company.name: OpenLight

company.description: Aventera Antibodies is a Swedish biotech spun out of Lund University in 2025, developing precision antibody therapies—so-called "super-antibodies"—derived from patients with natural protection against severe bacterial infections. Its lead program targets invasive Group A streptococcal disease ("flesh-eating bacteria"), a rare condition with roughly 30% mortality and no effective targeted therapy. The company is based at SmiLe Venture Hub in Medicon Village and has demonstrated proof of concept in animal models, with clinical trials planned for 2028.
company.name: Aventera Antibodies

company.description: Segura is a Brazilian insurtech building an AI-powered operating layer connecting insurance brokers and carriers across Latin America. Its platform manages quoting, policy administration, renewals, submissions, and billing across multiple carriers, with a WhatsApp-native broker workflow centered on Helena, an AI assistant that interprets policy data and SUSEP regulatory information. Co-founded in 2024 by Luis Alberto Nogueira, Lucca Buffara, and Pedro Nobrega, Segura emerged from stealth in April 2026 and reports more than 3,000 brokerage firms on the platform.
company.name: Segura

company.description: Kovon is a cross-border workforce mobility platform connecting India's blue- and grey-collar workers to overseas jobs across healthcare, hospitality, construction, and logistics. The company offers an end-to-end stack covering job discovery, upskilling, financing, placement, and post-deployment support, targeting labour-deficient markets in Europe, the UK, and the Middle East. Founded by Bibartan Roy and Swayamjeet Das, it aims to replace fragmented, often exploitative recruitment channels with verified, job-ready talent pipelines.
company.name: Kovon

company.description: Pictoria is a Tokyo-based AI character company that handles end-to-end planning, voice synthesis, 3D asset production, and operation of conversational AI characters via its proprietary platform. Its solutions are deployed by enterprises across publishing, banking, real estate, airports, sports, and municipal services, and its consumer titles include the AI VTuber Tsumugi Nen and the dialogue app AI KARESHIKUN.
company.name: Pictoria

company.description: Belo is a Buenos Aires-based crypto-native fintech that operates a multi-currency wallet and crypto Mastercard for Latin American consumers, letting users hold Argentine pesos, Brazilian reais, US-dollar stablecoins (USDC and USDT), and Bitcoin in a single account. Founded in 2021 by Manuel Beaudroit and Edwin Rager, Belo combines cross-border transfers, FX, and local spending — including Pix payments in Brazil — into one app, and has reportedly grown to over 3 million users while remaining profitable. The company positions stablecoin rails as the answer to slow, expensive cross-border money movement in emerging markets.
company.name: Belo

company.description: Custodia is a New York-based fintech building infrastructure for AI-driven corporate spend and expense management. Its platform automates corporate spending, expense approvals, and budgeting for enterprises, and exposes embedded spend-management primitives to issuers, processors, and channel partners launching dynamic payment products. Founded in 2018 by Elad Efraim, Jim Hackett, and Jonathan Heller, the company competes with Navan, Ramp, and Brex.
company.name: Custodia AI

company.description: Sonibel Instruments is a Vancouver-founded startup that builds a torch-mounted acoustic sensor paired with machine-learning models to detect welding defects in real time, replacing post-weld X-ray and ultrasound inspections with on-the-spot alerts on a small display. Founded by University of British Columbia graduates Sophia Millar (CEO), Hooman Pirouz (CPO), and George Hollo (CTO), the company is running its first paid pilot with a Fortune 100 power-transmission manufacturer and has a waitlist of roughly 50 fabrication shops. Sonibel has incorporated in Delaware and is laying the groundwork to relocate operations to the United States while maintaining a Canadian subsidiary.
company.name: Sonibel Instruments

company.description: Truentity Health is a Chapel Hill, NC-based clinical care delivery network that turns community pharmacies into continuous chronic-care delivery sites. The company combines AI-driven software, physician-governed clinical oversight, billing, and compliance infrastructure so pharmacies can run reimbursable Medicare and Medicaid chronic-care programs without adding staff. Its multi-state network has been adopted by partners including CPESN USA for a national Remote Care Initiative, with a focus on rural and underserved communities under the federal Rural Health Transformation Program.
company.name: Truentity Health

company.description: Always Friday is an Italian AI-native platform that automates corporate event planning end-to-end, covering venue sourcing, supplier selection, contracts, operations, and payments. Its proprietary AI agents contact venues, negotiate pricing and availability, and coordinate suppliers, automating up to 90% of operational tasks and compressing planning workflows from weeks to hours. The Latina-based company serves more than 100 enterprise and mid-market customers in Italy, including Sennder, Docebo, Hisense, Credit Agricole, and Dolce & Gabbana.
company.name: Always Friday

company.description: Lighthouse Pharmaceuticals is a Novato, California clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing small-molecule therapies for chronic neurodegenerative and inflammatory diseases. Its lead program, LHP588, is an oral, once-daily gingipain inhibitor in a Phase 2 trial (SPRING) targeting P. gingivalis-positive Alzheimer's disease, supported by a $49.2M NIA grant. The company recently relocated its headquarters to the Buck Institute for Research on Aging.
company.name: Lighthouse Pharmaceuticals

company.description: mbiomics is a Munich-based techbio company developing live biotherapeutic products (LBPs) — orally administered, pharmaceutical-grade complex microbial consortia — for severe and chronic diseases. Its proprietary platform combines AI/ML-driven consortia design, high-resolution microbiome profiling, and large-scale co-cultivation to make microbiome therapeutics scalable. The company's lead candidate MBX-116 is being advanced toward a Phase 1B clinical trial in 2027 as a co-therapy with immune checkpoint inhibitors in advanced melanoma, with a broader pipeline targeting autoimmune and neurodegenerative indications.
company.name: mbiomics

company.description: Pathos Logos provides an integrated HR SaaS platform serving over 300 large Japanese enterprises. Its products include the PathosLogos personnel database and Combosite payroll/HR software, with funding directed at AI agent capability expansion.
company.name: Pathos Logos

company.description: Credilio Financial Technologies is a Mumbai-based fintech that operates novio, a fixed-deposit-backed RuPay credit card app. Customers open an FD with a partner bank and receive a credit card without needing income proof or a prior credit history, while the deposit continues to earn interest. The platform targets early jobbers, students, gig workers, freelancers and small business owners outside India's formal credit system.
company.name: Credilio

company.description: Actively is a New York-based AI startup building an Intelligence-Led Revenue platform for enterprise sales teams, deploying a persistent AI agent for every account that researches, prospects and advances deals 24/7. Founded in 2022 by former Stanford AI researchers Mihir Garimella (CEO) and Anshul Gupta, the company integrates with existing tools like Salesforce, email and Slack to automate work that human reps would otherwise do manually. Customers include Ramp, Samsara, Attentive, Ironclad and Verkada.
company.name: Actively

company.description: Battery Smart operates India's largest battery-as-a-service (BaaS) network, letting drivers of electric two- and three-wheelers swap depleted lithium-ion batteries for fully charged ones in minutes through a partner-led station network. Headquartered in Gurugram and founded in 2019 by Pulkit Khurana and Siddharth Sikka, the company has scaled to roughly 1,500-1,600 stations across 50+ cities and powers last-mile delivery and passenger-transport drivers across major Indian urban and semi-urban markets.
company.name: Battery Smart

company.description: NUVA Digital is the software developer behind NUVA, a non-custodial marketplace for tokenized real-world asset (RWA) vaults. Users deposit USDC and receive yield-bearing nvAsset tokens with on-chain proof of reserves and no lockups, accessing curated vaults from leading issuers across multiple chains. Co-incubated by Animoca Brands and Nuva Labs (formerly Provenance Blockchain Labs), NUVA launches with vaults backed by Figure Technologies' SEC-registered YLDS yielding stablecoin and HELOC home equity loans.
company.name: Nuva

company.description: Zamp is a vertically integrated sales-tax compliance platform that combines AI agents with in-house tax and legal professionals to own the full compliance lifecycle — registration, filing, taxability, notices, and data normalization — across all 50 U.S. states and more than 100 countries. Marketed as the "Operating System of Sales Tax," the company sources its own tax data across 12,000+ jurisdictions and offers a penalty guarantee to its customers and accounting-firm partners. Founded in 2023 by CEO Rohit Bhadange, Zamp serves direct merchants such as Crumbl Cookies and Oats Overnight as well as 120+ accounting firms including Withum, Anchin, Frank Rimerman and Larson Gross.
company.name: Zamp

company.description: Manifest OS is a New York-based startup building the operating system for AI-native law firms, combining a unified brand (Manifest Law), an AI platform that automates client communications, document drafting and legal workflows, and a centralized back office for paralegals and admin staff. The company partners with attorneys to launch firms that use fixed-fee and outcomes-based pricing instead of the billable hour, starting with U.S. business immigration under Arizona's Alternative Business Structure programme. It plans to expand the model into global immigration and additional legal practice areas.
company.name: Manifest OS

company.description: Dex is a London-based AI-powered recruiting platform that bills itself as an AI talent agent for engineers and hiring teams. Candidates hold an open-ended voice or text conversation with the agent about their experience, motivations, and ambitions, and the platform then matches them to roles via a proprietary engine and hands warm intros to the hiring manager. Founded in early 2025 by ex-Atomico head of talent Paddy Lambros and engineer Harry Uglow, Dex focuses on AI researchers, software developers, and ML and quantitative engineers, and counts Lovable, ElevenLabs, Synthesia, Granola, and Fyxer among more than 50 customer companies.
company.name: Dex

company.description: Liquid Instruments builds software-defined test and measurement instruments, consolidating multiple lab tools into a single FPGA-based reconfigurable platform sold under the Moku product line. The company serves thousands of users across aerospace, defense, semiconductor, and quantum research, including national labs, university physics departments, and electronics manufacturers. Founded in 2014 with offices in San Diego and Canberra, it positions its platform as an AI-enabled alternative to dedicated bench instruments.
company.name: Liquid Instruments

company.description: Searchdoc is a Korean AI startup founded in 2025 that builds search and analysis technology for very large documents and industrial drawings — files that conventional large language models struggle to process. Its platform serves construction, EPC, and IT customers with precise document search and agent-based workflow automation, and is already generating revenue through contracts with major Korean enterprises.
company.name: Searchdoc

company.description: Cybord is an Israeli company that builds a Visual AI platform for inspecting, authenticating, and tracing electronic components directly on SMT production lines. Its software helps OEMs detect defects, counterfeits, tampering, and unauthorized substitutions before they reach the field, and is used across industries where component integrity is mission-critical, including data center infrastructure, aerospace and defense, automotive, telecom, and medical devices.
company.name: Cybord

company.description: Coultreon Biopharma, formerly Onco3R Therapeutics, is a Leuven-based biotechnology company developing oral therapies for autoimmune and metabolic diseases. Its lead program, COL-5671, is a highly selective once-daily SIK3 inhibitor in Phase 1 studies, originally developed by Galapagos and transferred to Coultreon in 2025. The company targets salt-inducible kinase biology to modulate inflammatory pathways across indications including psoriasis, ulcerative colitis and rheumatoid arthritis.
company.name: Coultreon Biopharma

company.description: Founded in 2006, IC Realtime is a Pompano Beach, Florida-based provider of professional-grade video surveillance and security technology, including cameras, digital video recorders, and management software sold to dealers, integrators, commercial accounts, and consumers across all 50 states and Canada. The company is expanding its AI and cloud-based analytics capabilities to evolve from hardware-led offerings toward a unified software platform combining video management, edge intelligence, and cloud analytics.
company.name: IC Realtime

company.description: Windmill is a New York-based AI-native performance management platform that automates performance reviews by syncing with workplace tools such as Slack, GitHub, Jira, Linear, Asana, and Salesforce to continuously collect work context. Its core product is a "context graph" for the workforce, surfaced through Windy (an AI agent), the Windmill web app, and MCP/API integrations, and serves over 100 customers including Kalshi, Rho, and Merge. Founded by CEO Max Shaw and Brian Distelburger, Windmill positions itself around the thesis that AI raises rather than diminishes the value of individual employees.
company.name: Windmill

company.description: Clarasight is a New York-based AI platform for enterprise travel and expense management. Its data management agents unify fragmented travel, expense, card, and HR data into a real-time AI-ready model so global travel, finance, sustainability, and procurement teams can automate workflows, optimize supplier strategy, and surface out-of-policy spend. The company, originally founded as Climate Club and rebranded to Clarasight in early 2024, now manages programs representing more than $5 billion in enterprise T&E spend.
company.name: Clarasight

company.description: Neutonic is a UK-founded functional drinks and supplements brand competing in the nootropics-led beverage category, with formulations built around research-backed ingredients aimed at focus, mental clarity, and sustained energy. Co-founded in 2023 by Modern Wisdom podcast host Chris Williamson and fitness entrepreneur James Smith with Luke Betts and Shan Hanif, the company has sold more than 7.5 million cans and is stocked in over 10,000 retail doors globally. It is rolling out across UK grocery (including Sainsbury's meal deal listings), US specialty retail (GNC, The Vitamin Shoppe, Central Market), and is preparing an Australian launch.
company.name: Neutonic

company.description: CleanDesign is a Toronto-based energy technology company building Hybrid Energy Management Systems (hEMS) that pair battery storage with proprietary software and AI to optimize power use on diesel-driven sites. Its trailer-scale units are deployed by oil and gas drillers and remote miners — including operations in Mexico and Canada's Arctic Circle — to cut fuel consumption by roughly 10-15% and emissions by up to 20% without replacing existing infrastructure. The hEMS platform collects 60,000 data points per second per unit to dynamically govern generators in real time.
company.name: CleanDesign

company.description: dehaze is a Munich-based healthtech AI company building a foundational model for chronic disease detection. Its causal AI ingests unharmonized patient data, harmonizes it into clean health trajectories, and flags individuals at elevated risk of chronic conditions for clinicians and payers — with the company claiming the approach can reduce medical loss ratios by up to 10 percent. Engineering, research, and product are headquartered in Munich while commercial deployments are global, supported by grants from the German Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space and the Federal Ministry of Economic Affairs and Energy.
company.name: dehaze

company.description: SkyfireAI is a Huntsville, Alabama-based startup building an AI-native autonomy platform for coordinated multi-drone operations. The software covers planning, deployment and oversight for public safety, defense and critical-infrastructure missions such as 911 response, search and rescue, perimeter defense and lifesaving medical delivery. Founded in 2022, the company is hardware-agnostic and aims to let a single operator supervise several drones at once instead of one pilot per aircraft.
company.name: SkyfireAI

company.description: Liquid is a New York-based mobile trading platform that started in 2025 as a perpetual-futures DEX aggregator and has expanded into a multi-asset leveraged exchange. The app lets retail traders take positions in stocks, crypto, foreign currencies, commodities, prediction-market shares, and pre-IPO secondaries from a single interface, with up to 200x leverage available in select jurisdictions. Founder Franklyn Wang, a former Two Sigma quantitative researcher, is positioning Liquid as a 24/7 cross-asset venue rather than a crypto-only exchange.
company.name: Liquid

company.description: Forest (operated by HumanForest) runs a shared electric bike service in London, which it has scaled into the world's largest shared e-bike market. Since launching in 2020, the company has grown to 1.5 million users completing roughly two million rides per month across 18 boroughs, and powers its fleet with zero-emissions energy while offering riders up to 30 free minutes of cycling daily.
company.name: Forest

company.description: Wiliot operates a Physical AI platform that gives supply chains continuous, scan-free visibility into the location and condition of physical items. Its battery-free Bluetooth IoT Pixels harvest energy from ambient radio signals and stream sensing data into the Wiliot Cloud, where purpose-built AI/ML models drive real-time condition monitoring, inventory intelligence and workflow automation. Founded in 2017 in Caesarea, Israel by Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim and Alon Yehezkely, the company processes billions of sensing events monthly across global retailers and logistics customers including Walmart.
company.name: Wiliot

company.description: House of Chikankari is a women-led D2C ethnic wear brand reimagining traditional Lucknowi chikankari embroidery for everyday modern wear. The Delhi-based startup works with a network of more than 10,000 artisans and sells kurtas, kurta sets, sarees, kaftans, and bottom wear through its own website and marketplaces such as Myntra, Amazon, and Nykaa. Founded in 2020 by mother-daughter duo Poonam and Aakriti Rawal, it ships to over 20 countries and operates at an annual revenue run rate of about Rs 50 crore.
company.name: House of Chikankari

company.description: Cnuic Technologies is an Edinburgh-based deep tech company developing a new class of photolithography equipment for the manufacture of photonic chips. Its prototype tool exploits the wave properties of light to enable rapid, reconfigurable production of photonic structures with 3D control, targeting applications from data-center interconnects and AI compute to metalenses, AR/VR waveguides, and 3D photonic crystals.
company.name: Cnuic Technologies

company.description: Siam Digital Lending (SiamDL) is a Bank of Thailand–licensed digital lender in Bangkok serving Thai borrowers shut out of traditional bank credit. The company combines AI-driven underwriting that reads and analyses Thai bank statements with personal-loan and nano-finance products designed for fast, paperwork-free approval. CEO Andy Thienkosol leads operations alongside co-founder Maxwell Meyer, who also runs financial-comparison platform Masii.
company.name: SiamDL

company.description: Performativ is a Copenhagen-based fintech building a cloud-native operating system for wealth and asset managers. The platform consolidates portfolio management, analytics, client reporting, compliance, multi-custodian data aggregation, and trading into a single SaaS, using embedded AI agents to automate manual workflows across front, middle, and back office. Founded in 2020, it has positioned itself as a leading European platform for small and mid-sized wealth managers and is now expanding into private banks and larger institutions.
company.name: Performativ

company.description: HyugaLife is an Indian e-commerce marketplace for health, nutrition and wellness products, listing 10,000+ SKUs across proteins, supplements, weight management and fitness from 450+ brands. The company sources directly from brands rather than third-party sellers and runs an in-house H-Tested programme that puts every product through independent NABL and FSSAI-grade lab testing for nutrition content and heavy metals. Founded in 2021 by Sachin Parikh, Anvi Shah and Neehar Modi, HyugaLife also sells its own Hyuga-branded line of creatine and whey protein.
company.name: HyugaLife

company.description: Tynapse is a Seoul-based AI security startup building an "AI Trust Layer," a runtime infrastructure that sits between enterprise applications and large language models to validate and control AI-agent outputs and actions in real time. Its two-stage detection-and-judgment architecture is designed to block hallucinations, data leakage, prompt injection, and unauthorized privilege escalation, while logging every decision as an audit-grade trail. The company is initially focused on Korean financial-services customers, with proof-of-concept work at major domestic banks, and plans to extend into healthcare, the public sector, and broader enterprise markets.
company.name: Tynapse

company.description: Tapaya is a Prague-based payments infrastructure startup providing an SDK that turns standard Android, iOS, tablet, and kiosk devices into PCI-compliant in-person payment terminals. It bundles compliance, processor integrations, and card-scheme connections into a single software layer so banks, fintechs, and software platforms can embed in-store card acceptance without dedicated hardware. The company is starting in Czech Republic and Central and Eastern Europe and targeting expansion across the Baltics and the rest of Europe.
company.name: Tapaya

company.description: OTee is a Norwegian industrial-automation company building a software-defined control platform that replaces traditional PLC hardware with virtual PLCs running on open, off-the-shelf Linux devices. Its platform lets engineers design, deploy, and centrally manage control systems across many sites without vendor lock-in, targeting energy, utilities, and manufacturing operators. The company was co-founded in 2022 out of the Antler programme by former ABB executive Henrik Pedersen and CTO Radek Janik, and now employs 21 people from 13 countries.
company.name: OTee

company.description: Dodai is an Ethiopian electric mobility company that locally assembles lithium-ion electric motorcycles in Addis Ababa and operates a proprietary battery-swapping network for commercial riders such as delivery drivers and motorcycle taxi operators. Founded in 2023 by Japanese entrepreneur Yuma Sasaki, the company has deployed more than 2,000 e-bikes and built a workforce that is roughly 97% Ethiopian. Its integrated model — vehicle assembly, financing through microfinance partners, and battery-swapping infrastructure — targets the price-sensitive commercial-rider segment that dominates African urban transport.
company.name: Dodai

company.description: Patronus is a Berlin-based healthtech company building digital safety and companion solutions for older adults, anchored by an emergency smartwatch with an integrated SIM that connects wearers to a 24/7 emergency call centre at the press of a button. The watch is paired with a family app that surfaces wear status, location and instant emergency alerts, replacing traditional pendant alarm devices that suffer from low daily-wear adoption. The company is expanding the platform with wellbeing features and an on-device AI companion aimed at reducing loneliness among elderly users.
company.name: Patronus

company.description: The peaceful use of space is essential for continued prosperity on Earth—from communications and finance to navigation and logistics. True Anomaly builds innovative technology at the intersection of spacecraft, software, and AI to enhance the capabilities of the U.S., its allies, and commercial partners. We safeguard global security by ensuring space access and sustainability for all.
company.name: True Anomaly

company.description: A High-Performance Computing company building a Neocloud platform and GPU cloud compute infrastructure to deliver AI-focused, high-density GPU compute and sovereign AI solutions.
company.name: SharonAI Holdings Inc.

company.description:
At Kashable, we're committed to a values-driven approach and are dedicated to helping reshape your financial journey. As a socially responsible financing solution seamlessly integrated into employer-sponsored benefits, we leverage innovative technology to provide quick access to affordable credit.

Eligible employees can experience the ease of securing a Kashable loan online within minutes, with convenient repayment through payroll.

Kashable is here to offer a purposeful financial solution to help ensure a smoother path for you. Choose Kashable for a brighter financial future.

Kashable is a consumer finance company licensed in AL, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, ND, NH, NJ, NM, NY, NV, OK, OR, PA, PR, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT,  VT, WA, WI, WY, and District of Columbia. 

NMLS #1373339 
https://www.nmlsconsumeraccess.org/
company.name: Kashable

company.description: London-based AI startup developing superhuman intelligence using reinforcement learning, focusing on systems that learn through experience and interaction rather than large language models.
company.name: Ineffable Intelligence

company.description: Quantum Art is an Israeli startup developing scalable full-stack quantum computers using advanced trapped-ion qubit technology and proprietary scale-up architecture, focusing on multi-qubit gates and dynamic multi-core designs to enable commercial quantum advantage.
company.name: Quantum Art

company.description: Helical Fusion is a private fusion energy developer based in Japan focused on commercializing fusion power using a stellarator design. The company aims to build the world's first commercially viable steady-state net power fusion plant by leveraging Japan's advanced manufacturing capabilities and decades of fusion research, particularly from the National Institute for Fusion Science and its Large Helical Device facility. Their Helix Program includes pilot plants Helix HARUKA and Helix KANATA, tar
company.name: Helical Fusion

company.description: Beijing Xingdong Jiyuan Technology is a Beijing-based humanoid robotics startup incubated by Tsinghua University's Institute of Interdisciplinary Information Science, focusing on the research, development, and mass production of humanoid robot hardware and software technologies.
company.name: Beijing Xingdong Jiyuan Technology Co., Ltd.

company.description: Sereact is a German robotics startup founded in 2021 that develops AI-powered robotics solutions using Vision Language Action Models (VLAM) to enable robots to understand and adapt to their environment in real-time without complex programming. Their software-first platform supports various robot hardware and is used in industries like logistics and manufacturing by clients such as BMW and Daimler Truck.
company.name: Sereact

company.description: Snabbit is a Bengaluru-based on-demand home services startup founded in 2024 that connects households with trained women experts for quick home chores such as cleaning, dishwashing, laundry, and kitchen prep, operating through a hyperlocal network promising service within minutes. The company has rapidly scaled to over 10,000 daily jobs and a 100% women-led workforce of 5,000 experts.
company.name: Snabbit

company.description: Nervonik is a medical device company developing an opioid-free peripheral nerve stimulation (PNS) system that delivers minimally invasive, patient-friendly therapy for personalized chronic pain relief using proprietary wirelessly powered and closed-loop sensing technologies.
company.name: Nervonik, Inc.

company.description:
Morphing Machines Pvt Ltd is a closely held fabless semiconductor company based in Bangalore.  Morphing Machines was launched from the Technology Entrepreneurship Initiative of the Indian Institute of Science at Bangalore.  The patented REDEFINE™ technology from Morphing Machines is a path-breaking new SoC architecture and platform for implementing run-time reconfigurable silicon cores for massively parallel and heterogeneous many-core processors.  A single REDEFINE™ based application core accelerates an entire class of related applications while optimizing space and power usage.  REDEFINE™ enables ASIC-like high performance at an affordable NRE cost for a much wider range of compute-intensive applications than has ever been possible before.  The REDEFINE™ Meta Compiler framework enables targeting application implementations to a REDEFINE™ application core through automatic concurrency analysis and generation of hardware reconfiguration meta-data, supported by cycle-accurate REDEFINE™ Simulators for pre-synthesis design validation.

REDEFINE™ silicon core IPs in multi-protocol cryptography and other application areas have been adopted by key customers in India and Europe.  Other REDEFINE™ and Morphing Machines IPs include XNOC network-on-chip, XFloat floating point and linear algebra engine, AES, ECC, SHA, and other crypto IPs, and many more.
company.name: Morphing Machines

company.description: Milky Mist Dairy Food is an Erode, Tamil Nadu-based packaged dairy company specializing in value-added products including paneer, cheese, yogurt, ice cream, butter, and ghee. Founded in 1985 as a milk trading business, it has grown into one of India's leading branded dairy FMCG players with revenue of Rs 2,349 crore in FY25. The company is preparing for an IPO of up to Rs 2,035 crore and does not sell liquid milk, focusing instead on higher-margin value-added dairy.
company.name: Milky Mist Dairy

company.description: Aurm is a Bengaluru-based vaulting infrastructure startup that operates fully automated, military-grade safe deposit lockers integrated directly into gated residential communities, corporate campuses and bank branches. Founded in 2023 by Vijay Arisetty, Suraj HS, Pratap Chandana and Ganesh Balakrishnan, the company offers a tech-led "vault-at-your-doorstep" model — 300 sq ft facilities housing up to 300 lockers with 24/7 access, multi-factor authentication and active intrusion-proof surveillance. Aurm partners with banks and real estate developers to address the structural shortage of bank locker capacity in India.
company.name: Aurm

company.description: Aureka Biotechnologies is a tech-bio company founded in 2023 with R&D operations in Suzhou and California that develops antibody therapeutics through its proprietary AuraIDE generative-AI platform. The platform pairs foundation models and AI agents with a high-throughput single-cell functional screening system to design differentiated antibodies for cardiometabolic, immune, and central-nervous-system disease targets that are difficult to address with conventional discovery methods. The company has built a BD/NewCo collaboration model with multiple multinational pharmaceutical companies and is preparing its first internally-designed antibody to enter clinical trials.
company.name: Aureka Biotechnologies

company.description: Flora is a Kyoto-based femtech startup that uses proprietary AI and women's health data to address the gender data gap in healthcare. It runs three products: Wellflow, a B2B platform that supports the well-being of female employees and informs corporate health-management decisions; Flora Expert, a research and product-development service that helps companies extract insights from women's health data; and Moonly, a consumer self-care app spanning menstruation, fertility, pregnancy, postpartum and menopause.
company.name: Flora

company.description: Zellify is a Web2App growth infrastructure platform that lets mobile app developers build web-based onboarding and payment funnels outside the App Store and Play Store, sidestepping the 30% platform fee and recovering the attribution data lost to iOS privacy changes. The product is no-code for marketing teams and pairs the funnel builder with a data layer covering ad click through Stripe payment to install. Customers include Lifesum, Opal, Blinkist, and Teknasyon.
company.name: Zellify

company.description: Golden Child is a Miami-founded direct-to-consumer pet wellness brand selling fresh frozen meals (Mains) and a shelf-stable functional liquid topper (Drizzles) for dogs, manufactured in U.S. facilities on human-grade supply chains. Co-founded in 2025 by Hims & Hers alumni Quentin Lacornerie, Jack Abraham and Hilary Coles in partnership with Atomic Labs, the company differentiates on a vet-nutritionist- and chef-developed recipe system aimed at the premium end of the U.S. fresh pet food market.
company.name: Golden Child

company.description: Smearcase is a US consumer brand that markets a high-protein frozen cottage cheese dessert under the FroCo label, positioning the format as a healthier alternative to ice cream. Each pint delivers around 40g of protein with collagen, and the company is rolling out nationwide via Sprouts Farmers Market and other grocers.
company.name: Smearcase

company.description: Cube is a Southeast Asia-focused e-commerce market intelligence company that aggregates data across countries, categories, and channels to give brands, retailers, and investors granular insights on online sales, pricing, and competition. Its products — including Astro for category tracking and Tradewinds for strategic market data — cover platforms such as Shopee, Lazada, and TikTok Shop. Headquartered in Singapore with an office in Bangkok, the company counts L'Oreal, IFC, IKEA, Daraz, and Little Farms among its enterprise customers.
company.name: Cube

company.description: Soloband Games is a Limassol, Cyprus-based mobile game studio building casual titles for a global audience. Its flagship World Tour Merge sits within the Merge 2 genre and has surpassed one million downloads with a 4.8 player rating. The 25-person team is led by CEO Aleksandr Kislitsyn and combines design and live-operations talent from studios such as Playrix and Melsoft.
company.name: Soloband Games

company.description: Redpine is a Stockholm-based startup operating an API-native platform that lets AI agents query, retrieve and pay for premium licensed data — clinical guidelines, case law, scientific research, financial markets data and quality news — on a per-token basis. The company licenses content directly from rights holders and shares revenue with them, positioning itself as "Spotify for data" for the agentic-AI economy. Founded in 2024 by ex-Antler partner Anders Hammarbäck and former early Spotify engineer David Österdahl, Redpine works with leading AI labs and biotech research firm AsedaSciences.
company.name: Redpine

company.description: Serenity Power is a Canadian cleantech company developing solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) systems for remote and distributed power generation. Its fuel-flexible cells run on natural gas, hydrogen, methane, or blended fuels, positioning them as a lower-emission alternative to diesel generators for industrial and off-grid sites. Operating from Calgary and Toronto, the company is moving from lab-scale validation to its first commercial fuel cell stack with pilot deployments planned in Italy and Alberta.
company.name: Serenity Power

company.description: Gel Coat Biomaterials is a University of Tokyo spinout commercializing Gel Coat™, a proprietary zwitterionic amoeba-hydrogel platform that encapsulates and stabilises enzymes, antibodies and nucleic acids without compromising activity. The technology lets fragile biomolecules survive heat, organic solvents and ambient storage, sharply lowering cost and complexity for biomanufacturers in food, chemicals and pharma. The company was founded by Professor Madoka Takai of the University of Tokyo Graduate School of Engineering and licenses Gel Coat™ to industrial partners.
company.name: Gel Coat Biomaterials

company.description: Tokyo-based startup spun out of Honda Motor's IGNITION new-business creation program in March 2026. PathAhead has developed Rising Sand, an artificial aggregate that granulates fine desert sand into uniform, high-hardness clusters using a patent-pending process — pitched as a sustainable alternative to natural sand and crushed stone for asphalt road paving and concrete. The company plans demonstration testing in Kenya, Tanzania and South Africa from 2027 and a first production plant in Kenya by 2028 to supply African road infrastructure markets.
company.name: PathAhead

company.description: Quantum computing startup spun out of the Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology (OIST), developing trapped-ion quantum hardware and software toolchains. Targeting commercial deployment of full-stack quantum systems for research and enterprise customers.
company.name: Qubitcore

company.description: Israeli fabless chipmaker designing low-power cellular IoT modems and systems-on-chip for LTE Cat-M, NB-IoT and 5G eRedCap connectivity. Recently spun out of Sony Semiconductor Solutions to operate again as an independent company focused on next-generation IoT and physical-AI connectivity.
company.name: Altair Semiconductor

company.description: Fathom Therapeutics, formerly Atommap, is a New York-based drug design company combining quantum chemistry, physics-based simulations, and AI to model protein motion at atomic resolution. Its Microcosmos platform generates the data needed to design small molecules with sharper therapeutic effects and reduced off-target risk. The company runs internal discovery programs and discovery partnerships, with a lead asset advanced into animal efficacy studies.
company.name: Fathom Therapeutics

company.description: Metasports Interactive is an Indian gaming studio behind Hitwicket, a multiplayer cricket game with a global community. The company focuses on competitive sports gaming experiences and player monetization.
company.name: Metasports Interactive

company.description: Fermeate is a precision-fermentation company applying optogenetics to dynamically control microbial gene expression in industrial bioreactors, aiming to lower the cost of fermentation-derived ingredients without retrofitting existing tanks.
company.name: Fermeate

company.description: Atech is a Copenhagen-based AI hardware startup building what it calls "vibe-engineering" for hardware — a platform that lets users describe a physical device in natural language and receive a working prototype, with PCB layout, component selection and firmware handled by the system. The company aims to lower the barrier to hardware prototyping in the same way no-code and AI tools have done for software, positioning itself within the broader Physical AI thesis. Founded in 2026 by Vladimir Baran (CCO), Tomas Erik Harmer (CEO) and David Stalmarck (CTO).
company.name: Atech

company.description: TriFetch is a San Francisco-based AI automation platform built for independent specialty clinics and multi-specialty groups. It targets the three workflows that consume the most clinic staff time — patient calls and scheduling, referral processing, and prior authorizations — via a multilingual voice agent and EMR-agnostic integrations with systems like NextGen, eCW, and Athena. The company was founded in 2024 by Varuni Sarwal (CEO) and Rosemary He (COO), who met during their PhDs at UCLA working on machine learning for healthcare.
company.name: TriFetch

company.description: Lola is a Bahrain-founded D2C foodtech platform that lets users design, customise, and order cakes — choosing flavour, size, photo prints, colours, and themes — through a single digital experience that replaces the back-and-forth with traditional bakeries. Founded in 2023 by Othman Janahi, the company started in Bahrain and now operates across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, and Kuwait, transitioning from a bakery-fulfilment model to centralised production paired with cloud bakeries as it positions itself as a broader celebrations platform across MENA.
company.name: Lola

company.description: PlaqueTec is a Cambridge-based medtech company developing an intracoronary liquid biopsy approach to identify inflammatory drivers of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease. Its proprietary device samples proteomic biomarkers directly at the site of plaque formation, feeding a clinical-and-proteomic data lake called BioCarta that powers patient endotyping, biomarker discovery and partnerships with pharmaceutical companies for cardiovascular drug development. The company is based at the Babraham Research Campus and runs the BIOPATTERN trial across a growing patient cohort.
company.name: PlaqueTec

company.description: Avoca builds AI voice and workflow automation for the home and field-services economy, answering inbound calls 24/7, booking jobs into customer CRMs, following up on outstanding estimates, and driving outbound lead campaigns based on technician capacity. Founded in 2022 by MIT alums Apurva Shrivastava and Tyson Chen, the company serves HVAC, plumbing, automotive and moving operators including Turnpoint, 1-800-GOT-JUNK?, Goettl, and Sila Services, with partnerships across ServiceTitan, Nexstar, and Clover.
company.name: Avoca

company.description: Four Pillars is a Seoul-based blockchain research and technology firm founded in 2023 that publishes multilingual technical research, advises institutional investors and protocols, and operates the Validated institutional-grade validator infrastructure. Following its Series A, the company is repositioning as a "Web3 Solutions Company" organised around three units (Research, Institution, Validated) and focuses on bridging Asian and global blockchain markets and connecting traditional finance to Web3 through stablecoins and tokenized real-world assets.
company.name: Four Pillars

company.description: Ethereal Machines is a Bengaluru-based deeptech manufacturing startup that designs proprietary multi-axis CNC machines and offers Machining-as-a-Service to deliver high-precision components on demand. Founded in 2014 by Kaushik Mudda and Navin Jain, the company serves the aerospace, healthcare and consumer electronics sectors with a focus on cost-effective and scalable precision engineering.
company.name: Ethereal Machines

company.description: Noreja is an Austrian startup building an AI-powered process intelligence platform that uses knowledge graphs to map operational data and surface inefficiencies in business processes. Spun out of the Vienna University of Economics and Business in 2021, it pitches its approach as Generative Process Intelligence, with AI agents that analyze process weaknesses and autonomously execute optimization tasks. The platform combines a dashboard, an interactive analyser, the Minerva AI assistant and a Jupyter-based workbench layered on its knowledge graph.
company.name: Noreja

company.description: Comfi is a UAE-based B2B embedded finance platform that helps SMEs across MENA close cash-flow gaps by letting suppliers receive payment within 24 hours while offering business buyers up to 90-day terms. Its product suite combines a B2B Buy Now Pay Later flow with invoice discounting and dealer financing, underwritten in near real time by an in-house AI risk engine. Founded in 2023 and headquartered in Dubai, the company works with more than 1,000 clients and 4,000+ finance leaders, and has processed over 15,000 invoices to date.
company.name: Comfi

company.description: Linexa is a Munich-based industrial AI startup developing a platform that decodes legacy programmable logic controller (PLC) code across vendors and translates it into a unified data model spanning PLC, HMI, and SCADA systems. The platform gives manufacturers end-to-end visibility into their automation logic and uses AI agents to monitor production lines and flag risks before unplanned downtime, accelerating changeovers between products. Founded in late 2025 by Viktor Stryczek, Alexandros Vassiliadis, and Tobias Drees out of Wayra, Telefonica's Munich innovation hub.
company.name: Linexa

company.description: QuoIntelligence is a Frankfurt-based cybersecurity company that delivers finished, analyst-curated threat intelligence to mid-market organisations across Europe. Its platform combines an AI engine called Mercury and a conversational AI analyst named KARLA with European analysts who contextualise cyber, physical, and geopolitical risk signals in the client's language and sector. The company is incorporated in Germany with operating entities in Spain and Italy, and stores all intelligence data under EU jurisdiction to meet NIS2 and DORA sovereignty requirements.
company.name: QuoIntelligence

company.description: Axomove is a French digital health company that develops a personalised digital prevention and rehabilitation platform combining software-driven care pathways with follow-up by healthcare professionals. Its solution is reimbursed by the French Social Security for chronic lower back pain and is used by hospitals, insurers, and corporates including Air France, Thales, Allianz, CHU de Lille, and Ramsay Santé. The company targets musculoskeletal disorders with movement-based therapy and reports measurable clinical and economic benefits for patients and employers.
company.name: Axomove

company.description: SquareMind is a Paris-based deep tech startup combining AI and robotics to modernize dermatology. Founded in 2019 by Ali Khachlouf and Tanguy Serrat, it builds Swan, a robotic full-body skin imaging platform that automatically scans and maps a patient's entire skin surface at dermoscopic resolution and uses AI software to flag and track lesions over time. The company sells the device to hospitals, clinics and private dermatologists in Europe and is preparing a US commercial launch.
company.name: SquareMind

company.description: Audion is a French adtech company that builds proprietary technology and AI agents for digital audio advertising on podcasts, streaming, and connected audio platforms. Founded in 2018 in Paris by Arthur Larrey and Kamel El Hadef, it lets brands and media agencies plan, customize, and measure audio campaigns at scale, and in early 2026 launched Audion AI, a suite of agents that indexes content on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and YouTube to optimize targeting and performance. The company reports more than 1,500 clients and around 50 employees across Paris, London, Milan, Brussels, Amsterdam, and Hamburg.
company.name: Audion

company.description: Point2 Technology, founded in 2016, develops RF-based interconnect technology for AI data centers. Its e-Tube platform transmits data as RF signals over plastic waveguides, powering Active RF Cables (ARC) that the company positions as an alternative to both copper and optical interconnects for accelerator-to-accelerator and rack-scale connectivity in dense AI clusters. With Silicon Valley operations in San Jose and strong Korean roots, it is the first Korean-linked startup to land a strategic investment from NVIDIA's NVentures.
company.name: Point2 Technology

company.description: Axis Finance Limited is a Mumbai-based non-banking financial company and a subsidiary of Axis Bank, providing niche financial solutions across wholesale, retail, and MSME segments including special situations financing, structured credit, and asset-backed lending. The NBFC reported FY26 profit after tax of Rs 806 crore (up 19% year-on-year) and net non-performing assets of 0.36%, positioning it among the better-performing non-bank lenders in India.
company.name: Axis Finance

company.description: greatEvent is a Rockville, Maryland-based hospitality technology startup that operates a patented Real-Time Booking (RTB) platform for events and meetings. The platform gives planners instant visibility into live venue availability and lets them confirm bookings in minutes, replacing the slow phone-and-email sales-and-catering processes that remain standard across hotels. Founded by CEO Dipak Thakker, the company recently partnered with Leonardo Worldwide to integrate hotel-managed visual content into its venue listings.
company.name: greatEvent

company.description: Integrant is a Sydney-based Australian biotechnology company founded in 2015 that develops AI-enhanced regenerative medicine and orthopaedic surgical products for bone healing, fracture management, and cartilage regeneration. Its flagship intra-articular injection therapy targets meniscus cartilage repair as a non-invasive, disease-modifying treatment for osteoarthritis, and its proprietary machine-learning platforms are used to design biomaterials, tailor regenerative protocols to individual patients, and predict clinical outcomes. The company's biologic processing systems and orthopaedic therapies are gaining traction across the Asia-Pacific region as it prepares for a Nasdaq listing under the ticker INGT.
company.name: Integrant

company.description: Project Prometheus is a secretive AI startup co-led by Jeff Bezos and Vik Bajaj, focusing on AI for engineering and manufacturing in sectors including computers, aerospace, and automobiles. The company is in stealth mode and aims to apply AI to the physical economy, with potential collaboration with Bezos' Blue Origin.
company.name: Project Prometheus

company.description: AI research company
company.name: Anthropic

company.description: DataCrunch is a Helsinki-based company providing high-performance AI infrastructure and cloud GPU solutions optimized for AI training and inference workloads. It offers a one-stop AI platform with advanced compute clusters, including NVIDIA HGX B200 systems, serving enterprises and educational institutions.
company.name: DataCrunch

company.description:
Series is a social network of AI friends and real people connecting you to who you need, when you need it.

Meet the right people at the right time_
company.name: Series

company.description: Cata is a Singapore-based white-label SaaS platform that lets food-and-beverage and retail operators launch and run their own branded consumer apps, bundling mobile ordering, payments, loyalty, promotions, CRM and analytics into one system. The platform pitches itself as bringing the kind of digital infrastructure used by Starbucks, McDonald's and delivery giants like Grab and foodpanda within reach of mid-market merchants, with deployments measured in days instead of months. Early users include Guzman y Gomez across all 23 of its Singapore restaurants.
company.name: Cata

company.description: Crewline AI develops a hardware-and-autonomy stack that retrofits standard vibratory rollers into self-driving machines for soil and asphalt compaction inside geo-fenced job-site zones. The San Francisco company targets the labor-constrained earthworks segment of the construction industry, with early production deployments at contractors including Dynamic Site Solutions and Watts Services. It was founded by CEO Frederik Filz-Reiterdank and CTO Mohamed Sadek out of Entrepreneurs First.
company.name: Crewline AI

company.description: Thoughtly is an AI engagement platform that embeds voice, SMS, and email agents directly into CRM workflows so revenue teams can call, qualify, and route every lead automatically. Its no-code builder is aimed at marketing, sales, and RevOps users in lead-heavy verticals such as real estate, mortgage, insurance, and education. Founded in 2023 by Torrey Leonard and Alex Casella and headquartered in New York, the company was the first to ship a Calendly-integrated voice agent and now positions itself as the conversational layer of the CRM stack.
company.name: Thoughtly

company.description: Web3 AI infrastructure project building an Agent-as-a-Service execution layer with callable policy services, autonomous agent orchestration, and Vault-native fund flow coordination for decentralized agent networks.
company.name: AIW3

company.description: Lightship is a Broomfield, Colorado hardware startup designing and manufacturing electric, aerodynamic recreational vehicles. Founded in 2020 by former Tesla engineers, the company produces a battery-powered travel trailer that operates without plug-in or propane power, with a factory expansion planned to lift annual output toward 500 units.
company.name: Lightship

company.description: Heihu Network Technology (Black Lake Technologies) is a Shanghai-based industrial software and AI company providing cloud-native production management software, supply-chain coordination, and AI agents for manufacturers. Its flagship products serve more than 40,000 factories across over 30 industries — including customers Mengniu, Mixue Bingcheng and Nongfu Spring — and the company holds a leading position in China's cloud-based MES market. Since 2023 it has invested in multi-scenario industrial AI agents that move factory workflows from AI-assisted to AI-autonomous decision-making.
company.name: Heihu Network

company.description: Holiday Robotics is a South Korean humanoid robot startup founded in April 2024 by serial entrepreneur Song Gi-young, who previously sold AI vision-inspection company Sualab to Cognex for roughly $200M. The company builds general-purpose humanoid robots focused on manipulation tasks for manufacturing, with proprietary tactile sensors, robotic hands and actuated joints, and simulation-based reinforcement learning. Its first humanoid, Friday, was unveiled in October 2025 and is targeted at parts assembly across factory lines, with planned expansion into service-industry and household applications.
company.name: Holiday Robotics

company.description: Omegga is a Munich-based deep-tech startup developing an AI-driven, non-invasive optical spectroscopy platform that determines the sex of chicken embryos in-ovo before day seven of incubation. The technology is built into hatchery incubators and is already in industrial use, giving poultry producers an ethical and economically viable alternative to the routine culling of male chicks. Beyond poultry, Omegga positions its spectral-intelligence platform as a cross-industry tool for non-invasive material analysis in agriculture, food production, and industrial quality control.
company.name: Omegga

company.description: Betterness is a Miami-based health-tech startup building agent-first infrastructure for the consumer and enterprise wellness market. Its three-layer stack pairs an open Model Context Protocol for health and wellness services with Bett-i, a voice-first autonomous life-coaching system, and an upcoming enterprise product for clinics, gyms, and longevity programs. The platform integrates with major US lab networks and consumer wearables under a HIPAA-compliant, SOC2-ready architecture.
company.name: Betterness

company.description: CloudWalk is a Brazilian financial-technology company that builds AI- and blockchain-based payments and credit infrastructure for small businesses. Its flagship platform InfinitePay lets Brazilian merchants accept card and instant payments and access working capital through advances on credit-card receivables, and it also operates JIM.com in the United States. By 2024 the company reported $1.088B in revenue and $120M in net income, making it one of Latin America's most profitable fintechs.
company.name: CloudWalk

company.description: Entelo Bio is an Oxford, UK-based AIxBio startup spun out of the University of Oxford's Botnar Institute for Musculoskeletal Sciences. The company combines a proprietary clinical network, wet-lab capabilities, and AI infrastructure to generate high-resolution human data and disease models targeting movement and ageing-related conditions that affect roughly 1.7 billion people worldwide. Led by CEO Dr Peter Crane and CTO Prof Adam Cribbs, Entelo Bio positions itself as a foundational data engine for precision medicine in human physical resilience, partnering on and developing therapeutics for chronic age-associated and rare genetic movement disorders.
company.name: Entelo Bio

company.description: San Francisco-based Comfy Org is the team behind ComfyUI, a node-based workflow platform that gives visual professionals granular control over AI image, video, 3D, and audio generation. Originally released as an open-source project in 2023, ComfyUI has grown to more than four million users and a 60,000-node community ecosystem, and is used by studios, ad agencies, and enterprise creative teams as production infrastructure. Alongside the open-source tool the company operates Comfy Cloud, a hosted runtime for teams that need collaboration, security, and managed compute.
company.name: Comfy

company.description: LighthouseAI is a Los Angeles-based SaaS and managed-services company that centralizes pharmaceutical state licensing and regulatory compliance for manufacturers, wholesale distributors, 3PLs, 503A/503B outsourcing facilities, pharmacies, repackagers, and reverse distributors. Its platform tracks federal and state rule changes across every U.S. jurisdiction, automates compliance workflows, and pairs software with an in-house team of licensing experts who handle filings, renewals, and audit readiness. Founded in 2023 by Sumeet Singh out of pharma consultancy Pharma Solutions, the company serves around 250 customers across the drug supply chain, including multiple enterprise pharmaceutical firms.
company.name: LighthouseAI

company.description: Quillon is a Sofia-based AI platform for technical accounting and financial reporting, used by accountants at US public companies. Its system combines a proprietary knowledge graph of accounting standards with SEC EDGAR integration to generate audit-grade memos whose every conclusion is traceable to source material. The company rebranded from Acclara AI and runs operations between Sofia and San Francisco.
company.name: Quillon

company.description: Foamlab is a Delft-based biomaterials startup developing high-performance, compostable foams made from bacterial nanocellulose as an alternative to fossil-based plastics like polystyrene. A 2024 spin-off from TU Delft's Biodesign Lab (Faculty of Industrial Design Engineering), the company uses microbial fermentation to grow foams whose mechanical, acoustic and aesthetic properties can be tuned at the nanoscale, targeting construction insulation, packaging, fashion and furniture.
company.name: Foamlab

company.description: Valitana is a Stamford, Connecticut-based financial technology company providing analytics, workflow, and portfolio management solutions for structured credit professionals. Its cloud-native platform spans two core products — Analytics for CLO tranche, deal, manager and market-level analytics, and Vantage, a multi-asset class trade and portfolio management platform — and is trusted by more than 90 institutions globally. The company was founded in 2017 by Alex Belgrade in partnership with Eagle Point Holdings.
company.name: Valitana

company.description: Paris-based Sillage builds a go-to-market AI platform that helps enterprise sales teams detect real buying intent from social, hiring, competitor and account signals, then routes qualified accounts to reps through Slack and existing CRMs. The company positions itself against fully-automated AI SDR tools, framing its product as a signal-driven copilot that preserves human relationships rather than replacing them. Founded in 2025 by Arnaud Weiss and Arthur Coudouy, early customers include Yoobic, Salesforce, FullEnrich, Figures and Sopht.
company.name: Sillage

company.description: Cohere is the leading security-first enterprise AI company. We build cutting-edge foundation models and end-to-end AI products designed to solve real-world business problems. We partner closely with companies to deliver seamless integration, full customization, and easy-to-use solutions for their workforce and customers. Our all-in-one platform offers enterprises the highest levels of data security, privacy and optionality to deploy across all major cloud providers, private cloud environments, or on-premises.
company.name: Cohere

company.description: Space surveillance and intelligence company developing space-based and ground-based infrastructure for space situational awareness, including the AIRA platform, SCOT constellation for surveillance, ALBATROSS for missile warning, and SKYGATE ground sensors.
company.name: Digantara

company.description: PvX Partners is a financing platform designed to help consumer and gaming apps scale by providing non-dilutive, flexible growth capital through its PvX Capital offering, supported by a machine-learning-driven intelligence platform called PvX Lambda.
company.name: PvX Partners

company.description: Think Nature is a Japanese nature-tech startup that operates a biodiversity big-data and AI analysis platform, integrating distribution data on more than 300,000 species with over 150 ecological and socio-economic indicators to deliver natural-capital and TNFD-aligned reporting. Spun out of the University of the Ryukyus in 2019 and based in Okinawa, the company sells visualization, due-diligence, and disclosure services (TN LEAD, GBNAT, TN FINANCE, TN PROTECT, Dugongs AI) to corporates such as Nissin Foods, Lion, and Tokyu Land, plus financial institutions and municipalities. It opened a Finland branch in 2025 to anchor its European expansion.
company.name: Think Nature

company.description: KEMARO is a Swiss robotics company that develops fully autonomous industrial floor-cleaning robots, with its flagship K900 designed for complex, high-debris environments such as logistics centers and manufacturing plants. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Eschlikon (Thurgau), the company has sold over 1,500 robots to more than 600 industrial clients including Unilever, Nestlé, BMW, and Amazon. KEMARO differentiates with a privacy-by-design approach, deliberately avoiding visual data capture and 360-degree cameras to address industrial-espionage and data-protection concerns.
company.name: KEMARO

company.description: RealGo is a mobile augmented-reality game that blends location-based exploration, AI, and Web3 mechanics, turning meme characters into capturable, battle-ready, and tradeable digital pets. Players use AR on their phones to find meme pets in real-world locations, level them up through Meme Battles, and earn Real Tokens. The game launched on BNB Chain in late 2025 and is positioning itself as core infrastructure for what its team calls "Meme 3.0" — an on-chain meme asset economy.
company.name: RealGo

company.description: Molmir is a Nara-based Japanese biotech founded in 2022 that builds a drug discovery platform around the ability to visualize and analyze the dynamics of intracellular molecules. Its proprietary CHEmir and MAGmir technologies combine analytical chemistry with solution NMR to make so-called "moving" proteins — about 70% of the proteome and historically considered undruggable — tractable targets, with a lead program in TDP-43 aggregation control for ALS and a partnership with Nara Medical University on acquired von Willebrand syndrome.
company.name: Molmir

company.description: Fasal Bio is a Zagreb-based renewable materials company developing Naturion, a wood-based composite engineered to replace conventional plastics in injection moulding and extrusion processes without requiring new manufacturing equipment. Founded in 2012 by plastics engineer Krešimir Hagljan, the company develops both materials and finished consumer goods across categories including animal chewables, children's toys, household items and clothing accessories, sold under proprietary brands such as D-Bone and FORMIQ. Its products are used in commercial production for international brands across the United States and Western Europe.
company.name: Fasal Bio

company.description: Fere AI is a Singapore-based platform of autonomous, self-improving AI agents that manage on-chain financial activity. Each agent has its own wallet and acts on natural-language objectives - researching, waiting, executing trades, and learning from outcomes via reinforcement learning across Ethereum, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, BNB Chain and Polymarket. The product has processed over 10 million autonomous agent actions and is positioned as the agentic-trading layer that will eventually extend beyond digital assets to other asset classes.
company.name: Fere AI

company.description: NEO Semiconductor develops next-generation 3D memory architectures including 3D X-DRAM, X-NAND, and X-HBM aimed at high-density, energy-efficient memory for AI workloads. Founded in 2012 by Andy Hsu and based in San Jose, California, the company recently validated its 3D X-DRAM technology with a working proof-of-concept fabricated using mature 3D NAND processes.
company.name: NEO Semiconductor

company.description: Frozen One is a high-protein ice cream brand based in Austin, Texas, offering pints with 40 grams of protein and under 400 calories in flavors including chocolate, apple pie and peanut butter chip. Founded in 2024 by Conner Mennig and Alan Chen, the company is formulated with skim milk, whey protein concentrate and allulose, and has rapidly expanded from Austin to roughly 2,300 retail doors nationwide, including Target, Wegmans, Bristol Farms and Central Market.
company.name: Frozen One

company.description: LQUOM is a Yokohama National University spin-out developing long-distance quantum communication infrastructure centered on quantum repeaters, quantum memory, and entanglement light sources. It is one of the few startups globally — and one of very few in Japan — focused on commercializing quantum repeaters for the future quantum internet, with applications spanning national security, finance, healthcare and distributed quantum computing.
