The company commercialized the LQ-PS-100 cavity-enhanced two-photon entangled light source in 2024 and runs joint research programs with major Japanese electronics manufacturers, telecom operators and the NEDO Post-5G program.
company.name: LQUOM

company.description: BLP Digital is a Zurich-based ETH spinoff founded in 2019 by brothers Tim and Sven Beck that builds agentic AI for enterprise resource planning automation. Its platform replaces legacy OCR, RPA, and workflow tooling with specialized AI agents — orchestrated by a Digital Twin — to handle high-volume finance processes such as accounts payable, financial closing, and reporting end-to-end inside ERPs like SAP, proALPHA, and Abas. The company serves more than 450 corporate customers across 40+ countries, including Swiss retail cooperative Migros, and has largely scaled without external funding while growing over 100% annually.
company.name: BLP Digital

company.description: Toronto-based AI-native biopharma founded in 2023 by former RBCx venture-capital director Anthony Mouchantaf and physician Alexander Mosa. Uses large language models and embedding techniques to mine publicly available data on late-stage drug candidates that failed their original trials, then buys or licenses the most commercially promising molecules from their original owners and advances them through new clinical studies. Operates late-stage programs spanning sickle cell disease, idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, glioblastoma, breast cancer, and Alzheimer's disease.
company.name: Biossil

company.description: BASE4 Biosciences is a Barcelona-based biotech building a multi-omic platform that infers the biological state and age of 47 tissues from a single blood sample, combining transcriptomics, genomics and AI across more than 15,000 genes and 330 metabolic pathways. Its sex-specific models target women's health, preventive screening and pharmacotranscriptomics, with initial programs in fertility and menopause biology. The company is led by molecular biologist Dr. Jose Manuel Soria and operates between Barcelona and Riyadh, backed by over €7M of prior research funding.
company.name: BASE4 Biosciences

company.description: G-Next Intelligence (formerly Griselda) is a Ukrainian defense-tech company that builds a modular platform for real-time data collection, processing, and analysis, fusing fragmented inputs including drone footage into a single operational picture with 3D models and mapping. The product is designed to cut decision-making time in combat and crisis environments, with AI tools, scalability, and secure deployment in closed networks, and is already in use by Ukraine's Defense Forces and critical infrastructure operators. Founded by CEO Oleksii Teplukhin, the company is positioning itself as a next-generation defense technology player with an international expansion roadmap.
company.name: G-Next Intelligence

company.description: Spectrum Security is a cybersecurity company that automates how enterprises build, test, deploy, and maintain threat detections. Its platform layers on top of existing SIEMs, data lakes, and EDRs to identify coverage gaps, author production-ready detection logic tailored to each environment, and continuously adapt as infrastructure changes. Led by CEO Meny Har and headquartered in San Francisco, Spectrum emerged from stealth in April 2026.
company.name: Spectrum Security

company.description: MODE, Inc. is a Silicon Valley-based startup founded in 2014 that develops BizStack, an IoT platform integrating real-time data from construction, manufacturing, logistics, and facility-management sites. BizStack pairs IoT gateways and a time-series database with generative AI via its BizStack Assistant for chat-based analysis and report automation. Customers include Kajima, Obayashi, Maeda Corporation, Panasonic Environmental Engineering, Nichirei Logistics Group, and JR East.
company.name: MODE

company.description: Sleepy Owl is an Indian direct-to-consumer coffee brand founded in 2016 by Ajai Thandi, Arman Sood, and Ashwajeet Singh, built around premium 100% Arabica products. Its portfolio spans instant coffee, hot and cold brew packs, ready-to-drink cold brews, ground coffee, and brewing kits, with a convenience-first pitch aimed at at-home coffee drinkers. The company competes in India's fast-growing premium coffee segment alongside brands such as Blue Tokai, Rage Coffee, Subko, and abCoffee.
company.name: Sleepy Owl Coffee

company.description: AITS (Affordable Innovative Techno Services) is the India-based parent company of Rysen School, a K-12 CBSE school network running 15 campuses across nine cities in Rajasthan. Founded in 2023 by a team of IITians and doctors, it operates an asset-light, technology-integrated model built around smart classrooms, blended learning tools and centralised academic analytics, and is scaling the Rysen footprint into Tier 2 and Tier 3 Indian cities with a target of 100,000 students.
company.name: Affordable Innovative Techno Services

company.description: Lumio is a Bengaluru-based consumer electronics brand founded by ex-Xiaomi and Flipkart executives, operating under the parent company Circuit House Technologies. The company designs premium smart televisions and projectors for the Indian mid-premium and premium segment, targeting customers frustrated by smart TVs that slow down over time. Lumio sells online via Amazon and Flipkart and, in its first year, built a customer base of over 20,000 buyers competing with incumbents like LG, Sony and Xiaomi.
company.name: Lumio

company.description: A1A is a Tokyo-based enterprise SaaS startup building UPCYCLE, a procurement data platform aimed at Japanese automotive manufacturers. The product structures quotation PDFs, drawings and supplier correspondence into reusable data so procurement teams can standardize cost assessments and surface cost-reduction opportunities. Founded in 2018 by former Keyence and VC operator Shuhei Matsubara, A1A has deployed UPCYCLE at OEMs and tier-1 parts makers and is now layering domain-specific AI on top of the accumulated procurement data.
company.name: A1A

company.description: Futures Music Group is an independent label collective founded in 2024 by Neon Gold co-founder Derek Davies and Avenue A co-founder Dave Wallace, offering artists and management partners equity-oriented deal structures alongside proprietary technology. The New York-based collective works with artists such as Lykke Li, Mt. Joy, Cavetown, Phantogram, The Knocks, Good Neighbours, and Barns Courtney, and holds minority stakes in music-tech companies including Indify, Splice, Chordal, Big Effect, and ITM Studio.
company.name: Futures Music Group

company.description: Tamber is a Los Angeles-based music technology startup building a sonic intelligence-powered creative suite for musicians and producers. The platform uses eight proprietary machine learning models to deliver assistive, non-generative AI tools that integrate directly into digital audio workstations, returning real-world samples, loops and one-shots in response to natural-language prompts about mood, place and texture. Founded by self-taught coder and musician Zoe Wrenn, the company pitches itself against generative AI by positioning its tools as augmentation rather than replacement of human artistry, with an official product launch scheduled for May 2026.
company.name: Tamber

company.description: Cluster Protocol is an AI deeptech and Web3 infrastructure company building the intelligence, orchestration, and execution rails for onchain applications, combining decentralized GPU compute with verifiable AI execution. Its flagship product, CodeXero, is a browser-native vibe-coding IDE for the EVM ecosystem that turns natural-language prompts into deployment-ready smart contracts and DeFi logic.
company.name: Cluster Protocol

company.description: Wenite is a Ghent-based HR tech company founded in 2021 that provides unified infrastructure for European HR service providers and consultants. Its white-label platform combines CRM, data collection, workflow automation, and AI-driven analytics, letting consultants centralise client and employee data, automate surveys and reporting, and deliver measurable outcomes through client-facing dashboards. It currently supports consultancies such as BDO, Springbok Coaching, and The Moris Company, serving over 500 end clients and 45,000 employees.
company.name: Wenite

company.description: Deep Algorithm Solutions is a Hyderabad-based AI cybersecurity startup specializing in Continuous Identity Risk Management using behavioral biometrics. Its flagship products adapID-AI and BotShield-AI detect identity fraud, bot attacks, and zero-day threats for BFSI, fintech, and government customers. Founded in 2021 by JP Mishra and incubated at IIIT Hyderabad, IIT Mandi, and IIT Kanpur, the company serves clients including Canara Bank, Karnataka Bank, DCB Bank, CSB, NCRTC, and BVH.
company.name: Deep Algorithm

company.description: Spill Games is a Bengaluru-based mobile gaming studio building a systems-driven, portfolio-led approach to casual and puzzle titles. Founded in 2024 by former PlaySimple operators, it develops games on an in-house engine called Compass that modularises analytics, retention, monetisation and live operations for rapid prototyping. The studio operates a live lineup including Cozy Finds, Sticker by Number and Zen Math Crossword, with several titles already at positive unit economics.
company.name: Spill Games

company.description: MINISH Technology is a dental health-tech company developing biomimetic restoration materials that mimic natural tooth enamel as a no-drill alternative to traditional veneers and crowns. Its 0.1mm-precision ceramic blocks and clinical-training ecosystem have been deployed across hundreds of clinicians in Asia, with active expansion into Japan and the United States. The company operates from Los Angeles with roots in Seoul and is led by CEO Dr. Jung-ho Kang.
company.name: Minish Technology

company.description: Iridius is a Seattle-based enterprise AI startup building a compliance-by-design platform for regulated industries, with an initial focus on life sciences and pharma. Its software translates regulatory standards and internal SOPs into machine-readable logic that is enforced continuously as AI agents and workflows run, automatically generating audit evidence along the way. Founded in 2024 by former Microsoft and AWS leaders, the company positions itself as integration infrastructure for existing systems like Veeva rather than a replacement for them.
company.name: Iridius

company.description: TheStorage is a Finnish cleantech company developing high-temperature industrial thermal energy storage that stores energy in moving sand at 800-1100°C. Its sand-in-motion technology lets factories electrify heat production, buy electricity during cheap hours, and release stored heat as steam or thermal oil when prices rise. Founded in 2023, the company targets up to 70% lower energy costs and up to 90% lower emissions versus fossil-based industrial heat.
company.name: TheStorage

company.description: BAND is an enterprise infrastructure startup building a shared interaction layer for multi-agent AI systems, letting autonomous agents across frameworks, clouds, and organizations discover one another, exchange context, delegate tasks, and operate under unified governance. The platform targets the coordination gap that emerges as companies deploy dozens or hundreds of agents across engineering, security, and operations workflows, offering cross-framework interoperability (LangChain, CrewAI, Claude Code, Codex) plus human-in-the-loop controls. Founded by CEO Arick Goomanovsky and CTO Vlad Luzin, with teams in Tel Aviv and San Francisco.
company.name: Band

company.description: Zócalo Health is a tech-enabled, community-based primary care provider serving high-need Medicaid populations in the United States. The Kenmore, Washington-headquartered company pairs community health workers (promotoras de salud) and virtual clinical teams with an AI-enabled platform to engage patients with complex medical, behavioral, and social needs. It contracts with Medicaid managed care organizations such as Anthem Blue Cross and Health Net under value-based arrangements tied to state initiatives including California CalAIM.
company.name: Zócalo Health

company.description: DOJO AI is an intelligent marketing platform built around a proprietary knowledge graph and specialized AI agents that watch paid, organic, SEO and content channels continuously, then act on what they see rather than just reporting on it. Founded in August 2024 and headquartered between London and Lisbon, the company pitches an agentic replacement for the fragmented martech stacks most teams assemble by hand, and counts CoinDesk, Morningstar, PensionBee and Broadvoice among 100+ brands using it across the US and UK.
company.name: DOJO AI

company.description: Pearl Diagnostics is a Baltimore-based biotech spun out of Johns Hopkins University in 2014 that builds non-invasive urine-based diagnostic tests for lung infections. Its flagship MycoMEIA Aspergillus urine assay, which received FDA 510(k) clearance in August 2025 and CE approval for the EU, replaces invasive bronchoalveolar lavage procedures used to detect aspergillosis in transplant recipients, cancer patients, and immunocompromised individuals. The company is extending the same antigen-detection platform to other fungal and bacterial pathogens, including a Pseudomonas assay for cystic fibrosis patients.
company.name: Pearl Diagnostics

company.description: Era (Era Computer) is building a software platform that serves as an intelligence layer for AI-powered hardware, letting device makers orchestrate more than 130 large language models from 14-plus providers across form factors like glasses, jewelry, pendants and home speakers. The company was founded in 2025 by CEO Liz Dorman (ex-Humane, HP), CTO Alex Ollman (ex-HP), and CPO Megan Gole (previously on the Jony Ive and Sam Altman io project at Sutter Hill Ventures), and aims to replace single-device app models with a shared agent infrastructure for the emerging AI-gadget category.
company.name: Era

company.description: STCH is an AI-native contract development and manufacturing organisation (CDMO) for the textile industry, headquartered in Bengaluru with operations in India, the UK, and the wider Asian supply chain. Its platform applies machine learning to fabric R&D, trend detection, and mill sourcing so global fashion brands can develop and deliver sustainable, high-performance fabrics at scale. Founded in 2025 by ex-Zetwerk operators Narahari Payala and Aseem Chitkara, STCH is building a "fabric GPT" to replace trial-and-error material development with data-driven formulation.
company.name: STCH

company.description: Cloneable is a Raleigh, North Carolina-based AI automation company that captures the institutional knowledge of experienced field experts and deploys it as autonomous agents for heavy industry operations. Its no-code platform lets utilities, infrastructure operators, manufacturers, and agriculture firms build and run AI agents on edge devices such as tablets, drones, and IoT hardware to inspect assets, run expert-dependent workflows, and bridge a widening field-knowledge gap.
company.name: Cloneable

company.description: Maxed is a Miami-based startup building an AI-native operating system for CPA firms, consolidating the fragmented software stack that accounting firms rely on into a single agentic platform. Its two AI agents — Max for back-office workflow monitoring and Ed for client-facing communications — automate administrative work so firms can serve more clients without adding headcount. Founded by 18-year-old solo founder Fifi Siddiqui, the platform is currently in beta with early design partners.
company.name: Maxed

company.description: Shade is an AI-powered cloud storage and media management platform built for creative teams handling large video libraries. Its natural-language search and auto-tagging system lets editors find specific scenes or assets across sprawling media archives without manual tagging, and its streamable file system mounts cloud storage locally so users can work on files before they finish downloading.
company.name: Shade

company.description: Affix Labs is a Dutch green chemtech startup developing a patented water-based solubility and controlled-release system that turns traditionally oily, water-insoluble active ingredients into long-lasting, neurotoxic-free insect barriers. Its flagship product, Repeltec, is a surface insect repellent authorised in Germany, France, Austria, and Norway and is designed as a compliant alternative as EU pesticide rules tighten. The company also licenses the technology to FMCG brands, hospitality operators, and pest-control manufacturers via a "Powered by Affix Labs" white-label model.
company.name: Affix Labs

company.description: Pudu Robotics, founded in 2016 and headquartered in Shenzhen's Nanshan district, designs and manufactures commercial service robots used for indoor delivery, cleaning, disinfection, and industrial logistics. Its product lines include the BellaBot and KettyBot service robots as well as the newer PUDU T150 and T300 industrial delivery robots, powered by in-house navigation, multi-robot scheduling, motion-control, and embodied-AI stacks. The company has shipped over 120,000 units to clients such as Carrefour, Walmart, and EDEKA across 80+ countries, and operates R&D centres in Chengdu and Hong Kong alongside a newly opened U.S. headquarters.
company.name: Pudu Robotics

company.description: Swoop is a consumer super-app startup headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria that launched its food-delivery service in April 2026 after originating in Eswatini as Thumo in August 2025. Co-founded by 19-year-old Thiel Fellow Aubrey Niederhoffer and Edwin Ruiz, the company uses food delivery as a high-frequency wedge to build toward a pan-African platform integrating groceries, ride-hailing and fintech, taking on local heavyweight Chowdeck and incumbents like Glovo and FoodCourt. Swoop runs an asset-light model with independent riders retaining 100% of delivery fees and monetises through restaurant commissions and a 7% customer handling fee.
company.name: Swoop

company.description: VREY (legally RE Joule GmbH) is a Berlin-based climate-tech company building an EnergyOS for multi-family buildings. As a certified smart metering operator and billing partner, it lets landlords deploy on-site solar and pass electricity directly to tenants under Germany's new Gemeinschaftliche Gebäudeversorgung (GGV) framework, without taking on energy-supplier obligations. Its platform also integrates batteries, heat pumps, and EV charging, and is live across a three-digit project pipeline in all 16 German federal states.
company.name: VREY

company.description: Almanac Health is a Boston-based clinical AI company building an evidence-based decision-support platform that brings specialist-grade medical knowledge to clinicians at the point of care inside existing electronic health record systems. The platform is grounded in peer-reviewed research, governed by institutional controls, and is undergoing clinical validation in academic medical centers. Founded by physician-researcher Cyril Zakka, MD, the company builds on his Stanford work introducing retrieval-augmented generation to clinical medicine.
company.name: Almanac Health

company.description: HrFlow.ai is a Paris-based AI infrastructure platform for the labor market, delivering an API-first system that structures, normalizes, and activates HR data across staffing agencies, job boards, ATS providers, and large employers. The platform combines three modules — Data Studio with 200+ connectors, AI Studio for explainable matching algorithms, and App Studio for building custom HR agents. Customers include Manpower, Kelly Services, Sanofi, Safran, and public-sector entities such as the French National Gendarmerie.
company.name: HrFlow.ai

company.description: Youtility Decision Science is a behavioral AI company, dual-headquartered in London and Richmond, Virginia, that analyses real-time behavioral signals from customer interactions to predict consumer decisions. Its Navier-Stokes platform classifies customers across eight mindset dimensions and delivers next-best-action recommendations via API into existing marketing and CRM stacks. The company serves financial institutions, data aggregators and consumer brands including Santander UK, Virgin Money and Monese.
company.name: Youtility Decision Science

company.description: Petual is a San Francisco-based enterprise audit and compliance platform that uses agentic AI to automate SOX testing and internal audit work. The system ingests evidence from structured and unstructured sources such as PDFs, spreadsheets, screenshots and emails to autonomously generate auditor-ready work papers. Early customers span S&P 500 and NASDAQ 100 companies across energy, SaaS, financial services, manufacturing and infrastructure.
company.name: Petual

company.description: Orkes is a Santa Clara, CA-based durable workflow and AI agent orchestration platform founded by engineers who originally built Netflix's Conductor microservices orchestration project. Its platform lets developers design, execute, and observe long-running workflows that combine LLM agents, microservices, and human-in-the-loop tasks with enterprise-grade reliability, governance, and observability. Customers span Fortune 100 enterprises including Twilio, LinkedIn, Quest Diagnostics, United Wholesale Mortgage, and Woodside Energy.
company.name: Orkes

company.description: Copperhelm is a Tel Aviv-based cybersecurity company building an agentic cloud security platform that uses specialized AI agents to continuously monitor cloud environments, investigate threats, and execute real-time remediation while keeping security teams in control. The platform is anchored by a proprietary Context Lake decision layer that structures cloud data across environments so purpose-built agents can analyze infrastructure, validate real risk, and deploy protections such as WAF rules without downtime. Founded in 2024 by cloud and security veterans from Unity, McAfee, and RSA, the company emerged from stealth in April 2026 already serving Fortune 500 enterprise customers.
company.name: Copperhelm

company.description: Rilian is an AI-native cybersecurity and defense systems integrator whose Caspian platform provides a single agentic command layer across an organization's security stack, using pre-trained AI agents to automate analyst workflows and accelerate deployment in sovereign clouds, on-premise systems, and air-gapped environments. The company works with governments and critical-infrastructure operators — including a UAE Cybersecurity Council deployment inside the country's National Security Operations Center — and orchestrates partner technologies from SentinelOne, Censys, SimSpace, hyperscalers, and foundational LLM developers.
company.name: Rilian

company.description: Qubitrium is a Turkish deep-tech company founded in 2020 as a spin-off from QuTech (Delft) and Özyeğin University, developing space-ready quantum hardware for secure communication and sensing. Its product portfolio includes entangled photon sources, single-photon detectors, quantum memories, and the QubitCore CubeSat payload, targeting Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) infrastructure for aerospace and defense. The company is building modular quantum technologies for both terrestrial and satellite networks, positioning itself in the emerging commercial quantum communications market.
company.name: Qubitrium

company.description: Astor is an SEC-registered, AI-native investment advisory platform that connects to a user's existing brokerage accounts and delivers personalized recommendations based on their actual holdings, performance, risk, and diversification. It targets the majority of American households shut out of traditional wealth management, where advisors typically require $500K+ in assets to engage a client. The company was founded by Bruno Koba (ex-Monashees, ex-Nubank) and Daniel Tulha (ex-Stripe, ex-Robinhood) and went through Y Combinator's Summer 2025 batch.
company.name: Astor

company.description: LightFury Games is a premier AAA gaming studio founded by industry veterans to develop high-quality original IP from India for a global audience. The studio is currently developing eCricket, a hyper-realistic sports simulation built on Unreal Engine 5, and operates development hubs in Bengaluru and London.
company.name: LightFury Games

company.description: Bubble Robotics develops integrated surface and subsea autonomous robotic systems for offshore infrastructure monitoring and maintenance. Founded by NASA and ETH Zürich alumni, the company utilizes resident subsea robots and the BubbleDock platform to remove human dependency and reduce inspection costs by up to 90%.
company.name: Bubble Robotics

company.description: Eccasion is a Dutch online marketplace for second-hand electric vehicles (EVs), founded by the creators of Swapfiets. The platform provides consumers with direct access to ex-lease EV auctions and handles battery health checks, quality inspections, and direct delivery to simplify the transition to sustainable mobility.
company.name: Eccasion

company.description: Incapto is a Barcelona-based specialty coffee startup that offers a sustainable alternative to coffee capsules. The company provides super-automatic bean-to-cup coffee machines paired with a subscription service for high-quality specialty coffee, serving both home consumers and business offices with its Coffee-as-a-Service model.
company.name: Incapto

company.description: Decade Energy is a logistics electrification and energy management company that helps fleet operators and property owners transition to electric transport. Founded by the team behind Volta Trucks, the company provides an integrated infrastructure solution—including grid connections, solar power, and battery storage—under a Zero-CapEx model.
company.name: Decade Energy

company.description: Naturbeads is a sustainable materials startup and University of Bath spin-out that develops 100% biodegradable cellulose-based microspheres. Their technology replaces harmful microplastics in cosmetics, paints, coatings, and detergents with cost-competitive, high-performance natural alternatives.
company.name: Naturbeads

company.description: Omni (Omni Analytics Inc.) is a business intelligence platform and enterprise semantic layer provider. Founded by the team that built Looker, Omni provides a unified business logic layer that connects data sources like Snowflake and Databricks to both traditional BI dashboards and modern AI data agents, ensuring accurate and governed data insights.
company.name: Omni

company.description: Cloudsmith provides a cloud-native software supply chain management platform that secures and governs the distribution of software artifacts. Its platform is designed to handle the increasing volume of AI-generated code, providing enterprise-grade visibility, control, and automated security for modern development teams.
company.name: Cloudsmith

company.description: Univity is a space technology company building a shared satellite-based telecommunications infrastructure. The company develops a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) constellation that acts as a wholesale network for telecom operators, allowing them to offer low-latency 5G connectivity worldwide using their existing spectrum.
company.name: Univity

company.description: Loc.ai (Locai) is a UK deeptech startup building off-cloud AI infrastructure that shifts model inference from centralized cloud servers onto users own edge devices. Its control-plane software lets developers and enterprises deploy, manage and scale AI models on-device, turning variable per-token cloud costs into predictable fixed costs while keeping data and model weights private. Founded in 2025, it targets SaaS and desktop AI vendors plus regulated industries that need data sovereignty.
company.name: Loc.ai

company.description: AI research company
company.name: Anthropic

company.description: SokoFresh is a Kenyan agritech operating a cooling-as-a-service model: solar-powered, IoT-monitored mobile cold-storage units paired with aggregation and market-linkage services for smallholder farmers across horticulture, fresh produce, and cereal value chains. Its units extend produce shelf life from under two days to more than ten, helping growers cut post-harvest losses and access higher-paying buyers, including avocado exports to the EU and UAE. Founded in 2019 and led by CEO Denis Karema, SokoFresh works in partnership with development financiers including UNCDF, Acumen, and the Dutch Fund for Climate and Development.
company.name: SokoFresh

company.description: Dnotitia is a South Korean AI infrastructure company building long-term memory and semiconductor-integrated solutions for generative AI workloads. Its core products are Seahorse, a vector database delivered as both SaaS and on-premises software, and VDPU (Vector Data Processing Unit), a dedicated chip designed to accelerate vector data search and processing. The company is preparing for an IPO on the Korea Exchange with Korea Investment & Securities and Shinhan Securities as joint lead managers.
company.name: Dnotitia

company.description: Sooth Labs is a Pittsburgh-based AI research lab building continuously trained world models for long-horizon forecasting of geopolitical and market events. Its system ingests verifiable propositions and returns calibrated probabilities with a structured causal timeline of driving events, targeting institutional users across finance, defense, insurance, and energy. The company emerged from stealth in April 2026 with a $50M seed at a $335M post-money valuation.
company.name: Sooth Labs

company.description: Fortem Technologies is a Utah-based airspace security firm that builds an end-to-end counter-unmanned aerial system stack — its SkyDome platform combines TrueView AI radar sensors, command-and-control software, and the autonomous, net-firing DroneHunter interceptor drone. The company sells primarily to defense and homeland-security customers, with deployments validated in Ukraine, the Middle East and East Asia, plus active U.S. Army and Department of Homeland Security contracts. Fortem positions itself as a low-cost-per-engagement alternative to kinetic interceptors as small-drone threats proliferate.
company.name: Fortem Technologies

company.description: Cresora Commerce is a Nashville, Tennessee-based AI-native commerce infrastructure platform that sits as a unified gateway and orchestration layer between payers and payees, handling payments, settlement, reconciliation and reporting. The processor-agnostic platform uses AI to automate integrations, normalize transaction data and detect anomalies, and is initially focused on healthcare before expanding to other enterprise verticals. It was founded in 2026 by alumni of AxiaMed, the healthcare payments platform acquired by Bank of America in 2021.
company.name: Cresora Commerce

company.description: San Francisco Bay Area startup building frontier AI for molecular-level protein characterization across the life sciences. Spun out of Nobel laureate Carolyn Bertozzi's Stanford lab in December 2025 (Y Combinator W26), the platform combines deterministic chemistry/biology algorithms with AI agents that interpret mass spectrometry data, delivering automated, explainable characterization of biologic therapeutics in minutes rather than weeks. Initial customers include pharmaceutical and biotech partners using the tool to accelerate drug development decisions.
company.name: 10x Science

company.description: AuX Labs is a Toronto-based food technology startup using precision fermentation to produce recombinant casein, the structural protein behind cheese melt and stretch. The company leverages existing global brewing capacity to scale animal-free dairy proteins at price points that compete with conventional dairy, and is targeting foodservice partners such as pizzerias and cafes for its first launches. AuX Labs self-affirmed its casein ingredient as Generally Recognized as Safe (GRAS) in April 2025.
company.name: AuX Labs

company.description: SCATR is a Cleveland-based cybersecurity company that pioneered the Zero Trust Transit category, a software-defined platform that protects data while it moves across untrusted networks rather than only at access points or endpoints. Built on issued US patents covering Data Camouflage, intelligent multi-path routing, and AI/ML-driven adaptive obfuscation, the platform fragments and randomly distributes traffic to defeat metadata analysis and Harvest Now, Decrypt Later collection. The company says its solution has been deployed across more than 50 operational environments on six continents, serving government and enterprise customers with AES-256 and ML-KEM quantum-resistant encryption.
company.name: SCATR

company.description: GRAI is a Warsaw-based AI music startup building tools that let consumers remix, transform and share existing tracks rather than generate new music from scratch. Its proprietary derivatives pipeline and real-time audio systems are designed to preserve the identity of original recordings while enabling user-driven variations, with an opt-in framework so artists and labels can earn royalties on derivative works. The company targets Gen Z and Gen Alpha listeners through early iOS remixing and Android playground apps.
company.name: GRAI

company.description: Brave group is a Tokyo-based entertainment company that creates and operates IP centered on VTubers, including the Vspo! group, alongside an animation studio (Brave Pictures) and a marketing arm (Brave Marketing) that helps brands reach Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences using VTubers, Roblox, and XR. Founded in October 2017 and led by CEO Keito Noguchi, the company combines IP production, distribution, and a proprietary PLAY Model marketing framework to take Japanese virtual-talent IP into global markets.
company.name: Brave Group

company.description: Courier Health is a New York-based AI-driven patient experience platform for the life-sciences industry. Its software orchestrates and personalises patient journeys across biopharma therapies — surfacing next-best actions for patient-support teams, automating case management, and unifying data from hubs, specialty pharmacies, and payer systems. Founded by CEO Danny Sigurdson, the company more than 4x'd its customer base and therapy footprint in 2025 and doubled headcount.
company.name: Courier Health

company.description: PrimeInvestor is a Chennai-based wealth-tech platform that combines independent research, portfolio-review services, and discretionary Portfolio Management Services for retail and high-net-worth Indian investors. Operating a fee-only, zero-commission model, it runs three PMS strategies — Prime Vision (multi-asset mutual funds), Prime Synergy (mutual funds plus direct equity), and Prime Velocity (equity multi-cap) — with a ₹50 lakh minimum and annual fees of 0.6-1.2%. Founded in 2020 by ex-FundsIndia executives Srikanth Meenakshi, Vidya Bala, and Bhavana Acharya.
company.name: PrimeInvestor

company.description: Calibre is a London-based healthtech startup introducing what it calls Causal Health Navigation — a clinician-led AI platform that connects medical history, daily behaviours, and environmental context with diagnostic tests to identify the root drivers of individual health and deliver personalised, proactive care plans. Membership starts at £69 a month, with clinical delivery through CQC-registered partner DocTap. Founded in 2025 by Alexander Weber (former N26 CGO), Ben Levy (former Elvie and Manual), and Dr. Reinhold Innerhofer, the company emerged from stealth in April 2026.
company.name: Calibre

company.description: Lucra operates a B2B white-label SDK that lets consumer brands embed peer-to-peer tournaments and cash-or-credit competitions inside their own apps and venues, turning loyalty programs into interactive esports-style events. The platform handles compliance, payments, fraud prevention, and settlement so partners like Five Iron Golf, Puttshack, Dave & Buster's, Chess Kings, and TouchTunes can run low-to-no-stakes competitions without building the gaming stack themselves. Founded in 2022 and led by CEO Dylan Robbins, Lucra is on pace to surpass 1 million unique tournament signups in 2026.
company.name: Lucra

company.description: Netbank is a Philippine banking-as-a-service provider that operates under a full domestic banking licence and lets fintechs, lending platforms, and payroll/HR software embed accounts, payments, cards, and lending directly into their own products. Its white-label, Partnership Based Banking model consolidates regulated financial infrastructure under one licensed entity, removing the need for partners to stitch together multiple providers. The company reported 88% year-on-year revenue growth and reached profitability in FY2025.
company.name: Netbank

company.description: Guangzhou-based chip designer (Chinese name 奕行智能, English EVAS Intelligence) building full-stack RISC-V AI computing chips and platforms for intelligent vehicles, data centres and AI inference workloads. Its flagship Epoch series pairs a TPU-like compute fabric with the open-source RISC-V instruction set and natively supports FP8 as well as FP4 / MXFP4 low-precision formats, with the product matrix spanning chiplets, PCIe cards, servers and intelligent computing clusters. Founded in January 2022 by CEO Liu Hui — a 20-year semiconductor veteran who led product chip R&D at ZTE and was China GM of US ASIC firm eSilicon — the company was seeded by GAC Capital and has since pulled in strategic capital from Beijing's municipal industrial-upgrade funds.
company.name: Yixing Intelligence

company.description: Crypto casino and sportsbook launched in late 2024 by the FanDuel co-founders — CEO Nigel Eccles along with Rob Jones and Ryan Anderson — combining a consumer-facing iGaming product on the BetHog brand with Sentient Studios, a B2B division spun up alongside the Series A that sells AI-powered live dealers to other casino operators. The company says its basic AI blackjack dealer has been tested for six months against human equivalents and is roughly 10× more popular, with materially better player retention and satisfaction. Based in Cyprus, BetHog has raised $16M in total to date ($6M seed led by 6MV in 2024 plus this $10M Series A co-led by Will Ventures and RockawayX).
company.name: BetHog

company.description: Mountain View-based AI software company building "mechanical intuition" into machines to close the gap between a finished digital product design and the factory-ready production plan needed to actually make it. Its flagship product AutoAssembler automates process planning — the step where engineers hand-translate CAD models into assembly instructions, CNC routings and ECO reviews — compressing workflows that typically take weeks into minutes, and it is already deployed inside Global Fortune 100 manufacturers as well as mid-market shops. Co-founded in 2023 by CEO Sai Nelaturi together with AI-veterans Johan de Kleer and Mats Bergstrom.
company.name: C-Infinity

company.description: AI-native denial-management and revenue-recovery platform for US healthcare providers. Amperos pairs agentic AI that automates the end-to-end appeal and collections workflow on denied insurance claims with a human team of subject-matter experts who handle the hard cases, so billing departments recover more of the roughly $260B/year stuck in denied claims. The company says it already serves 3,000+ clinical locations across all 50 states and recovers nearly $700M of revenue per year across 500,000+ claims. Founded in 2023 by CEO Michal Miernowski (ex-Onex / Goldman Sachs), CPO Alvin Wu (ex-Samsara / Fitbit) and CTO Wilson Wang.
company.name: Amperos Health

company.description: Berlin-based startup founded in 2025 that is building a new generation of pan-European overnight trains with fully private rooms, positioning itself as an alternative to short-haul flights for both leisure and business travellers. The company combines a modular, high-density cabin design with standardised operations and a hospitality-focused service model, aiming to launch its first routes in 2027. Nox was co-founded by railway YouTuber Thibault Constant (Simply Railway), Janek Smalla, and Artur Hasselbach.
company.name: Nox Mobility

company.description: ATMOS Space Cargo is a German-French space logistics company developing PHOENIX, a family of reusable orbital transfer and return vehicles that use an Inflatable Atmospheric Decelerator (IAD) as a combined heat shield and aerodynamic brake. From facilities in Lichtenau, Germany and Strasbourg, France, the company operates the first European private re-entry service — it flew and recovered data from PHOENIX 1 on SpaceX's Bandwagon-3 rideshare in April 2025, and holds Portugal's first commercial re-entry licence for splashdowns off the Azores. ATMOS serves microgravity research, in-orbit manufacturing, and dual-use government and defence customers who need sovereign return from Low Earth Orbit.
company.name: ATMOS Space Cargo

company.description: Helsinki-based AI platform for enterprise revenue teams that turns a company's go-to-market knowledge — products, pipeline, strategies, past wins — into a structured context graph and deploys AI agents against it to draft RFP responses, security questionnaires, and other deal-defining materials. Customers typically approve 70-80% of Realm's output as-is, with edits feeding back into the graph to compound institutional memory. Founded in 2023 by former Slush leaders Mikko Mäntylä, Miika Huttunen and Johan Jern; counts Visma, Aiven and Hostaway among its customers.
company.name: Realm

company.description: Mosaic is a New York-based software platform that automates deal modeling for private markets investors and their advisors. The product combines deterministic, rules-based calculations with AI-driven document ingestion and agentic workflows to generate LBO, DCF, and related fundamental analyses — work historically performed manually in Excel. Mosaic counts leading private equity, private credit, and investment banking firms among its customers, including Warburg Pincus, Bridgepoint, CVC, New Mountain, and Evercore.
company.name: Mosaic

company.description: Monk is an AI-native accounts receivable platform for B2B companies, automating the full contract-to-cash lifecycle from invoicing and collections through cash application and dispute resolution. The company wraps each model call in deterministic code so AI-generated financial actions stay auditable, and counts AI-native firms such as ElevenLabs and Profound among its customers. Founded by George Kurdin and Joe Zhou and based in New York, Monk publicly reports managing over $1B in customer receivables.
company.name: Monk

company.description: FanBase is a Brazilian sports-technology company offering a white-label fan-engagement SaaS platform for sports clubs and leagues. It unifies ticketing, e-commerce, food and beverage ordering, payments and fan data into a single system, with products such as Fan ID (a unified login/identity for supporters) and a branded FanApp. The platform is used by roughly 30% of Brazil's top-tier football clubs (including Vasco da Gama, Atletico Mineiro, Bahia and Ceara) and is expanding across South America.
company.name: FanBase

company.description:
The future of Europe's economy is startups, scale-ups & mid-market businesses, but their finance stack is broken. It's fragmented, manual and costly. And it's holding ambitious businesses back by slowing them down, reducing financial clarity and costing them money. 

That’s why we’re building the financial home for European startups. Seapoint is a business account that automates payroll, expenses, invoice payment, reporting and more. Join us and claim your unfair financial advantage.
company.name: Seapoint

company.description: Hot Cloud Storage company providing affordable, predictable cloud storage with no egress or API fees, expanding into AI infrastructure and cyber-resilient data services.
company.name: Wasabi Technologies

company.description: Syenta is an Australian semiconductor startup developing patented chip packaging technology to build high-speed interconnects between chiplets, aiming to overcome memory bandwidth bottlenecks and enhance AI and quantum computing performance.
company.name: Syenta

company.description: Tamatem is the leading Arabic mobile games publisher in MENA, founded in 2013 by Hussam Hammo. The company has published over 70 localised games reaching 300 million downloads and 3 million monthly active users, and operates Tamatem Plus — a payments network integrating 45+ regional payment methods through a single API. Following the 2026 acquisition of Playable Factory, Tamatem is repositioning as an AI-first gaming platform spanning content, distribution, payments and ad tech, with 180 staff across Amman, Riyadh, Abu Dhabi, Cairo, Istanbul and Baghdad.
company.name: Tamatem Games

company.description: Tokyo-based developer of humanoid robots for public safety, urban maintenance, and other mission-critical environments, applying physical AI to combine intelligence, dexterity, and resilience. Co-founded by Android creator Andy Rubin, the stealth-mode company emerged from prior entity Simple Things LLC and is backed by Andreessen Horowitz, DCM, AMD, Incubate Fund, and X&. It hit a roughly $1B valuation at Series A in April 2026, less than a year after its $50M seed.
company.name: Genki Robotics

company.description: DeepWay develops new-energy autonomous heavy-duty freight trucks, designing vehicle platforms, electric powertrains, and Level 4 driving software in-house rather than retrofitting diesel chassis. Founded in 2020 as a joint venture between Baidu and Chinese trucking firm Lionbridge, the Hefei-based company has delivered around 6,400 intelligent electric trucks across China since starting commercial operations in 2023 and filed for a Hong Kong listing in November 2025.
company.name: DeepWay

company.description: Collov Labs is a San Francisco-based AI research and product company building visual intelligence systems that combine diffusion models, spatial reasoning, and agentic workflows to turn images and camera input into real-world actions. Founded in 2021 by Stanford, Berkeley, and Yale researchers, it operates consumer apps Collov AI and CozyAI, with more than 1 million users across retail, design, and e-commerce, and was the first consumer application integrated into Qualcomm's on-device Hexagon NPU stack.
company.name: Collov Labs

company.description: Addi es una empresa de tecnología que busca impulsar y habilitar el comercio digital en Latinoamérica. En Addi queremos que las personas compren lo que quieran, cuando quieran, de forma fácil, rápida y transparente. Como debe ser.
company.name: Addi

company.description: Stellar Cyber is a San Jose, California-based cybersecurity company that operates an AI-native, open and unified security operations (SecOps) platform built for managed security service providers and enterprise security teams. The platform consolidates SIEM, network detection and response, identity threat detection, and Multi-Layer AI in a single environment with more than 500 third-party integrations. Founded in 2015, Stellar Cyber serves over 14,000 organizations across 50+ countries through its MSSP partner network and counts roughly one-third of the world's top 250 MSSPs as customers.
company.name: Stellar Cyber

company.description: HQLAX is a Luxembourg-based financial technology firm that uses distributed ledger technology to power digital collateral mobility for the global securities finance and repo markets. Its platform lets banks and asset managers move high-quality liquid assets between counterparties in real time, frictionlessly, and with regulator-grade auditability. The company partners with major custodians and market infrastructure providers and is migrating to the Canton Network to expand interoperability across regulated capital markets.
company.name: HQLAX

company.description: AXIA Time is a Warren, New Jersey-based luxury watchmaker that designs and produces heirloom-quality, Swiss Made commemorative timepieces. Founded in 2018 by John Kanaras, the company focuses on custom and limited-edition watches built around milestone events, partnering with sports organizations, universities, and government agencies. Notable engagements include serving as the Official Timepiece of the College Football Playoff and collaborations with the Heisman Trophy program.
company.name: AXIA Time

company.description: Serif Biomedicines is a Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotechnology company developing Modified DNA as a new therapeutic class. Founded in 2021 within Flagship Labs and emerged from stealth in April 2026, Serif combines chemically modified DNA with mRNA co-factors and lipid nanoparticles to enable programmable, redosable gene expression without altering the genome. The platform targets rare diseases and immune programming and is protected by more than 20 patent families.
company.name: Serif Biomedicines

company.description: Alesi Surgical is a Cardiff-based medical device company commercialising Ultravision, an electrostatic-precipitation platform that actively removes surgical smoke from operating theatres without disrupting laparoscopic, robotic, or open-surgery workflows. Its first-generation system has been used in more than 50,000 keyhole procedures across Europe, the US, and Japan, and its FDA-approved and CE-marked Ultravision2 device addresses the visibility, infection, and toxicity hazards posed by smoke generated by electrosurgical tools.
company.name: Alesi Surgical

company.description: A&K Robotics is a Vancouver-based deep-tech company building autonomous passenger mobility infrastructure for complex indoor environments, with airports as its initial focus. Its flagship product, the Cruz pod, is a self-driving mobility robot powered by the company's Kinesos AI platform that navigates dense, dynamic terminals to transport passengers — particularly travelers with mobility limitations — between locations such as check-in and the gate. Cruz is already deployed with operators including Vancouver International Airport (YVR) and Aena's Madrid-Barajas Airport (MAD).
company.name: A&K Robotics

company.description: Aro is a Brazilian fintech that uses an AI conversational agent named Wendy to guide middle and lower-income users through credit decisions, analyzing behavioral data and Open Finance signals to recommend personalized credit products. Founded in 2024 and headquartered in São Paulo, the company is building an AI-native lending stack aimed at the roughly 80 million Brazilians underserved by traditional credit scoring. By April 2026 it had attracted more than 100,000 users and facilitated over USD 400,000 in loans through the Wendy assistant.
company.name: Aro

company.description: Schematic is a Boulder-based runtime monetization platform for SaaS and AI companies. Its entitlements infrastructure decouples pricing, packaging, and feature-access rules from application code, letting non-technical teams change offers and permissions through a dashboard with a native Stripe integration. Customers like Plotly report cutting pricing-change cycles from weeks to ten minutes. Founded in 2023 by Fynn Glover (CEO), Gio Hobbins (CPO), and Ben Papillon (CTO).
company.name: Schematic

company.description: SpartanX is an AI-native offensive security company that operates a fully autonomous full-stack red teaming platform. The system orchestrates 500+ AI agents across web applications, APIs, networks, cloud infrastructure, identity systems, and AI targets, validating each finding with a real exploit and generating code-level remediation mapped to SOC2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and ISO 27001. Founded by CEO Diego Spahn, the company is headquartered in Boston.
company.name: SpartanX

company.description: Shorta is a Latin American premium vertical-series streaming platform that commissions short-form, mobile-first scripted fiction — horror, sci-fi, thriller, comedy and romance — across 1-to-2-minute episodes produced by third-party showrunners and creators. Founded in 2025 by Oscar-winning Birdman co-writer Armando Bo, Cuevana co-founder Tomás Escobar, and NXTP managing partner Ariel Arrieta, the Buenos Aires-based company operates as a financing engine for indie producers and aims to release more than 100 originals in 2026 and scale to 500 regionally within two years. Monetises via a hybrid AVOD + premium-subscription model and claims a production cost roughly 1/50th of traditional streamers.
company.name: Shorta

company.description: NeoCognition is an AI research lab building self-learning agents designed to specialize into domain experts by continuously learning the workflows and constraints of the environments they operate in. Founded in 2025 by Ohio State University AI researchers Yu Su, Xiang Deng, and Yu Gu, the company targets enterprise deployments where today's general-purpose agents are too unreliable for complex or high-stakes tasks. Headquartered in Palo Alto, NeoCognition draws on the team's prior academic work — including the Mind2Web, MMMU, and SeeAct benchmarks widely used across frontier labs — to pursue what it calls specialized intelligence.
company.name: NeoCognition

company.description: Afresh is a San Francisco-based AI platform for the grocery industry, founded in 2017 by Matt Schwartz, Nathan Fenner, and Volodymyr Kuleshov. Its software orchestrates store-level ordering, inventory management, production planning, and distribution-center buying across fresh, center store, frozen, and general merchandise. Afresh operates in more than 12,500 departments across 40 U.S. states and partners with grocers including Albertsons Companies, Meijer, Stater Bros., and Wakefern.
company.name: Afresh

company.description: Carbonyx is a Vancouver-based carbon-removal startup that runs electrochemical reactors on mining, construction, and manufacturing waste rock. The process uses electricity and water to mineralize atmospheric CO2 into permanent carbonate rock while yielding industrial by-products such as silica and calcium carbonate for the cement, coatings, and glass markets. Spun out of UBC's Department of Chemistry, the company positions itself as a materials business that pays for carbon removal rather than relying on offset markets.
company.name: Carbonyx

company.description: Lawyered is a Delhi NCR-based legal-tech startup that delivers AI-powered on-road and compliance legal assistance for individuals and businesses. Its flagship products LOTS247 (instant lawyer consultation), ChallanPay (traffic-challan resolution), and Highway Heroes+ (subscription cover for truck drivers) are built on top of a network of roughly 80,000 lawyers across India, with over 2 million vehicles and 800+ business clients served and 200,000+ legal matters resolved. Founded in 2017 by former LexisNexis India product lead Himanshu Gupta, the company is now extending its platform beyond mobility into finance, real estate, and healthcare.
company.name: Lawyered

company.description: NudgeBee is a Pune-based enterprise software company building agentic AI assistants for cloud operations, site reliability, and FinOps teams. Its platform sits over cloud or on-premise infrastructure, ingesting telemetry, logs, and incident history into a semantic context layer that pre-built agents use to troubleshoot, remediate, and optimize cost autonomously. Founded in 2024 by Rakesh Rajendran and Shiv Pratap Singh, the company counts Rackspace among its early production customers and targets US mid-market enterprises and India-based global capability centers.
company.name: NudgeBee

company.description: AcuityMD is a Boston-based commercial intelligence platform for the medical-device industry, combining claims data, FDA filings, reimbursement signals, and a proprietary MedTech ontology to show sales and marketing teams which physicians, facilities, and procedures to prioritize. Its AcuityAI layer surfaces agentic answers tied to each customer CRM, contracting, and territory data so reps can act on market shifts in real time. The company serves more than 400 MedTech customers — including 16 of the top 20 device makers — and was named to Forbes 2025 Next Billion-Dollar Startups list.
company.name: AcuityMD

company.description: Tava Health is a Salt Lake City-based behavioral health technology company that operates a full-stack mental health platform serving clinicians, employers, and health plans. Its network is in-network with more than 200 health plans across all 50 states, with first-session availability in as little as 12 hours, and its product suite spans an AI-assisted clinical operating system (Symphony), an employer benefit offering (TavaCare), and a care-navigation hub for payers (Tava Guide). Co-founded and led by CEO Dallen Allred, the company reports that 87% of its clients show measurable clinical improvement.
company.name: Tava Health

company.description: Ray Therapeutics is a Berkeley, California clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing optogenetic gene therapies to restore vision in patients with retinal degenerative diseases. Its approach delivers a bioengineered, highly light-sensitive protein to surviving retinal cells via a single intravitreal injection, aiming to restore functional sight regardless of the underlying genetic mutation. Lead candidate RTx-015 is in clinical development for retinitis pigmentosa and holds FDA Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation, while second program RTx-021 targets macular conditions such as Stargardt disease and geographic atrophy.
company.name: Ray Therapeutics

company.description: Reliable Robotics develops the Reliable Autonomy System, a retrofit autonomy stack designed to enable fully automated operation of fixed-wing aircraft such as the Cessna 208B Caravan without changes to the National Airspace System. Founded by former SpaceX and Tesla engineers and headquartered in Mountain View, California, the company is pursuing FAA certification of its system for civilian cargo operations while also supplying it to the U.S. Air Force for contested-logistics missions. It has been selected for the U.S. DOT/FAA Advanced Air Mobility Integration Pilot Program and has secured commitments for more than 200 systems from commercial and military customers.
company.name: Reliable Robotics

company.description: Deep Blue Medical Advances is a Duke University spin-out developing next-generation surgical devices for soft tissue repair and hernia surgery. Its platform centers on the T-Line Mesh, Suture, and Aesthetic Scaffold product lines, purpose-built to prevent suture pull-through and reinforce tissue during closure. Backed by 18 peer-reviewed publications and 21 issued patents, its lockstitch technology has been deployed in more than 20,000 implanted procedures across U.S. hospital systems.
company.name: Deep Blue Medical

company.description: Firenze is a Manchester-based fintech building an embedded-finance platform that puts Lombard lending — traditionally a private-bank-only product — into the toolkit of wealth managers, IFAs, and investment platforms serving mass-affluent clients. The platform lets partners originate investment-backed loans without requiring clients to sell assets, typically funding in under 24 hours, and is operated alongside a SaaS proposition for banks. Founded in January 2024 by David Newman and Anna Curtis, Firenze works with UK firms representing nearly £200bn in assets under management and counts former TSB and Virgin Money CEO Paul Pester as chairman.
company.name: Firenze

company.description: Bfree is a Lagos-headquartered credit management fintech founded in 2020 by Julian Flosbach, Chukwudi Enyi, and Moses Nmor, specializing in ethical debt recovery and the acquisition of distressed loan portfolios across Africa. Its AI-driven platform scores borrower repayment capacity and runs non-punitive, automated engagement, replacing harassment-led collection tactics common in emerging markets. The company operates across Nigeria, Ghana, and Kenya, having handled over $740 million in distressed loans for more than 30 financial institutions including commercial banks and digital lenders.
company.name: Bfree

company.description: Fascano is a Muscat-based hospitality technology startup founded in 2021. It provides a comprehensive cloud-based platform for hotels, restaurants, and cafés to streamline operations, including reservations, digital menus, tableside ordering, and QR-based payments. The company aims to digitize core workflows and improve the guest experience across the MENA region.
company.name: Fascano

company.description: Tortugas Neuroscience is a clinical-stage biotechnology company based in Framingham, Massachusetts. It focuses on developing potential treatments for major neurology and neuropsychiatric disorders, including schizophrenia, tinnitus, focal epilepsy, and reversible encephalopathies. The company's pipeline includes assets licensed from Eisai and Hansoh Pharmaceutical, with a focus on small molecules with de-risked mechanisms of action.
company.name: Tortugas Neuroscience

company.description: Qualitate is a New York City-based AI-native primary intelligence platform that automates expert interviews and synthesizes the output into structured, queryable insights for investment and corporate strategy teams. Its AI Moderator runs thousands of expert discussions per month, feeding a time-series dataset that covers more than 10,000 public and private companies and serves hedge funds, private equity, credit funds, venture firms, and Fortune-class enterprises. Founded and led by Sagar Kadakia, the company positions itself as an always-on alternative to traditional expert networks, delivering custom research in days rather than weeks at roughly one-third the cost.
company.name: Qualitate

company.description: Cosaic — formerly Cultivated Biosciences (rebranded April 2025) — is a Swiss food-tech company that ferments non-GMO oleaginous yeast strains to produce Cosaic Neo, a single multifunctional ingredient that delivers creaminess, stability, and emulsification for dairy-free, clean-label food and beverage formulations. Cosaic Neo composition is roughly 8-15% protein, 45-60% fat, and 25-45% fibre, replacing single-purpose emulsifiers derived from dairy, eggs, or industrial plant-based formulations. Applications include RTD protein shakes, coffee creamers, non-dairy milks, mayo, sauces, and creamy liqueurs. Led by co-founder and CEO Tomas Turner; strategic commercialisation partnership with Ingredion announced November 2025.
company.name: Cosaic (Cultivated Biosciences)

company.description: Cerca Magnetics is a UK medical-technology company spun out of the University of Nottingham's School of Physics and Astronomy, developing wearable brain-imaging systems based on magnetoencephalography (MEG) and optically pumped magnetometer (OPM) quantum sensors. Its lightweight, motion-tolerant scanner allows patients to move naturally during imaging — enabling MEG studies on infants, children, and dementia patients that are impractical with traditional fixed scanners. The company sells its systems to neuroscience centres worldwide and is advancing clinical-approval pathways in the UK and United States for applications in epilepsy, multiple sclerosis, and dementia.
company.name: Cerca Magnetics

company.description: Smart Robotics is a Dutch robotics company that develops AI-driven pick-and-place solutions for the logistics and packaging industries. Its flexible, software-centric platform enables robots to perform complex tasks such as item picking, palletizing, and depalletizing with high accuracy and minimal human intervention. Spun out from Eindhoven University of Technology, the company serves global 3PLs and warehouse operators.
company.name: Smart Robotics

company.description: Exergy3 is a University of Edinburgh spinout commercialising a modular high-temperature thermal-energy-storage (TES) technology — a "thermal battery" that stores heat at up to 1,200°C with significantly higher energy density than incumbent systems. The platform converts curtailed or surplus renewable electricity into clean industrial heat, replacing fossil-fuel boilers in sectors like distilling, food & beverage, chemicals, and manufacturing. Co-founded in May 2023 by CEO Dr Markus Rondé and CSO Dr Adam Robinson (technology inventor, School of Engineering), with a flagship first-of-a-kind deployment at the Annandale Distillery demonstrating real-world operation.
company.name: Exergy3

company.description: Sinai.ai is an Egypt-based AI-native books platform transforming traditional published content into adaptive, interactive experiences rather than static text. Its core product is aiBook™, a trademarked and patent-backed format that layers real-time conversation, custom study guides, multilingual access, audio/read switching, and in-context visualisation on top of the books a user actually owns — built on 100 percent licensed full-text content sourced directly from publishers rather than scraped material. Founded in 2024 by CEO Ahmed Kamel with Mohamed Elshamy, Mohamed Elshenawy, Hana Malhas, and Abdullah Moatasem, the company launches with thousands of titles and has already secured partnerships with double-digit publishers across the industry.
company.name: Sinai.ai

company.description: &Charge is a German EV-charging-technology company that layers a hybrid SaaS + field-partner model on top of public charging networks to close the gap between declared uptime and actual in-the-field performance. Common real-world failures — damaged cables, blocked parking bays, poor signage, restricted access — often go undetected by standard monitoring; &Charge uses a combined user network and local service partners to identify, prioritise, resolve and verify these issues in a closed loop. Under CEO and co-founder Eugen Letkemann, the platform supports more than 50,000 charging points across Europe for operators including Ionity, Electra and Allego.
company.name: &Charge

company.description: VisioLab is an Osnabrück, Germany-based AI self-checkout company (with a US office in Boston) whose iPad-based systems use computer vision and edge AI to recognise both packaged and unpackaged food items at point of sale — eliminating barcode scanning and infrastructure modifications. Operator-reported data shows up to 70 percent reductions in average transaction times and roughly 10-minute setup by existing staff. Founded in 2019, the company serves corporate dining, higher education, sports and entertainment, and travel and leisure — with anchor deployments at Orlando Magics Kia Center, Inter Miami CFs Nu Stadium, and an ongoing rollout with Live Nation Entertainment.
company.name: VisioLab

company.description: Zynt is a Warsaw-based AI startup building a "signal intelligence" platform for B2B sales that monitors news, social media, job postings, press releases, product launches and other online signals to identify when a specific prospect is actually close to a buying decision. Its ELT pipeline combines machine learning, natural-language processing and elements of game theory to learn which signals matter inside a given customers pipeline, shifting outreach from "who to contact" to "when and why now". Founded by Cezary Reszel (CEO) and Wojciech Ozimek (CPTO), the duo relocated to New York City and conducted roughly 1,200 conversations with sales professionals before building the product — current customer base is concentrated among Polish companies operating globally, with a planned US market push as the next expansion step.
company.name: Zynt

company.description: Orange Quantum Systems (OrangeQS) is a Delft-based deep-tech company building the only ready-to-use, automated testing infrastructure for quantum chips at scale — flagship OrangeQS MAX is the only commercial machine on the market capable of characterising large 100+ qubit chips, compressing test cycles that historically took six-to-eighteen months in a research lab down to hours. Spun out of TNO and QuTech in 2020 under executive director Garrelt Alberts with co-founders Thorsten Last and Adriaan Rol, the company also ships OrangeQS FLEX (research-lab configurable), Juice (open-source quantum control OS), and Quantum Care (consultancy). Customers include IQM, the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, Berkeley Labs Advanced Quantum Testbed and QuTech; the OrangeQS MAX Partnership Programme aligns its roadmap directly with Rigetti Computing, QuantWare and Peak Quantum.
company.name: OrangeQS

company.description: inploi is a London-based enterprise hiring-technology company that sits between the candidate market and enterprise applicant tracking systems, owning the candidate-experience layer for high-volume recruitment. Its white-labelled platform streamlines sourcing, screening, communications and scheduling via AI agents branded "Talent Agents", integrates with existing ATS deployments, and has to date facilitated millions of job applications across more than 70,000 vacancies for enterprise clients including Compass Group, Wagamama, Gails, PureGym and several NHS trusts across hospitality, leisure, healthcare, construction, government and retail. Founded in 2016 by Matthew de la Hey and Alex Hanson-Smith and pivoted in 2021 from a labour marketplace to enterprise SaaS, inploi has acquired over 30 enterprise customers and recently entered the US market.
company.name: inploi

company.description: ALE (株式会社ALE) is a Tokyo-based space company whose stated mission is to "connect science with society and turn space into a cultural sphere". Its flagship project is Sky Canvas, an artificial-shooting-star entertainment service using purpose-built small satellites to release radiant-material pellets into the upper atmosphere; alongside it ALE runs a data-services business, small-satellite R&D, and early space-exploration programmes. Founded 1 September 2011 by CEO Lena Okajima (a Tohoku University-derived startup recognised under Japans J-Startup programme), the company has since pivoted commercial focus toward space-entertainment business development.
company.name: ALE

company.description: Magic Shields is a Hamamatsu, Shizuoka-based medtech company building "Koroyawa" (ころやわ®), a cushioned floor and mat engineered to absorb the impact of a fall without impeding normal walking — a deliberate rejection of the motion-restricting approach that dominates fall-prevention today. The product is deployed across more than 1,600 hospitals, care facilities and home-care settings as of April 2026, and positions itself as infrastructure for Japans broader attempt to curb the ~¥2 trillion annual cost burden of fall-related fractures in an ageing population. Founded in November 2019 by CEO Akishi Shimomura, the company frames its mission as "creating a world where people can stand back up no matter how many times they fall," and is now positioning for international expansion into other ageing markets.
company.name: Magic Shields

company.description: Blue Energy develops modular nuclear power plants manufactured in existing fab yards and shipyards, using submerged light-water reactor designs to improve safety and simplify construction. The company emphasizes prefabrication and fixed-price contracting to enable predictable timelines and project finance, targeting high-density energy users such as AI data centers and industrial offtakers. Its approach focuses on passive safety features, modular scalability, and the ability to provide interim natural-gas bridge power while licensing and installing nuclear islands.
company.name: Blue Energy

company.description: Nucleus RadioPharma is a Rochester, Minnesota-based contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) focused on radiopharmaceutical therapies for cancer patients. The company provides an integrated, end-to-end platform spanning formulation, analytical development, regulatory documentation and drug-product manufacturing, with regional facilities designed to deliver short half-life therapies before they decay. Founded in late 2022 with backing from Mayo Clinic and Eclipse Ventures, Nucleus operates a flagship site on the Mayo Clinic campus and is building a second hub outside Philadelphia.
company.name: Nucleus RadioPharma

company.description: Issuer and operator of the Japanese yen-pegged stablecoin JPYC, supporting Avalanche, Ethereum, and Polygon for financial and Web3 ecosystems.
company.name: JPYC Inc.

company.description: X Square Robot develops general-purpose embodied intelligence robots with high-precision manipulation capabilities, including models like WALL-A and robots such as Quanta X1 and Quanta X2 for applications in manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare.
company.name: X Square Robot

company.description: AlixLabs AB is a Swedish deep-tech company building a new generation of semiconductor manufacturing solutions, spun off from Lund University in 2019. The company specializes in Atomic Layer Etching Pitch Splitting (APS™) technology, which enables more cost-effective and energy-efficient leading-edge chip manufacturing.
company.name: AlixLabs AB

company.description: Ray (operating as Stingray) is a Dubai-based powerbank-sharing service that runs a network of portable charging stations in restaurants, cafes, malls, hotels and transport hubs across the UAE. Its differentiator is a Tap-to-Pay flow that lets users rent a powerbank in roughly 15 seconds via bank card, Apple Pay or Google Pay without downloading an app or registering. Founded in 2025 by Igor Kosolap and Roman Averianov, the company is currently live in Dubai and Abu Dhabi and is scaling toward 2,000 locations across the UAE before expanding through the GCC.
company.name: Ray

company.description: Archil is a San Francisco-based startup building a high-performance cloud file system designed for AI workloads. Its serverless platform turns Amazon S3 buckets into a local-like, infinitely scalable file system, letting AI inference, model training, and agent applications access large datasets without the latency or capacity limits of conventional storage. Founded in 2024 by Hunter Leath, a former lead on Amazon EFS and Netflix's cloud storage team, the YC F24 company is building file systems as the substrate that connects AI to its data.
company.name: Archil

company.description: South Korean fintech startup applying AI to the structured derivatives market. RiskX builds pre-RFQ pricing, risk-analysis and product-explanation tools for financial institutions on the B2B side, plus consumer-facing experiences for ELS investors and on-chain structured products for XRP holders. The platform supports end-to-end digitization of structured derivatives workflows from product design and pricing review through risk analysis and disclosure preparation.
company.name: RiskX

company.description: Elegoo is a Shenzhen-based consumer 3D printer manufacturer that began in 2015 selling STEM education kits before launching its Mars resin printer in 2018, growing into one of the four largest global suppliers of entry-level consumer 3D printers alongside Bambu Lab, Creality and Anycubic. The company sells direct-to-consumer through online channels, with international markets accounting for over 90% of revenue, and is shifting product strategy toward FDM machines such as the Centauri Carbon series after building its early reputation on resin printing.
company.name: Elegoo

company.description: KAIO is an Abu Dhabi-regulated tokenization infrastructure firm that lets asset managers package institutional fund products from managers like BlackRock, Brevan Howard and Hamilton Lane as on-chain feeder tokens with minimum tickets near $100. The platform handles compliant distribution across regulated regimes including Abu Dhabi, the Cayman Islands and Singapore, and has tokenized roughly $100M in assets while processing over $500M in transactions. KAIO was originally created by WebN Group together with Nomura-backed Laser Digital and is partnering with Mubadala Capital on tokenized private market funds.
company.name: KAIO

company.description: Renewable Metals is a Perth-based clean-tech company commercialising a proprietary alkali-based hydrometallurgical process that recovers more than 95% of lithium, cobalt, nickel, copper and manganese from end-of-life lithium-ion batteries. Its single-line modular plants handle NMC and LFP chemistries together at far lower capital intensity than conventional facilities, targeting Western supply chains for critical minerals. The company is operating a commercial prototype plant in Kewdale, Western Australia, and planning a first commercial-scale facility in the Hunter region of New South Wales.
company.name: Renewable Metals

company.description: Coral is a New York-based healthcare automation platform for US specialty providers — durable medical equipment suppliers, infusion centers, specialty pharmacy, and radiology practices. Rather than replacing legacy infrastructure, it connects to existing EHR systems, fax lines, and payer portals to automate patient intake, prior authorization, fax processing, and payer communications end-to-end. Founded in 2024, the company uses document-processing models tuned for healthcare back-office artifacts — handwritten forms, scanned insurance cards, authorization templates — to compress workflows that previously took up to 30 minutes into under five.
company.name: Coral

company.description: Hata is Malaysia's only dual-licensed digital asset exchange, operating under licenses from both the Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) and the Labuan Financial Services Authority (LFSA). The platform lets retail and institutional investors buy, sell, trade and custody digital assets with fiat, and has expanded into staking (starting with Solana) and dollar-cost-averaging (Auto-Invest). Launched in May 2023 and founded by a team led by former Luno Asia Pacific general manager David Low, Hata surpassed 209,000 registered users and processed RM1.04 billion in transaction volume in 2025.
company.name: Hata

company.description: Axten Hospitals is an Indian surgical-care startup that operates specialised hospital facilities under a standardised clinical-delivery model with fixed-price, transparent surgical packages branded "Honest Surgery Price". Part of TAH Global Healthcare, the company launched its pilot site in East of Kailash, New Delhi and focuses on high-volume procedures across general surgery, orthopaedics, gynaecology, urology, ENT, and oncology. It is building an asset-light network of smart surgical hospitals across India's top metros, combining operational efficiency with a technology layer for patient discovery, booking, and post-operative care.
company.name: Axten Hospitals

company.description: Salmon Group is a Philippines-focused digital consumer finance company founded in July 2022 by former Tinkoff executives Pavel Fedorov, George Chesakov, and Raffy Montemayor. It operates a BSP-licensed digital bank (Salmon Bank), an SEC-licensed financing company (Salmon Finance), and a mobile app offering lending, credit lines, motorbike loans, and deposits to underbanked Filipinos.
