The company uses behavioural and digital data for real-time credit scoring, and by 2026 had raised roughly $310M in combined equity and Nordic-market bond financing.
company.name: Salmon Group

company.description: ViewsML is a Vancouver-based digital-pathology company building the world's first virtual biomarker library — a growing catalogue of AI models that generate per-cell spatial biomarker insights directly from routine H&E pathology images, without physical immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining. The platform compresses a traditionally days-to-weeks wet-lab workflow into minutes of software, preserving scarce tissue samples and letting pharma, diagnostic labs, and hospitals extract more value from existing pathology archives. CEO Kenneth To and co-founder / Chief Scientific Officer Christopher Jackson (co-inventor of the virtual IHC technology and a practicing clinical pathologist) lead the company, which counts Mayo Clinic, Dartmouth Health, Providence Health Care, and iProcess among its clinical and commercial partners.
company.name: ViewsML

company.description: Cala Health is a bioelectronic medicine company transforming the care of patients with chronic illness. Its flagship product, Cala kIQ, is a wearable neuromodulation therapy that delivers personalized stimulation to the nerves in the wrist, providing relief from hand tremors associated with essential tremor and Parkinson’s disease.
company.name: Cala Health

company.description: Signit is a Saudi Arabian technology company specializing in digital signatures and AI-powered contract management. As a licensed Trust Service Provider, it provides a secure platform for organizations to create, sign, and manage legally binding agreements. The company is evolving into a full Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) provider, using AI to automate drafting, negotiation, and compliance workflows.
company.name: Signit

company.description: Ideally is an AI-powered market research platform that enables brands and agencies to gather consumer insights overnight. Its technology automates the creative testing process, providing rapid feedback at the earliest stages of development. Based in New York and Auckland, Ideally serves over 250 brands globally, including Google, KFC, and Burger King.
company.name: Ideally

company.description: Rivan Industries is a UK-based synthetic fuel startup that vertically integrates every step of production — from renewable energy generation and green hydrogen, to direct-air-capture CO2 and Sabatier-reactor methane synthesis — to produce Synthetic Natural Gas (SNG) for injection into the gas grid. Its primary customers are heavy industries that are hard to electrify at scale, including steel, cement, chemicals and aviation. Founded in 2024 by Harvey Hodd, the company designs and manufactures its hardware in the UK from scratch and aims to reach fossil-fuel-competitive pricing within the next 2-3 years.
company.name: Rivan

company.description: Clarity Labs is a Gurugram-based outcome-driven functional personal-care D2C brand for Indian consumers. The company designs clinically-formulated daily-use skincare products — starting with its flagship "BAR" line of functional soaps launched March 2026 (Anti-Acne, De-Tan, Sensitive Skin, and Muscle Relief variants) — positioning itself as a simpler, single-step alternative to multi-product Indian skincare routines. Founded in November 2025 by Karan Dokras; products are engineered with clinically proven actives and no sulphates, and sold direct-to-consumer through discoverclaritylabs.com.
company.name: Clarity Labs

company.description: Recursive Superintelligence is a London-based AI lab founded in December 2025 by a group of recognisable AI researchers — Richard Socher (former Salesforce Chief Scientist, founder of You.com), Tim Rocktäschel, Josh Tobin, Jeff Clune and Tim Shi — to attack what the team calls the hardest open problem in AI: building systems that can improve themselves. The thesis is to automate the full AI development pipeline end-to-end — evaluation, data selection, training, post-training and even research-direction selection — so that model-building bottlenecks compound rather than plateau. At the time of this round the company had about 20 employees, no shipped product, and was targeting a public launch around mid-May 2026.
company.name: Recursive Superintelligence

company.description: Mitsumore (Meetsmore) is a Tokyo-based marketplace-and-SaaS company operating three interconnected products for Japans service economy: Mitsumore, an online quote/booking marketplace with a nationwide provider network exceeding 100,000 businesses; ProOne, a field-industry vertical SaaS covering sales, operations and accounting for on-site service operators; and Hatchu, a B2B procurement platform for corporate ordering. Founded in February 2017 by CEO Ayako Ishikawa, the company is building what it describes as an AI-native "OS for the on-site industries", layering automation and agentic workflows on top of demand generation, quoting, delivery and procurement. Lifetime funding has now reached approximately ¥8.9 billion with this Series B Strategic extension, and the company is explicitly positioning itself to produce "blue-collar billionaires" by lifting Japans labour-heavy service-economy productivity.
company.name: Mitsumore

company.description: ECOMMIT is a Kagoshima-based circular-economy infrastructure company building the collection, sorting and re-distribution layer for Japans reuse economy. The company operates PASSTO, a consumer resource-circulation service with more than 6,000 drop-off points across post offices, retail centres and residential buildings, and runs seven nationwide circular-sorting and logistics centres plus the Tokyo Circular Center which it is upgrading into a DX- and automation-led clothing and general-goods circulation hub. Founded in October 2008 by CEO Teruyuki Kawano, ECOMMIT processes roughly 14,000 tonnes of goods a year — around 11,000 tonnes of it clothing — and is targeting 45,000 tonnes in three years under partnerships with Mercari and other retail and logistics heavyweights.
company.name: ECOMMIT

company.description: ScreenPoint Medical is a Nijmegen, Netherlands-based breast-AI company whose Transpara Breast AI Suite automates analysis of 2D and 3D mammograms to support radiologists in earlier detection of breast cancer. Transpara has been deployed across more than 30 countries and processed over 12 million mammograms to date, with a landmark Lancet-published randomised controlled trial (MASAI) reporting improved cancer detection and reduced radiologist workload versus standard double reading. Founded in 2014 as a spin-off from Radboud University Medical Centre by professors Nico Karssemeijer and Sir Michael Brady, the company is led by CEO Pieter Kroese and is backed by Insight Partners and Siemens Healthineers.
company.name: ScreenPoint Medical

company.description: Logicc is a Hamburg-based secure-AI platform that gives professionals in compliance-heavy sectors — lawyers, doctors, tax advisors and public institutions — a single interface to frontier models such as ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini without breaching data-protection obligations. The platform is engineered for GDPR, Section 203 of the German Criminal Code, German-only hosting, zero data retention and zero-knowledge architecture, layered with industry-specific assistants and team collaboration features. Founded in 2025 by Emil Woermann, Jacob Leffers, Benjamin Seifert and Santiago Martinez-Avial, the company serves more than 1,800 organisations and crossed €1 million ARR within roughly six months of launch.
company.name: Logicc

company.description: brainjo is a Regensburg-based digital health company building virtual-reality therapy applications for mental-health conditions, beginning with a prescribable ADHD therapy for children. Its platform translates evidence-based psychotherapy content, including homework and coping-skill tasks, into immersive VR environments engineered to complement — not replace — traditional guideline-based multimodal therapy, and is designed to be approved as a DiGA reimbursable by German statutory health insurance. Founded in 2022 by Markus Wensauer and Christian Gnerlich, brainjo operates with an interdisciplinary team spanning technology, psychology, and healthcare, and has developed its ADHD product in partnership with MEDICE, a European pharmaceutical company specialising in ADHD medication.
company.name: brainjo

company.description: Planetary is a Swiss full-stack fermentation company that builds, owns, and operates biomanufacturing facilities to produce bio-based food ingredients at industrial scale. Its BioBlocks platform spans bioprocess design, scale-up, and manufacturing, with mycoprotein commercialization via a nationwide ALDI Suisse launch at price parity and ongoing roll-outs across Europe under its B2B Libre brand. The company licenses its sugar-to-protein upcycling technology to agro-industrial partners in sucrose-rich regions and operates industrial-scale production in Aarberg, Switzerland.
company.name: Planetary Group

company.description: TextQL builds an AI data analytics platform that replaces SQL writing with natural-language queries against enterprise databases. Its core agent, Ana, lets non-technical users investigate massive datasets directly, automating the traditional analyst workflow end-to-end. The NYC-based company counts Blackstone Innovations Investments among its strategic backers.
company.name: TextQL

company.description: Pulnovo Medical develops interventional therapies for pulmonary hypertension and heart failure, anchored by its proprietary Pulmonary Artery Denervation (PADN) System. The minimally-invasive therapy targets overactive sympathetic nerves implicated in cardiopulmonary disease and has supported approximately 1,500 procedures across seven regulator-approved countries. The NYC-headquartered company holds FDA Breakthrough Device designation and runs active IDE trials in the United States.
company.name: Pulnovo Medical

company.description: Sage Haven is a messaging and voice-calling app for children, with AI that screens outgoing messages for harmful content and nudges kids toward more respectful communication. Parents approve each contact, monitor conversations, and receive AI-generated summaries and alerts. The San Francisco company was co-founded by Kate Doerksen and Anne Pizzuti.
company.name: Sage Haven

company.description: NOC Energy builds hybrid industrial heating systems that let cement and glass factories switch between electricity and fossil fuels on the fly. Its induction coil technology runs at lower temperatures than conventional alternatives, letting plants exploit cheap grid electricity while keeping fossil infrastructure as backup. The Houston-based climatetech startup is incubated at Greentown Labs and is running pilots with multinational cement and glass manufacturers.
company.name: NOC Energy

company.description: Rhea Space Activity is a Washington, D.C.-based space-technology startup developing AutoNav, an optical-celestial navigation system that lets spacecraft fix their position by photographing moving in-orbit and celestial objects — satellites, moons, planets, asteroids, comets — and matching the imagery against cataloged data, rather than depending on GPS. The underlying autonomous-navigation approach traces to NASAs Jet Propulsion Laboratory, and Rhea has been adapting it for defense reentry vehicles and cislunar missions where GPS and radio signals are jammed, unavailable, or blocked by plasma blackout. The company flies hosted payloads on Varda Space Industries reentry capsules under Space Force and AFRL sponsorship, and has separately announced plans to fly a navigation payload on a lunar lander.
company.name: Rhea Space Activity

company.description: Schematik is an AI-native hardware development platform that turns plain-English prompts into working code, wiring diagrams, bills of materials, and step-by-step assembly instructions for off-the-shelf boards like Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi Pico. Branded as the "Cursor for Hardware", it lets makers, hobbyists, and engineers ship physical projects without electrical-engineering background or CAD tooling. The company was founded by Samuel Beek, who previously helped scale video editor VEED to $50M+ ARR.
company.name: Schematik

company.description: Loca.us is an Austin-based community-driven platform that pays customers real cash to discover, visit, and share local-business experiences — an explicit rejection of the per-business loyalty-app model that fatigues both consumers and small operators. Customers use one shared app to earn cash for visits, receipt uploads, referrals, and short user-generated videos or photos, which local owners then get back as authentic word-of-mouth content, stronger local SEO, and measurable foot traffic — with no custom app development and no ad spend. Founded in 2023 by serial entrepreneur Joe Edgar (previously CEO of TenantCloud and Rentler), Loca.us has passed 25,000 consumer downloads and 1,200 local businesses onboarded across Austin, Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Eugene, and Portland.
company.name: Loca.us

company.description: NYC-based AI-native billing and revenue automation platform for B2B companies. Provides a quote-to-cash platform that automates complex subscription and usage-based billing, manages receivables, and delivers analytics. Built with agentic AI capabilities to handle the increasingly complex pricing models of modern SaaS and AI-era businesses.
company.name: Zenskar

company.description: WOTA is a Tokyo-based water-technology company building small-scale decentralized water recycling systems and autonomous water-treatment control technology to reuse domestic wastewater. Its product line includes WOTA BOX and WOSH, and the company operates a Water 2040 Fund for Japanese municipalities facing aging pipeline infrastructure, plus the JWAD disaster mutual-aid platform.
company.name: WOTA

company.description: Ricursive Intelligence is a frontier AI lab building the compute foundation for the next generation of AI. Founded by Dr. Anna Goldie and Dr. Azalia Mirhoseini, the scientists who pioneered AI for chip design and co-created AlphaChip, Ricursive Intelligence applies AI and distributed computing to drastically compress semiconductor development timelines. The company's platform creates a recursive self-improvement cycle where AI designs silicon that powers the next generation of AI. By removing th
company.name: Ricursive Intelligence

company.description: Cursor is an AI-powered coding assistant startup focused on developing advanced AI models for software development, including its Composer model.
company.name: Cursor

company.description: Sharing Energy Co., Ltd. is a Japanese company focused on developing distributed low-voltage solar power plants, primarily on residential rooftops, and providing energy supply and management services.
company.name: Sharing Energy Co., Ltd.

company.description: Maxi Mobilite is a Turkish technology company building AI-powered software for fleet management and mobility operations. Its platform covers operational tracking, performance analytics, partner service-network management, custom software, and digitised insurance and service workflows. Clients include Ziraat Filo Mobilite, HDY Filo, Teknofix, Dokay Filo, Qcar Mobilite, and EasyGo.
company.name: Maxi Mobilite

company.description: BOND is a Brazilian AI-powered fintech that operates as a full-service accounting firm for small and medium-sized enterprises, automating tax, payroll, and bookkeeping workflows while keeping human accountants in the loop. Founded in late 2025 by Matheus Oliveira (ex-Loft, FW Capital) and Gabriel Jordao (former CPO at JusBrasil), the company targets the underserved mid-market segment of SMEs with annual revenues between $720K and $10M, where Brazil's fragmented accounting industry historically relies on outdated software and manual processes.
company.name: BOND

company.description: Resolv (legal name Resolv Systems Inc.) is a California-based AI-native software and services company that automates the back-office for home-services contractors — finding, filing and tracking utility and government rebates, filing building and mechanical permits, and registering manufacturer warranties after each install. The platform integrates directly with ServiceTitan and Housecall Pro and offers upfront rebate financing so contractors are paid immediately instead of waiting months for program reimbursement. In its first full year of operations (2025) the company secured roughly $10M in rebates for nearly 100 contractors in California.
company.name: Resolv

company.description: Fifth Sense is a Gurugram-based D2C premium fragrance brand reimagining perfumes for Indian consumers — climate-adapted formulations at 25%+ oil concentration (versus the 12-15% typical of cold-climate perfumery) paired with emotion-led storytelling. The brand launched with four debut fragrances and sells directly through itsfifthsense.com. Founded in 2025 by IIM Kozhikode alumni Bharat Gupta and Prateek Gupta, the company is positioned against mass-premium imported perfume brands in India's fast-growing fragrance market.
company.name: Fifth Sense

company.description: Stockholm-based biotech scale-up developing laminin-based technologies that enable next-generation cell therapies for serious chronic diseases. The company's recombinant laminin products support stem cell technologies targeting conditions including type 1 diabetes, Parkinson's disease, and heart failure. Provides the critical cell culture matrices that pharmaceutical and biotech companies need to manufacture advanced therapy medicinal products (ATMPs).
company.name: BioLamina

company.description: Cupertino-based startup building what it calls a "Super Agent" — a persistent, action-taking AI that goes beyond conversation to execute digital work end-to-end. The platform enables one person to do the work of an entire team by automating complex multi-step workflows across digital tools. Positions itself not as another AI model but as an execution layer that takes action on behalf of users.
company.name: Creao AI

company.description: Cape Town-based medical technology company developing AI-powered screening tools for tuberculosis. Its flagship product, the Ostium digital stethoscope paired with the AI.TB model, enables frontline nurses and community health workers to detect TB through lung sound analysis at the point of care without specialist equipment or X-ray infrastructure. Holds SAHPRA approval and has screened over 1,000 patients across clinical research in more than 10 countries in Africa and Asia.
company.name: AI Diagnostics

company.description: Santa Clara-based developer of integrated optical interconnect solutions for AI data center connectivity. Builds optical circuit switching technology that enables faster, more efficient data transfer between GPUs and compute nodes in AI infrastructure. Transitioning from lab-grade photonics to repeatable manufacturing and customer deployments for next-generation AI data centers.
company.name: nEye.ai

company.description: Redwood City-based student transportation platform that has reimagined K-12 school transit — the largest mass transit system in the US. Manages every vehicle, driver, passenger, parent, and ride on a single Connected Mobility Experience (CMX) platform. Founded by Ritu Narayan, Zum began as a private car service for parents before pivoting to a $1.3B+ enterprise serving school districts nationwide. Named to Fast Company's Most Innovative Companies list.
company.name: Zum

company.description: Boston-based clinical-stage biotech developing precision therapies for autoimmune and inflammatory diseases. Emerged from stealth with five programs licensed from Bristol Myers Squibb, including Phase 2 lupus candidate afimetoran and novel IL-2 assets. Led by CEO Saqib Islam, who previously guided Pfizer's immunology portfolio. Focuses on precision immunology — targeting the right patients with the right therapy at the right time.
company.name: Beeline Medicines

company.description: Saudi Arabia-based fashion e-commerce platform specializing in modest fashion, starting with abayas — a $3.5B category in Saudi alone. Uses a data-driven, on-demand production model that designs and delivers fashion based on real-time demand signals rather than seasonal forecasting. Founded in 2024, Aya launched in May 2024 and is expanding across categories to serve the GCC market from a single platform.
company.name: Aya

company.description: Philadelphia-based healthcare technology company providing AI-driven automation for prior authorization. The platform stitches together the healthcare ecosystem by empowering providers and health systems to navigate the prior authorization process more efficiently, reducing administrative burden and accelerating patient access to care.
company.name: Ethermed

company.description: Spanish SaaS platform providing AI agents for customer service and process automation. Automates up to 90% of customer support tickets using advanced AI while delivering responses with human-level quality. Designed for businesses looking to scale support operations without proportional headcount growth.
company.name: GuruSup

company.description: San Francisco-based supply chain AI startup building purpose-built verticalized AI for logistics. The platform predicts supply chain disruptions, enabling freight shippers and carriers to optimize operations across logistics, finance, and supply chain workflows. Uses domain-specific data models and infrastructure designed for the logistics industry rather than general-purpose AI.
company.name: Loop Payments

company.description: UrFuture (urfuture.io) is a Manchester-based UK startup that runs a Gen-Z–targeted hiring marketplace purpose-built for entry-level, apprenticeship, graduate and no-experience-required roles. Its app replaces the traditional CV with a behavioural-science + ML career-match score across 18 careers, then serves jobs to candidates in a personalised content-feed format and lets employers search a Gen-Z talent database via its RubyAI assistant. Launched in September 2025 by CEO Keighley, the platform claims 4.3x better job-performance prediction than CVs and counts Amazon, British Gas, British Airways, the Metropolitan Police and the NHS among its active employer roster, with 35,000+ downloads by mid-January 2026.
company.name: Urfuture

company.description: Ultralight (formerly Vibrant Practice; Ultralight, Inc.) is a San Francisco-based healthtech company building an AI-native operating system for modern, subscription-based medical practices focused on preventive, personalised and longevity medicine. The platform unifies EHR, practice management, clinical workflow and patient engagement into a single vertical SaaS stack with AI natively integrated — automating documentation, surfacing clinical insights, and freeing clinicians from the administrative burden imposed by legacy fee-for-service systems. Co-founded by CEO Sunita Mohanty and Pedro Tabio, Ultralight is live in 75+ clinics with 1,200+ clinicians, growing through clinician-community word-of-mouth, and ~60% of new customers fully replace their incumbent EHR on adoption.
company.name: Ultralight

company.description: Atomic Tessellator is an Auckland-based deep-tech materials science company that operates a "digital laboratory" combining quantum physics and AI to design new materials. The company is developing alternatives to rare-earth materials for defense, semiconductor and aerospace supply chains, with flagship material Vireon — built from Australia-mined elements — claimed to outperform traditional rare-earth magnets. Founded by former Google engineer Alain Richardt, the company says its platform compresses materials discovery timelines from years to weeks.
company.name: Atomic Tessellator

company.description: Develops hybrid-electric eVTOL air taxis for urban air mobility in India, focusing on high payload capacity, affordability, and short-range flights to address traffic congestion.
company.name: Sarla Aviation

company.description: Startup developing an autonomous site reliability engineer (SRE) tool that automatically maintains software systems.
company.name: Resolve AI

company.description: Digital-first Mexican fintech offering consumer credit and a cash‑rewards credit card (Plata Card); builds proprietary AI-driven core banking infrastructure to serve underbanked customers and is preparing to launch full banking services.
company.name: Plata

company.description: Solidroad is an AI-driven quality assurance and training platform designed to enhance customer experience teams by analyzing 100% of customer interactions to provide personalized training simulations for human agents and improve AI agent performance at scale without increasing team overhead.
company.name: Solidroad, Inc.

company.description: Slash is a business banking platform that offers checking accounts, automated accounting tools, virtual and physical payment cards, and industry-specific financial solutions for online entrepreneurs and businesses across various verticals.
company.name: Slash

company.description:
Cerebras Systems is the world's fastest AI inference. We are powering the future of generative AI. 

We’re a team of pioneering computer architects, deep learning researchers, and engineers building a new class of AI supercomputers from the ground up.

Our flagship system, Cerebras CS-3, is powered by the Wafer Scale Engine 3—the world’s largest and fastest AI processor. CS-3s are effortlessly clustered to create the largest AI supercomputers on Earth, while abstracting away the complexity of traditional distributed computing.

From sub-second inference speeds to breakthrough training performance, Cerebras makes it easier to build and deploy state-of-the-art AI—from proprietary enterprise models to open-source projects downloaded millions of times.

Here’s what makes our platform different:
🔦 Sub-second reasoning – Instant intelligence and real-time responsiveness, even at massive scale
⚡ Blazing-fast inference – Up to 100x performance gains over traditional AI infrastructure
🧠 Agentic AI in action – Models that can plan, act, and adapt autonomously
🌍 Scalable infrastructure – Built to move from prototype to global deployment without friction

Cerebras solutions are available in the Cerebras Cloud or on-prem, serving leading enterprises, research labs, and government agencies worldwide.

👉 Learn more: www.cerebras.ai 
Join us: https://cerebras.net/careers/
company.name: Cerebras Systems

company.description: Factory is an AI company, backed by Sequoia Capital, bringing autonomy to software engineering.
company.name: Factory

company.description: Booster Robotics is a Beijing-based humanoid robot company founded in 2023 by Tsinghua University graduate Cheng Hao, with a core team drawn from the Tsinghua Robot Control Lab and the Tsinghua Hephaestus RoboCup team. The company designs small-form-factor bipedal humanoids — its flagship Booster K1 and Booster T1 platforms — for embodied AI research, education, and RoboCup-style competition, and has won multiple championships at RoboCup and the World Humanoid Robot Sports Games. K1 has shipped over 1,000 units to 400 customers across more than 20 countries, giving Booster a global sales lead in the small humanoid robot category.
company.name: Booster Robotics

company.description: BlockInvest is a Milan-based fintech building institutional infrastructure for the tokenization of real-world assets in Europe. Its platform enables banks and corporates to natively issue and manage digital financial instruments — bonds, minibonds, structured notes and equity — on public blockchains in compliance with the DLT Pilot Regime and MiCAR. The company has powered several firsts in Italy under the country's FinTech Law, working with UniCredit, CDP and Crédit Agricole Italia.
company.name: BlockInvest

company.description: Nas.com is an AI platform built for solopreneurs that turns an idea into a running online business without requiring technical, marketing, or design skills. The product handles AI-driven product creation, storefront building, marketing automation, community tools, and global payments at $29 per month, and reports 3.5 million members across 150+ countries. The company was founded by Nuseir Yassin, the creator behind the Nas Daily content brand.
company.name: Nas.com

company.description: Velpha Inc. is a Tokyo-based fintech startup building an AI investment agent that supports individual investors across the full investment cycle — stock discovery, trade execution, market monitoring, and portfolio rebalancing. Its flagship "Stock Universe" tool visualizes performance and fundamentals (ROE, PBR, and other metrics) across roughly 10,000 Japanese and U.S. listed equities and delivers portfolio-aware recommendations through a chat interface. Founded in June 2023 and led by CEO Shusuke Takamizawa, the company targets Japan's retail investor base activated by the expanded NISA program.
company.name: Velpha

company.description: PamDx, the tradename of PamGene International B.V., is a Dutch biotechnology company founded in 2000 and headquartered in 's-Hertogenbosch, the Netherlands. The company specialises in kinase activity profiling, operating a proprietary microarray platform (PamStation / PamChip) used for biomarker discovery and therapy selection in oncology. Its clinical pipeline is led by IOpener, a blood-based diagnostic that measures protein kinase activity to predict patient response to immune checkpoint inhibitors in melanoma and non-small cell lung cancer.
company.name: Pamdx

company.description: ONWARD Medical is a commercial-stage neurotechnology company developing ARC Therapy — targeted spinal-cord stimulation designed to restore movement, function, and independence after spinal-cord injury. Its FDA-cleared ARC-EX® system delivers non-invasive stimulation and is commercially available in the US and Europe; the investigational ARC-IM® implantable platform is in pivotal trials (Empower BP) for blood-pressure instability and can be combined with a brain-computer interface for thought-driven movement. The company holds 10 FDA Breakthrough Device Designations and is headquartered in Eindhoven with a Swiss Science & Engineering Centre (EPFL origins) and a US office in Boston. ONWARD is publicly listed on Euronext Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam (ONWD) with US ADRs on OTCQX (ONWRY).
company.name: ONWARD Medical

company.description: Domestic trading arm of TechCoop, a Vietnamese integrated agritech platform. Farmnet operates agricultural supply chain trade flows, helping partners move toward higher-value finished products that meet global standards. The parent company TechCoop serves as an end-to-end agricultural supply chain operator in Vietnam, connecting smallholder farmers to international markets.
company.name: Farmnet (TechCoop)

company.description: NYC-based startup building simulation tools for a new generation of robot builders — positioned as the "Cursor for physical AI." The platform accelerates robotics testing and validation by bringing software-speed iteration to hardware development, enabling developers to simulate autonomous robot behavior before deploying to the real world.
company.name: Antioch

company.description: Toulouse-area startup developing fully automated drone-based inspection solutions for aircraft. Combines autonomous UAVs (no pilot required) with computer vision algorithms to detect potential damages and defects in real time, inspecting aircraft 20x faster than traditional manual methods. Serves airlines and MRO providers looking to reduce aircraft turnaround times and improve maintenance quality.
company.name: Donecle

company.description: Maharashtra-based rooftop solar EPC services provider focused on commercial and industrial (C&I) customers. Develops, builds, and operates distributed solar installations across India. Expanded its portfolio to 125 MWp in FY26, achieving over 50% revenue growth and 75% profit increase. Serves businesses transitioning to clean energy through rooftop and group captive solar projects.
company.name: Roofsol Energy

company.description: Jaipur-based integrated smart metering solutions provider delivering end-to-end Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) capabilities across meter manufacturing, deployment, and data management. Executing over 10 million smart electricity meters and 1.6 million smart gas meters across India with manufacturing capacity of 5 million units per year. Enables rural and urban consumers to access reliable energy and optimize electricity expenses via mobile.
company.name: Polaris Smart Metering

company.description: Ahmedabad-based ice cream brand that has grown rapidly to cross ₹530 crore in revenue for FY26. One of India's fastest-growing consumer food brands, Hocco manufactures and distributes ice cream products across a widening distribution network. Valued at ₹2,500 crore pre-money, the company aims to double revenues by FY27 through capacity expansion and broader retail reach.
company.name: Hocco

company.description: Eigen is a San Francisco-based stealth-stage AI social-product company founded in 2025 by German entrepreneur Paul Scherer (22) after he moved to the United States on an O-1 visa in May 2025. The company's thesis is deliberately contrarian to the "AI best friend" race: rather than building hyper-personalised AI companions (Replika/Character.ai style), Eigen aims to create shared, synchronous experiences that bind people together — pitched by the team as "the world's first mutual friend." Product specifics remain under wraps; the founding team blends deeply experienced tech veterans with unusually young creative builders (engineers who think like PMs, plus a screenwriter hire), and the seed capital is earmarked primarily for hiring "the best team on earth."
company.name: Eigen

company.description: Aliste Technologies is a Noida-based energy-management and IoT company founded in 2021 by Anant Ohri, Aakarsh Nayyar, Bhavya Kansal, Konark Gautam and Shreyansh Jain that builds proprietary IoT hardware and software to help enterprises monitor, control and optimise electricity use across commercial properties. After a pivot from a Shark Tank India-featured D2C smart-home brand into an enterprise-first platform, Aliste now manages 1.5 lakh+ IoT assets across 20+ Indian cities and tracks ~3 million units of monthly energy consumption for customers in hospitality, co-living, retail and QSR. The company claims up to 20% reductions in customer energy bills and ~328,000 kg of CO₂ savings per month — roughly equivalent to planting 13,000 mature trees monthly.
company.name: Aliste Technologies

company.description: Orthogon Therapeutics is a Canton, Massachusetts preclinical-stage antiviral biotech developing first-in-class small-molecule oral therapies for BK virus and related human polyomaviruses — major causes of graft loss in kidney, solid-organ and stem-cell transplant patients for which no approved treatments exist. The company targets two hard viral proteins (VP1 capsid and large T antigen / LTAg) via structure-based drug design, enabling prolonged intracellular control of viral replication across multiple BK strains. Founded by CEO Ali H. Munawar, PhD as an LLC affiliated with the Pledge Therapeutics discovery platform, with operations in Greater Boston and a research branch in Leuven, Belgium.
company.name: Orthogon Therapeutics

company.description: Balerion AI is a San Francisco-based fintech startup that builds an agentic AI platform for mortgage loan origination. Its flagship product, Balerion Loan Intelligence, is an end-to-end reasoning engine that embeds directly into existing lender origination workflows, automating the full loan manufacturing lifecycle — from income calculation and derogatory-condition detection in bank and credit data through underwriting, Fannie Mae / Freddie Mac / FHA / non-QM compliance checks, and closing. Founded in 2026 by CEO Naren Krishna, Balerion is already deployed with residential lenders including FM Home Loans ($2B+ loan volume) and targets the ~$12K average cost-to-originate that has dogged the US mortgage industry.
company.name: Balerion AI

company.description: Wealth.com is a Phoenix/Tempe-based AI-powered platform that modernises how wealth-management firms deliver estate and tax planning to advisors and their clients. Its proprietary AI engine — Ester Intelligence (Ester™) — automates the production of estate plans, intake workflows and tax-planning workpapers, and is embedded as an "infrastructure layer" inside enterprise platforms including Dynasty Financial Partners' Dynasty Desktop. The product targets the 50,000+ US financial advisors who today still assemble estate and tax deliverables with fragmented point tools, and the company plans to open a New York City office in May 2026 to accelerate enterprise distribution.
company.name: Wealth.com

company.description: K1x (K1X, Inc.) is a Morristown, NJ-based AI-native tax-data operations platform that digitizes the full K-1 ecosystem for the private-markets industry. Its patented platform automates extraction, aggregation and standardization of tax data from K-1s, 1099s, W-2s and IRS-certified 990 filings, turning fragmented workflows into structured, decision-ready data and eliminating the manual/spreadsheet grind that has long dominated private-fund tax reporting. Led by CEO John LaMancuso, K1x serves 16 of the top 25 US accounting firms and 44 of the top 100 institutional investors, targeting a market the company frames as over 90% unserved and increasingly critical as the $20T+ alternatives landscape expands and pass-through structures multiply.
company.name: K1x

company.description: Traza is a New York-based AI-native procurement platform founded in 2025 by three Spanish entrepreneurs — Silvestre Jara Montes, Santiago Martínez Bragado and Sergio Ayala Miñano — who relocated to the US via the Exponential Fellowship. The company deploys autonomous AI workers that embed into existing procurement workflows at large manufacturers and construction firms, executing multi-step operational tasks (vendor management, RFQ processing, order tracking, supplier communications, invoice reconciliation) that traditionally consume up to 70% of procurement teams' time. Unlike recommendation-only "AI for procurement" incumbents, Traza's agents take action while keeping humans in the loop for contractually-sensitive approvals, targeting the $8.89B procurement software market growing ~11% CAGR.
company.name: Traza

company.description: Expo (legal entity 650 Industries Inc.) is a Palo Alto-based developer-platform company that builds the open-source framework and toolchain used by millions of React Native developers to ship universal iOS, Android and web applications from a single codebase. Co-founded by Charlie Cheever, Expo has evolved from a popular open-source framework into an infrastructure layer for cross-platform app development, used by startups and enterprises to ship mobile products without maintaining separate native codebases. The Series B also funded the launch of Expo Agent — an AI coding agent targeted at compressing the idea-to-production loop for the 3M+ React Native developers in the Expo ecosystem.
company.name: Expo

company.description: Audrey AI is a Dublin-based startup building an agentic AI platform purpose-built for financial auditors. The system automates the manual backbone of audit engagements — intelligent client-data requests, evidence gathering, transaction testing, and review — and learns each firm's methodology as it is used. Founded in 2025 by CEO Ryan Loughran and CTO David Burke (who met on the Dogpatch Labs Founders programme), Audrey has already piloted at top-10 and top-20 audit firms with 85%+ time savings on key workflows.
company.name: Audrey AI

company.description: Joyful Health is a New York-based AI financial infrastructure company for healthcare revenue operations. Its platform connects fragmented revenue-cycle systems — electronic health records, billing platforms, clearinghouses, payer portals, banks — into a unified financial system of record, then uses AI (via its Adam denial-management tool) to surface, prioritize and recover unpaid claims. Founded in 2023 by CEO Eliana Berger, the company has processed over $1.4 billion in transactions with a 95%+ recovery rate, targeting the estimated $125 billion annual revenue US providers lose to fragmented claims data.
company.name: Joyful Health

company.description: Worki is a San Francisco-based healthcare workforce infrastructure company. Its AI-native platform sits between existing health-system stacks — Workday, UKG, Oracle, ServiceNow, ATS, LMS, and AI point solutions — unifying workforce and HR data into a context layer that powers AI-driven decisions and functional-role agents. Led by CEO Craig Ahrens, the company claims its infrastructure can strip hundreds of millions in overhead from partner health systems.
company.name: Worki

company.description: GetWhys is a Boise, Idaho-based AI-powered customer intelligence and go-to-market platform. Its InsightDB aggregates a continuously-compounding proprietary dataset of verified B2B buyer interviews, which AI workflows translate into GTM-ready outputs — messaging, competitive content, and sales enablement materials for revenue teams at companies like Intel and Verizon. Founded in 2023 by Philippe and Tyler, both 10-year veterans of B2B market research, with satellite offices in Portland, New York City and Bozeman.
company.name: GetWhys

company.description: ALL THINGS is a UK-based premium dairy brand founded in 2023 by chef Thomas Straker and Toby Hopkinson. Launched as All Things Butter from Straker's viral cooking content, the company has expanded into cottage cheese and a broader dairy range, distributing through 2,500+ UK stores including Sainsbury's, Waitrose, Morrisons, Ocado and Co-op. The brand donates 1% of revenue to the British agricultural industry and is building a vertically-integrated supply chain as it expands into the US and Middle East.
company.name: ALL THINGS

company.description: Lua (legally Lua Global Inc.) is a London-based operating system for human-agent collaboration in the workspace. The Y Combinator-backed company lets non-technical teams build, deploy, govern and measure an AI agent workforce without running their own agent infrastructure. Its product is positioned above developer frameworks as a managed layer, with a growing Lua Implementation Network of partners deploying agent workforces into vertical markets.
company.name: Lua

company.description: InsightFinder AI is a Durham, North Carolina-based AI observability and reliability platform. Using unsupervised machine learning over performance metrics and log data, the company detects, diagnoses, remediates and prevents IT incidents — and, more recently, failures in agentic AI systems running in production — via its Autonomous Reliability Insights (ARI) agent. Founded by CEO Helen Gu, a computer science professor at NC State University formerly at IBM and Google, the company counts multiple Fortune 500 customers including a recent Fortune 50 deal.
company.name: InsightFinder

company.description: Flare is a Copenhagen-based knowledge-validation platform building trust infrastructure for the AI era. It combines transparent structured systems with collective intelligence to evaluate claims and surface what is actually supported — a response to AI's ability to generate convincing statements faster than humans can verify them. Founded in 2026 by CEO Nicolai Frost Kolborg Jacobsen and team alumni from Google, Unity, IBM, Amazon, and Corti.
company.name: Flare

company.description: Caruso is an AI-native fund administration platform for private-market managers in real estate, private credit, and private equity. Spun out as an internal tool inside a fund manager, it now serves 900+ funds globally and processes $55B+ in assets under administration. The company was founded in 2022 in Auckland by co-CEOs Mark Hurley and Oliver Shaw and has expanded into Australia and the US.
company.name: Caruso

company.description: STORM Therapeutics is a Cambridge, UK biotech pioneering RNA-epigenetic cancer therapies. Its lead asset STC-15, co-discovered with Evotec, is a first-in-class oral small-molecule inhibitor of the RNA methyltransferase METTL3 that reprograms tumor biology and activates anti-cancer immune responses. The company is advancing STC-15 through a Phase 2 sarcoma study and a Phase 1b/2 combination trial with toripalimab in non-small cell lung cancer.
company.name: STORM Therapeutics

company.description: Proteins.1 is a Finnish deep-tech diagnostics company spun out of VTT. Its patented physics-based single-molecule protein amplification platform detects proteins up to 1,000x more sensitively than current gold-standard immunoassays by replacing enzymatic amplification with a magnetic cycling mechanism. The company targets ultra-early disease detection in oncology, neurology, cardiovascular, and immunology from minimal blood samples.
company.name: Proteins.1

company.description: spektr is a Copenhagen-based compliance infrastructure platform for banks and fintechs. It deploys networks of specialized AI agents to automate the manual KYC and KYB work behind onboarding and ongoing monitoring — researching companies, mapping ownership, interpreting documents, and generating risk assessments. Production customers include Pleo, Santander Leasing, Mercuryo, Phantom, and Monta.
company.name: spektr

company.description: Qalzy is a London-based consumer health startup that builds an AI-powered kitchen scale with a built-in camera. The device identifies, weighs, and logs food with a single tap and an OpenAI-powered vision model trained on millions of ingredient images — pairing computer-vision speed with kitchen-scale accuracy for calorie and macro tracking. Founded in 2023 by former Microsoft engineers Kostas Koukoravas (CEO) and Michael Michelis (COO).
company.name: Qalzy

company.description: Cohoma Coffee Company is an Indian specialty coffee startup that runs an integrated direct-to-consumer model spanning sourcing, roasting, and coffee-machine manufacturing. Founded in 2019 by Paritosh Birla and Kanika Birla, it markets a fast-brew specialty coffee experience backed by its own equipment so cafes, offices, and households can serve consistent barista-grade cups. The brand is expanding beyond India through its proprietary machine platform.
company.name: Cohoma Coffee

company.description: Intellithink is a Bengaluru-based industrial AI startup that provides a full-stack machine health platform for rotating equipment. Its IoT-enabled sensors combined with AI-driven vibration and current signature analysis monitor factory machines 24/7, detecting anomalies and recommending fixes. The company serves 50+ enterprise customers including Jindal Steel, JSW Steel, ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel, Adani, Ultratech, and L&T.
company.name: Intellithink

company.description: The Hosteller is India's largest self-operated branded backpacker hostel chain. Founded in 2014 and led by CEO Pranav Dangi, it operates over 75 properties across 13 states — from Goa's beaches to Leh — and has hosted more than 2 million travellers. The company is scaling toward 25,000 beds and building a full-stack travel platform spanning accommodation, F&B, transport, and travel services.
company.name: The Hosteller

company.description: Vilnius-based agentic AI startup founded in 2025 by CEO Will Nauseda, Edvinas Rakickas, and Rytis Dereskevicius. Outcraft AI builds autonomous revenue agents that execute real-time customer engagement across voice, SMS, email, and WhatsApp — triggered by signals like new leads, abandoned checkouts, or failed payments. The platform integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Shopify, and Stripe; early customers include Pulsetto, Omnisend, Kiloverse, and Warmy.
company.name: Outcraft AI

company.description: CoreWeave is a GPU-native cloud provider purpose-built for AI training and inference at scale. Founded in 2017, the company operates infrastructure optimized around modern AI stacks with tightly integrated networking, storage, and orchestration systems. CoreWeave serves AI labs, startups, and large enterprises needing burstable high-throughput compute without the capex of building GPU farms. The company completed its public listing on Nasdaq in March 2025 under ticker CRWV.
company.name: CoreWeave

company.description:
Founded in 2018 as a spinout from the Cambridge Graphene Centre (CGC), the company has developed the first commercially scalable Graphene transceivers for use in advanced photonic communications. Unlike Silicon and InP photonics, graphene delivers exceptional performance in terms of cost of data, bandwidth, latency and environmental stability. All critical drivers for the new generation of 5G/6G smart antennas and AI-HPC GPU to stacked memory communications links as well as ADAS sensor chipsets.

R&D and pilot production is based in Pisa, Italy and our UK head office is in Cambridge.
company.name: CamGraPhIC Srl

company.description: Builds AI systems that automate expert decision-making in regulated laboratory environments using Limited Sample Model (LSM) on raw instrument data for pharmaceuticals, manufacturing, food-safety testing, and industrial workflows.
company.name: Expert Intelligence

company.description: AI company specializing in enterprise-grade generative AI solutions and document processing engines.
company.name: Upstage

company.description: Wayve Technologies Ltd is a London-based artificial intelligence startup specializing in autonomous driving technology, developing platforms powered by NVIDIA chips to advance self-driving systems.
company.name: Wayve Technologies Ltd

company.description: Dutch clinical-stage biotech developing ronopterin, a first-in-class iNOS inhibitor targeting secondary brain damage in severe traumatic brain injury, with a European Phase 3b trial planned across multiple countries.
company.name: TIBEAY Biosciences

company.description: Share is an open-access internet infrastructure network building Africa's connectivity backbone, aggregating thousands of fragmented Internet Service Providers (ISPs) onto a unified fiber and routing platform that connects directly to subsea cables and global content delivery networks. The company operates a city-level metropolitan fiber ring and carrier-grade points of presence inside neutral data centers, layering stablecoin-based settlement and on-chain financing on top of a physical DePIN network. Initially deployed in Mombasa, Kenya and expanding to Nairobi, Share's open-access model now reaches more than 8 million people across thousands of kilometers of fibre, ten data centers and six subsea cable routes.
company.name: SHARE

company.description: Verismo Therapeutics is a clinical-stage cell therapy company developing a multi-chain KIR-CAR platform that adapts NK-cell killer immunoglobulin-like receptor and DAP12 signaling to extend CAR T cell therapy beyond blood cancers into solid tumors. Founded by inventors of the Kymriah CAR-T (including Carl June), the company is advancing two Phase 1 programs: SynKIR-110 in mesothelin-positive solid tumors and SynKIR-310 in relapsed/refractory B cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma. It is a U.S. subsidiary of KOSDAQ-listed HLB Innovation.
company.name: Verismo Therapeutics

company.description: HICKY is a Tokyo-based medical device startup developing a stent-type neuromodulation device to treat central sleep apnea (CSA) in heart failure patients. The endovascular implant stimulates the phrenic nerve and uses wireless power transfer, eliminating the leads and subcutaneous generators required by existing therapies. The company spun out of the Japan Biodesign program, a joint initiative between the University of Tokyo and Stanford University.
company.name: HICKY

company.description: HockeyStack is a San Francisco-based enterprise revenue intelligence platform that builds AI Revenue Agents for go-to-market teams. Its event-based data architecture and machine-learning Blueprint model power autonomous agents that work alongside reps on prospecting, new business, and account expansion, with customers including Microsoft, New Relic, and Harvey.
company.name: HockeyStack

company.description: Totalis is building a derivatives layer for prediction markets that lets users construct multi-asset, accumulator-style bets across categories such as geopolitics, cryptocurrencies and sports rather than wagering on a single outcome. The startup is targeting persistent issues in the sector — single-position exposure, fragmented liquidity and low capital efficiency — by acting as a cross-venue trading and market-making layer. Founded in 2025 and admitted to Y Combinator's Spring 2026 batch, the company was previously known as UCALLEDIT.
company.name: Totalis

company.description: Votre is a New York-based on-chain investment bank that issues same-day USD loans collateralized by Bitcoin, with loan sizes from roughly $25,000 to $5 million. The non-custodial platform settles on Coinbase's Base layer-2 and uses smart contracts for automated margin control, real-time monitoring, and on-chain transparency, targeting high-net-worth individuals and institutions seeking liquidity without selling their BTC.
company.name: Votre

company.description: Earthletica is a Sydney-based, B Corp-certified sustainable activewear brand co-founded in 2020 by triple Olympic gold medalist Bronte Campbell with Libby Babet and Chris Raleigh. The company designs high-performance women's activewear from recycled materials, free of PFAS forever chemicals, and is developing proprietary garment technologies aimed at eliminating virgin plastics from athletic apparel. Earthletica sells direct-to-consumer in Australia.
company.name: Earthletica

company.description: Clean Slate Clinic is an Australian social-enterprise telehealth platform that delivers doctor-led, at-home detox and recovery programs for people with alcohol, stimulant, or cannabis dependence. Founded in 2020, it has supported more than 3,000 clients and secured reimbursement coverage with the majority of Australia's private health insurers, positioning clinician-supervised withdrawal at home as an alternative to hospital and residential rehabilitation.
company.name: Clean Slate Clinic

company.description: Link-Touch (BluePoint Touch / 蓝点触控) is a Beijing-headquartered Chinese developer of robotic force sensors, with a fully automated production base of more than 10,000 square metres in Guangdong. Its portfolio spans six-axis force sensors for humanoid robots, joint and general-purpose force sensors, integrated force-control solutions and composite robotic workstations, built on four in-house technology platforms covering elastomer design, embedded electronics, structural decoupling and six-axis calibration. The company is the dominant supplier of six-axis force sensors to Chinese humanoid-robot makers and counts Agibot, Xiaomi, Xpeng, Galbot, EngineAI, UBTech and Galaxea AI as customers.
company.name: Link-Touch

company.description: PongBot is a Chinese sports-technology company building AI-powered ball-launching robots that act as personalized training partners for tennis, table tennis, pickleball and padel players. Its Pace-series tennis robots and Nova/Omni table-tennis robots combine custom motors and computer vision with large-language-model voice coaching to deliver multi-ball drills, shot-placement variation and real-time technique feedback. Founded in 2019 by Zhang Haibo, PongBot positions itself against legacy mechanical ball machines by emphasising training data, repeatable spin and speed, and an AI coach that adapts to each player's level.
company.name: PongBot

company.description: Mirage Privacy is a Swiss protocol enabling confidential stablecoin transfers on Ethereum and EVM-compatible networks. Instead of pooling user funds in shared mixers, the non-custodial protocol uses transaction-specific escrow that makes a transfer look like ordinary onchain activity, with settlement under 90 seconds on Ethereum mainnet and under 6 seconds on networks such as Arbitrum and Tempo. It targets payroll, contractor payments, treasury operations, and B2B payments, and exposes a JavaScript SDK so wallets, custodians, and dApps can integrate without changing the assets or chains their users already rely on.
company.name: Mirage Privacy

company.description: Istanbul-based voice-first AI language learning platform that uses a proprietary Speech Language Model (SLM) to deliver real-time pronunciation, fluency and grammar coaching through speech-to-speech conversations — without typing or waiting for text responses. In roughly nine months the platform has reached over three million users across 40+ countries, with 45% in Europe, 44% in the USA and 11% in LATAM and Asia, logging billions of minutes of conversational data. Co-founded by Mustafa Girgin, Lucida sells a B2B corporate English-learning product adopted by Turkish retailer eBebek and other enterprise teams.
company.name: Lucida AI

company.description: Mission Control Games is a mobile game studio developing casual puzzle titles with AI-assisted design, headquartered across London and Istanbul. Co-founded in 2026 by Kıvanç Okutur (CEO) and Murat Gürel (CTO) — both veterans of the Merge Dragons franchise — the studio focuses on rapid iteration to build new puzzle sub-genres rather than clone existing formats. Its stated ambition is to operate as the largest mobile gaming company with the smallest team, using emerging AI capabilities as a core part of the development process.
company.name: Mission Control Games

company.description: GameRun is a New York-based AI platform for athlete performance and injury-risk management, combining computer vision, biomechanics, and physics to turn game film into near-real-time performance reports. Founded in 2024 by fintech veteran Kapil Rathi, the company serves more than 40 universities, leagues, and training programs across baseball, basketball, hockey, and soccer, and powers player evaluation for USA Baseball's National Team Identification Series.
company.name: GameRun

company.description: Osteoboost Health (BoneHealth Technologies) is a medtech company that develops wearable therapeutic devices for bone health. Its flagship product, Osteoboost, is an FDA-cleared wearable belt that uses targeted vibration therapy to slow the loss of bone density in postmenopausal women with osteopenia, providing a non-pharmacological alternative to preventing osteoporosis.
company.name: Osteoboost Health

company.description: Alloy Therapeutics is a Boston-based biotechnology ecosystem company building tech-enabled infrastructure for drug discovery and development, with AI/ML models, real-world data and wet-lab execution federated into an on-demand services layer used by more than 200 partners. Through its collaborative model Alloy has supported over 100 licensed therapeutic programs across antibodies, bispecifics, TCRms, genetic medicines, cell therapies and drug delivery, of which 22 have advanced to clinical development and two are already in Phase 3. Founded in 2017 by Errik Anderson, the company now operates centers of excellence across the US, Japan and the Middle East and is positioning itself as the infrastructure layer that virtual biotechs and lean development teams lean on rather than building in-house.
company.name: Alloy Therapeutics

company.description: Sennos is a Durham, North Carolina-based sensing, analytics and automation company for the fermentation and fluidics industries. Its Sennosystem platform combines patent-pending SennosM3 in-tank sensor hardware, the SennosIQ AI analytics engine and the Sennoslink application to stream multi-parameter measurements — gravity, temperature, pH, pressure, conductivity and dissolved oxygen — into continuous, predictive control rather than the static spreadsheet-era sampling most fermentation producers still rely on. Founded in 2017 by Jared Resnick, the company serves producers of all sizes across fermented beverage and other high-value fluid-based processes and claims to operate the worlds largest real-time fermentation data repository.
company.name: Sennos

company.description: Ulysses is a San Francisco-based technology company building The Ocean Company — networked fleets of low-cost, autonomous surface and underwater vehicles engineered for persistent operation in harsh maritime environments. The platform spans mine countermeasures and intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance for contested waters, protection of subsea critical infrastructure, offshore inspection, and ocean-conservation work such as seagrass restoration; flagship products include MAKO, a small autonomous underwater vehicle positioned as the "Model T for the ocean", and Leviathan, an autonomous surface mothership and maritime domain awareness platform. Founded in Dublin in 2023 by Akhil Voorakkara, Will OBrien, Jamie Wedderburn and Colm OBrien, the company now operates from 1640 17th Street, San Francisco, and counts the US Navy among its active customers.
company.name: Ulysses

company.description: ONESTRUCTION builds a suite of AI products to transition construction data from AI-ready to AI-powered, centered on the openBIM international standard. Its portfolio includes the BIM data quality management tool OpenAEC, the construction-specialized AI platform Ishigaki, and the construction data assetization software Contex (scheduled for 2026 release). The company collaborates with Japanese government agencies, construction firms, and research institutions to structure previously unstructured construction data for AI-integrated operations.
company.name: ONESTRUCTION

company.description: Onto Health is an AI-enabled, physician-led fertility and longevity care provider that combines evidence-based reproductive medicine with diagnostics driven by artificial intelligence and clinical automation. Founded by Dr. Roohi Jeelani (REI, OB/GYN), the Chicago-based company treats reproductive care as part of a broader lifelong health journey rather than a single episode of treatment. Its first clinic opened in River North in February 2026 with further locations planned across Illinois and expansion into the Gulf Cooperation Council.
company.name: Onto Health

company.description: Tokyo-based biotech developing regenerative gene therapy for retinal diseases. The company's approach uses a single injection to treat eye conditions, aiming to improve overall quality of life for patients with vision loss. Founded in 2016 as a spinout from Japanese academic research, Restore Vision is advancing its pipeline through clinical development with support from major pharmaceutical corporate venture arms.
company.name: Restore Vision

company.description: ugo, Inc. (formerly Mira Robotics) is a Tokyo-based robotics company founded in 2018 that designs, manufactures and deploys business-DX (digital transformation) robots for Japanese enterprises facing acute labour shortages. Its core product — the ugo avatar robot — combines teleoperation with AI-driven autonomy to take on security patrols, facility inspections, customer guidance and other repetitive on-site tasks. CEO Ken Matsui leads a 51-200 person team that has integrated the robot with partners across real estate, manufacturing, utilities and security services, including NTT West, Tokyo Gas, Daiwa House, JR East, Yaskawa Electric and Kawasaki Heavy Industries.
company.name: ugo

company.description: Hilbert's AI (legally Hilbert AI Co.) is a San Francisco-based analytics and decision-automation software company that builds an AI-native growth platform for consumer (B2C) brands. The product unifies marketing, product and commerce data, uses deep-learning agents to interpret customer behaviour and recommend next-best actions, and quantifies the financial upside of each recommendation before execution — compressing multi-month analytics projects into weeks. Walmart and other consumer brands use Hilbert's to turn AI spend into measurable growth outcomes, with the platform also serving as a forecasting engine for customer-base and revenue trajectories.
company.name: Hilbert's AI

company.description: openmoove is a Cardiff-based B2B proptech platform that streamlines UK property transactions by providing a single communication layer across estate agents, conveyancers, and mortgage brokers. It integrates with the back-office systems each participant already uses, building a shared source of truth and common comms flow without forcing users to adopt new software. Founded in 2024 by Welsh entrepreneurs Ross McKenzie and Cai Gwinnutt, the company spent 18 months developing and testing the platform with early customers before raising its pre-seed round.
company.name: openmoove

company.description: The Little Loop (rebranding to 'Lloop' in 2026) is a Brighton-based B2B2C clothing take-back and resale platform that lets retailers embed circular-economy trade-in flows into their own storefronts without running the logistics in-house. Consumers trade in used garments for credits or voucher codes redeemable with partner brands; items are graded and cleaned at the company's Hampshire warehouse before being resold. Founded in 2019 by CEO Charlotte Morley and initially backed on Dragons' Den, the company counts John Lewis, JoJo Maman Bébé, Roarsome, and Little Green Radicals as retail partners; 2025 saw 250% YoY revenue growth with 39,000+ items sold.
company.name: The Little Loop (Lloop)

company.description: Ascension Earth Resources is a University of Oxford Earth Sciences spin-out (founded 2024 via Oxford University Innovation) developing geothermal-powered in-situ extraction of rare-earth elements and other critical minerals from volcanic rocks. Its approach replaces conventional surface mining with heat-plus-solvent chemistry that recovers targeted metals underground, supplying downstream markets in electric vehicles, wind turbines, and advanced defense. Based at AgileLab, Oxford University Science Park Begbroke.
company.name: Ascension Earth Resources

company.description: Lightcast Discovery Ltd is a Cambridge, UK-based life-sciences tools company spun out in 2019 that builds the Envisia benchtop platform for functional single-cell analysis. Its proprietary light-controlled droplet-manipulation technology interrogates tens of thousands of picoliter-scale droplets in parallel, capturing direct functional readouts at single-cell resolution rather than inferring cell behaviour from genomic or transcriptomic data. The platform targets antibody discovery, cell-therapy development, immunology and oncology research, and is being prepared for full commercial launch in 2026 after an early-access programme with pharmaceutical and academic collaborators.
company.name: Lightcast

company.description: INVIA is a Cairo-based fintech company building an AI-powered financial operating system for small and medium-sized enterprises in Egypt. Its platform unifies bookkeeping, cash-flow tracking, inventory, and manufacturing management into a single interface, with owners able to interact via text, voice notes, or invoices. Founded in 2023 by CEO Yehia Ashour, Ahmed Zeinhom and Omar Aboulmagd, the company is extending the platform toward HR, POS and CRM — positioning itself as the operating layer for an underserved SME segment.
company.name: INVIA

company.description: Stendr is a Norwegian defence technology company based in Oslo, building an AI-native, vertically integrated stack of hardware, software and AI for counter-drone and multi-sensor systems. Founded by ex-Sky Mavis co-founder Aleksander Leonard Larsen (CEO) alongside Robin Alexander Holm Pedersen (CTO) and Markus Leonhard Hansen (hardware), the company positions itself as a new Nordic defence prime focused on sovereign European capability.
company.name: Stendr

company.description: Terremoto Biosciences is a clinical-stage biotechnology company based in South San Francisco and San Diego, developing highly targeted small-molecule medicines via a covalent drug-discovery engine. Its lead program TER-2013 — a selective AKT1 inhibitor — is in Phase 1 for solid tumors with PIK3CA, AKT, or PTEN alterations, and TER-4480 is advancing toward clinical entry for hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT). The company is led by CEO Charles Baum, M.D., Ph.D., former CEO of Mirati Therapeutics.
company.name: Terremoto

company.description: Artemis Security is a New York-based AI-native cyber defence platform that emerged from stealth in April 2026. It is building a centralized AI "brain" and agent network to detect and contain threats across applications, users, machines, and infrastructure — replacing rule-based SIEM/EDR patterns with adaptive models that keep pace with AI-powered attacks. Founded in late 2025 by CEO Shachar Hirshberg (formerly product lead on Amazon GuardDuty) and CTO Dan Shiebler.
company.name: Artemis

company.description: Skye Renewables Energy is a Singapore-headquartered commercial & industrial (C&I) solar developer and renewable asset owner operating across Southeast Asia. Founded in 2021 by CEO Ross Coull, the company develops both onsite/rooftop solar and offsite/grid-connected renewables for corporate off-takers, with focus markets in Malaysia, Singapore, Vietnam, and the Philippines. It is also backed by Japanese energy group Idemitsu Kosan and UK development finance institution British International Investment.
company.name: Skye Renewables Energy

company.description: SONIRE Therapeutics is a Tokyo-based clinical-stage medical device company developing a next-generation high-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) system for solid-tumor ablation. Its proprietary platform combines real-time imaging guidance with patented cavitation-bubble-assisted "Trigger HIFU" acoustic engineering, enabling a 20-minute outpatient procedure for pancreatic cancer. Spun out of Japanese academic research, the company is now expanding clinical development into the United States.
company.name: Sonire Therapeutics

company.description: Auctor is a New York-based enterprise-software startup founded in 2025 through Y Combinator's X25 batch by William Sun, Xinan Rahman, Matthew Blackburn and Anthony Sky Ng-Thow-Hing. It builds an AI-native "system of action" for the entire software-implementation lifecycle — recording discovery, design, delivery and go-live interactions; auto-generating rough orders of magnitude, resource plans, process flows and user stories; and turning project context into reusable institutional knowledge for system integrators and professional-services teams. The company positions itself against ServiceNow, Salesforce and the major ERP vendors as a dedicated agentic OS for the services layer that wraps every software deployment.
company.name: Auctor

company.description: Turion Space is an Irvine, California–based space domain awareness and on-orbit servicing startup founded in 2021 by veterans of SpaceX, Lockheed Martin Skunk Works, and Boeing Phantom Works. Its maneuverable Droid-series satellites track objects in orbit, monitor debris, and conduct non-Earth imaging — inspecting other spacecraft up close. The company operates a mission-control software platform called Starfire and has secured 28 US government contracts, including from Space Force, NASA, and the National Reconnaissance Office.
company.name: Turion Space

company.description: BALLAS is a Tokyo-based construction-tech company that runs BALLAS SCM, Japan's largest online marketplace for custom construction parts connecting construction companies with fabricators, and BALLAS LINKS, a project-management SaaS launched in 2026 for construction workflows. Its standardized-module approach targets the ¥75 trillion Japanese and $17 trillion global construction-parts market, with applications spanning data centers, logistics facilities, plants, and commercial buildings. GMV has compounded at 130% CAGR since Series A.
company.name: BALLAS

company.description: VivaJets is a Lagos-based business aviation company operating under Falcon Aerospace Limited, offering premium private jet charter, aircraft management, and flight support across Africa. The Nigerian-licensed operator targets executives, institutions, and industry leaders with a growing fleet that includes Hawker 850XPs and other mid-to-heavy business jets. VivaJets holds a Nigerian Air Operator Certificate and is expanding into Francophone West Africa through a new hub in Abidjan.
company.name: Viva-jets

company.description: Italian FinTech startup founded in 2024 by CEO Marco Gambardella, CTO Felice Cupane, and COO Francesco Varuzza. Smartwage reimagines corporate welfare by letting employees spend their welfare credit with any merchant through a digital platform, using regulated payment rails (cards, bank transfers) and an AI welfare coach. The Italian corporate welfare market exceeds €7 billion, and roughly 20% of budgets go unused annually due to closed merchant networks.
company.name: Smartwage

company.description: San Mateo-based AI code-validation startup founded in 2024 by CEO Ali-Reza Adl-Tabatabai (veteran of Intel Labs, Google, and Uber). Gitar deploys AI agents that perform code reviews, manage continuous integration workflows, and run security and maintenance operations — positioning code validation as the counterweight to AI-generated code overload. Engineering teams can build custom agents on the platform.
company.name: Gitar

company.description: Seoul-based AI-native content studio founded in 2025 by CEO Juwon Jung, developing original IP across film, drama, and animation on a proprietary AI production pipeline. The creative team previously won the 2024 Korea International AI Film Festival Grand Prize with short film "Mateo". Flagship feature RAPHAEL, an 80-minute AI-generated film co-produced with MBC C&I's AI Contents Lab, premieres September 2026.
company.name: Mateo AI Studio

company.description: San Francisco-based AI voice platform helping businesses optimize phone conversations for revenue, efficiency, and customer experience. The platform answers millions of calls monthly across thousands of businesses, with customers including Vacatia and Curb.
company.name: Phonely

company.description: American-made navigation and spatial intelligence platform for drones and robots, replacing GPS-dependent systems with visual positioning technology. Backed by Craft Ventures.
company.name: Hyfix Spatial Intelligence

company.description: Mumbai-based brain health startup founded in 2024 by Issac John and Rahul Krishnan. Transforms the ageing experience by nurturing sharper minds through early detection of neurodegenerative risks. Makes cognitive screenings as routine as heart or liver checkups.
company.name: Ivory

company.description: NanoTech Materials develops high-performance coatings using its patented Insulative Ceramic Particle (ICP) technology to reduce heat transfer and improve fire resilience in buildings and infrastructure. The company's products help commercial and industrial property owners control energy costs and harden assets against extreme heat and wildfires.
company.name: NanoTech Materials

company.description: Tel Aviv-based runtime security layer for enterprise AI agents. Prevents agents from being manipulated, misbehaving, or exfiltrating data at runtime. Founded in 2025 by Naor Paz (ex-F5, Unit 8200) and Lidan Hazout (ex-VP R&D at Securedtouch/Ping Identity, Transmit Security). No proxies, gateways, or SDKs needed.
company.name: Capsule Security

company.description: Kitar is an Indonesia-based second-hand e-commerce platform focused on Southeast Asia, starting with smartphones and expanding to broader consumer electronics and durable goods. Founded by ex-Shopee executive Frank Zhou, the company combines B2C standardized resale with a C2C marketplace, using AI-driven quality inspection to build trust in pre-owned transactions. Kitar operates physical stores and workstations across Indonesia and partners with Shopee for device trade-in services.
company.name: Kitar

company.description: AI-powered learning platform making studying addictive for 13 million learners worldwide through gamified flashcards and AI-generated study materials.
company.name: Gizmo

company.description: Agriodor is a French agritech company spun off from INRAE that develops scent-based biocontrol solutions to protect crops from insect pests without chemical pesticides. Its patented Insulative Ceramic Particle technology reproduces natural plant scents that attract, repel, or disrupt pest insects, offering development costs up to 10x lower and half the timeline of traditional insecticides. The company operates labs in Rennes and Aix-en-Provence, with its first product INSIOR® Gr A distributed by Syngenta for sugar beet protection in France.
company.name: Agriodor

company.description: YC F25 startup replacing marketing agencies with AI. Generates hundreds of ads with matching landing pages and steers ad spend across Meta, Google, and TikTok, delivering 20-50% better ROI. Already created thousands of ads and landing pages for customers.
company.name: Uplane

company.description: Indian AI startup building data analytics and business intelligence solutions. Series A led by Susquehanna Venture Capital with InfoEdge Ventures and Kae Capital.
company.name: GobbleCube

company.description: Keebler Health is a Durham, NC-based AI startup building an LLM-native risk adjustment platform for value-based care organizations. The platform processes unstructured clinical documentation — approximately 80% of healthcare data that traditional NLP cannot handle — to generate accurate HCC coding and actionable insights at the point of care. Founded in 2023 by Isaac Park and team, the company has raised $23M to date and is expanding into compliance, audit workflows, and RADV audit readiness.
company.name: Keebler Health

company.description: Payment infrastructure for the agentic economy, enabling AI agents to transact autonomously across platforms. Integrates natively into agent workflows for Claude, ChatGPT, and emerging AI ecosystems.
company.name: SolvaPay

company.description: FX infrastructure startup providing institutional-grade financial infrastructure for real-time cross-border payments using modern technology for faster and cheaper global money movement.
company.name: OpenFX

company.description: Mumbai-based eco-friendly luggage and travel accessories brand that designs and manufactures sustainable travel gear including hard luggage, backpacks, duffel bags, and office satchels. Products are GRS certified, 100% made in India, priced Rs 3,500-5,000.
company.name: Uppercase

company.description: An applied AI lab developing an AI-native security platform called General Security Intelligence that understands code, business logic, and infrastructure to detect vulnerabilities, reduce false positives, and provide fixes.
company.name: depthfirst

company.description: Brickken is a Barcelona-based startup founded in 2020 that provides a SaaS platform enabling businesses to tokenize and manage real-world assets such as equity, debt, and revenue-sharing models without coding. The platform incorporates AI-driven operations and supports global market access, focusing on real-world asset tokenization using blockchain technology.
company.name: Brickken
