company.description: Emerald AI develops an AI-driven compute orchestration platform called Conductor that transforms energy-intensive data centers into flexible grid assets by dynamically managing AI workloads to optimize power consumption and support electric grid stability.
company.name: Emerald AI

company.description: Also Inc. is a micromobility startup spun out of Rivian, focused on developing small electric vehicles including e-bikes and compact three- and four-wheel EVs for urban and commercial use. The company leverages Rivian's technology stack adapted for smaller form factors and aims to launch its flagship products initially in the US and Europe, with plans for global expansion.
company.name: Also Inc.

company.description: Green Genius is a renewable energy company based in Lithuania, specializing in the development of large-scale solar power projects and battery energy storage systems.
company.name: Green Genius

company.description: TENEX.AI is an AI-native cybersecurity company based in Sarasota, Florida, specializing in advanced Managed Detection and Response (MDR) services that combine artificial intelligence, automation, and human expertise to enhance threat detection, triage, and response for enterprises.
company.name: TENEX.AI

company.description: Crosby is a New York-based hybrid AI-powered law firm that accelerates contract review and negotiation using proprietary AI technology combined with licensed attorney oversight. It serves startups and fast-growing companies by delivering legal advice through a Slack-native interface, enabling rapid contract turnaround typically within an hour.
company.name: Crosby

company.description: Graphmatech is an Uppsala-based deeptech company commercialising Aros Graphene, a scalable graphene-enhanced material used in conductive polymers, gas barrier composites and metal alloys. Spun out of Uppsala University in 2017, it sells into hydrogen storage, electronics and additive manufacturing, and in 2026 pivoted toward the defense sector with PFAS-replacement composites and radar-absorbing stealth coatings for drones. Customers include hydrogen-economy OEMs and European defense suppliers; the company is led by CEO Olivia Nestius.
company.name: Graphmatech

company.description: Kumonos Corporation is an Osaka-based 3D measurement and surveying technology firm serving Japan's social infrastructure sector — roads, bridges, dams, and large facilities. Its flagship products include the KUMONOS high-precision crack-measurement system, which uses non-prism total stations, and TRINOS, a point-cloud data management platform for the building lifecycle. The company has exported surveying instruments to more than 20 countries since 2008 and is moving toward subscription-based 3D digital twin and remote inspection services.
company.name: Kumonos Corporation

company.description: Latitude is a global payments infrastructure company that uses stablecoin rails to settle cross-border business payments into local fiat currencies. Its flagship Global Payouts product lets US businesses send funds to individuals across 50+ countries with near-instant local-currency settlement, a flat 50bps fee, and interbank FX rates, while its Latitude Liquidity Network also offers embeddable on/off ramps for fintechs and digital wallets. The company was founded in 2024 by payments veterans from Stripe, Coinbase, Uber, Meta, and Zero Hash.
company.name: Latitude

company.description: Capsule Corporation is an Italian aerospace startup developing WaterCube, a water-based propulsion system for CubeSats and small satellites. Using demineralised water as propellant with metal additive manufacturing and proprietary heat-exchanger technology, WaterCube cuts integration time from months to weeks and costs by up to 50% versus hydrazine-based alternatives. Founded in 2024 by CEO Francesco Marino, the company is advancing toward an ESA-supervised in-orbit demonstration in Q4 2026 and commercial market entry in 2027.
company.name: Capsule Corporation

company.description: Also Inc. is a micromobility startup spun out of Rivian, focused on developing small electric vehicles including e-bikes and compact three- and four-wheel EVs for urban and commercial use. The company leverages Rivian's technology stack adapted for smaller form factors and aims to launch its flagship products initially in the US and Europe, with plans for global expansion.
company.name: Also Inc.

company.description: PhageLab is a Chilean biotechnology company developing bacteriophage-based solutions as alternatives to antibiotics in the livestock and poultry industries. The company uses phages — natural enemies of bacteria — to control pathogens in animal health and the food supply chain. With operations in Chile, Brazil, and Spain, PhageLab has raised approximately $40 million in total funding and is the only biotech in Kaszek Ventures' portfolio of 107 Latin American investments.
company.name: PhageLab

company.description: Zmiyar is a Ukrainian defense technology company developing advanced military hardware. The company started by producing initiation boards for drones, of which it sold 30,000+ units, before expanding into smart mine systems in 2025. Its flagship product — a remote minefield control system — allows a single operator to control up to 200 mines at distances of 20+ km. The system works with PTM-3 and TM-62 anti-vehicle mines. Founded by Viktor Shapovalov, Zmiyar has delivered over 5,000 units of battle-proven systems to the Armed Forces of Ukraine.
company.name: Zmiyar

company.description: At Mezza, we're reimagining how restaurants access funding and how diners experience dining. We empower restaurants with upfront capital by purchasing large amounts of dining credits. Our members can enjoy cash back, rewards, and seamless dining across our network of partner restaurants. For restaurants, Mezza unlocks a new, sustainable funding channel to fuel growth—without debt, equity dilution, or complex financing. For diners, Mezza means more value at the restaurants they love, with up to 30% back on every bill.
company.name: Mezza

company.description: Delta Labs is a Zurich-based AI and data-driven customer intelligence company founded in 2021 by Dr. Cindy Candrian and Dr. Anne Scherer. The company develops Elaiia, an Artificial Customer Intelligence platform that simulates customer behavior by generating synthetic populations — AI twins that mirror real-world audiences using large-scale behavioral, survey, and transaction data. Companies use Elaiia to predict how different customer segments respond to product, pricing, messaging, and strategy decisions. In validation tests, platform results aligned with human study outcomes up to 95%.
company.name: Delta Labs

company.description: Lucida Medical is a Cambridge-based AI medtech company that develops artificial intelligence software to support the detection, diagnosis, and management of cancer. Its flagship product, Pi (Prostate Intelligence), is a CE-marked medical device that analyses prostate MRI scans to identify suspicious lesions and generate risk scores, assisting radiologists in detecting clinically significant prostate cancer. The system is trained on thousands of patient scans and is deployed across multiple NHS hospitals in the UK, with EU approval and plans to seek US FDA approval. Founded in 2019 by Anthony and Evis Sala.
company.name: Lucida Medical

company.description: Mesabi Metallics (an Essar Group company) is developing the first new iron ore mine and pellet plant in Minnesota in nearly 50 years. The company is constructing a 7 million metric ton per year facility to produce direct-reduction grade iron ore pellets, supporting US steel supply chain and green steel transition.
company.name: Mesabi Metallics

company.description: Leading the evolution of energy infrastructure, Aypa Power has redefined utility-scale battery energy storage as a critical asset class, supporting grid reliability and modernizing energy markets. As a Blackstone portfolio company, Aypa develops, owns, and operates large-scale energy storage and hybrid renewable energy projects across North America. Our portfolio includes 24 assets either operational or under construction, alongside a 22-gigawatt (GW) development pipeline, with many projects backed by long-term agreements.
company.name: Aypa Power

company.description: Nxtra Data (Nxtra by Airtel) is India's largest data center company, a subsidiary of Bharti Airtel. It operates 14+ data centers across India with over 300 MW of capacity, providing colocation and cloud infrastructure services. The company is expanding its AI-ready data center footprint to meet growing demand from hyperscalers and enterprises.
company.name: Nxtra Data

company.description: ContractorHUB is an industry-backed, AI-native software platform that helps home service businesses scale with confidence. Developed from firsthand experience, it was built to give business owners back their most valuable asset—time. ContractorHUB turns information into action, aligning data, driving performance, and fueling growth. Unlike CRMs, data aggregators, or performance frameworks, ContractorHUB centralizes your most important resources using advanced technology to implement systems proven to scale contracting businesses.
company.name: ContractorHUB

company.description: iBase-t is a Lake Forest, California-based provider of manufacturing operations management (MOM) software tailored for the aerospace and defense industry. Its flagship Solumina platform integrates manufacturing execution, supplier quality management, enterprise quality management, and maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) into a unified cloud-native environment. Customers include Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, GE Aerospace, Rolls-Royce, Pratt and Whitney, and Textron.
company.name: iBase-t

company.description: CoreWeave is a GPU-native cloud provider purpose-built for AI training and inference at scale. Founded in 2017, the company operates infrastructure optimized around modern AI stacks with tightly integrated networking, storage, and orchestration systems. CoreWeave serves AI labs, startups, and large enterprises needing burstable high-throughput compute without the capex of building GPU farms. The company completed its public listing on Nasdaq in March 2025 under ticker CRWV.
company.name: CoreWeave

company.description: SurgePay is a fintech infrastructure company building the rails for borderless money movement. We power seamless cross-border payments through secure, scalable, cloud-first technology designed for global reach. Our platform is built to deliver speed, compliance, and reliability—making it easier for businesses and individuals to move money without friction. Our mission is simple: Money should just work—because when money moves freely, opportunities come easily.
company.name: SurgePay

company.description: Lebby Snacks brings the best of both worlds to snacking—health and happiness. Our artisanal Mediterranean recipe dates back over 500 years, bringing chickpea snacks from the street sellers of our childhood straight to you with modern, bold, delicious flavors. Lebby chickpeas are dry roasted with no oil, no salt, no additives—using only earth's elements: fire, water, air.
company.name: Lebby Snacks

company.description: Coral is a Montreal-based provider of a virtual healthcare platform dedicated to women navigating perimenopause, menopause, and midlife health. By integrating medical expertise with advanced technology and health coaching, Coral offers a personalized, evidence-based roadmap for menopause symptom management, weight loss, and health longevity. Led by CEO Fiona Lake Waslander, the platform uses AI to deliver individualized care for midlife women.
company.name: Coral

company.description: Uniblock is a Toronto-based provider of a managed infrastructure layer for blockchain applications. A single API connection offers access to 300+ blockchains and 55 data partners through patented auto-routing with intelligent orchestration. The platform includes AI-native developer tools, an MCP server, LLM-optimized documentation, and Agent Skills. Over 3,000 projects and 4,000 developers run on the platform.
company.name: Uniblock

company.description: ANDA is an Angolan startup professionalising urban transport through an integrated mobility and logistics ecosystem. Founded in 2022 by Sergio Tati and Joerg Nuehrmann, ANDA offers a "drive-to-own" model that empowers drivers of two-, three- and four-wheeled vehicles through asset financing, training, and assistance at its "safe stops". The company serves urban transport needs across Angola while expanding into electric vehicles and logistics services for businesses and institutions.
company.name: ANDA

company.description: TRX Services is a leading provider of payment processing solutions, processing more than $4.5 billion in transaction volume across credit card, debit card, ACH and online payment transactions. The company also provides accounts receivable and payable management and identity and fraud solutions, supporting nearly 4,000 merchants across insurance, professional services, restaurants, travel and dealerships in the United States and Canada.
company.name: TRX Services

company.description: ecoworks is a Berlin-based renovation technology company founded in 2019, pioneering serial renovation for climate-neutral building decarbonisation across Europe. With 130+ engineers, architects and digital experts, it uses AI-powered planning, digital twins and industrial prefabrication to retrofit residential buildings at scale. The company industrially prefabricates facade modules, insulation, windows and PV systems ex-factory, dramatically reducing on-site construction time. Its mission is to save one gigaton of CO₂ by 2045.
company.name: ecoworks

company.description: Brilliance RGB is an Enschede-based deeptech startup that manufactures integrated RGB laser chips for augmented reality applications. Using a patented silicon nitride-based photonic integrated circuit (PIC) platform with built-in laser technology, its chips replace complex optical assemblies with a compact solution that reduces power consumption by a factor of ten, enables outdoor-grade brightness, and maximizes field of view for immersive AR experiences. The technology supports display architectures including AR glasses, automotive head-up displays, and industrial laser systems. Co-founded in 2023 by CEO Tim Tiek and CTO Douwe Geuzebroek.
company.name: Brilliance RGB

company.description: Memebox (MBX) is a Korean beauty startup founded in 2012 by Hyungseok Ha, the first Korean founder backed by Y Combinator. The company operates five K-Beauty brands: makeup labels I'm Meme, Kaja, and Pony Effect, along with skincare and haircare brands nooni and I Dew Care. Roughly 65% of revenue is generated in the US market, with expanded partnerships with Silicon2 and 2020 retailers.
company.name: Memebox

company.description: AI-powered IT services platform that automates and manages IT infrastructure operations for enterprises. YC-backed startup reinventing IT services with AI.
company.name: Conductor

company.description: Voltify is a US-Israeli startup building a dynamic electrification platform for freight rail. The company retrofits diesel locomotives with battery power and deploys on-route fast-charging infrastructure and microgrids, targeting the $11 billion diesel rail energy market. Voltify claims to reduce rail energy costs by over 20% while avoiding the massive capital expenditure of traditional overhead wire electrification.
company.name: Voltify

company.description: Sub-Q Bionics is a medical device company developing a next-generation implantable lymphatic drainage system to manage lymphedema, a chronic condition often experienced by cancer survivors. Based on scientific innovations from the Weizmann Institute of Science and Shamir Medical Center, the company's bionic lymph node technology provides subcutaneous fluid management to reduce swelling, pain, and mobility limitations. Co-founded by CEO Jordan Pollack.
company.name: Sub-Q Bionics

company.description: Avo is an EHR-integrated clinical AI platform that synthesizes patient data, care guidelines, and payer policies to guide clinicians through documentation, diagnosis, and care management workflows.
company.name: Avo

company.description: Acclaim (formerly Aiphoria) is a voice-first AI customer experience platform built for banking, fintech, and other regulated industries. It enables enterprises to deploy goal-oriented AI agents that resolve service issues, recover payments, support sales, and automate high-volume customer interactions with built-in compliance guardrails and auditable interaction trails.
company.name: Acclaim

company.description: ThinkLabs AI is a Toronto-based startup spun out of GE Vernova that builds physics-informed AI models to simulate and optimize electric grid behavior. The company's platform compresses month-long grid planning studies into minutes, running 10 million scenarios in 10 minutes with over 99.7% accuracy on power flow calculations. ThinkLabs helps utilities assess the grid impact of connecting large loads like AI data centers, EV chargers, and renewable energy sources, addressing the growing gap between surging electricity demand and legacy planning tools. Founded by CEO Josh Wong.
company.name: ThinkLabs AI

company.description: Treeline is a San Francisco-based AI-powered IT, security, and compliance platform for growing tech companies, founded by Peter Doyle (CEO) and Hussain Kader (CTO). The platform acts as a full external IT and security team, offering 24/7 AI-enhanced help desk, automated workflows, continuous monitoring, and integrations with tools like Okta, Crowdstrike, Google Workspace, and Vanta. Treeline delivers 80% faster response times and automates 98% of IT tickets, positioning itself as a modern IT operating system for fast-scaling businesses.
company.name: Treeline

company.description: Monarch Quantum is a San Diego-based developer of integrated photonics hardware for quantum technologies, founded in 2025 by Dr. Timothy Day. The company builds Quantum Light Engines™ — photonic control systems that replace complex laboratory setups with manufacturable, scalable hardware for quantum computing, sensing, and networking. It entered 2026 with over $60 million in customer contracts from leading quantum organizations including Quantinuum, Infleqtion (NYSE: INFQ), and NASA.
company.name: Monarch Quantum

company.description: EnerVenue is a California-based energy storage company developing metal-hydrogen battery technology originally derived from NASA's nickel-hydrogen space battery chemistry. The company produces durable, lithium-free battery solutions designed for grid-scale, utility-scale, commercial, and industrial applications, with a 30-year product lifetime and resilience in extreme temperatures and harsh environments. EnerVenue's aqueous metal-hydrogen batteries target renewable energy integration, grid stabilization, and AI data center backup power, and the company is scaling manufacturing toward 1 GWh capacity at a North Carolina facility.
company.name: EnerVenue

company.description: Cara is a New York-based AI platform built for insurance brokerages and agencies. The platform automates coverage comparisons, proposal and certificate of insurance (COI) generation, ACORD and supplemental form fill, errors and omissions (E&O) review, and handles client requests via voice and email AI agents. It acts as a 24/7 AI workforce that integrates directly with existing agency management systems (AMS) and CRM platforms, enabling insurance teams to increase productivity without adding headcount. Founded by Vic Yeh, Nikhil Kansal, and Jonathan Patel, Cara serves customers including The McGowan Companies, Atlas Insurance Brokers, ISU Steadfast, Combined Agents of America, and FirstChoice.
company.name: Cara

company.description: memQ is a Chicago-based quantum networking company founded in 2021 as a technology spin-out from the University of Chicago. The company develops quantum network interface controllers (QNICs) and quantum memory modules (QMMs) that enable different types of quantum computers to interconnect and work together over standard optical telecom links without collapsing the quantum state. memQ's chip-scale xQNA portfolio provides modular quantum networking solutions enabling scale-out configurations, cooperative processing, and blind cloud quantum computing for distributed quantum computing infrastructure.
company.name: memQ

company.description: Nomadic (NomadicML) is an AI-powered data infrastructure company that helps autonomous vehicle companies, robotics firms, and autonomous equipment developers turn raw video footage into structured, searchable datasets. Founded by CEO Mustafa Bal and CTO Varun Krishnan, the company's platform uses vision language models to organize and catalog video data for fleet monitoring, reinforcement learning dataset creation, and faster model iteration. Customers include Zoox, Mitsubishi Electric, Natix Network, and Zendar.
company.name: Nomadic

company.description: Earlyasset is a Park City, Utah-based startup building critical infrastructure for the venture secondary market. The company enables shareholders in venture-backed private companies to access liquidity pathways by reducing the friction, legal costs, and operational burden that has historically limited smaller secondary transactions. Earlyasset's platform allows shareholders to register private holdings, access pricing insights, and request liquidity as the company builds its network of investors and capital partners.
company.name: Earlyasset

company.description: LIS Technologies Inc. (LIST) is a US-based proprietary developer of patented advanced laser uranium enrichment technology. The company's Condensation Repression Isotope Selective Laser Activation (CRISLA) technology uses infrared lasers to selectively excite molecules of desired isotopes, making it the world's only proven US-origin and patented laser uranium enrichment method. Selected as one of six domestic companies for the DOE's Low-Enriched Uranium (LEU) Enrichment Acquisition Program, which allocates up to $3.4 billion over ten years, the company is developing a commercial laser enrichment facility at the historic K-25 Site in Oak Ridge, Tennessee.
company.name: LIS Technologies

company.description: Dugar Finance is a Chennai-based impact-driven, non-deposit-taking NBFC primarily serving tier 3 and tier 4 markets across India. Led by Ramesh Dugar, the company provides secured MSME lending and vehicle finance solutions, with presence in six Indian states. It aims to grow its assets under management from ₹400 crore to ₹2,000 crore over 3-4 years, targeting expansion to 10 states by deepening its presence in semi-urban and rural markets.
company.name: Dugar Finance

company.description: Cross River Bank is a US federally chartered bank and fintech infrastructure provider offering banking-as-a-service for payments, embedded finance, crypto, and lending. The company partners with fintech companies to provide regulated banking infrastructure via API-first financial products across consumer lending, payments, and digital assets, serving major fintech platforms at scale.
company.name: Cross River Bank

company.description: Jimini Health is a New York-based AI behavioral health platform that provides clinically supervised mental healthcare tools. The company builds patient-facing AI chatbot infrastructure for mental health providers, enabling clinically safe AI interactions alongside human care. Founded to address the growing demand for scalable behavioral health support with clinical oversight.
company.name: Jimini Health

company.description: Variance (formerly Intrinsic) is a San Francisco-based AI platform that deploys investigative agents for risk and compliance, automating KYC, KYB, AML, transaction monitoring, and fraud investigation workflows for financial institutions and Fortune 500 companies. Founded in 2023 by Karine Mellata (CEO) and Michael Lin, formerly of Apple's fraud engineering team. The platform processes over 70 million context signals per day and executes ~300,000 automated enforcement actions across customer environments.
company.name: Variance

company.description: Scala Biodesign is a Tel Aviv-based biotech company transforming protein development through advanced computational protein design technology. Its ScalaOS platform embeds computational protein design directly into R&D workflows, integrating physics-based modeling, evolutionary data, and LLM-based AI models to optimize proteins for therapeutics, vaccines, and industrial applications. Founded in 2022 by Dr. Ravit Netzer, Dr. Adi Goldenzweig, and Chief Scientist Prof. Sarel Fleishman of the Weizmann Institute of Science. Nine of the world's top 20 pharma companies use ScalaOS, including Boehringer Ingelheim.
company.name: Scala Biodesign

company.description: Censys is the trusted authority for Internet intelligence and insights, delivering the most comprehensive and real-time global map of Internet infrastructure. Founded by University of Michigan researchers, the company provides industry-leading solutions for attack surface management, threat hunting, and proactive incident response. Trusted by more than 300,000 security practitioners worldwide, including organizations representing over 50% of the Fortune 500, global governments, and critical infrastructure providers.
company.name: Censys

company.description: Ambrosia Biosciences is a Boulder, CO-based drug discovery company developing next-generation oral small-molecule GLP-1 therapies for cardiometabolic conditions. Founded by former Array BioPharma scientists, the company's pipeline targets type 2 diabetes, obesity, and related metabolic conditions using oral small-molecule agonists as alternatives to injectable GLP-1 therapies.
company.name: Ambrosia Biosciences

company.description: AccessPay is a Manchester-based fintech company providing bank integration and payment automation solutions for enterprise organisations. The platform connects back-office finance systems (ERP, TMS, HCM) directly to banks, enabling organisations to drive finance transformation, achieve AI readiness, and automate payment processes. Backed by ProVen VCTs and Maven prior to the Accel-KKR investment.
company.name: AccessPay

company.description: WHOOP is a wearable health and performance technology company founded in 2012 by Will Ahmed. The company makes a screenless fitness band that continuously tracks biometric data including heart rate, sleep, recovery, strain, and respiratory rate. With over 15 million members including professional athletes across the NFL, NBA, PGA Tour, and elite performers worldwide, WHOOP provides personalized health insights and coaching. The company is expanding globally with a focus on the GCC region.
company.name: Whoop

company.description: Antaris is the creator of the AI-powered Antaris Intelligence™ platform that simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellite missions. Its Full Mission Virtualization™ platform and TrueTwin™ simulation environment enable customers to virtually fly entire missions before committing to specific hardware choices. company.description:
Aetherflux's mission is to deliver energy to planet Earth. Our work will make energy more accessible, establish American energy independence, power underdeveloped and disaster-stricken areas, and protect American warfighters. 

Aetherflux is taking a new approach to an old idea – space solar power. Our satellites will collect sunlight in Low Earth Orbit and transmit that power through infrared lasers to portable ground receivers, providing energy access where traditional delivery is expensive, challenging, or dangerous. 

Current energy systems rely on complex logistics and outdated infrastructure, making them vulnerable to natural disasters, extreme weather, and physical and cyber attacks. Aetherflux overcomes these challenges by enabling direct, on-demand, secure, and scalable 24-hour energy distribution from space. This can revolutionize energy generation and delivery, and enable new capabilities as we deliver power to remote regions, military bases, bespoke operations, and unmanned autonomous systems. 

Baiju Bhatt founded Aetherflux in 2024. Inspired by his father’s career at NASA, Baiju earned his B.S. in Physics and M.S. in Mathematics at Stanford before co-founding Robinhood, now a public company that has helped over 20 million Americans access the financial system. Aetherflux raised $60 million from Index Ventures, Interlagos, Breakthrough Energy Ventures, Andreessen Horowitz, NEA, and others. 

This is an ambitious mission that demands extraordinary talent. Aetherflux’s team has worked at places like SpaceX, NASA, Anduril, and the U.S. Navy, and is based in San Carlos, CA. If you're ready to solve complex technical challenges and help build the most important energy company in the world, we want to hear from you.
company.name: Aetherflux

company.description: AI robotics startup specializing in warehouse automation and intelligence.

company.description: Rebellions is a Korean AI semiconductor startup focusing on advanced chip technologies.
company.name: Rebellions

company.description:
Tazapay is a Singapore-based fintech company redefining the cross-border payment experience for global businesses. Founded in 2021 by industry veterans, Tazapay enables companies to scale effortlessly with payment solutions across 70+ markets.
The company provides a comprehensive payments infrastructure spanning local collections, payouts, cards and virtual accounts on modern rails — all underpinned by institutional-grade compliance and security.
Tazapay has raised Series B funding from Peak XV Partners, Ripple, Circle Ventures, Norinchukin Capital, and GMO VenturePartners, with continued support from earlier investors including Sequoia Capital Southeast Asia, the PayPal Alumni Fund, January Capital, RTP Global, and ARC180.

The platform allows businesses to transact with ease in 173+ countries, offering an array of card and local payment options. Its unified interface simplifies the onboarding process, allowing businesses to partner with just one entity, thereby streamlining their payment process. 

Tazapay continues to drive global business growth by offering a trusted and accessible platform for cross-border e-commerce transactions.

Disclaimer: Stablecoin solutions are offered solely by Tazapay Canada Corp.
company.name: Tazapay

company.description: New Innovations is a Japanese startup developing AI-driven coffee robotics including the ROOT C unmanned cafe system, blending hardware engineering with operational software for self-serve beverage retail.
company.name: New Innovations

company.description: Aurora Hydrogen is a Canadian clean energy company developing microwave-driven methane pyrolysis technology to produce clean hydrogen without CO2 emissions. Founded in 2021 and headquartered in Edmonton, the company targets hydrogen production for industrial decarbonization, including maritime shipping.
company.name: Aurora Hydrogen

company.description: Sawa Energy develops, finances, builds, owns, and operates solar photovoltaic and energy storage systems for commercial and industrial clients under long-term service contracts (10-25 years). Through its energy-as-a-service model, businesses access reliable and affordable clean electricity without upfront capital investment, while reducing reliance on diesel generators. The company has deployed over 65 solar installations across Rwanda and Uganda, serving sectors including agro-processing, manufacturing, mining, hospitality, education, and commercial real estate.
company.name: Sawa Energy

company.description: Brazil's leading e-prescription healthtech platform connecting patients, providers, pharmacies, and services across the healthcare journey. Offers digital prescriptions, scheduling, and pharmacy services.
company.name: Mevo

company.description: Elmed Life Sciences is a Hyderabad-based science-led biotech company developing probiotic and biological solutions for animal health, aquaculture, agriculture, and human health. The company manufactures microbiome-based products designed to boost productivity, immunity, and sustainability across multiple sectors. Elmed partners with pharmaceutical and agricultural firms including Aurobindo and Apex Laboratories, and focuses on expanding microbiome innovation across India and globally.
company.name: Elmed Life Sciences

company.description: Enclave is a Tel Aviv-based application security company that emerged from stealth in 2026. The startup builds tools to identify the most critical security vulnerabilities in AI-generated code, providing independent security oversight for the AI era of software development. Its platform helps engineering teams detect and remediate dangerous flaws buried in code written by AI coding assistants.
company.name: Enclave

company.description: Sepion Technologies is an advanced materials company developing polymer coatings and membrane technologies to improve the performance, safety, and cost of energy storage systems. Its ultra-thin, drop-in separator coatings replace legacy ceramic materials, enabling higher energy density, faster charging, and improved durability without changes to existing battery manufacturing lines. Founded by Peter Frischmann, Sepion's products support customer qualification with leading global battery manufacturers for EV, ESS, and consumer electronics applications. The company's polymer platform also extends into adjacent markets including critical material separations, gas separations, chemical sensing, and water treatment.
company.name: Sepion Technologies

company.description: The Sentience Company builds personal AI that thinks, remembers, and acts like you. The platform captures your entire digital life and creates a high-fidelity digital simulation that understands your knowledge, reflects your voice, and operates with your context — retrieving what matters and acting on your behalf.
company.name: The Sentience Company

company.description: BeastLife is an Indian D2C nutrition brand selling protein powders, creatine, mass gainers and other supplements through its website, e-commerce platforms, and quick commerce channels. Co-founded by fitness influencer Gaurav Taneja and former mCaffeine executive Raj Vikram Gupta in 2024, the company is currently net profitable and projected to close FY26 with approximately Rs 100 crore in revenue.
company.name: BeastLife

company.description: Megapot is a crypto-powered global lottery platform built on blockchain technology. The company enables users to participate in online jackpots for $1 per entry using cryptocurrency, with the goal of bringing a billion people onto blockchain networks via accessible lottery products. Megapot is backed by leading crypto venture investors and positions itself as a gateway to blockchain adoption for mainstream audiences.
company.name: Megapot

company.description: Payy Network (formerly Polybase) is a New York-based stablecoin startup building a privacy-first Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain where transactions are private by default using zero-knowledge proofs. The platform enables private stablecoin payments and offers a wallet and Visa card for end users. Payy targets individuals and businesses seeking confidential on-chain transactions without sacrificing programmability or composability.
company.name: Payy Network

company.description: Pluri Inc. develops a proprietary three-dimensional cell expansion platform to produce scalable, consistent cell-based solutions for multiple industries. Its technology is applied across regenerative medicine, immunotherapy, CDMO services, foodtech and agtech, enabling manufacture of human, animal and plant cells. Pluri also develops placenta-derived cell therapy candidates such as PLX-PAD and PLX-R18 for inflammatory, muscle injury and hematologic conditions.
company.name: Pluri Inc.

company.description: SolidWatts designs and manufactures solid-state Radio Frequency (RF) heating systems for industrial process heating, enabling electrification of energy-intensive industrial processes. Their platform delivers high-efficiency dielectric heating from 1 kW to megawatt-scale, targeting machine builders, integrators and end users across food processing, chemical processing and recycling. Founded in 2022 and based in Pully, Switzerland, the company emphasizes scalable power, digital process control and long service life as differentiators.
company.name: SolidWatts SA

company.description: MESH is a Swiss construction-technology startup and ETH Zurich spin-off that builds a robotics platform to automate rebar handling, placement and joining for reinforced concrete. Its system combines planning software with industrial robotics to let rebar teams change project specifications without complex reprogramming, targeting contractors and prefab rebar manufacturers in commercial and infrastructure construction.
company.name: MESH

company.description: LOVB Salt Lake is a professional women’s volleyball team that competes in League One Volleyball (LOVB), a U.S.-based youth-to-pro volleyball league. The team features a roster of professional athletes and serves as a bridge between youth volleyball clubs and the professional game. LOVB Salt Lake is focused on growing local fan engagement and developing facilities and community programs to support the sport’s growth in the region.

company.description: Andesa Services provides cloud-based policy lifecycle and administration solutions for the life insurance and annuities market, supporting products such as BOLI, COLI, ICOLI and private placement business. Its core offering combines a Financial Administration System (AFAS) with third-party administration and product/actuarial support to help carriers, brokers and plan sponsors manage transactions, billing, reporting and claims. Headquartered in Allentown, Pennsylvania, Andesa emphasizes long client relationships, data security, and configurable, transaction-based policy administration tools.

company.description: Helix Earth develops accelerated liquid–gas chemistry hardware derived from NASA aerospace research to improve commercial HVAC performance, humidity management, industrial filtration, and CO2 capture. The company’s retrofit systems enable rapid liquid–gas reactions to reduce energy use and lower the cost and footprint of capture equipment, targeting building owners, facility managers, and industrial operators. Headquartered in Houston, Helix Earth’s technology originated from Rice University research and is positioned for manufacturing scale-up and commercial deployment.

The product emphasizes compliance features such as TCPA opt-in management and multi-year retention, targeting RIAs and advisory teams looking for a modern, compliant client-communication solution. company.description: VITL builds an operating system for cash-pay healthcare focused on e-prescribing and pharmacy management for clinics operating outside traditional insurance. Its platform provides a single login to replace multiple pharmacy portals, real-time price comparisons across verified compounding pharmacies, multi-pharmacy ordering in one transaction, and end-to-end patient tracking from prescription to doorstep. VITL primarily serves cash-pay medical practices such as med spas, hormone and peptide clinics, anti-aging and weight-loss centers, functional medicine, and concierge medicine providers.

The company partners with hospitals, health systems, universities, and financial institutions across the U.S. to scale retention-driven hiring programs.

Its product suite includes a benefits admin platform, teleconsultations, health checkups, and a claims experience aimed at reducing processing time through automation and integrations with insurers and HRIS tools. Plum serves thousands of companies across India and focuses on improving the end-to-end healthcare experience for employees through data-driven workflows and customer-focused service.

company.description: Terrestrial (formerly Vaxess Technologies) develops a temperature-stable microarray patch (MAP) platform for transdermal delivery of therapies, enabling self-administration without needles or refrigeration. The company provides autonomy software and integrated aircraft and sensor solutions for military and allied customers to enable operations in GPS- and communications-denied environments. Founded in 2015 and headquartered in San Diego, Shield AI focuses on mission-critical autonomy, edge software and simulation for air operations.

company.description:
Inventors of URTHCOR®. To achieve this, we’ve built a passionate team of polymer engineering, corrugated paper, plastic manufacturing and sustainable packaging experts, all fuelled by a desire for sustainable innovation.

Our platform helps healthcare brands identify which healthcare consumers are most likely to convert before campaigns launch - reducing guesswork, improving efficiency, and accelerating growth.

company.description: Deccan AI builds high-quality, human-verified datasets and evaluation tooling for training and post-training of large AI models.
